The practical value of the study lies in the proposal of a methodology for assessing the impact of cybersecurity on brand sustainability and the effectiveness of digital communications.

title: Deep Learning-Based Brain Tumor Detection in MRI Using a Hybrid YOLOv8 and InceptionV3 Model
authors: Asem Khmag, Mawada Shhob, Shymaa Khmag
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: Brain tumor detection and localization from Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) scans represent a critical task in medical image analysis, where accuracy and robustness are essential for clinical decision support. This study proposes an intelligent deep learning-based framework for automated brain tumor detection using a hybrid architecture that integrates YOLOv8 with InceptionV3. The proposed model is designed to accurately identify and localize different types of brain tumors, including glioma, meningioma, and pituitary tumors, within complex MRI environments. The workflow begins with an advanced data preparation stage that includes preprocessing, normalization, and optimized cropping to enhance region-of-interest (ROI) representation while reducing irrelevant background information. The hybrid model leverages YOLOv8's real-time object detection capabilities alongside InceptionV3's multi-scale feature extraction to improve localization precision and classification accuracy. Model optimization is performed using the AdamW optimizer ensure stable convergence and efficient training. The proposed framework offers a reliable and efficient computer-aided diagnosis (CAD) solution, highlighting its potential for assisting medical professionals in early and accurate brain tumor detection. Furthermore, the proposed method shows quantitative performance with mAP 98.40% Precision 97.20%, Recall 95.80 % and F1-Score 0.96. In particular, the high sensitivity obtained for malignant tumor classes indicates the system's effectiveness in reducing false negative cases, which is critical in clinical diagnosis.

title: A Blockchain-Based ECDSA Framework for Drug Authentication and Traceability in Bangladeshi Healthcare
authors: Sri Apurbo Saha, Mst Shimla Sinthia, Shamim Ahmad, Istiaque Ahmed
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Counterfeit pharmaceuticals pose a critical public health threat in Bangladesh and globally. According to the World Health Organization (WHO), around 10% of medical products in low- and middle-income countries are substandard or falsified, with some regions exceeding 30%. In Bangladesh, industry reports indicate that nearly 40% of medicines are counterfeit or substandard, accounting for over BDT 1,500 crore in illegal sales annually. These fake drugs lead to therapeutic failure, adverse reactions, antimicrobial resistance, and preventable deaths-eroding trust in the healthcare system. To combat this, we propose a hybrid blockchain-based authentication and traceability framework tailored for the Bangladeshi pharmaceutical supply chain. It integrates a permissioned Hyperledger Fabric network for immutable drug lifecycle logging and a two-layered cryptographic scheme. Certificateless aggregate signatures enable efficient participant authentication without traditional certificates, while ECDSA-signed QR codes on packaging allow users to verify authenticity using public blockchain records. The system marks a product as “Sold” upon first verification to prevent reuse, with zero cost for subsequent checks. Smart contracts manage identity, shipment, and sale updates while ensuring privacy and throughput. This model offers a scalable, transparent, and secure approach to mitigating drug counterfeiting in Bangladesh.

title: A MERN Stack Framework for Real Estate Platforms Leveraging Conversational AI and Predictive Analytics
authors: Jahnvi Tiwari, Praveen Raj, Anushka Garg, Mahendra Pratap Yadav, Priyanka Singh, Dheeraj Dubey
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: SmartEstate is an AI-augmented real estate platform developed with the MERN stack to rectify inefficiencies in online property search and management. The system offers secure, role-based access for buyers, sellers, and administrators through JWT and Google OAuth, and incorporates an NLP-driven conversational chatbot for natural language property discovery. A rental price prediction engine utilising ensemble learning (Random Forest Regressor) was developed using a dataset of 3,335 properties in Bengaluru, achieving great predicted accuracy (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.94 for training, 0.80 for testing). The platform features an administrative dashboard, real-time listing management, and scalable REST APIs hosted on cloud infrastructure. Load-testing outcomes validate dependable responsiveness during simultaneous usage, indicating practicality for real-world implementation. SmartEstate enhances transparency, personalisation, and decision-making in property searches, while offering a modular framework for future enhancements, like virtual tours, regulatory API connection, and language support.

title: Hybrid Multi-Stage Clustering for the General Pickup and Delivery Problem
authors: Hanen Aydi, Sameh Chtourou, Adnen El-Amraoui, Ahmed Frikha, François Delmotte
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: This paper addresses the General Pickup and Delivery Problem (GPDP), a complex combinatorial optimization challenge characterized by strict operational constraints. It introduces a hybrid clustering framework that combines capacitated K-Medoids for feasibility, Basic Variable Neighborhood Search (BVNS) to escape local optima, and Ward's algorithm to enhance spatial compactness and separation. The proposed approach is evaluated on the Li & Lim benchmark (56 instances with 50 pickup–delivery requests (100 nodes) each) across Location-Clustered (LC), Location-Random (LR), and Location-Clustered-Random (LRC) topologies. Clustering quality is assessed through a multi-criteria evaluation using the Analytic Hierarchy Process (AHP). The results show that BVNS + Ward's consistently outperforms K-Medoids, yielding higher explained variance, improved compactness, and better load balance, thus demonstrating its robustness and practical value for real-world logistics applications. The optimal threshold <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R_{T}^{2}$</tex> refers to the target explained variance used in Ward's algorithm to determine when to stop merging clusters, ensuring both compactness and operational feasibility of the solution.

title: Real-Time Mine Safety Alert System for Gas Leakage Detection Using Hybrid GWO-PSO Optimized Machine Learning
authors: P Srinivasan, T Keerthi Nethra, K Arulaanandham, M Santhosh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Gas leakage is a problematic safety aspect in both residential and industrial settings, such that gas leakage detection must occur with high accuracy in real time. The important aspect of this research is to develop a smart gas leakage detection system by incorporating gas detection with machine learning hybrid with different optimization algorithm. The acquired gas concentration data from sensors are processed through an Arduino microcontroller and analyzed on an optimized decision ML model. The GWO - PSO algorithm with hybrid to random forest and XG Boost dynamically optimizes the thresholds of detection and system parameters, hence improving the accuracy and reducing the false alarm probabilities. Upon detecting abnormal levels of gas concentration, the activated mechanisms include a local alert and sending notifications via wireless communication modules for remote monitoring. Experimental results prove that the response time, accuracy of detection, and reliability of the proposed system are better than conventional approaches that use only fixed thresholds, and therefore, this work suits well for real-time applications intended for safety purposes.

title: An Analysis of Insects in Thai Literature
authors: Wanwiwat Ruttanalum
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Cybernetics and Innovations (ICCI)
abstract: This study originated from an interest in Patrick Lafcadio Hearn, a literary scholar and writer renowned for introducing Japanese ghost stories and the supernatural to Western readers. One area of Hearn's serious scholarly engagement is the representation of insects in literature, as demonstrated in his influential work Insect Literature, a comparative study of insects in the literary traditions of Greek, English, French, and Japanese. Hearn's work prompted a reconsideration of insects in Thai literature, revealing a significant research gap, as no existing studies have treated insects as a central object of literary analysis. Previous research has generally addressed insects only as part of the natural world, grouping them with other animals rather than examining their distinct symbolic and cultural roles. This research, therefore, aims to investigate the roles and significance of insects in Thai literature through the framework of cultural entomology. As an initial phase, the study focuses on analyzing insect appearances in Thai literary texts. A survey of 420 Thai literary works composed from the Sukhothai period to the reign of King Rama VIII of the Rattanakosin era reveals that 208 texts contain references to insects. These references are classified into eighteen categories. The five most frequently mentioned insect groups are: (1) bees, (2) blood-feeding insects, (3) cicadas, (4) fireflies, and (5) houseflies/fruit flies. Among these groups, bees, cicadas, and houseflies/fruit flies appear prominently in both Thai and foreign literary traditions, whereas blood-feeding insects and fireflies are infrequently discussed in foreign literature but occur frequently in Thai poetry. The presence of insects in Thai literature thus reflects both shared worldviews between Thai poets and those of other cultures, as well as culturally specific perceptions of insects. Notably, bees occupy a particularly significant position: they are referred to by as many as nineteen different terms and are employed by poets not only in literal descriptions but also as metaphors related to sexuality, gender relations, and human sexual behavior.

title: Enhancing Connectivity, Security, and Energy Efficiency in Contemporary Residential Settings using AI with Li-Fi Technology for Smart Homes
authors: V Vijayalakshmi, V.C. Ranganayaki, S. Saranya, Sami Askar, Pallavi Giri, G. Karthikeyan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Smart homes offer unparalleled convenience, automation, and connectivity, but they also pose security, energy efficiency, and network dependability issues. We investigate the integration of artificial intelligence (AI) with Light Fidelity (Li-Fi) technology as a cutting-edge solution to improve household connectivity, security, and energy efficiency. Li-Fi, which transmits data via visible light, provides high-speed internet while reducing RF interference and congestion. AI-driven systems improve data transmission, resource allocation, and real-time decision-making in smart homes, making them more efficient and safer. Our approach leverages AI algorithms to intelligently manage the allocation of network resources in Li-Fi-enabled smart homes, ensuring seamless connectivity even as the number of devices and data demands increase. Moreover, AI-driven security systems are employed to detect and mitigate potential threats, providing robust protection against cyberattacks and unauthorized access. In terms of energy efficiency, AI models predict usage patterns and dynamically adjust lighting, appliances, and Li-Fi-based communication to reduce energy consumption. Not only can the combination of artificial intelligence with $\mathbf{L i}$-Fi improve the user experience, but it also helps to promote environmentally responsible energy habits and strengthens network security. The findings of this study reveal that artificial intelligence and lithium-ion batteries have the potential to transform smart home infrastructure by providing a solution that is both scalable and energy-efficient, thereby satisfying the ever-increasing desires of contemporary residential settings.

title: A High-Precision Simulation Method of Electromagnetic Scattering Based on Convolutional Neural Network
authors: Jie Luo, Lingfeng Kong, Rongying Shao
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: With the refinement of electromagnetic wave theory, electromagnetic technology has developed rapidly. The forward and inverse problems of electromagnetic scattering, as fundamental theories for electromagnetic simulation, have significant research value. Traditional computational electromagnetics methods face challenges in solving complex high-dimensional problems. This paper proposes a high-precision simulation method for electromagnetic scattering forward and inverse problems based on convolutional neural networks (CNNs). By integrating neural networks into the solution process, it establishes data-driven mapping relationships, avoiding complex iterative calculations of traditional numerical methods. During training, parallel processing techniques can accelerate convolutional operations and pooling operations, enabling the model to achieve stronger generalization capabilities with less data volume. This reduces traditional methods' dependence on initial data, facilitating neural network modeling under small datasets for efficient simulations.

title: Research on PPG Signal Denoising Based on Improved Grey Wolf Optimizer and Variational Mode Decomposition
authors: Hao Yang, Mei-lin Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: This study aims to develop an adaptive denoising framework for photoplethysmography (PPG) signals to improve waveform fidelity under complex noise conditions. An IGWO-VMD-WT method is proposed, in which an improved grey wolf optimizer (IGWO) is employed to jointly optimize the mode number and penalty factor of variational mode decomposition (VMD) using minimum envelope entropy as the fitness function. Based on the optimized VMD results, noise-dominated and signal-related modes are identified and reconstructed through wavelet refinement to suppress electromyographic interference and baseline drift. The method is evaluated on both simulated PPG signals and real clinical data. Quantitative results show that the proposed method achieves an SNR of 20.74 dB, RMSE of 0.0504, and NCC of 0.9958 on simulated data, outperforming WT, EMD, and conventional VMD. The results demonstrate that the proposed approach effectively enhances denoising performance while preserving pulse waveform morphology.

title: Implementation of Machine Learning (ML) in Industrial IoT Systems: Smart Manufacturing Lines Fault Detection and Maintenance Prediction in Real Time
authors: Shiva Shankar Mummidi, Safeyah Tawil, Naga Sekhar Madala, D. Kirubakaran, P. Hareesh, Y Krishna Bhargavi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Since Industry 4.0 is growing rapidly, IIoT technologies are being incorporated into smart industrial environments. IIoT allows real-time data collection, networked gear, and intelligent automation, but unexpected equipment failures, unscheduled downtime, and inefficient maintenance plans still plague manufacturing systems. Rule-based monitoring and reactive maintenance cannot handle the amount, speed, and complexity of sensor data provided by modern manufacturing lines. A framework for smart production lines enabled by the Industrial Internet of Things (IoT) that employ Machine Learning to detect defects and estimate maintenance needs in real time is presented in this paper. Supervised, unsupervised, and hybrid ML methods are used to analyse operating logs, machine performance measures, and high-frequency sensor data. Discovering early concerns and estimating the lifespan of industrial assets are made possible through predictive modeling, anomaly detection, and better feature extraction. System optimization in dynamic operating settings is made possible by adaptive learning and rapid data streaming. When compared to traditional monitoring systems, ML-based defect detection significantly improves detection accuracy, reduces the number of false alarms, and allows for proactive scheduling of maintenance. This strategy is applicable to many different businesses since it decreases equipment downtime, saves time, and increases asset life.

title: LeafLife: An Explainable Deep Learning Framework with Robustness for Grape Leaf Disease Recognition
authors: B. M. ShahriaAlam, Md. Nasim Ahmed
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Plant disease diagnosis is essential to farmers’ management choices because plant diseases frequently lower crop yield and product quality. For harvests to flourish and agricultural productivity to boost, grape leaf disease detection is important. The plant disease dataset contains grape leaf diseases total of 9,032 images of four classes, among them three classes are leaf diseases, and the other one is healthy leaves. After rigorous pre-processing dataset was split (70% training, 20% validation, 10% testing), and two pre-trained models were deployed: InceptionV3 and Xception. Xception shows a promising result of 96.23% accuracy, which is remarkable than InceptionV3. Adversarial Training is used for robustness, along with more transparency. Grad-CAM is integrated to confirm the leaf disease. Finally deployed a web application using Streamlit with a heatmap visualization and prediction with confidence level for robust grape leaf disease classification.

title: Simplified Methodology to Estimate Minimized Land Impact for Renewable Self-Sufficiency in Small Energy Systems
authors: David Dorta-Hernández, David Cañadillas-Ramallo, Paula Pérez-Romero, José Francisco Gómez, Benjamín González-Díaz, Ricardo Guerrero-Lemus
publish: 2026 IEEE Green Technologies Conference (GreenTech)
abstract: This study evaluates how to minimize land use and achieve renewable energy self-sufficiency in small systems, using Fuerteventura, Spain, as a case study. The analysis focuses on the island’s 2030 and 2040 decarbonization targets. The energy mix relies on solar and wind resources, prioritizing self-consumption, with storage using batteries and hydrogen. A conservative approach to full electrification is applied, considering three storage stages: the first 20% of renewable generation operates without significant storage, the next 60% is supported by one day of battery storage, and the final 20% relies on hydrogen storage covering 14 days of consumption. Geographic analysis of existing installations estimates land requirements. Results indicate land demand ranges from 12.3 km<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> in fully photovoltaic scenarios with high self-consumption to 81.7 km<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> in fully onshore wind scenarios. Findings highlight that, with simple methodologies, small systems can check decarbonization targets, with balanced renewable mixes and optimized storage, minimizing land impact.

title: Service Robotics for Automated Tourist Assistance in Smart Destinations
authors: Maher Odeh Falah Alshamaileh, Lama'a Al-Orainat, Hassan Ali Al-Ababneh, Fatin A. Elhaj, Ahmed Ameen, Mhran Alzoubi, Ahmed Farhan Al-Amayreh
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: As tourist contexts become more complex and the need for personalized, real-time requests grows within smart destinations, such needs call for innovative technology solutions. Service robotics is a creative way to do so by using autonomous navigation, contextual awareness and interactive services. This paper applies a Design Science Research methodology to design and analyze a service robotics framework for automated tourist assistance in smart destination ecosystems. The research begins with the identification of operational and informational gaps in existing digital tourist services, followed by the definition of design objectives focused on adaptability, accessibility, and scalability. Based on these objectives, a modular robotic assistance artifact is developed, integrating mobile robotic agents, cloudbased knowledge management, and context-aware interaction mechanisms. The proposed artifact is demonstrated through representative tourism scenarios, including urban navigation, transport hub assistance, and event-based visitor support. The evaluation is conducted using scenario-based analysis, focusing on functional coverage, service responsiveness, and system integration capabilities. The evaluation uses quantitative serviceperformance indicators (SCR, RTRI, CSQI, SLRC) to compare human-only versus robotic-assisted assistance across representative scenarios. The results show improved coverage, reduced response time, extended availability, and measurable workload redistribution, providing an actionable KPI toolkit for smartdestination planning. Abbreviations and notation - SCR - Service Coverage Ratio; RTRI - Response Time Reduction Index; CSQI - Composite Service Quality Index; SLRC - Service Load Redistribution Coefficient; Vd - daily assistance requests; Th - mean duration of human-assisted service; Ch - cost per human interaction; Lh - number of supported languages (human-based); Ah - service availability (hours/day); Tr - average robotic assistance time; Ar - robotic service availability; Cr - cost per robotic interaction; Ns - successfully serviced requests; Nh - requests handled exclusively by human staff.

title: The Impact of AI-Enhanced E-portfolios on EFL Learners' Writing Proficiency and Self-efficacy: A Quasi-Experimental Study
authors: Qi-Min Xiao, Ning-Hua Liu
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 9th International Conference on Education and E-Learning
abstract: None

title: Event-Triggered MPC With Linear Inter-Event Control for AV Path Tracking
authors: Zhaodong Zhou, Jun Chen
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Model predictive control (MPC) is widely used for autonomous vehicle path tracking due to its ability to handle system constraints and optimize performance over a prediction horizon. However, frequent online optimization imposes high computational demands, making the real-time implementation of MPC challenging. Event-triggered MPC aims to solve this issue by updating control actions only when a predefined condition is met, but it executes precomputed control sequences in an open-loop fashion between events, potentially allowing errors to accumulate. This paper proposes an event-triggered MPC framework integrated with a linear inter-event control mechanism to address this limitation. The proposed inter-event controller applies a least-squares-based linear model to generate control inputs in real-time during inter-event periods, enabling continuous feedback corrections. Experimental results on a Quanser QCar2 platform demonstrate that the proposed approach improves tracking accuracy by 10% while significantly reducing the number of MPC optimizations compared to standard event-triggered MPC, offering an efficient solution for real-time path tracking problem.

title: RAW-PM: Reliability-Aware Weighted Polar Mapping for Hard-Decision Decoding in 3-D NAND Flash Memory
authors: Debao Wei, Huqi Xiang, Dixin Ma, Yongchao Wang, Liyan Qiao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
abstract: As 3-D <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">nand</small> flash memory continues to scale toward higher density, significant reliability disparities arise among different pages in triple-level cell (TLC) flash, posing severe challenges to error-correction codes (ECCs) in the middle and late stages of flash lifetime. Polar codes have been introduced into flash storage systems due to their near-capacity performance; however, their decoding heavily relies on the accuracy of log-likelihood ratios (LLRs). Hard-decision sensing in flash memory leads to severe loss of soft information, thereby limiting the achievable error-correction performance. To address these challenges, this article proposes a page-aware reliability optimization scheme for polar codes in 3-D TLC flash memory. First, a page-aware polar bit mapping (PA-PBM) scheme is proposed, which exploits the mismatch between polar bit importance and page reliability by mapping critical bits to highly reliable pages and frozen bits to less reliable pages, thereby reducing the probability of critical errors. Building upon PA-PBM, a reliability-aware weighted polar mapping (RAW-PM) scheme is further developed, which incorporates page-level raw bit error rate (RBER) information to construct confidence-differentiated pseudo-soft LLRs and refines the bit mapping strategy, enabling weighted path metric (PM) correction and improving decoding accuracy under hard-decision sensing. Extensive experiments are conducted on a flash memory test platform to evaluate the proposed schemes. The results demonstrate that RAW-PM significantly improves the frame error-correction success rate in the middle and late stages of flash lifetime and reduces the post-ECC uncorrectable bit error rate (UBER) by more than one order of magnitude. Under typical large-scale access, RAW-PM introduces no additional read operations, requires no changes to the decoder architecture, and achieves these gains with minimal control and computational overhead, demonstrating excellent practical feasibility.

title: Image Dehazing of Unmanned Aerial Vehicles Based on Physics-Guided Diffusion Model
authors: Yuxin Zhu, Jun Li, Qinghong Sheng, Xiao Ling, Bo Wang, Fan Gao, Piji Li
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Drone vision is often degraded by cloud occlusion, which causes blurring or loss of surface observation information and significantly hinders subsequent image processing and interpretation tasks. Notably, fine details of ground objects can sometimes remain partially visible beneath thin clouds. Meanwhile, recent advances in diffusion models have demonstrated strong generative capabilities, making them promising candidates for addressing thin cloud occlusion. In this paper, we propose a physics-guided image dehazing framework for drone vision based on diffusion model. The framework operates in two stages. In the first stage, a multi-scale non-local U-Net, guided by the atmospheric scattering model, is designed to estimate physical parameters such as the transmittance map and global atmospheric light. In the second stage, the strong generative capacity of the diffusion model is integrated with the physical priors obtained in the first stage, endowing the diffusion process with physical awareness to better compensate for information loss caused by clouds.Extensive experiments on both synthetic and real-world datasets demonstrate that the proposed method achieves competitive performance compared to state-of-the-art approaches.

title: Performance Evaluation of Electrical Models for Bifacial PV Modules
authors: Sarah Djabali, Shamsodin Taheri, Ana-Maria Cretu, Edris Pouresmaeil
publish: IEEE Journal of Photovoltaics
abstract: This article presents a robust and realistic evaluation of bifacial photovoltaic models. The approach mirrors standard characterization practice, enabling assessment not only of the intrinsic model accuracy but also its practical expandability under real operating conditions. The evaluation extends beyond conventional bifacial analysis and explicitly addresses rear-only performance, often neglected in the literature. The models examined are as follows: first, standard single-diode model (SDM), second, parallel-SDM, third, photocurrent-SDM, fourth, SeriesRes-SDM, and fifth, DiodeRes-SDM. Experimental measurements using Renogy RSP220DT-G1 module were conducted under different meteorological conditions, tilt angles, and surface albedo for validation. The results indicate that the DiodeRes-SDM achieves the highest bifacial accuracy [mean absolute error (MAE): 4.89%], while the SeriesRes-SDM is a comparable, simpler alternative (5.48%). Photocurrent-SDM and Parallel-SDM are most accurate for rear-only operation but perform poorly in bifacial mode. The Standard-SDM, while the simplest, introduces higher errors (7.92%). The evaluation confirms that rear operation exhibits distinct dynamics that require the development of rear-specific models. Therefore, choosing the appropriate bifacial model based on the specific application, bifacial yield prediction, system design, or installation condition optimization, is crucial for optimizing both modeling accuracy and computational efficiency.

title: CLCT: Complementary Local Consensus Transformer for Two-View Correspondence Pruning_supp1-3687024.pdf
authors: Jinhui Tang
publish: N/A
abstract: Correspondence pruning extracts accurate correspondences (inliers) from initial ones, playing a crucial role in computer vision and robotics. This process is challenging due to the dominance of outliers and uneven correspondence distributions, which prevent fixed neighbor selection from reliably capturing local consistency and hinder the effective integration of local consistency into global modeling. To tackle these challenges, we introduce local consistency into the Transformer as a guidance signal for the first time, which allows it to adaptively perceive and enforce local matching constraints and consequently enhances global relationship modeling under uneven correspondence distributions. Motivated by this, we propose the Complementary Local Consensus Transformer (CLCT), which captures complementary local consistency by the Adaptive Feature Pruning (AFP) Graph and the Dynamic Weighted (DW) Graph and integrates them into Transformers to enhance global modeling. Specifically, the AFP graph adopts an adaptive pruning strategy to select high-quality correspondences with dynamic neighborhood adjustment, while the DW graph employs a Feature-Aware Distance block with Structural Salient Convolution to refine weighted neighborhood relationships. These graphs guide AFPTransformer and DWGTransformer, enabling a cooperative “point selection + edge weighting” mechanism that integrates local reliability with global reasoning. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed CLCT outperforms existing methods across multiple tasks.

title: EHiJackNet: A Quantized Hierarchical Deep Learning Framework for Efficient and Robust Jackfruit Disease Diagnosis on Edge Devices
authors: V Sarvesh, S Hemanth Raja, Pranav Jeevanantham, Swarna Priya Ramu
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Jackfruit cultivation is highly susceptible to various leaf and fruit diseases, which significantly impact agricultural yield and economy. Traditional manual identification of disease is labor-intensive, time-expensive, and human error may occur. While modern Deep Learning (DL) models have shown promising results and improvements in automated agricultural disease detection, standard flat-classification architectures often struggle with inter-domain confusion thereby frequently misclassifying fruit diseases as leaf textures due to background noise. Furthermore, heavy architectures like ResNet-50 are computationally expensive, limiting their deployment on resource-constrained edge devices. To address these challenges, this research proposes EHiJackNet, an Efficient Hierarchical DL framework for the accurate diagnosis of jackfruit diseases. EHiJackNet employs a two-stage "divide-and-conquer" routing mechanism built upon an optimized EfficientNetV2-S backbone. The first stage contextually routes the input image as either a leaf or a fruit, ensuring that the subsequent fine-grained disease classifiers process only relevant visual data. Evaluated on the Jackfruit AgroVision dataset containing 2,195 images across six classes, the model proposed by this research achieves a superior classification accuracy of 96.36%, exceeding state-of-the-art models including a Custom CNN (75.24%), GoogLeNet (87.33%), and ResNet-50 (92.38%). Comprehensive computational analysis reveals that while the modular three-model pipeline of EHiJackNet requires more storage space of 544 MB, it splits the computational load, processing each image with highly specialized networks to achieve an unprecedented 96.36% accuracy, effectively outperforming the flat-classification approach of ResNet-50. EHiJackNet achieves high speed by processing 64.53 images every second. We also used model quantization to lower memory usage. We used Float16 and INT8 methods to compress the system. The FLOAT16 version reduced the size of individual model components to only 38.52 megabytes. These updates make the framework highly efficient for on-device inference in resource-constrained rural environments.

title: An Adaptive AI-Driven Threat Intelligence Framework for Proactive Cyber Defense in Dynamic Healthcare Information Systems
authors: Tirumala Ashish Kumar Manne, Mahmood Afzal Hussain, Prashant Sumanprasad Bhadoria, Balakrishnan Devaraj, Karthik Bojja, Rishiraj Kohli
publish: 2026 International Conference on Machine Learning and Autonomous Systems (ICMLAS)
abstract: Digitization of healthcare systems, which has become more prominent due to the use of technologies such as cloud computing, the Internet of Medical Things (IoMT), and interoperable electronic health records, has put healthcare infrastructures in a greater place at risk of cyber threats. Conventional reactive security systems are not sufficient in the fight against sophisticated, dynamic and zero-day attacks on sensitive patient information and vital clinical services. The paper presents an Adaptive AI-Driven Threat Intelligence Framework that would be used to proactively counter the effects of cyberattacks in dynamic health care setting. The framework incorporates machine learning and deep learning applications of constant monitoring, behavioral analysis, and predictive detection of threats in heterogeneous healthcare systems. It identifies new and suspicious patterns of attacks by collecting and comparing real-time data on network traffic, system logs, user logs, and external threat information sources. The dynamic aspect of the framework also enables real-time adaptation of the framework, whereby automated detection model and security policy changes are made. Concentrated on scalability, automation, and explainability, it improves the speed of threat detection early, decreases response time, and positively impacts cyber resilience in healthcare systems besides being compliant with healthcare data protection laws.

title: Adaptive Spatial-Temporal Feature Fusion Network for Multilingual Text Recognition
authors: Praetawan Jarutan, Sirawan Phiphitphatphaisit, Zagon Bussabong, Artitayaporn Rojarath, Olarik Surinta
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Cybernetics and Innovations (ICCI)
abstract: Multilingual scene text recognition presents significant technical complexity due to diverse character structures, varying scripts, background clutter, and contextual dependencies among character sequences. This study proposes an Adaptive Spatial-Temporal Feature Fusion Network (ASTFF-Net) for multilingual scene text recognition. The framework incorporates a multi-layer adaptive spatialtemporal feature fusion architecture into a sequence-based text recognition, integrating a spatiotemporal feature extractor with a Connectionist Temporal Classification (CTC) decoder. A deep ResNet-50 backbone captures hierarchical spatial representations, which are reshaped into ordered sequences to preserve horizontal structural information. Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) layers model contextual dependencies across adjacent character regions, while a one-dimensional convolution enhances temporal consistency. An adaptive fusion mechanism combines spatial and temporal features to generate discriminative representations that encode both visual structure and contextual continuity. The end-to-end training strategy with the CTC objective supports alignment-free sequence learning without character-level annotation. Experimental evaluation on the Multi-language Video Subtitle (MLVS) and Thai-English Multi-script Text Image (MSTI) datasets show that the proposed method consistently outperforms convolutional-recurrent and attention-based baselines. The proposed ASTFF-Net achieves competitive character error rates, yielding 1.63 % on MLVS and 13.22 % on the challenging MSTI dataset, while exhibiting stable convergence and robust generalization.

title: Feature-Optimized Blood Glucose Level Prediction with Explainable AI (XAI)
authors: Aditya Das Gupta, Md. Nahin Irtiza, Shihab-Al-Rashid
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Diabetes Mellitus is a global health problem where precise blood glucose prediction is crucial for patient well-being. Although machine learning (ML) models have shown promise, their “black-box” nature often hinders clinical use due to lack of transparency. This study offers a clear and accessible way to develop a blood glucose prediction model that is both accurate and reliable. This research introduces a step-by-step process with a comparison of different feature optimization techniques, and demonstrates that removing outliers can greatly enhance the model’s performance. The study uses a public dataset of 16,969 physiological records to train and validate the models, and shows that removing outliers can significantly improve the model’s performance. The enhanced LightGBM model achieved a Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) of $15.57 \mathrm{mg} / \mathrm{dL}$ on the test set. Importantly, our work includes modern Explainable AI (XAI) methods, such as SHAP and LIME, to make the model’s decision process fully transparent. This work helps bridge the gap between predictive accuracy and clinical trust, offering a strong framework for creating trustworthy AI in diabetes management.

title: Rolling Bearing Fault Diagnosis Based on RBMO-VMD and Multi-Scale Deep Attention Network
authors: Pengyuan Zou
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: This paper addresses the issue that convolutional neural networks (CNNs) compromise signal temporal characteristics during feature extraction. It proposes a rolling bearing fault diagnosis method combining Red-billed Blue Magpie Optimisation Variational Modal Decomposition (RBMO-VMD) with a Multi-scale Deep Attention Network (MDANet). Firstly, the RBMO algorithm adaptively optimises key VMD parameters, effectively preventing modal aliasing and achieving precise vibration signal decomposition. Building upon this, fourteen dimensionless and dimension-dependent features are extracted to form a feature set. For fault classification, a multiscale deep attention network is designed. This architecture captures features across different temporal scales through parallel multiscale convolutional layers, while incorporating an attention mechanism to adaptively enhance key information. Combined with deep fully-connected layers and regularisation techniques, this enhances the model's generalisation capability. Experiments on the CWRU bearing dataset demonstrate that this approach achieves an average diagnostic accuracy of 99.87% for ten fault states, validating the proposed method's effectiveness and robustness in feature extraction and fault classification.

title: Analysis of Concentric Circular Antenna Array Based Radar for Conformable Search and Multiobject Hunting
authors: Md. Farhan Shahriar, Md. Selim Hossain, Md. Mominul Hasan Shawon
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Hunting multiple objects within the same range, azimuth, and elevation cell is a key challenge in radar technology. This is mainly because objects can overlap in space. Multiple objects are often affected by noise and environmental factors. Traditional radars excel at detecting a single target. The performance of the traditional radars decreases when it comes to identifying and tracking several objects at the same time. This research introduces Concentric Circular Antenna Array (CCAA)-based radar to overcome the challenge. The system utilizes an advanced model that features volume search, cued search, and tracking modes. This proposed system adapts to a complex and challenging environment. Advanced filtering techniques are employed to improve detection and reduce side-lobes. Simulations run in MATLAB demonstrate that the proposed CCAA-based radar can effectively search and track multiple moving targets with an impressive accuracy of about 98.80%. This proposed model gives precise details about their position, range, and velocity. These findings suggest that the CCAA-based radar holds great promise for detecting multi-objects in challenging conditions.

title: Design of Wireless Charging Unmanned Vehicle Based on Electromagnetic Compatibility
authors: Haiyan Wu, Liqiang Sheng, Xicheng Zhu, Haoyu Cui
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: To enhance the system stability, charging efficiency, and operational safety of wirelessly charged autonomous vehicles in complex electromagnetic environments, this paper conducts a systematic design study addressing electromagnetic compatibility (EMC) issues. By analyzing the coupling mechanism between the high-frequency electromagnetic fields of wireless charging systems and the sensitive electronic equipment in autonomous vehicles, an interference propagation model is established, and a comprehensive EMC design approach is proposed, encompassing interference source suppression, propagation path control, and protection of disturbed devices. The paper elaborates in detail on the electromagnetic optimization of the wireless charging system' s circuit topology, layout and shielding strategies for onboard electronic equipment, vehicle-level grounding and filtering network design, as well as interference mitigation techniques based on software algorithms. Research results demonstrate that by establishing a multi-level EMC protection framework, internal and external electromagnetic interference can be significantly reduced, achieving highly reliable coordination between wireless charging and autonomous driving functions. Real-vehicle test data indicate that the optimized vehicle meets the requirements of international standards such as ISO 11451 and CISPR 25 in terms of both radiated emissions and immunity, with peak radiated emissions reduced by up to 16 dB, the proportion of time exceeding the threshold for radar ranging error decreased by 94.9%, and GNSS positioning loss-of-lock phenomena completely eliminated. Under dynamic charging conditions, the vehicle's path-tracking lateral error is reduced by 65.6%, validating the feasibility and superiority of the proposed solution in improving overall vehicle EMC performancet.

title: Polynomial Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks for Fault Detection and Diagnosis in Industrial Systems
authors: Lazhar Khriji, Majdi Mansouri, Khadija Attouri, Abdelmalek Kouadri
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: Fault Detection and Diagnosis (FDD) is critical for ensuring safety, reliability, and efficiency in modern industrial systems. Data-driven approaches have gained prominence due to their ability to handle nonlinear processes without relying on exact physical models. However, many machine learning methods lack interpretability, limiting their deployment in safety-critical environments. This paper proposes the use of Polynomial Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (PolyKAN) for FDD in nonlinear industrial processes. PolyKAN employs polynomial basis functions in a Kolmogorov-Arnold Network framework, enabling transparent and structured nonlinear modeling. The approach is validated on a real cement rotary kiln process under healthy and faulty conditions. Experimental results demonstrate high fault classification accuracy with improved interpretability compared to conventional deep learning models. PolyKAN provides a practical and explainable framework for industrial fault diagnosis.

title: A Design Thinking Approach to Developing Songtun for Student Assignment Management in Digital Learning Environments
authors: Thanakrit Janchidfah, Thanayos JindaKaew, Bunyalak Ruamsap, Pisit Chansuek
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Cybernetics and Innovations (ICCI)
abstract: Task management has become a critical challenge for university students in the digital era, particularly in relation to missed deadlines and delayed assignment submissions, which can negatively impact learning performance and time management skills. Although various digital tools are available to support task organization, many existing solutions are not fully developed through genuinely learner-centered approaches, especially within the context of Thai higher education. This study aims to design and develop a mobile application named “Songtun” to assist students in recording, tracking, and managing academic assignments by adopting the Design Thinking methodology as a human-centered innovation framework. The research involved 125 undergraduate students who participated in a real classroom implementation of the Songtun application. The research instruments included the developed application and a five-point Likert-scale satisfaction questionnaire. The development and evaluation process followed the five stages of Design Thinking: empathizing with users' needs, defining key problems, generating solution ideas, prototyping the application, and testing its usability in authentic learning environments. Participants used the application continuously over a two-week period, after which both quantitative and qualitative data were collected to assess system effectiveness and user experience. The findings indicate that the Songtun application effectively supports students in organizing academic tasks, reducing the likelihood of forgetting assignments, and encouraging more timely submission behaviors. Overall user satisfaction was reported at a high level <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(M=4.36, S D=0.52)$</tex>, with usability receiving the highest ratings among evaluated dimensions. These results suggest that integrating Design Thinking principles into the development of educational digital tools can significantly enhance learner experience and promote self-regulated learning practices. The study highlights the potential of the Songtun application as an innovative academic support solution tailored to the needs of students in Thai higher education institutions.

title: Unequal Error Protection Scheme Based on Implicit Partial Product-LDPC Codes
authors: Xuemei Zhu, Jiawei Duan, Zhicheng Huang, Xiaodong Zhang, Kuan Wu, Qianfan Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: This paper proposes an unequal error protection (UEP) scheme based on implicit partial product-LDPC codes. All rows of the source data array are encoded by a low-density parity-check (LDPC) code, while only a prescribed subset of information columns is further protected by a Bose–Chaudhuri–Hocquenghem (BCH) code. The check information generated by the column code is not transmitted explicitly. Instead, it is superimposed on the row LDPC codewords through a free-ride coding mechanism. In this way, the selected columns, referred to as the most important data (MID), are protected by both the row code and the column code, whereas the remaining columns, referred to as the less important data (LID), are protected only by the row code. A turbo-like iterative decoding algorithm is then adopted for the proposed scheme, consisting of free-ride decoding, row decoding, column decoding, and row re-decoding. We further present an approximate performance characterization for the doubly protected MID and a genie-aided lower bound for the singly protected LID. Simulation results show that the proposed scheme provides a substantial reliability gain for the MID, while the LID performance is not sacrificed and can even outperform that of standalone LDPC coding in the medium-to-high signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) region.

title: A Compact Multi-Band Microstrip Antenna with π–H Radiator and Plus-Shaped DGS for mmWave 5G/6G Applications
authors: Kuldip Kumar, Basit Mohi Ud Din Naikoo
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: This Research paper describes a plus-shaped defected ground structure (DGS) based high-gain triple-band π– H slotted microstrip patch antenna, and is simulated and designed for millimeter-wave (mmWave) applications in 5G and 6G communication. The design idea is motivated by the bandwidth enhancement techniques explained in [1], wherein the optimization of a π-slotted dual-band rectangular microstrip antenna was made with response surface methodology (RSM).Here, another H-slot is incorporated within the π-slot structure to enhance impedance matching further and create another resonance, and a plus-shaped DGS is implemented in the ground plane to increase bandwidth and gain.The antenna is proposed employing Rogers RT6002 substrate having a dielectric constant of 2.94 and thickness of 1.6 mm, simulated in CST Microwave Studio. The designed antenna has triple-band operation at 53 GHz, 59 GHz, and 71 GHz with reflection coefficients of −33 dB, −50 dB, and −37 dB, respectively. The VSWR values are 1.049, 1.0084, and 1.0286, which are good impedance matches in all the bands. The antenna offers gains of 5.45 dBi, 4.13 dBi, and 6.10 dBi and directivities of 6.9 dBi, 6.09 dBi, and 8.02 dBi, respectively. The introduction of the π–H slot combination and plus-shaped DGS greatly increases radiation efficiency and multiband behavior, rendering the antenna a good choice for compact, high-gain 5G and mmWave devices.

title: Designing with AI: Exploring Interactive UX Workflows for Adaptive UI Prototyping
authors: Suraj Davariya
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The integration of artificial intelligence into user experience (UX) design is reshaping how designers prototype and refine digital interfaces. This study introduces Canvil, an AI-powered design assistant embedded within Figma, enabling iterative refinement of language-based UI components. Through a participatory user study with professional designers, it explores how AI-generated content influences UX workflows, comparing structured prompting with freeform interaction models. Findings highlight key challenges in AI-assisted design, including balancing automation with creative control and refining AI-generated suggestions to align with user intent. The results contribute insights into optimizing human-AI collaboration in interactive design environments, paving the way for more adaptive and responsive UX tools.

title: Lightweight Data Sharing Scheme Based on Proxy Re-Encryption in IIoT
authors: Bin Sun, Li Wang, Yujie Fu, Zhen Yang, Fengqun Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: As cloud computing advances rapidly, Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) devices increasingly delegate substantial amounts of data to the cloud for storage and dissemination. Before uploading data to the cloud, data owners have to encrypt it to guarantee data confidentiality in untrusted cloud environments. Due to its capacity to provide fine-grained data sharing, attribute-based encryption (ABE) is extensively used in IIoT. However, ABE decryption necessitates expensive pairing operations, which puts a significant computational strain on IIoT users with constrained resources. While traditional ABE outsourced decryption approaches can lower decryption computation overhead of the data user, they do not permit updating the access policy, and outsourced decryption results might not be accurate. In this paper, a lightweight data sharing system using proxy re-encryption (PRE) for IIoT is developed. By converting ABE ciphertext into an identity-based encryption ciphertext, flexible data sharing is implemented in addition to lowering the decryption computation cost. The correctness of the re-encrypted ciphertext is confirmed. Additionally, this approach accomplishes effective user revocation. The security of the proposed scheme is formally proven, and the computing efficiency and practicality for implementation in IIoT are confirmed by both theoretical analysis and experimental evaluation.

title: Smart Dietary Swaps: Machine Learning for Nutrient-Aware Food Substitution
authors: Mulakala Vrisheeka, Malladi Nanda Gopal, Suresh Anand M
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: This study introduces Smart Dietary Swaps, a machine-learning–driven, nutrient-aware food substitution system that makes recommendations to improve diet while taking into account user health conditions and preferences. Foods were categorized into healthy, moderate, and unhealthy food classes based on nutritional variables including calories, protein, carbohydrate, fat, fiber, sodium, micronutrients, and food type. Two datasets used for training were introduced: nutrient profiles and a large, comprehensive recipe dataset, and four methods were trained, including KNN, RF, a PCA-based MLP, and GNN. Each method was then evaluated using accuracy, precision, recall, and F1 score variables. The GNN outperformed the other methods for both datasets, achieving accuracy scores ranging from 87 to 89% and an F1 score of 95% that remained stable, further suggesting that the GNN generalized well and was balanced in its classification scheme. The PCA-based MLP performed well with accuracy scores of 86-85% and an F1 score of 93%, while KNN and RF achieved moderate results. The system captures user-centric health indicators (i.e., BMI, allergies, age-based nutrient requirements, etc.) and biochemical markers (i.e., iron, zinc, cholesterol, glucose), providing flexibility and personalized food substitution recommendations. In summary, the findings of this study suggest that graph-based learning, particularly the GNN approach, can be used as a reliable and effective basis for developing intelligent, data-driven personalized dietary recommendation systems.

title: Comprehensive Performance and Risk Profiling of ERIDS: A Resilient Ensemble-Based Intrusion Detection Framework
authors: Niyaz Hussain AMJ, Naveen Kilari, Seeniappan Kaliappan, Ramya M
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Modern networks face increasingly sophisticated threats that outpace signature-based defences. This paper presents ERIDS, a resilient ensemble intrusion-detection system engineered to boost detection accuracy, reliability, and resistance to adversarial manipulation. ERIDS fuses heterogeneous base detectors classical classifiers (SVM, RF), deep temporal models (CNN, LSTM, Transformer), and reconstruction autoencoders using calibrated stacking and an out-of-fold meta-learner, and augments alerts with a structured asset-impact risk-profiling module. The Evaluation of ERIDS on CIC-IDS2017, UNSW-NB15, and proprietary zero-day traces, including tests with adversarial perturbations, measuring detection performance, latency, and resilience. ERIDS achieves F1 ≈ 0.96, AUC ≈ 0.992, latency ≈ 3.2 ms/flow, and robustness ≈ 0.89, outperforming single models and naïve ensembles. Our results indicate that ensemble diversity combined with calibrated risk scoring provides actionable decisions for real-time SOC operations, while highlighting the necessity of continuous calibration and drift monitoring. Researcher also report compute-cost trade-offs and demonstrate improved operational triage in realistic SOC deployment scenarios.

title: Joint Temporal-Structural Representation Learning for Distributed Fault Discrimination in Microservice Architectures
authors: Yihan Xue, Yuxiao Wang, Ao Zhu, Xiaoxuan Sun, Chong Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Addressing the diverse fault morphologies, complex dependencies, and time-varying operational states in microservice distributed systems, this paper proposes a distributed fault discrimination model based on temporal graph neural networks. This model characterizes the microservice operation process as a dynamic graph sequence evolving, and performs joint representation learning of temporal modeling and structural interactions within a unified framework. First, service-level multisource observation signals are aligned and characterized to construct node feature sequences and their corresponding time-dependent dependencies. Then, a temporal coding module is introduced to extract the dynamic evolution representation of service states, and at each time step, attention-based structured message passing is used to characterize dependency interactions and propagation associations, forming a structure-enhanced temporal node representation. Furthermore, a dual readout mechanism is employed to aggregate the node and temporal dimensions, obtaining a system-level global representation and outputting the fault category distribution. Finally, supervised learning objectives are used to optimize model parameters, enabling the model to learn stable discrimination evidence under complex interactions and multi-source noise conditions. Comparative experimental results show that the proposed method achieves superior performance on multiple evaluation metrics, validating the effectiveness of jointly modeling temporal evolution and dependency structures in improving the distributed fault discrimination capability of microservices.

title: Self-Adaptive Artificial Intelligence Systems for Large-Scale Data-Driven Decision Making
authors: Md Jubayar Hossain, Mohammed Majid Bakhsh, Mohammad Abdus Sami, Md. Ishtiaque Alam, Md Mehedi Hassan Melon, Mia Md Tofayel Gonee Manik
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The growing magnitude, diversity, and speed of data produced across contemporary cyber-physical, enterprise, and cloud-edge systems has revealed inherent constraints with the inflexible artificial intelligence (AI) models regarding providing real-time choices. Self-adaptive artificial intelligence systems have become an attractive paradigm to overcome these challenges through enabling continuous learning, dynamic model reconfiguration and context-specific optimization in the changing data distributions and operational constraints. In this paper, a detailed self-adaptive AI architecture to serve large-scale decision making with data is provided, which involves online learning, feedback-based adjustment, and automatic policy optimization. The suggested architecture takes advantage of the hybrid intelligence methods merging deep learning, reinforcement learning, and meta-learning to reach strong performance in non-stationary and uncertain environments. Adaptive control loops are incorporated to check data drift, system performance and the consumption of resources and recalibration of a model can be done in real time without involving human intervention. The comprehensive test on large-scale datasets indicates that the proposed solution is much more accurate in making decisions, flexible and resilient to systems against the traditional non-adaptable and semi-adaptive AI models. The outcomes confirm the effectiveness of self-adaptive intelligence in aiding scalable, dependable, and explainable decision-making of dynamic data-intensive applications including smart infrastructure, autonomous systems, and intelligent business analytics.

title: SmartData Interlock: An Access Control Architecture and Data Exchange Model for Secure Data Ecosystem in Smart Buildings
authors: Ravi Sharma, Avinash Ramachandruni, Ekaterina Petrova, Pieter Pauwels
publish: IEEE Transactions on Privacy
abstract: Smart buildings increasingly rely on heterogeneous data sources, including real-time Internet of Things (IoT) streams, point cloud scans, and Building Information Modelling (BIM), to optimise energy consumption, safety, and operational efficiency. However, the integration of these data sources introduces significant challenges, particularly in terms of ensuring user privacy and data sovereignty across distributed systems. Building operators, tenants, service providers, and emergency responders often require access to overlapping datasets with different legal, organisational, and contextual constraints. Without appropriate governance mechanisms, data sharing risks violating privacy regulations and exposing sensitive information. To address these challenges, this paper first discusses the development of Personalised Data Hubs (PDHs) based on the International Data Spaces (IDS) concept, which integrates multiple data sets into a unified, compliant framework. Building on PDHs, we present the SmartData Interlock (SDI) model, a graph-based, sovereignty-aware access control and data exchange architecture that enables controlled data sharing, allows users to define their own access policies, and ensures regulatory compliance. SDI extends Category Based Data Access (CBDA) models with purpose binding, delegation semantics, contextual constraint enforcement, and executable policy graphs for lifecycle-aware smart building governance. Using a realistic smart building policy graph, this work evaluates SDI performance and shows that secure data exchange can be achieved with bounded computational overhead, confirming its practical viability in smart building applications and urban planning.

title: Remote Sensing Cross Model Text Image Retrieval Based on Attention Correction and Filtering
authors: Chandrakala.R, N. Bharatha Devi
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The establishment of a robust semantic relationship between images and their accompanying text queries is crucial for the effective use of remote sensing data. Conventional methods frequently suffer from misalignment, noise and modality gaps, which impair retrieval performance. A cross-modal retrieval framework for distant sensing image–text alignment that is improved with attention correction and filtering algorithms are proposed to overcome these difficulties. In order to reduce noise and maintain image information, the system starts with preprocessing employing an adaptive Wiener filter. For classification, a hybrid LSTM–Vision Transformer (ViT) model is used, producing reliable image embeddings. In parallel, a transformer-based text feature extractor processes text queries or captions to generate high-quality text embeddings. Following that, both modalities are projected into a common embedding space with semantic alignment imposed. Filtering removes superfluous or deceptive information, whereas attention correction highlights essential cross-modal correlations to further improve the embedding quality. Moreover, cross-model retrieval makes precise text-to-image matching possible, facilitating uses like environmental study, disaster relief, and land cover interpretation with the highest accuracy of 94.56%. The proposed approach validation from Python software shows how well filtering and attention-based correction combined to provide dependable and semantically consistent retrieval in challenging remote sensing situations.

title: A Multi-Stage BERT-Based Cognitive Framework for Conversational Business Intelligence in SMEs
authors: Dipali Baviskar, Harsh Halwai, Anand Patekar, Anoushka Kachewar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Social media is becoming a more important tool for small and medium-sized businesses (SMBs) to watch competitors, evaluate customer sentiment, and identify new market opportunities. However, it is challenging to convert raw signals into understandable, actionable insights due to the unstructured, fast-moving nature of cross-platform data. A conversational business intelligence layer and a multi-platform trend analytics pipeline were established in earlier work on this project. The multi-stage family of BERT-based models that serve as that system’s cognitive foundation is the particular subject of this research. The approach uses Sentiment-BERT for polarity-sensitive refinement, NER-BERT for business-entity extraction, SBERT for semantic retrieval over trend-aware embeddings, and Intent-BERT for query categorization. Within a single pipeline, each model maintains compatibility while addressing a different reasoning layer. We explain how these models are modified for SMB use scenarios, how they work together to facilitate end-to-end conversational intelligence, and how they open up new avenues for real-time decision support, low-resource fine-tuning, and domain adaptability. This paper focuses specifically on the cognitive foundation of that system, presenting a unified multistage architecture built on BERT-based models.

title: Enhancing Battery Efficiency and Driving Safety: Advanced State of Charge Estimation Using the Extended Kalman Filter Algorithm
authors: Ashraf A. Shanaq, Subramaniam Ganesan, Debatosh Debnath, Mohamed A. Zohdy
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Accurately estimating the battery module's state of charge (SOC) is critical in electric vehicle design. The process is essential in providing operators with precise and continuous mileage information. The accuracy of SOC estimation also helps mitigate the risk of overcharging or discharging the battery, ensuring overall driving safety. A fundamental aspect of SOC estimation methodology involves constructing a suitable battery model to achieve heightened response accuracy. This study proposes an electric vehicle SOC model that utilizes the extended Kalman filter algorithm. The research focuses on lithium-ion power batteries and uses real-world data to conduct SOC estimation investigations. The study's findings demonstrate that the proposed model exhibits accuracy when the internal battery temperature aligns with the ambient temperature at 25°C. The model has minimal terminal voltage differences, and the average voltage difference is 9mV. Applying the extended Kalman filter algorithm at room temperature significantly improves the recovery percentage of SOC voltage, surpassing an average of 73%, thereby demonstrating high accuracy. The study employs the extended Kalman algorithm to estimate SOC current waveforms, specifically emphasizing discharge currents of 4A. The simulation results showcase exceptional estimation accuracy and robust applicability in estimating SOC under these conditions. The proposed SOC model utilizing the extended Kalman filter algorithm enhances accuracy and reliability in estimating SOC for lithium-ion power batteries. The results of this study have significant implications for the design and development of electric vehicles, particularly in optimizing battery performance and ensuring driving safety.

title: Exploring Generative Pretrained Language Models for Opinion Mining in Large-Scale Twitter Social Media Data
authors: Siddhesh Amrale
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Opinion mining has gained a significant role in the last few years for the text data analysis coming from various sources like Twitter, Facebook, Google, and Amazon. It enables companies to enhance their business strategies in a proactive manner. The term "sentiment analysis" refers to the practice of collecting and categorizing customer feedback according to their positive, neutral, or negative feelings using specialized software. This paper article presents an enhanced sentiment analysis framework, which relies on the transformer-based language model to classify and study social media data. The proposed approach delineates the textual context and the semantic relations very clearly, therefore, gives better precision than the traditional machine learning models. The model uses the preprocessing, tokenization, and fine-tuning methods to deal with a variety of linguistic expressions and noisy user-generated text. Experimental findings show that the proposed GPT and ETC model attained 99.23% and 96.10 accuracy, which is much higher than the current models including Naive Bayes (NB), CNN-BiLSTM, SVC (Support Vector Classifier), Multilayer Perceptron (MLP) and Random Forest (RF). The general result is that transformer-based architecture, especially GPT, is capable of reaching out to long-range textual dependencies and emotional context which leads to higher performance in the classification. These results confirm GPT’s robustness, adaptability, and high generalization potential for sentiment analysis across complex and noisy social media data.

title: Source Enumeration by Integrating Toeplitz Reconstruction with Elbow-based Method
authors: Xueqin Song, Xiaogang Liu, Bingbing Qi, Desheng Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: An improved Toeplitz-constrained elbow-based source enumeration (EBSE) algorithm is proposed for robust estimation of the number of sources in adverse environments. First, the Toeplitz structure is exploited to modify the covariance of the received data; subsequently, eigenvalue decomposition is performed to extract the eigenvalues, which serve as the input to the ensuing EBSE algorithm. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves stable source enumeration under conditions of low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR), small snapshots, and coherent sources, significantly enhancing performance at only a modest computational cost.

title: A Data-Driven–Expert Hybrid Model for Real-Time Battery State of Health Diagnostics
authors: Ibrahim Zafar, Chodagam Srinivas, Regadi Madhurima, Kenchappa Gari Yogananda, Tanguturi Mohammad Sadiq, Gopireddy Sindhusree
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Accurate estimation of a battery’s State of Health (SOH) is essential for the safety and longevity of energy storage systems used in electric vehicles and renewable energy applications. This paper presents a hybrid intelligent battery management framework that fuses fuzzy logic inference with support vector machine (SVM) regression to improve SOH prediction under diverse operating conditions. Multi-sensor data capacity fade, internal resistance, temperature, vibration, and humidity were synthesized in MATLAB to evaluate realistic degradation behavior over 100 charge-discharge cycles. A Mamdani fuzzy inference system first encodes expert knowledge to generate a preliminary SOH estimate under uncertain or noisy conditions. This estimate is then combined with the original sensor features and processed by an SVM regressor with optimized hyper-parameters, enabling the model to capture complex nonlinear relationships. Comparative evaluations show that the hybrid method reduces estimation bias, achieves lower RMSE, and provides higher R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> values than standalone FIS or SVM approaches. The results confirm that integrating expert rules with machine learning improves robustness and interpretability, offering a practical solution for real-time SOH monitoring in advanced battery management systems.

title: Impact of Deployment Strategies on Wireless Sensor Networks Performance under Security Threats
authors: Aina Mehta, Jasminder Kaur Sandhu, Harshal Patil
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Due to their decentralized architecture, Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) are particularly vulnerable to attacks targeting the network layer. This paper investigates the impact of three deployment strategies—Random, Grid, and Hierarchical—on WSN performance under sinkhole attack scenarios. Simulation-based analysis is conducted to evaluate key performance metrics, including E2E, throughput, and packet delivery ratio (PDR). Results indicate that Grid deployment achieves the highest resilience, with a PDR of 98.1%, while Hierarchical deployment offers balanced performance. In contrast, Random deployment experiences severe degradation, with PDR dropping to 26.8% and delay increasing significantly. These findings underscore the critical role of deployment strategy in ensuring network robustness under adversarial conditions. Future work will explore additional network-layer threats such as wormhole and blackhole attacks and focus on prioritizing the integration of intelligent learning models with simulation tools to enable adaptive detection and mitigation of security threats in dynamic WSN environments.

title: Revisiting 1998 Torque Data: a Machine Learning Analysis of Time Series Data for Tapping Experiments
authors: Alaa Al Najjar, Nakul Havaldar, Valentin Plenk, Marco Linß
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: This paper addresses the challenge of real-time tool condition monitoring in tapping processes using machine learning techniques, with a focus on cross-material generalization and robust fault detection. The study leverages a historical dataset from 1988, comprising 2,195 tapping experiments on two steel alloys- 16 MnCr 5 and 42 CrMo 4 -monitoring torque (Mz) signals to predict binary quality outcomes (good/bad) based on defined quality criteria. To overcome limitations in prior work, the authors introduce a feature extraction method that captures both amplitude and duration characteristics across distinct phases of the torque signal,. The evaluation framework includes increasingly challenging train/test splits: random, run-wise holdout, and cross-material (training on 16 MnCr 5, testing on 42 CrMo 4), enabling assessment of real-world generalizability. Multiple machine learning models are tested using both raw time-series data (after cleaning and normalization) and engineered features. Results show Matthews Correlation Coefficients (MCC) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.40-0.41$</tex> under cross-material testing-indicating moderate but meaningful generalization across materials with different mechanical properties. This performance level suggests that fundamental physical regularities in successful tapping produce consistent torque signatures, enabling transferable detection of anomalies without retraining. Findings support the feasibility of plug-and-play monitoring systems in agile manufacturing environments, where minimal setup and broad applicability are essential.

title: A Secure and Energy-Efficient Cyber-Physical System Framework for Next-Generation Industrial Automation
authors: Divyashree H S, VidyaSagar Adabala, Kasi Viswanathan, Vinay Kulkarni, A Mutharasan, Hazari Naresh
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The dynamic increase of on-line physical connections of the next-generation industrial automation demands a safe and energy efficient system to boost the potentialities of real time operation, predictive analytic and high control capabilities. The world that is already industrialized should not only possess the relevant systems which will keep watch over the communications routes, but also possess systems which will be able to optimize the use of the energy besides being dependable alongside the manner in which the networked devices interact with the networks. The model proposes an adjustable structure that can be utilised in achieving confidentiality, integrity, and availability of industrial information using artificial intelligence, blockchain-linked trusts, and spindly packages of cryptography. It will consume less electric power and will show fewer delays at the end in case you collocate energy conscious technologies as well as smart technologies at the border. An emergency countermeasures approach confronts the occurrence of immature threats and intelligent surveillance targets aberration accomplishment. Moreover, the framework concerns the interaction of the heterogeneous hardware with the infrastructures of the clouds computing, and the increase in the outlay at the needs of users on the very large scale of industries. Energy optimization is achieved in regards to dynamic load balancing, and dynamically based choices. The model can help to intimately automate significant infrastructure because security and efficiency form two basic constituents. The plan forms an avenue to the strengthening, intelligent, and nomadic industrial systems according to the objectives of industry 5.0. The high criteria required were successfully met by the proposed system with 96% data integrity of security and 95% policy flexibility, which showed utmost reliability.

title: Integrate Wearable Data into Digital Twins: Using Real-Time Biomedical Feedback for Diabetes Management and Hypoglycemic Event Prediction
authors: Arvind Narsing, Raed Alazaidah, P V Indira, S. Ramkumar, M. Sindhu, Y. Kamala Raju
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: A comprehensive strategy for managing diabetes is necessary to avoid hypoglycemia and its complications; this strategy should incorporate ongoing monitoring, rapid intervention, and personalised treatment programs. New implantable biomedical sensors track vitals, including heart rate, blood sugar, exercise, and sleep. We will be able to use this continuous deluge of data for therapeutic purposes when a significant amount of time has passed. The use of real-time data enables digital twin technology to update a model of a person's physiological state continuously.
