Early identification and precise analysis of CVDs are pivotal for effective management and sustained outcomes. Retinal imaging has emerged as a safe and practical method for CVD prediction. This study plans to foster a profound learning [1] model utilising convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and MobileNet design to predict CVDs from retinal images. The proposed model leverages CNNs’ ability to extract relevant features from retinal images and MobileNet’s lightweight architecture for efficient organisation. To improve the quality and variety of retinal images for CVD patients, the images are preprocessed by resizing and colour and quality standardisation. It is further used for modelling and assessments. The CNN model engineering is planned to integrate MobileNet as the base architecture, with additional layers for adaptation to the specific CVD prediction task. Through broad preparation and streamlining, the model learns to classify retinal images as either indicative of CVD presence or absence with high precision. Precision and other conventional metrics are used to lead execution evaluations. The growing model offers potential benefits in early detection, risk assessment, and practical discovery, showing encouraging results in predicting CVDs from retinal images. As a potential support system, this model can help medical professionals make informed decisions, which in turn can facilitate easier mediations and preventive medical procedures. Once clinical ethics and settings have been approved, the clinical utility and effect can be assessed.

title: Enhancing Chronic Disease Prediction in IoMT Enabled Healthcare 5.0 Using Deep Learning
authors: Iqra Rizvi, Ashish Tiwari
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The rise of healthcare 5.0 which is powered by the Artificial Intelligence and Internet of Medical Things (IoMT), and extensive connectivity brings to light great opportunities for intelligent real time health care solutions. Chronic conditions like diabetes, which require ongoing care and quick intervention to avert serious complications are a focus. This research presents a Deep Learning based approach for early diagnosis of diabetes in an IoMT enabled Health care 5.0 setting. The research uses real time data from medical IoT devices and very advanced neural network structures which in turn do a great job at identifying complex indicators of diabetes risk. Also, the research study using public health diabetes data shows that the developed system does indeed outperform ancient machine learning in terms of accuracy, sensitivity and specificity. The findings emphasize the promise of combining deep learning with IoMT for improving predictive analytics, enabling proactive clinical decision-making, and facilitating next-generation proactive management of chronic ailments.

title: Physics-Informed Diffusion Models for Smooth and Goal-Directed Trajectory Generation
authors: Zhihua Fang, Heng Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: The widespread application of location-based services has generated massive amounts of mobility data, highlighting the need for synthetic trajectory generation to address privacy concerns and data scarcity. While recent Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models (DDPMs) have shown exceptional capabilities in modeling continuous coordinate trajectories, existing models treat trajectory points simply as abstract numeric values. This ignores the underlying physical properties of human mobility, often resulting in unnatural jitter, sharp implausible turns, and a failure to accurately reach intended destinations. In this paper, we propose KineDiff, a lightweight, physics-informed diffusion framework designed to generate highly realistic, smooth, and goal-directed trajectories. Instead of relying on computationally heavy architectural modifications or external map matching, we introduce a novel composite loss function that explicitly regularizes the diffusion training process. Specifically, we design a Spatially-Adaptive Kinematic Regularization Loss to penalize high-order motion instability (i.e., irregular acceleration) while preserving intricate intersection maneuvers, and an Endpoint Anchor Loss to ensure task-specific destination accuracy. Experiments on real-world urban trajectory datasets demonstrate that our method significantly outperforms traditional generative baselines and standard diffusion models. Comprehensive ablation studies and dual-metric convergence analyses further confirm that KineDiff reduces the trajectory length distribution error by over 90% while maintaining highly realistic spatial density patterns.

title: Efficient Graph Convolutional Networks with Attention Mechanisms for Precise Prediction of Cardiovascular Diseases
authors: Nagarjuna Nallametti, S. Kavitha, Lakshmi H.N
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The novel system is bringing to the table is a way to increase in diagnostic-accuracy for cardiovascular disorders. Whereas, the architecture that relies on Sparse Graph Convolutional Networks (GCNs) is used to model direct dependencies between various nodes, is such that the model provides data flexibility enabling the model to incorporate computational efficiency and scalability. A mechanism to prioritize urgent features and relationships are introduced to the attention model that can reconfigure its focus on the medical indicators and correlations that matter. In attention, the GCN architecture to be used is data that may be of different types like clinical record, image, and molecular biological data which it processes into a comprehensive risk assessment. This system stands out for the following, development of a new graph representation which is sparse to be efficient in terms of computation, the introduction of a novel design of the attention layers focusing on adaptive feature weighting and the proposal of the ability to deal with voluminous datasets with minimal information leakage accordingly. Experiments that were conducted on benchmark cardiovascular datasets reveal.

title: Privacy-Preserving and Explainable Hybrid Learning Framework for Career Guidance Using Psychometric Indicators: A Synthetic-Data-Driven Study
authors: Sameer Nanivadekar, Sivaneasan Bala Krishnan, Dilip Kumar Jang Bahadur Saini, Prasun Chakrabartiline
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Data-driven modeling has been a key factor in developing career guidance systems by matching students' attributes with changing occupational categories. However, centralized machine learning approaches have raised concerns about privacy of the user's data, fairness of the results and interpretability of the models. In this study, a hybrid framework for privacy-preserving and explainable learning is presented. The proposed Privacy-Preserving and Explainable Hybrid Learning Framework (PX-CGF) integrates both psychometric indicators and skill-based profiles as well as interpretable reasoning techniques through federated learning. To evaluate this new framework, a synthetic dataset was created with the psychometric traits, academic indicators and occupational fit categories. Evaluation experiments showed that PX-CGF resulted in greater accuracy compared to baseline models, as well as increased fairness and interpretability. Several tables were created to illustrate the dataset used, comparisons of the models evaluated, and the metrics used to measure these evaluations, which demonstrate the professional structure of the proposed framework.

title: Construction of A New Power System Line Loss Management And Evaluation System Based On Automation Technology
authors: Haibo Luo, Lingyong Li, Zhenjie Li, Ruqin Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: In view of the problems of single dimension (over-reliance on line loss rate), insufficient dynamic prediction ability and low credibility of multi-source data in the current distribution substation line loss management effect evaluation system, this study constructed a new power system line loss management evaluation system based on automation technology, by establishing a multi-dimensional evaluation model covering technical indicators (line loss rate reduction, voltage qualification rate), economic indicators (investment return rate, operation and maintenance cost reduction) and social indicators (user power outage perception, power supply reliability). The LSTM-Attention hybrid network is innovatively used to predict the line loss rate change one year after the management, and the fuzzy comprehensive evaluation method and entropy method are integrated to achieve dynamic optimization of indicator weights. The empirical results of 12 distribution substations in eastern Guangdong show that the system has achieved an accuracy rate of 98.5% in line loss anomaly identification, an average increase of 41.6% in the return on investment of management projects compared with traditional methods, and the average annual power outage time of urban users has been reduced from 12.4 minutes to 6.7 minutes, forming a "prediction-evaluation-feedback" closed-loop mechanism, which provides a quantitative decision-making basis for the improvement of line loss management efficiency and precise resource allocation of power grid enterprises.

title: Adsorptive Removal of Heavy Metals from Wastewater: A Review on ANN based Predictive Models
authors: Hamsa Vardhini M, Bhavana S, Ramya R, K. Balageeta, Nidhi Sharma, Hemavathy R V
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Water bodies contaminated by heavy metals such as Pb2+, Cr6+, Zn2+, Ni2+, and Hg2+ pose severe health and environmental risks. Although adsorption is an effective remediation technique, it is complex and non-linear, making predictive modeling difficult. Artificial Neural Networks (ANNs) have shown great promise in addressing this challenge, offering high accuracy in modeling heavy metal adsorption. This review systematically analyzes recent literature, emphasizing the correlation between ANN architecture and adsorbent characteristics. A rigorous search was conducted across Scopus, Web of Science, and PubMed, followed by careful screening and critical evaluation to ensure transparency and reliability. Findings reveal that Feedforward ANNs (FF-ANNs) achieve excellent predictive performance (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> > 0.98) for carbon- and metal- based adsorbents, while Radial Basis Function ANNs (RBF-ANNs) are better suited for bio-based and clay- based materials with localized, non-linear interactions. Hybrid ANN models, which integrate optimization algorithms, consistently deliver the highest accuracy (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> > 0.99) and are essential for complex systems like nanocomposites. The review highlights the importance of selecting ANN models based on adsorbent type, validating methodological transparency. This integrated framework enables researchers and practitioners to apply predictive modeling efficiently, reliably, and effectively to environmental remediation.

title: Artificial Intelligence-Based Fake News Detection Methods in Social Media: A Comprehensive Survey and Future Directions
authors: Anjali Bhadre, Rushikesh Hippargekar, Omkar Pokale, Sachin Choure, Harshvardhan Ghongade
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The rampant dissemination of misinformation on social media threatens informational quality and public discourse. In this paper we survey the recent state of AI-based detection of fake news, covering 120 papers from 2022-2025, from traditional machine learning to deep learning methods, with hybrid and ensemble approaches reporting accuracies over 95%. Our analysis finds three trends for methods: the fusion of different modalities, including textual and visual information as well as graph-based factors related to the network itself; transformer architectures to capture more context; and explainability of the detection models. We note persistent challenges, such as adversarial attacks, and domain transferability, as well as deepfake media detection. Finally, we highlight five open challenges in the detection of fake news, including poor multilingual approaches, not detecting news in real time, poor benchmark standards, a neglect for adapting to evolving fake news, and little study to the ethics of automated content moderation. This survey will provide a blueprint of current techniques for researchers and illuminate paths towards more robust, scalable and responsible detection technologies.

title: Edge-GIS: Precision Monitoring of Soil Erosion and Moisture Using Georeferenced Smart Cameras
authors: Naji Guedri, Ali Nsaibi, Rached Gharbi
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: Critical irrigation water losses and decreased productivity result from agricultural soil erosion, which is made worse by climate variations such as heavy rainfall, earthquakes, and snowfall. This article introduces Edge-GIS, an edge computing system that uses a network of georeferenced Hikvision smart cameras placed at different locations throughout a farmland plot to precisely monitor soil erosion and moisture. Before every irrigation, photos are taken remotely using PoE injectors and locally processed using OpenCV and Python (in VSCode) to identify erosion contours using pixel and geometric shape analysis. An interactive map (geojson.io, QGIS Cloud, or Leaflet) is displayed in real time using <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4 \mathrm{G} / \text{LoRa}$</tex> after detected anomalies are automatically injected into a Geographic Information System (GIS) in GeoJSON format with accurate GPS coordinates. Our field experiments in Tunisia show accurate detection after climate events, improving irrigation choices. For precision agriculture, Edge-GIS offers an affordable, scalable, and non-invasive solution.

title: Performance Comparison and Simulation Analysis of Key Technologies in MIMO Communication Systems Under Rayleigh Fading Channels
authors: Yihao Zhao
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: This paper presents a performance comparison and analysis of key technologies in Multiple-Input Multiple-Output (MIMO) communication systems under Rayleigh flat-fading channels. The research focuses on Alamouti space-time coding, which exploits diversity gain, and the VBLAST architecture, which targets spatial multiplexing. For the VBLAST, this paper investigates and simulates Zero-Forcing (ZF) and Minimum Mean Square Error (MMSE) linear detection algorithms, as well as non-linear detection algorithms incorporating Successive Interference Cancellation (SIC). Through Monte Carlo simulations under Rayleigh fading channels with different antenna configurations and various modulation schemes (4-QAM, 16-QAM, 64-QAM), this paper evaluates the performance of each scheme using Bit Error Rate (BER) as the core metric. Simulation results demonstrate that Alamouti coding provides optimal reliability at low signal-to-noise ratios due to its superior diversity gain, while VBLAST combined with MMSE-SIC algorithms can effectively enhance the capacity of multiplexing systems at medium-to-high signal-to-noise ratios, although its performance is limited by error propagation. The research findings clarify the performance trade-offs among different technologies, providing guidance for technology selection and receiver design in MIMO systems under various scenarios.

title: DGE-1DNet: Efficient 1D Channel Estimation for 5G NTN LEO Satellites
authors: Zhimin Huang, Gaosai Liu, Huiling Hou, Guang Liang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite communication is a cornerstone of 5G Non-Terrestrial Networks (NTN). However, the substantial computational overhead of deep learning-based channel estimation poses a significant challenge for energy-constrained satellite payloads. To bridge this gap, this paper proposes DGE-1DNet, a highly lightweight 1D convolutional framework optimized for on-chip deployment. We depart from the high-latency 2D “image-based” paradigm, adopting a symbollevel 1D sequence architecture that eliminates buffering latency. The core of our design is a Ghost-based Inverted Residual structure, which explicitly decouples “intrinsic” spectral features from redundant “ghost” maps using cheap linear operations, drastically reducing parameter count. To compensate for the performance loss inherent in lightweight models under high-mobility LEO channels, a Dynamic Spectrum-Aware Attention mechanism is incorporated to adaptively recalibrate features. Simulation results based on 3GPP TDL-D models show that DGE-1DNet achieves estimation accuracy comparable to heavyweight baselines (e.g., DnCNN) while reducing Floating Point Operations (FLOPs) by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 0. 5 \%}$</tex>. This architecture provides an optimal trade-off between computational efficiency and spectral robustness for next-generation satellite terminals.

title: Explainable AI and Federated Learning for Privacy-Preserving Loan Default Prediction in Cooperative Banking Networks
authors: Shilpa Shivshankar Mathpati, Abhijit Chirputkar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Co-operative banking networks in emerging economies, like India, have to perform the increasingly arduous task of manage loan defaults with ever increasing needs for data Privacy and demands for transparency in decision making. Centralized machine learning models have been widely used for The task of loan default prediction typically require data centralization that exposes sensitive customer information to data breaches, among other similar incidents, thus breaking laws. such as the DPDP Act 2023 of India. Further, complex models, are usually such that is based on deep neural networks. Not transparent, therefore able to undermine regulatory compliance. and may further erode stakeholder trust. This work proposes a robust framework that synergistically integrates Federated Few-shot learning with explainable AI to enable privacy preserving by: and interpretable loan default predictions within cooperative banking networks. In this work, a synthetically generated dataset of 30,000 instances is used, similar to the UCI Credit Card Default dataset, in emulating a federated setup across three cooperative banks with non IID data distributions. Here, the core model is a Random Forest classifier trained via FedAvg aggregation, augmented with SHAP for both global and local explanations. Extensive experiments show that the federated model achieves an accuracy of 81.98%, precision of 64.87%, recall of 44.89%, and F1-score of 52.84%, close to the centralized baseline of 82.34% accuracy, with no data sharing. The feature attribution analysis underlines repayment status as the most important predictor, namely PAY 0, which contributes as much as 25% to the default risk scores. Ablation studies confirm the efficacy of XAI in explaining model decisions, with SHAP values permitting counterfactual explanations at the level of individual predictions. Further assessments of adding differential privacy noise provide additional protection against inference attacks. The contribution of the proposed architecture will be to towards a decrease in NPA, improved regulatory compliance and ethics in AI deployments. Our approach links the strong privacy guarantees of FL with the transparency afforded by XAI and provides a scalable paradigm for cooperative banks; It would probably help lower the default rates by 5–10% with proactive interventions. Extensions of this work may be done Using Blockchain for Secure Aggregations and Temporal Dynamics in credit histories.

title: AI-Driven Legal Chatbot: Enhancing Legal Information Accessibility
authors: Onkar Somvanshi, Ankur Jain, Ajay Kumar Phulre, Vipin Jain, Varun Singh, Priscilla Dinkar Moyya
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: This paper proposes an AI-driven legal chatbot that uses NLP and deep learning to facilitate access to legal information. Precisely, we identify the crucial challenges in the development of legal question-answering systems: the inherent difficulty of legal language and concepts, contextualization, and response generation in real time. Our proposed framework incorporates both macro and micro retrieval approaches that jointly extract the best answers from domain-specific models, such as Legal-BERT and T5, which are fine-tuned over various legal corpora. Given that our chatbot implements contemporary explainable artificial intelligence techniques and simple continuous learning, the information we provide is accurate and compliant with ethical standards. Experimental evaluations conducted on the Hindu Marriage Act dataset demonstrate that our proposed Lexicon-based Hybrid approach achieved an accuracy of 92%, significantly higher than that of purely generative models, thus being able to effectively democratize legal access for professionals and laypersons alike.

title: Meta-Memory for Large Language Models
authors: Bin Liang, Cai Ke, Runcong Zhao, Qinglin Zhu, Lin Gui, Yue Yu, Hui Wang, Ruifeng Xu, Kam-Fai Wong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Audio, Speech and Language Processing
abstract: Integrating memory components into large language models (LLMs) can improve the generation quality for long-term conversations. However, existing memory management methods largely overlook the cognition and regulation of the memory process, lacking the capability to dynamically manage and utilize memory on demand. To address this challenge, this paper approaches Meta-Memory for Memory Management (M$^{4}$), a novel paradigm that equips LLMs with the ability for self-monitoring and self-reflective memory management. In long-term conversations, where dialogue history accumulates continuously, the meta-memory capability of M$^{4}$ enables LLMs to autonomously 1) identify what knowledge needs to be memorized; 2) determine how to construct and store memory; 3) monitor the correctness and validity of the acquired information; and 4) decide when to learn more and how to retrieve information to refine their responses. Experimental results on two long-term conversation datasets and two long-term question-answering datasets demonstrate that our M$^{4}$ significantly enhances the memory management capacity of LLMs in long-term information learning, achieving more efficient storage and higher-quality response generation.

title: Land Use and Land Cover Classification Using Reflectance-Based Remote Sensing Techniques
authors: Apeksha P. Sonare, Rohini S. Ochawar, Komal Chambhare, Huda T. Fatema, Shreya Verma
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: For sustainable environmental management, accurate Land Use and Land Cover (LULC) analysis is essential. By examining reflectance values across several spectral bands, this study employs high-resolution multispectral pictures from the Maharashtra Remote Sensing Application Centre (MRSAC) to differentiate between vegetated (trees) and non-vegetated (land) locations. The Sentinel Application Platform (SNAP) was used to calculate reflectance, acquire datasets, and perform preprocessing (normalization, radiometric calibration, and atmospheric correction). The findings demonstrate clear spectral patterns: land has lower reflectance in the same bands as trees, which have higher reflectance from Bands 1 to 4, with the highest reflectance in the near-infrared (NIR) band (RT = 0.59718). Land, on the other hand, aids in separation by displaying higher reflectance in the shortwave infrared (SWIR) bands (particularly RL = 0.37787 in Band 6). These results demonstrate how well a combined NIR and SWIR-based strategy works for improved LULC categorization. The suggested system offers a scalable basis for environmental monitoring and greatly increases mapping accuracy in challenging terrains. In order to enhance classification robustness and facilitate real-time land management applications, future research will concentrate on integrating machine learning approaches.

title: A Novel Design for Private Information Retrieval with Logarithmic Client Overhead
authors: Wenshuo Guo, Li Li, Xuelei Li, Luying Li, Fei Gao, Ruyang Li
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Private information retrieval enables a client to retrieve records from a database without revealing the query intent. This paper presents a novel design for the conventional PIR model without replication that employs the technique of block-wise computation with recursive reduction. In contrast to the Kushilevitz-Ostrovsky protocol, which reshapes the database into a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$d$</tex>-dimensional matrix and incurs a client overhead of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathcal{O}(d \sqrt[d]{\eta})$</tex>, our approach achieves a lower overhead of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathcal{O}\left(l \log _{l}(\eta)\right)$</tex>, with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$l$</tex> representing the block size and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\eta$</tex> the database size.

title: A UB-ETDA Based Spaceborne SAR Parameter Design Method for Enhanced Extended Target Detection Performance
authors: Huaping Xu, Chonghang Wang, Jingwen Li, Bing Sun, Wei Liu, Xianghua Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: For extended target detection, most existing space-borne SAR parameter design methods primarily focus on ensuring normal system operation, with limited consideration of the impact of SAR parameters on extended target detection performance. To enhance the extended target detection performance of spaceborne SAR from a parameter design perspective, a novel parameter design method based on an upper bound on extended target detection accuracy (UB-ETDA) is proposed, which comprehensively accounts for key factors affecting extended target detection performance, including aspect-dependent scattering characteristics of the extended target and spatial correlation properties of clutter. By employing UB-ETDA as a new performance metric to guide spaceborne SAR parameter design, the extended target detection performance of the system can be effectively improved. First, a statistical signal model for spaceborne SAR extended target detection is developed, systematically characterizing the stochastic properties of the extended target, background clutter and noise components in SAR images. Then, the expression of UB-ETDA for SAR system is rigorously derived, along with a stable and statistically efficient numerical computation method. Finally, with UB-ETDA as the primary performance metric and existing performance metrics as constraints, a spaceborne SAR parameter design method is proposed for improving extended target detection performance. Simulation results validate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Classifying ADHD Presentations Using Temporally Segmented Behavioral Data from a Serious Game
authors: Seung-Jae Kim, Jun-Su Kim, Unhui Jo, Yoo Joo Jeong, Su Jin Jun, Jin-Yeop Park, Hyang-Sook Hoe, Jeong-Heon Song
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Distinguishing attention deficit/hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) presentations, such as predominantly inattentive (ADHD-I) and hyperactive/impulsive (ADHD-HI), is clinically important for management. However, traditional ADHD assessments often rely on subjective evaluations, potentially introducing biases and limiting diagnostic accuracy. As a more objective approach, this study aimed to develop a machine learning-based classification model using behavioral data derived from serious games performed by children diagnosed with ADHD. A total of 51 children aged 6 to 13 participated, and both parent-reported Korean-ADHD Rating Scale scores and serious game performance data were collected. Behavioral data collected from serious games were temporally segmented into early and late phases of gameplay, and discriminative behavioral features were identified through a machine learning-based feature selection process. Three machine learning models, Random Forest, Support Vector Machine, and eXtreme Gradient Boosting, were trained and evaluated. Models using temporally segmented behavioral features showed strong classification performance, particularly for the ensemble-based models. Among them, the Random Forest model demonstrated the best test performance, achieving an accuracy of 81.818%, an F1-score of 85.714%, and an AUROC of 83.333%. Furthermore, behavioral differences between the ADHD-I and ADHD-HI groups became more pronounced during the latter half of gameplay. In particular, children with ADHD-I showed longer decision times, longer times to the final touch, more unnecessary touches, and lower maximum difficulty levels than those with ADHD-HI, indicating that cognitive processing becomes slower and response strategies become less efficient as gameplay progresses into the later phase. These findings indicate that models using serious game-derived behavioral data, particularly with temporal feature engineering, can effectively classify ADHD presentations and may serve as a scalable, quantitative tool to support clinical assessment and the development of personalized interventions.

title: SHARE-U: Semantic-aware Human Avatar Reconstruction with Efficient Gaussians via Uncertainty-weighted Contrastive Learning_supp1-3685541.pdf
authors: Zheheng Jiang
publish: N/A
abstract: We present SHARE-U, a 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS)-based human model, represented by significantly fewer Gaussians, achieving high-fidelity quality with fast training and real-time rendering speed. To constrain 3D Gaussian optimization to adapt it for monocular human modeling, regularization-based models rely on rigid rules, while another trend is employing more flexible contrastive learning. However, existing methods typically require a large number of Gaussians to represent a human avatar, increasing memory consumption and reducing rendering efficiency, and struggle to achieve photo-realistic rendering with fewer Gaussians. This limitation stems from their uniform 3D constraints to all Gaussians, which fail to distinguish between well-observed and poorly-observed Gaussians, leading to over-constraints on poorly-observed ones with large reconstruction errors and thereby degrading local details. To address these challenges, SHARE-U relies on uncertainty-weighted contrastive learning and semantic-aware optimization. Specifically, SHARE-U applies Hessian-derived uncertainty to distinguish and weight each Gaussian sample in contrastive learning, strengthening supervision for low-uncertainty Gaussians and attenuating the influence of high-uncertainty ones. During optimization, we innovatively propose a semantic-aware densification strategy to adaptively control Gaussians' density according to different body parts' granularity. For modeling finer textures without increasing the Gaussian number, we further suggest a Fourier loss to align texture within each semantic part in the frequency domain. Therefore, this cohesive framework forms the key insight of SHARE-U: the semantic-aware optimization yields an efficient but under-constrained Gaussian representation, and the uncertainty-weighted contrastive learning specifically enables its robust optimization. Extensive experiments on the ZJU_Mocap and MonoCap datasets demonstrate that SHARE-U outperforms previous state-of-the-art human models both quantitatively and qualitatively.

title: Implement the Use of Artificial Intelligence in Urban Mobility Systems: Optimising Traffic in Real-Time and Using the Internet of Things Sensors for Smart Routing
authors: R. Sundar, M Monisha, V.G Dharsana Dharani, P M Vijayan, G M Karpura Dheepan, A. Muthukrishnan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Urban transportation systems are already in a precarious position, and the combination of increased demand, unexpected traffic, and more efficient transit networks has only made matters worse. Because of human error and fixed signal timings, older traffic management systems couldn’t handle the complexity and urbanity of today’s metropolitan areas. Our all-encompassing method relies on data collected from scattered internet of things sensors to enhance the real-time traffic flow in urban transportation networks by integrating artificial intelligence. Intelligent traffic routing and spatiotemporal learning are features of the proposed system. This capability includes intelligent predictive modelling, context-based mobility decisions, and adaptive signal management. There is a lot of room for creative design in terms of potential data sources. In this framework, we can find equipment such as environmental monitoring devices, smart traffic lights, public transportation systems, and street sensors. Artificial intelligence algorithms scour these streams of real-time data for patterns of future traffic, possible congestion hotspots, and anomalies. Reinforcement learning agents can improve throughput and reduce wait times by swiftly modifying their traffic signals and routing algorithms. Among the numerous types of data that planners can evaluate with predictive analytics are peak-hour trends, route occupancy, and the consequences of events. Smart routing algorithms use realtime sensor data to increase transportation reliability while decreasing emissions.

title: Information Security Management for Securing Large Intelligent Networked Infrastructures in Banks and Financial Institutions
authors: Prakash Chandra Mondal, Pritu Parna Sarkar, Md. Nasim Adnan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: To drive financial business using cutting-edge information technology, owners are required to adopt information security frameworks to establish policies, procedures, and standard practices that keep the information security risks of IT assets within acceptable limits. This study proposes a model to assess an organization's overall information security risk posture and incident probability by continuously evaluating its current compliance status with ICT security controls, identifying gaps in control implementation, highlighting vulnerable components, analyzing historical compliance patterns and incident record datasets, and employing a Random Forest (RF) Model. The model introduces a proactive reporting mechanism to combat the risk of information security in large information infrastructures in organizations (banks or FIs) through the deployment of measures and countermeasures. Ultimately, the results of this research minimize the mean time to detect (MTTD) and regulatory fines, increase system-wide visibility, strengthen business continuity, and uphold essential security principles, including confidentiality, integrity, and availability (CIA), thus enhancing the strength of information security controls.

title: Optimization of Passive Cooperative Positioning and Dynamic Deployment for Multi-UAV Systems in Wide-Area Early Warning
authors: Liyuan Yang, Yongping Hao
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: In battlefield countermeasure environments, passive radar systems have become a critical means of air defence early warning due to their superior stealth characteristics and extensive detection range. Addressing the challenges of dynamic deployment and positioning accuracy optimisation for unmanned aerial vehicles within multi-station passive radar networks, a comprehensive framework has been established. This encompasses theoretical lower bound analysis for positioning accuracy, design of near-optimal deployment algorithms, and online trajectory optimisation. First, the passive radar positioning problem is modelled geometri-cally as the common intersection points of multiple hyperbolic surfaces. A nonlinear positioning equation system based on Time Difference of Arrival (TDOA) is constructed, utilizing the Chan algorithm to obtain an initial trajectory estimate for the early warning aircraft that accounts for positioning ambiguity and missing data. Building upon this foundation, the system derives the Fisher information matrix and Cramér-Rao bound (CRB) for the TDOA positioning model, quantitatively analysing the dominant influence of the geometric dilution of precision (GDOP) on the lower bound of positioning error. Secondly, we propose a mo-mentum-accelerated multi-start gradient descent algorithm that integrates a collaborative optimisation strategy combining pseudoinverse computation with weighted least squares. This approach significantly enhances the algorithm's robustness and global convergence ca-pability in non-convex, multi-extremum scenarios while maintaining computational effi-ciency. Finally, for wide-area surveillance missions, a sequential coverage optimisation model under spatiotemporal constraints is constructed. This incorporates trajectory segmentation, aggregation, and a multi-constraint task matching mechanism, enabling adaptive deployment of unmanned aerial vehicles under continuous velocity constraints and dynamic environmental conditions. Simulation results indicate that as the number of UAVs increases, positioning performance progressively approaches the CRB, with errors controllable within <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{3 0 - 4 0}$</tex> metres at a 300 -kilometre early warning range. Concurrently, expanding the baseline spacing significantly reduces the CRB, validating the pivotal role of geometric configuration optimisation in enhancing both system positioning performance and cost-effectiveness. These findings offer a scalable solution for border patrol and military reconnaissance missions that strikes a balance between theoretical optimality and engineering feasibility.

title: A Deep Learning Framework for the Classification of AI-Generated and Authentic Images
authors: Aaryan Yadav, Ankita Nainwal, Garima Sharma, Vipashi Kansal, Upma Jain
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The convergence of art and technology has presented a profound challenge in categorizing photographs, exacerbated by recent advancements in artificial intelligence (AI) capable of producing visuals nearly indistinguishable from genuine ones. Our research aims to discern between AI-generated images and those depicting real-world scenes like paintings and murals. This dynamic intersection of art and technology poses a complex problem: distinguishing between authentic and AI-generated pictures. Our study underscores the blurred boundaries between genuine and AI-generated image clusters in our dataset, contributing to the broader discourse on image categorization. The study focuses on building an approach and comparing our approach with deep learning models such as ResNet50, VGG16 and VGG19. Our model is able to achieve an accuracy of 92.59 % thereby increasing the performance of the system.

title: Efficient Sentiment Analysis of Amazon Reviews Using TF-IDF and Logistic Regression
authors: C Jackulni, S Raja, A Karthikeyan, A Kanchana, Karthik Venkatraman, M Karthikeyan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Customer reviews play a crucial role in the fast-growing e-commerce space, where vital information about product quality and overall brand perception is needed. They are extremely influential in what is bought and help organisations determine the degree to which consumers accept their products. Contemporary transformer-based models such as BERT may be highly accurate but require substantial computational resources, which can slow down real-time client-side execution. This work proposes a lightweight framework that combines Logistic Regression with TF-IDF, incorporating preprocessing and data transformation techniques to make it feasible. This is done by training a Logistic Regression classifier on feature vectors to classify reviews a having positive or negative sentiment. The results of the experiment suggest that the proposed method achieves 85.4 per cent accuracy, demonstrating the technique’s efficiency and computational simplicity. In addition to the algorithm’s performance, what is new in this work is the practical implementation and deployment of the model as a real-time sentiment classification Chrome browser extension that directly classifies sentiment in Amazon product page reviews. This study offers a scalable structure for integrating high-accuracy sentiment classification into low-resource client-side settings, bridging the gap between academic frameworks and web applications.

title: Structural Strength Prediction and Experimental Verification of Key Aircraft Components Combined with Neural Network
authors: Yao Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The structural strength prediction of key components of aircraft under complex and variable loads faces the problems of large finite element calculation, difficulty in accurately capturing nonlinear responses, and low prediction efficiency. To solve these bottlenecks, this paper introduces a structural strength prediction model based on a multi-layer perceptron neural network, uses historical test data and finite element simulation data for model training, and realizes rapid and accurate prediction of component strength under complex load conditions. Combined with physical loading tests for system verification, the reliability and applicability of the model are improved. The results show that this method significantly reduces the computational cost and improves the prediction accuracy. The prediction errors of the six key components are between ±0.05MPa, and the confidence interval widths are all around 25 MPa. It provides efficient technical support for aircraft structural design and safety assessment.

title: Term Preterm Birth Prediction Using Various Biomarkers
authors: Ramkumar Manjalkar, Tejashwini Hosamani, Shivani Mokashi, Mayur Marathe, Salma Shahapur, Veena Badiger
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Low gestational age, defined as births before 37 weeks, is still a major health issue globally, where neonatal deaths will reach millions of newborns. This study performed a comparative review of Machine Learning algorithms that focused on the use of Biomarkers from Genetics and Biochemistry, rather than Uterine Signals, to predict preterm birth. The use of serum-based Biomarkers, such as Progesterone, C-Reactive Protein (CRP), Interleukin-6 (IL-6), Fetal Fibronectin (FFN) and Single-Nucleotide Polymorphisms (SNPs), were examined as predictors for preterm birth. The study evaluated several Machine Learning models based on pre-processing and feature-selection procedures. The models trained and tested included Gradient Boosting, Random Forest, Decision Tree, K-Nearest Neighbors, Support Vector Machine and Deep Neural Network (DNN). Gradient Boosting achieved the best predictive accuracy of 87%. Gradient Boosting is noted for its superiority in generalizability and interpretability. The findings of this study emphasize the feasibility of utilizing Machine Learning with Biomarkers for assessing obstetric risk and pave the way for developing clinically deployable Decision Support Systems.

title: DeepFake detection for highly compressed social media and streaming: Scalability and efficiency evaluation of a High-Frequency Enhancement Network (HFEN)
authors: Atul Kumar Gupta, Jhankar Moolchandani
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Deepfake technology has developed at breakneck speed, bringing difficulties in ensuring the validity of digital information. Traditional deepfake detection technologies fail to fulfill the requirements for scalability and performance, especially in scenarios involving compressed data in social media platforms. We constructed a fresh strategy, the High-Frequency Enhancement Network, a dedicated architecture intended to discover edited content in a compressed environment. The suggested approach integrates frequency domain discovery and fusion with lightweight convolutional structures to improve detection results while being computation-efficient. Extensive experiments with multiple benchmark datasets indicate that HFEN achieves better detection accuracy than existing algorithms for the advanced current on compressed data with a reduced computing load. These findings demonstrate the potential of the HFEN for scalable and real-time DeepFake detection in real-world media situations.

title: End-to-End Deep Learning Framework for Automated Epilepsy Diagnosis from EEG Data
authors: M.Nagoor Meeral, A. Ameer Rashed Khan, Abhishek Mishra, Nandita Pradhan, Cuddapah Anitha, Reshma M
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Diagnosis of epilepsy based on electroencephalogram (EEG) data has remained a complicated question because the brain signals charged are extremely dynamic, inter subject difference and the inability to draw the line between seizure-induced activity and normal activity. Traditional deep learning approaches have been demonstrated to be effective in automating the practice but tend to fail to offer the time varied and space interaction of electrodes used in EEG data as well as fail to provide interpretability and ability to adjust to varied clinical scenarios. To overcome such shortcomings, the paper determines a new platform called Dual-Level Attention-Guided Fusion Network (DLAF-Net), in which temporal self-attention and spatial graph-based feature learning are combined with the multimodal signal fusion so as to effectively detect and categorize seizures. The framework uses multi-head focus in time under attention to emphasize significant oscillatory effects with different times scale and graph neural networks simulate the electrode connection so as to maintain the spatial-connection. Simultaneously, frequency domain data based on application of wavelet transformations is fused with provisions of raw signal representations through cross-modal integration. Contrastive learning policy improves generality across the subjects and knowledge distillation plan makes models to be efficient to use in clinical practice. The experimental validation shows that the proposed framework is more accurate, robust, and interpretable, which means the promising aspect of the offered structure as a clinically viable solution to help facilitate automated epilepsy diagnosis. The suggested DLAF-Net architecture attained a total accuracy of 93.4% in the automatic diagnosis of epilepsy using EEG data.

title: Research on Intelligent Machine Vision Inspection Method for Customized Personal Care Product Push-Cap Sealing Quality Based on YOLOv7
authors: Wentan Wang, Jian Huang, Jie Du, Shan Pang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Intelligent machine vision inspection of packaging quality for personal care products plays a crucial role in enhancing production quality and efficiency. This paper proposes a rapid and accurate detection method for assessing the sealing quality of press-top caps on personal care bottles using the deep learning-based YOLOv7 model for target recognition. First, based on the characteristics of customized press-top cap personal care products, a standardized annotation scheme is proposed that focuses solely on the bottle body, normal caps, and popped-up caps. Multiple model training and testing rounds were conducted using datasets with varying sample sizes. Experimental results demonstrate the accuracy and effectiveness of the proposed method: a model trained on over 2,000 images achieved a detection success rate of 99% and a MAP value of 100%. This approach significantly enhances the intelligent inspection capabilities for customized bottle cap sealing in personal care products.

title: Synthetic Sensor Data Augmentation using Generative Adversarial Networks for Improving Industrial Asset Reliability in Predictive Maintenance - A Systematic Review
authors: Stephen Mhetre, Sagar Shinde, Pramod Patil, Pranav Narwade, Prajwal Ramse, Rahul Patil
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Predictive Maintenance (PdM) is a critical strategy for industrial operators and reliability engineers, enabling them to forecast the remaining useful life (RUL) of assets in manufacturing, energy and aerospace industries. Contributing to reducing the downtime by usage of sensor time-series data. The PdM paradox- Due to the scarce amount of minor class data or run-to-failure data from cost and safety constraints, leads to the creation of imbalanced datasets in which there is less than five percent fault samples. The systematic review examines the use of generative adversarial networks (GANs) for synthetic sensor data augmentation in PdM, guided by PRISMA principles. Sequential run-to-failure trajectory generation with advances in multi-modal fusion is demonstrated by a taxonomy of GAN variants, including vanilla and conditional GANs, TimeGAN, and Wasserstein GANs with gradient penalty, This results in ten to thirty-five percent improvements in accuracy, F1-score, and RUL estimation over baselines like SMOTE on benchmarks like C-MAPSS and IMS. Training instability, mode collapse, and explainability deficiencies in multisensor scenarios are major obstacles. The review provides a pros-and-cons analysis, points out physical plausibility gaps, and describes future directions with a focus on explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) integration through techniques like SHAP for reliable augmentation and physics-informed GAN hybrids for unseen conditions. The synthesis aligns with the Industry 4.0 applications for developing interpretable frameworks.

title: Neuro Symbolic Ai For Context-Aware Decision Making In Real Time Systems
authors: Reshma Thakkallapelly, Sri Lekha Bandla, Siri Chandana Chava, Kalpesh Rathod, Venkata Ramana Gudelli, Quazi Taif Sadat
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Neuro-symbolic Artificial Intelligence (NS-AI) has become an exciting paradigm that combines perceptual learning of neural networks with the reasoning and interpretability of symbolic systems. The paper introduces a context-sensitive neuro-symbolic approach to real-time decision making, which aims at overcoming the weaknesses of the fully-data-driven models in highly-dynamical and safety-relevant scenarios. The suggested solution integrates deep neural representations of real-time perception of sensory data with symbolic knowledge graphs and symbolic reasoning to facilitate semantically based, explainable, and adaptable decision-making processes. Under uncertainty, contextual information such as environmental states, time limits and domain rules is constantly deduced and adjusted to inform decision policies. A hybrid inference pipeline is constructed to guarantee low-latency execution at the expense of logic consistency and explainability. The experimental testing in realistic representative real-time conditions shows that the suggested NS-AI framework can greatly enhance the accuracy, robustness and interpretability of the decisions made in comparison with standard neural and symbolic baselines. The findings emphasize the usefulness of neuro-symbolic integration to the development of reliable and context-sensitive intelligence to the next generation real-time systems like autonomous cyber-physical, intelligent monitoring systems, and adaptive control applications.

title: DUSeg: A Lightweight Cascaded Dual-U Network with Multi-Receptive Field for Segmentation
authors: Lexin Song, Doudou Si
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Optical Coherence Tomography Angiography (OCTA) enables visualization of retinal microvasculature, yet accurate vessel segmentation remains challenging due to low-contrast capillaries and lesion-induced artifacts. This paper presents DUSeg, a novel dual-U cascaded framework designed to specifically address vascular discontinuity and noise in OCTA images. The core innovation lies in a coarse segmentation-edge synergistic guidance mechanism. Coarse masks, generated by a lightweight TinyNet, are fused with edge features from an Edge Detection Stream (EDS) to serve as complementary inputs, directing a second U-Net to focus on refining boundaries and restoring continuity. Furthermore, we introduce Cascaded Receptive Field Spatial Encoding (CRSE) modules, which employ a “decomposition-recalibration” strategy. This involves using cascaded depthwise separable convolutions to efficiently capture multi-scale context, followed by an enhanced channel attention mechanism to dynamically recalibrate fused features and suppress redundancy. Experiments on the OCTA-500 dataset demonstrate that DUSeg achieves superior performance (91.77% Dice on OCTA-3M, 90.06% Dice on OCTA-6M and 94.61% Dice on ROSSA) with significantly reduced parameters and computational complexity compared to state-of-the-art methods. Ablation studies confirm the synergistic contribution of each component. DUSeg offers an efficient and promising solution for clinical OCTA analysis.

title: Research on Logistics Cost Control and Optimization in Automotive Manufacturing Supply Chain Based on DMAIC Model
authors: Junchun Ding
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Under the increasingly complex supply chain environment in the automotive manufacturing industry, logistics cost control has become a crucial factor in enhancing enterprise operational efficiency and market competitiveness. Based on the Six Sigma management philosophy, this study constructs a supply chain logistics cost control and optimization framework with the DMAIC model at its core. The research begins with a systematic analysis of cost composition, employing process mapping and cause-effect analysis tools to thoroughly identify and quantitatively diagnose issues in key supply chain logistics segments, including transportation organization, inventory configuration, and information coordination. Building on this foundation, targeted optimization strategies are proposed, such as route planning optimization, operational process reengineering, and information system integration, to improve logistics performance and reduce cost levels. Simultaneously, a process monitoring and performance feedback mechanism is established to ensure the stable operation and continuous improvement of the optimization solutions. The findings demonstrate that the DMAIC model can effectively support automotive manufacturers in identifying critical cost factors within logistics systems and achieve substantial cost control and logistics efficiency improvements through systematic methodologies, exhibiting strong practical application value.

title: A Systematic Review On Current Condition in Medical Healthcare Using Blockchain Technology
authors: Varsha Srivastava, Harshal Patil, Lalit Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Blockchain technology is increasingly acknowledged as a revolutionary catalyst for facilitating secure and streamlined data interchange processes. The technology is being positioned as a paradigm-shifting innovation that could fundamentally enhance the security, transparency, and efficiency of healthcare data management and interoperability systems. The healthcare domain has been identified as a pivotal sector where it collects and stores patients’ information on their database and keeps these data secure while also making sure that different systems work together. The main motive for integrating blockchain technology with the healthcare system is to have easy access to patients’ past information, which helps doctors to make critical decisions in emergency situations, which leads to improved patients’ health. A systematic review has been done on the medical healthcare system, and the paper offers an integration of blockchain technology within Electronic Health Records (EHR) and broader digital healthcare systems. The result shows that most researchers aimed to offer a framework, architecture, or model. The paper discusses the challenges related to EHR and blockchain and also provides a brief current condition of the healthcare system for Indian patients. The review systematically analyzes emergent technological innovations, evaluates implementation constraints, and outlines prospective research avenues, placing particular emphasis on advancing security protocols, privacy safeguards, and interoperability standards in healthcare data management ecosystems. The primary objective is to explore the potential applications of blockchain technology in the healthcare sector while identifying existing challenges and prospective research directions. Initially, relevant background information is presented to establish context. This phase is followed by an in-depth analysis of the findings, including an examination of the collected data, its characteristics, and the outcomes derived from the literature quality assessment.

title: Integration of IoT-Enabled Wearable Devices for Continuous Remote Healthcare Monitoring
authors: Sowmini Bandaru, Bhavya V, Syed Riyaz Ahammed, Shivakrishna Deepak Veeravalli, Poornima J, Ashok Vajravelu
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The introduction of remote healthcare monitoring with the assistance of IoT-based wearable gadgets has become one of the major requirements of the contemporary medical frameworks. These devices can record real-time physiological information including heart rate, blood oxygen, glucose levels, and body temperature that can be used to diagnose in early fifties in addition to being able to predict and manage their health. The suggested implementation of IoT enables data stability, patient confidentiality, and the secure collaboration of cloud computing technology to guarantee the reliability of data via the blockchain authentication detection, federated learning, and the application of edge computing. The system will be able to improve medical decision-making by adding wearable sensors to the use of smart analytics to minimize hospital dependency and the time taken by the hospital to react to emergency. Edge-assisted AI processing enables localized processing to guarantee energy savings and quicker insights. The framework provides the data privacy by lightweight encryption and interoperability between the multivendor IoT ecosystems. This convergence will enable round-the-clock monitoring of patients and healthcare prevention and offers scalable, flexible, and intelligent care to chronic and because of critical diseases, which will eventually lead to improved patient safety and better personal care outcomes. The proposed system was more effective than the current techniques, with its highest accuracy of 95.7% accuracy and 94.2% of efficient transmission.

title: PSO-Optimized Transformer for Intelligent Routing in Space-Air-Ground Integrated Communication Networks
authors: Jinling Liu, Zheng Li, Jinghan Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: The Space-Air-Ground Integrated Communication Network(SAGIN), integrating satellites, unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), and ground nodes, offers advantages such as wide coverage and strong anti-destruction capability. However, complex and dynamic network topology, time-varying link quality, and multi-constraint optimization requirements pose significant challenges to routing decisions. Traditional routing algorithms suffer from weak adaptability, reliance on manual rule design, and difficulty in handling complex scenarios. To address these issues, an intelligent routing algorithm based on Particle Swarm Optimization(PSO)and Transformer is proposed. Firstly, stratified sampling and standardization are applied to network state feature data to ensure data distribution balance and model training stability. Secondly, a Transformer-based routing classifier is designed, leveraging the self-attention mechanism to capture long-range dependencies among network features and combining positional encoding to enhance temporal dynamic perception. Thirdly, the PSO algorithm is adopted to optimize model hyperparameters(learning rate, weight decay, number of attention heads), and a multi-index fusion fitness function is utilized to improve comprehensive model performance. Finally, experiments are conducted on a simulated SAGIN dataset, comparing with traditional algorithms such as Decision Tree, XGBoost, and Random Forest. Experiments show that across multiple metrics (MAPE, RMSE, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2}$</tex>), the proposed algorithm outperforms traditional machine learning methods (e.g., Decision Tree, XGBoost): its MAPE is as low as 10.64%, RMSE is 13.28, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2}$</tex> reaches 0.975, enabling routing decisions with high precision and reliability.

title: A CBAM-Driven Multi-Scale YOLOv8 Architecture for High-Precision Small Debris Detection in LEO
authors: Mahant D. H., Veeresh S. H., Amana Z. Garag, Bhavana K., Sharada Shiragudikar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Detection of space debris is of paramount importance so as to maintain the safety in general of satellite operation, particularly in densely populated orbital zones where tiny and dim pieces pose serious hazard. Identifying these low-signal objects is difficult due to the most challenging issues being background noise, cluttered star fields and extremely small size. In this work, we present an improved YOLOv8 low-light satellite image adaptively targeted detection framework and evaluate its performance on a curated dataset that contains both synthetic and real space-like images. In fact, our model has strong robustness and allowed the detection of very faint debris. This was done in order to have a better understanding of the advantages of our approach. We also implemented RetinaNet and EfficientDet-D0 making a performance comparison using precision, recall, F1-score, and inference speed. The proposed YOLOv8–CBAM model has given the best result in general with stable convergence and superior detection quality for faint debris with a high balance of precision–recall in every case with strong mAP@0.5 and mAP@0.5:0.95 scores across the validation experiments. EfficientDet-D0 reached a higher recall of 0.85 and an F1-score of 0.81 thereby recovering more instances of debris in an effective manner. This in-depth analysis provides practical guidelines on selecting lightweight and efficient models for real-world space debris monitoring.

title: Complex Low-voltage Distribution Network Topology Identification Method Based on Characteristic Current Spectrum
authors: Xingyuan Fan, Haitong Gu, Bin Guo, Hui Li, Miaozhuang Cai, Shangda Liang
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: In view of the poor recognition performance and poor dynamic adaptability of traditional methods in complex low-voltage distribution network topology identification, this paper proposes a topology identification method based on characteristic current spectrum analysis. By establishing a physical mapping model between current high-frequency components and topology, a multi-scale spectrum energy entropy feature extraction algorithm is designed, and an improved transfer attention convolutional neural network is constructed to achieve robust recognition. The spectrum reconstruction technology is used to simultaneously identify line aging and topology mutation. Experiments show that this method achieves a topology recognition accuracy of up to 98.2%, an average node positioning error of 0.28 meters, and a recognition speed of less than 2 seconds in a 128-node hybrid line real-life platform, providing a high-precision and high-efficiency solution for the dynamic perception of complex low-voltage distribution network topology.

title: Structure-Aware Multi-Task Learning for Robust Vision-Language OCR
authors: Zhehan Li
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Optical Character Recognition (OCR) remains a core component of document understanding systems, yet existing OCR pipelines often struggle with complex layouts and incorrect reading order in real-world documents. Recent vision-language (VL) models have improved text recognition by jointly modeling visual and textual information, but document structure is still commonly treated as an implicit or downstream problem. As a result, layout misinterpretation has become a major source of errors in end-to-end document parsing. In this work, we propose a structure-aware multi-task learning framework built upon a compact vision-language OCR model. The proposed approach jointly optimizes text recognition, layout semantic classification, and reading order prediction within a unified training pipeline. By introducing explicit structural supervision as tightly coupled auxiliary objectives, the model is encouraged to internalize document-level organization while preserving the efficiency and modularity of practical OCR systems. Extensive experiments on diverse document benchmarks demonstrate that the proposed method consistently improves layout understanding and reading order accuracy, while also yielding gains in overall document parsing robustness. In particular, the structure-aware training framework shows clear advantages on documents with complex layouts and multilingual content, without introducing significant inference overhead. These results suggest that explicit modeling of document structure is a crucial step toward robust and scalable OCR-based document understanding.

title: Texture Mapping and Surface Models for Augmented Reality Applications
authors: Mukesh Kumar Rohil, Yash Agarwal
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: This paper demonstrates an examination of the technologies focused specifically on texture mapping and surface modelling in the context of their applications for Augmented Reality (AR) applications. The article highlights the significance of these technologies in modelling immersive and realistic AR experiences while addressing the challenges posed during their implementation. Additionally, the paper dives into the technical intricacies of texture mapping and surface modelling, along with exploring the latest advancements in these domains. Since the texture mapping is desirable to render with realism the virtual 3D models superimposed in an AR environment, hence, the texture mapping and surface modeling necessarily be considered simultaneously. It is observed that as the tech industry is moving towards mixed reality, organizations need to improve the processes and technologies to integrate the real and virtual worlds to support seamless immersive experience for the user interface involving the interactive capabilities with the virtual objects.

title: Grid-Based Localization and Speed Estimation with Extended Risk Evaluation Metrics for Edge-Fog-Assisted Blind Spot Monitoring Using Digital Twin Simulation
authors: Shifa Mohammad, Ashish Tiwari, Meera. M Dhabu
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: This paper outlines a new framework for blind spot avoidance and collision risk assessment using grid-based vehicle localization, velocity estimation, and multimodal risk assessment in edge-fog design. Traditional visibility models typically consider visibility as a fixed parameter for assessment, but this framework introduces four different dynamic risk metrics to support proactive risk awareness of hazards. These hazards are represented through dynamic calculations as a function of visibility, distance, object type, and/or speed. This framework is capable of operating on Raspberry Pi edge devices and fog nodes using YOLOv5 for object detection and ultrasonic sensors for distance estimation, and components of the predictive risk model demonstrate improved predictions, fewer false alarms, and greater collision avoidance based on traffic density and weather conditions in Unity 3D digital twin simulations. This framework offers predictable, scalable, and cost-effective deployments for onboard systems in intelligent driver assistance and autonomous vehicle systems.

title: Hybrid Deep Learning – Based Process Big Data Mining for Complex Workflow Analysis
authors: Bo Hu, Jian Cui, Yuqi Shi
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: To improve the analysis of complex workflow big data, a workflow mining framework based on hybrid deep learning was designed. This framework integrates multimodal data to construct a unified tensor representation and utilizes CNN, LSTM, and attention mechanisms to collaboratively model spatiotemporal dependencies and dynamic features, achieving high-precision prediction and anomaly detection of workflow behavior. Experiments show that this method outperforms traditional methods (Markov models, decision trees, random forests) and single-model deep learning methods (DeepLog, SAFA, standalone LSTMs, GAN-based anomaly detectors) in both activity prediction and anomaly recognition tasks, and all models adopt identical input representations and training protocols to ensure fair comparison, validating its effectiveness and robustness in practical applications.

title: Single Imputation for Missing Data via Linear Regression
authors: Xiaomei Feng
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Missing data is a significant issue in various fields of research. Data imputation can effectively address the problems associated with missing data. This study focuses on using the regression imputation method from statistical techniques to fill in missing values. The advantages of this imputation method lie in its ability to leverage existing data relationships for accurate predictions. Regression imputation was applied to the missing values generated after integrating the datasets and compared the imputation results with the original datasets. Hypothesis testing methods were employed to validate the outcomes. The results indicate that the performance of the dataset after regression imputation improved overall, with accuracy showing significant enhancement compared to the original datasets. Specifically, an accuracy rate of 99.85% was achieved using the AdaBoost classification model, which is 4.98% higher than the average accuracy of the original dataset.

title: Advanced Deep Learning Framework for Highly Accurate Infant Cry Signal Classification
authors: Pankaj Saini, Burungale Swapnil Birudev, Diksha Holkar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Accurate infant cry signal classification is a critical aspect in infant distress root cause identification and resolution, enabling timely effective interventions. Difficulty is present due to the complexity and severe class imbalance of infant cry datasets. A novel deep learning strategy based on convolutional neural networks with a policy of data augmentation to enhance multi-class performance in a 9-class infant cry audio dataset is presented here. Our boosting method effectively balances the classes without inducing bias, permitting better feature learning. Experimental outcomes demonstrate that our proposed approach is superior to the available state-of-the-art models with a combined accuracy of approximately 98%. Extensive per-class testing and confusion matrix analysis confirm the robust performance of the model on every infant cry class. This work establishes a new benchmark for infant cry message prediction and demonstrates the promise of real-time surveillance in pediatric medicine.

title: Power Quality Analysis in Setting up EV Charging with Microgrid System
authors: Jitender Kaushal
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The fast inclusion of Electric Vehicle (EV) charging stations into contemporary power systems poses considerable hurdles to sustaining power quality (PQ) within microgrids. As the global energy landscape shifts towards decarbonization, microgrid-based electric vehicle charging infrastructures provide sustainable and resilient alternatives. Nonlinear charging loads, variable renewable generation, and bidirectional power flow can result in harmonics, voltage fluctuations, and unbalanced loading. This paper introduces a thorough approach for power quality analysis of EV charging incorporated into a microgrid system. The study employs simulation-based analysis utilizing MATLAB/Simulink to assess factors like total harmonic distortion (THD), voltage regulation, reactive power compensation, and frequency deviation. The paper further examines mitigating options, encompassing active power filters, demand-side management, and the synchronization of renewable energy. The suggested framework facilitates the design of PQ-compliant EV charging networks that improve grid reliability and operational efficiency.

title: Energy-Efficient 6T SRAM Design: Comparative Analysis of 45 nm CMOS and 18 nm FinFET Technologies
authors: J Harsha, Anuradha C, Suja N, Abisha Joylin I, Vivek Singh, Ajay Kumar Dwivedi
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: This paper describes the design and comparative performance of a 6T SRAM cell using 45nm CMOS and 18nm FinFET technology. As technology scales further, traditional planar CMOS is plagued by short channel effects, leakage currents, and lowered reliability, which makes FinFET a viable option given its better gate control and lower leakage. The SRAM cell is implemented and simulated using Cadence Virtuoso, and its performance is quantified with respect to read/write delay, power consumption, and static noise margin (SNM). Results indicate that FinFET-based 6T SRAM requires lower power consumption, is more stable, and possesses greater noise margins than CMOS, while ensuring competitive switching speed. These results point out that FinFET improves the robustness and efficiency of 6T SRAM cells such that they are well-suited for low-power and high-performance VLSI designs, whereas CMOS provides a valuable baseline for performance comparison.

title: Energy-Aware Neural Adaptation for Sustainable AI Inference on Resource-Constrained Systems
authors: Tejas Pravinbhai Patel, Milan Parikh, Jeevan Shanbhag, Amit Kumar Padhy, Vinay Soni
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: The proliferation of deep neural networks (DNNs) on edge and mobile devices has intensified concerns regarding energy consumption and carbon footprint. Static neural network execution results in significant energy waste—particularly for simple inputs that could be processed with reduced computational effort. This paper presents an energy-aware neural adaptation framework that dynamically adjusts inference execution based on input characteristics and available energy budgets. Our approach combines three complementary mechanisms: adaptive precision scaling (4-bit to 16-bit quantization), confidence-based early exit, and dynamic path selection. Through comprehensive evaluation on ARM Cortex-A53 and NVIDIA Jetson Nano platforms using MobileNetV2, ResNet-18, and EfficientNet-B0 on ImageNet and CIFAR-100 datasets, we demonstrate energy reductions of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$42-58 \%$</tex> while maintaining accuracy within <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.2 \%$</tex> of baseline models. On deployments processing 1 million inferences daily, our framework reduces energy consumption from 2.8 kWh to 1.3 kWh, translating to measurable sustainability improvements in largescale edge AI deployments.

title: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
authors: N/A
publish: N/A

title: Detection of Deepfake Image Tampering using Self-supervised and Adversarial Feature Similarity Learning
authors: Sweta Maurya, Narander Kumar, Shishir Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Deepfake technology has been a significant advancement in the field of artificial intelligence (AI). Deepfakes are AI-generated fake media created using Deep learning. In recent years, these media have spread quickly on the internet. Apart from positive uses, it is often used negatively, compromising the privacy, security, and trustworthiness of digital multimedia available on the internet. That’s why it is essential to have robust detection mechanisms to verify the factual accuracy of the real content.
