To fulfil this objective, this paper proposes a hybrid model based on two techniques: self-supervised learning and adversarial feature similarity learning, to perform deepfake detection using the open-source Deepfake-vs-Real-8K dataset. The proposed hybrid model uses SimSiam, a framework in self-supervised learning to learn useful features by creating two different transformed versions of the same image. This framework teaches the encoder about the similarity between the two versions of an image. The model is fine-tuned with Adversarial feature similarity learning, which compares the features of two images and adjusts their distance from each other based on the similarity between them. By utilising feature similarity learning, the proposed model detects whether the image is fake or real. The dataset involves a balanced set of real and manipulated images, which are utilised for evaluating the performance. The proposed model achieved an accuracy of 99.67% and a precision of 100% for fake images, which shows its superiority over FCNN, DCNN, VGG16, and MobileNetV2 models in the detection of deepfake images.

title: Developing Advanced Machine Learning Models to Preserve Privacy and Ensure Data Security for IoT-Enabled Smart Consumer Devices within Intelligent Smart Homes
authors: Monika Khatkar, Tejpal Singh, Sandeep Pandey, B. Papachary, Shraddha Tiwari, S M Shakina
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Rapid implementation of smart devices connected with IoT technology has changed home automation, turning regular homes into smart ones with facilities of automation, customization, and usability. Nonetheless, the consistent transfer of information between the devices, sensors and cloud platforms will jeopardize privacy, data leaks, and illegal intrusions. In such regards, machine learning would turn out to be an effective tool in securing the data and ensuring user privacy in smart home ecosystem. In the proposed paper, we present an innovative framework, which uses sophisticated machine learning models in order to detect threats and detect anomalies and protect user data in real-time. The proposed system uses a combination of deep learning and federated learning technology in order to keep the data local and at the same time update the detection models in a collaborative way. It encompasses encryption-based feature selection and lightweight models of prediction to deal with the resource limitations of the consumer-level IoT gadgets. Adaptive learning is incorporated, and the real-time analytics are conducted without violating the privacy of the user. We also test our framework on publicly available IoT datasets showing high detection accuracy, low rate of false positive and better resistance to privacy invasion attacks. This paper establishes the future course of intelligent security to smart homes that undertakes privacy-sensitive machine learning strategies to match the steadily increasing requirements of safe and trustful connectivity living conditions. Their proposed FL-Secure IoT model has attained short-term prediction accuracy of 96.18 and long-term anomaly detection F1-score of 92.86 indicating it as the best among any other model on the test results.

title: SLIM: Shuffling-based Image Encryption Using the Logistic Map
authors: Soumya Mandal, Nandan Pradhan, Biswarup Yogi, Souvik Maitra
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The proposed method is a lightweight encryption based on pixel shuffle and the chaotic logistic map. The scheme provides high security with intense confusion and diffusion in the spatial domain. An image’s pixels are scrambled using the secret key, while their intensities are scrambled with a chaos sequence generated from the logistic map. We analyzed the performance using histogram analysis, information entropy, MSE, PSNR, NPCR, UACI, and correlation coefficients. The secret-key entropy is 7.999, NPCR is 99.60%, and UACI is 49.88%, indicating high randomness and sensitivity of the scheme. The average MSE and PSNR values are 105.66 dB and 27.89 dB, respectively, indicating the best reconstruction. The correlation coefficients analyzed are on the verge of zero, which means reduced statistical dependency between neighbouring pixels. The method is fast and secure, and thus can be used in resource-constrained real-time applications.

title: Opportunities and Challenges Related to AI in Education
authors: Ashutosh, Abhiraj Dash, Monika Bhardwaj, Mani Joshi
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) in education is a structural shift from traditional learnings, that carries both risks and potential to transform individuals, communities or a nation. This paper examines the dual nature of AI's influence on education. It examines the main advantages, such as the ability to provide guidance to individuals at a large scale, increasing the productivity through automation, and promote greater accessibility for a range of learners. At the same time, it tackles the crucial problem that need to be overcome for responsible execution and implementation of AI. These include concerns about data privacy and security, the possibility of academic dishonesty and the dehumanization of learning, the threat of algorithmic bias sustaining social bias and prejudice, and the danger of the digital divide growing the gap of poor and rich further. The paper makes the case that a purposeful, human-centric framework is necessary to both realize AI's potential and mitigate its risks.

title: Coordinated control of Energy Management system in a Solar Based Charging Station having Grid Integration
authors: Ankita Singh, Dr Sandeep Bhongade
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: In this paper, a coordinated Energy Management System (EMS) is developed for a solar-powered electric vehicle (EV) charging station consisting of a battery storage system and connection to the utility power grid. The energy management system (EMS) suggested during the research makes use of a PF_GenOpt (Pareto-Front Genetic Optimization) algorithm to get the best real-time power flow from the solar panels, the station battery, and the EVs to the grid. This is done under changing operating conditions. The proposed method empowers multi-objective optimization targeting the maximization of solar energy consumption, maintaining the DC link voltage, and reliability of EV charging even under variable irradiance. The simulated outcomes confirm excellent performance of the system: the solar irradiation reductions from 1000 W/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> to 600 W/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> and further to 400 W/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> were expertly controlled, and the DC link voltage was held at about 800 V with very slight ripple. The solar plant was able to deliver power in the range of 12-25 kW, the voltage for EV charging was kept at around 210 V with the current at approximately 8 A which allowed for continuous charging even during the transition of loads. The battery had a very precise operation with SOC varying from 99.997% to 99.987%, thus proving that energy buffering was very efficient. The grid voltage and current waveforms were balanced throughout the operation at about ±400 V and ±400 A, which means that the system was reliable. In conclusion, the PF_GenOpt-based EMS has been shown to improve energy efficiency, stability, and scalability of the future big-scale solar-integrated EV charging infrastructures.

title: Multiscale Convolutional Wavelet-BiGRU Network in Wearable Sensor-Based Human Activity Recognition
authors: Jiarui Zhang, Qin Wei
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Human activity recognition (HAR) based on wearable sensors is crucial for applications such as health monitoring, fitness tracking, and human-computer interaction. However, traditional methods that rely only on raw time-series data are often difficult to effectively capture the multi-scale time information required to distinguish complex activities. To solve this problem, we propose a multiscale convolutional wavelet-BiGRU network for wearable sensor-based human activity recognition (MCW-BiGRU), which uses multi-scale temporal patterns, time-frequency representation, and contextual dependency modeling to improve activity recognition performance. We evaluated MCW-BiGRU on two well-known HAR datasets (WISDM and PAMAP2) and achieved accuracies of 99.44% and 96.15%, respectively, demonstrating that the proposed network is competitive.

title: Task-Interdependency-Aware Scheduling for Data Relay Satellite Networks
authors: Haiyang Zheng, Xinqin Gao, Huanyu Wang, Yining Yang, Mingshun Yang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: With the increasing demand for continuous space-based data transmission, relay satellite scheduling faces growing challenges under limited resources and complex operational constraints. In practical scenarios, many service requests are composed of interdependent subtasks with strict precedence and temporal interval constraints, which are insufficiently considered in most existing studies. This paper proposes a task-interdependency-aware scheduling method for relay satellite networks, in which tasks are organized into task groups and scheduled as integral units. A mathematical model is formulated to explicitly characterize inter-task precedence relations, temporal constraints, and antenna switching times, with the objective of maximizing the weighted completion of task groups. To solve the proposed problem, a Group-based Forward Scheduling Heuristic (GFSH) algorithm is developed, which determines task group priorities based on equivalent execution duration and adopts a forward time-domain scheduling strategy to ensure sequence integrity. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method significantly improves both task group completion rate and weighted completion performance under different task load conditions.

title: Human-Guided Physical Adversarial Makeup Attack for Natural and Effective Face Recognition Evasion
authors: Jiang-Yi Zeng, Yen-Chia Kuo, Meng-Ze Chen, Hsin-Hung Cho, Min-Yan Tsai, Fan-Hsun Tseng, Chi-Yuan Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Cybernetics and Innovations (ICCI)
abstract: Physical adversarial attacks on face recognition remain challenging because digitally generated perturbations are difficult to reproduce accurately on a human face. Although makeup-based attacks provide a natural and unobtrusive medium, existing methods do not offer a practical way for users to apply adversarial patterns with precision and consistency. This work presents a human-guided physical adversarial makeup framework that integrates facial segmentation, landmark localization, a Genetic Algorithm (GA) for makeup optimization, and real-time feedback during application. The framework is designed to balance attack effectiveness and physical realizability. Experimental results show that users can apply natural-looking adversarial makeup with the proposed guidance framework, leading to substantial shifts in model predictions and targeted impersonation in the tested real-world attack cases.

title: CodeVision: A Self-Explaining Intelligence Platform for Understanding Complex Software Repositories
authors: R. Thirumalai Murugan, R Shreevathsan, V Kumara Ayyappan, M Yogapriya, J Udhayakumar, S Nagomiya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: As software repositories scale to become monorepos, containing millions of lines of code, the cognitive chasm between the metadata from the repository and the developer’s knowledge of the system expands. This is called “Ownership Blindness,” thus creating knowledge silos and a delay in code reviews in pull requests, and unintended architectural regressions. This paper presents CodeVision (also developed internally as CodeFamily), a production-grade, full-stack web application designed to bridge this visibility gap. CodeVision implements a decoupled three-tier architecture, consisting of a React 18 frontend for visualization, a high-performance.NET 8 backend for orchestration, and a specialized Node.js sidecar service for multilingual Syntax Tree parsing. Unlike conventional static analysis software, the CodeVision system employs a dynamic Semantic Ownership Algorithm that uses commit volume, frequency, and recency to distinguish genuine subject-matter experts. Moreover, it adds a “Collaborative Context Layer"-a 9-table relational schema that preserves sticky notes, discussions, and line-level comments, including human-like, natural-language comments directly on the code artefacts. Experimental evaluation on open-source code repositories clearly shows that CodeVision’s predictive dependency model can forecast merge conflicts and greatly reduce the time needed to onboard new engineers and identify code owners.

title: ARGUS: An AI-based Real-time Global Unified Deepfake Screening System
authors: G Subashini, S Swetha, N Sowmiya, N. Kalpana
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Detecting manipulated audiovisual content on a global scale, ARGUS is a unified, multi-modal, real-time screening system. The system deploys an ensemble of framelevel forgery detectors, physiological-signal detectors, temporal consistency transformers, audio anti-spoofing models, and provenance verification across cloud infrastructure and edge nodes. ARGUS places a strong emphasis on resilience to invisible manipulation techniques through generalizable representations, such as self-blended augmentation methods and blending-boundary X-ray analysis. The system includes human-in- the-loop verification, explainability mechanisms, and continuous federated updates. With real-time processing capabilities with latency under 500 ms, we assess ARGUS on significant public benchmarks and in-the-wild data streams, attaining 97.8% accuracy on FaceForensics++ and 94.2% on DFDC. For worldwide deepfake detection, our open blueprint allows for scalable, privacy-conscious deployment.

title: Real-Time Detection of Weapon-Carrying Individuals Using Deep Learning-Based Visual Surveillance
authors: Ekkanit Sunsuwan, Mahasak Ketcham
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Cybernetics and Innovations (ICCI)
abstract: This paper presents a privacy-aware multi-sensor intelligent framework for detecting weapon-carrying individuals in indoor environments. To preserve privacy and avoid capturing personally identifiable RGB information, the proposed system integrates thermal imaging and millimeterwave radar features while maintaining robust detection performance under challenging indoor conditions. A hierarchical two-stage detection architecture is developed. In Stage 1, human presence is detected using fused thermal-radar features. In Stage 2, weapon-carrying risk is assessed through a structured two-step classification strategy: (i) weapon versus noweapon detection and (ii) weapon-type classification (gun versus knife). Compared with conventional single-stage multi-class approaches, the proposed hierarchical design reduces computational complexity, mitigates false alarms, and improves decision stability. Experimental results demonstrate that XGBoost achieves a Macro-F1 score of 0.9947 for human detection and 0.9212 for weapon detection, while Random Forest achieves 0.8656 for weapon-type classification. The results confirm that multi-sensor feature fusion significantly enhances robustness and reliability in privacy-sensitive indoor surveillance scenarios. The proposed framework offers a practical solution for next-generation privacy-preserving security systems.

title: Accurate and Robust Estimation of Single-Diode PV Model Parameters Using the Artificial Lemming Algorithm
authors: Krishnapada Mondal, Md. Abu Salman, Md. Ferdus Mahmud Fahim, Md. Khabirul Islam, Shahed Ahmed, Mehadi Hasan Emon
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: To improve the effectiveness, accuracy, and safety of solar power generation in line with the criteria of applicable standards, it is important to accurately assess the characteristics of photovoltaic (PV) models. This paper introduces the concept of Artificial Lemming Algorithm (ALA), a novel inspired optimization procedure based on the behaviors and the collective movement of lemmings in the nature environment. The latter method, linked to the Newton-Raphson method, was utilized to identify parameters in the Single Diode Model (SDM). The Wilcoxon signed-rank test (WSRT) was utilized to scrutinize the beneficial effect of ALA against another threeoptimization procedure. As observed from the test outcomes, it can be concluded that the suggested ALA algorithm is statistically superior to Whale Optimization Algorithm (WOA), Harris Hawks Optimizer (HHO), and Gray Wolf Optimizer (GWO), with all p-values of $\mathbf{2. 0 0} \times \mathbf{1 0}^{-\mathbf{6}}$ and at least favorable test statistics. Simulated results show that the ALA method is better than other methods, which converge faster, are more stable, and obtain minimal RMSE. The ALA shows the lowest RMSE for SDM, which is $\mathbf{7. 7 3} \boldsymbol{\times} \mathbf{1 0}^{\mathbf{- 4}}$, utilizing the MATLAB platform with the France R.T.C solar cell dataset. These findings indicate that ALA is a powerful and practical approach to predict PV parameters reliably. It could also significantly improve the accuracy of calculations in solar energy applications.

title: HLMS-TL: An Improved T-L Magnetic Compensation Algorithm Based on the Huber-LMS Method
authors: Juan Li, Xinyu Mei, Xuerong Cui, Jingyao Zhang, Bin Jiang, Lei Li
publish: IEEE Sensors Letters
abstract: Aircraft-generated magnetic interference significantly degrades the accuracy of aeromagnetic surveys. Effective compensation is thus essential for extracting valid target signatures. To address the limitations of the traditional Tolles-Lawson (T-L) model in handling dynamic gradients and non-Gaussian noise, this paper proposes an enhanced compensation algorithm termed HLMS-TL. The core innovations include: 1) a gradient-attitude joint model incorporating geomagnetic gradient tensors and inertial parameters to resolve field projection distortion; 2) a dynamic band-pass filter based on cubic spline curvature extremum detection for adaptive interference boundary identification; and 3) a hierarchical Huber-LMS framework for robust noise reduction. Experimental results from AFRL datasets and UAV field tests demonstrate that HLMS-TL achieves an interference improvement ratio (IR) of 20.61 and 14.05, respectively. This represents a 2.44-fold performance increase over the traditional T-L model, validating its efficacy for high-precision aeromagnetic surveys in complex environments.

title: Impact-Aware Robust Convex Model Predictive Control for Quadruped Locomotion on Uncertain Terrain
authors: Kuikui Xue, Xin Xin, Jiangyong Hu
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Deploying legged robots in complex environments requires robust traversal capabilities on unstructured terrain. However, existing locomotion controllers may exhibit degraded robustness when subjected to rapidly varying terrain-induced impact disturbances. To address this challenge, we propose an impact-aware robust convex model predictive control (IRCMPC) for quadruped locomotion by explicitly modeling terrain-induced impacts as bounded uncertainties in the single rigid body (SRB) dynamics. The proposed IRCMPC integrates robust control principles into the model predictive control to handle the impact disturbances experienced by the robot. First, we model a momentum-based bound on the maximum potential impact at each step and introduce a relaxation factor that reduces conservatism when severe impacts are unlikely to occur. We then formulate a robust min-max MPC problem and transform this problem into a standard quadratic optimization problem to ensure computational efficiency. Comparative simulation and hardware experiments on a Unitree Go2 robot demonstrate that IRCMPC improves locomotion robustness under intensified impact conditions where conventional convex model predictive control (CMPC) fails. The results further show that, in the tested scenarios, IRCMPC achieves a more favorable trade-off between traversal robustness and tracking performance than adaptive nonlinear centroidal model predictive control (ANCMPC).

title: SCZ-HA: A Seamless Cross-Zone Handover Authentication Scheme for V2I Communication
authors: Xia Feng, Ruomeng Lin, Kaiping Cui, Liangmin Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Vehicle-to-infrastructure (V2I) communication connects vehicles and transportation infrastructures (e.g., road-side units, 5G base stations), facilitating bidirectional information exchanges. As a matter of fact, V2I communication is constrained by the limited wireless coverage, which forces vehicles to undergo cross-zone handovers frequently. Existing protocols for cross-zone handover either incur high overhead or neglect the risks of attacks during service disconnections. To tackle these concerns, we propose a seamless cross-zone authentication scheme tailored for V2I communication, called SCZ-HA. Drawing inspiration from the zone encryption scheme (EuroS&P 2020), we integrate long-term and short-term credentials to optimize efficiency and enhance security. When entering a new zone, vehicles use their valid long-term credentials to request short-term credentials from the local infrastructure. These short-term credentials are then encapsulated and distributed to neighboring zones using Shamir's secret sharing, thereby reducing communication overhead. During cross-zone handovers, vehicles only present encapsulations for verification by the target infrastructure, thus eliminating the need to validate full credential contents. Unlike previous schemes, SCZ-HA avoids redundant verifications, enabling millisecond-level handovers and supporting traceability of malicious vehicles. Experimental results show that our scheme achieves a substantial reduction in authentication latency, with an average improvement of 70.54% compared to state-of-the-art schemes.

title: Event-Driven Cloud-Native Deep Neural Framework for Autonomous Tuberculosis Diagnostics Using Fully Serverless Pipelines
authors: Akash K, Naresh R
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The goal of this work is to address the continuing challenge of early and reliable tuberculosis detection, especially in resource-limited areas characterized by thin diagnostic infrastructure and where delays in screening contribute to continued transmission. This study proposes a cloud-native neural network architecture deployed as part of a fully serverless computing model; this approach allows for scalable, cost-efficient, and on-demand processing of medical imaging data. The aim will be to provide a highly accessible, automated diagnostic pipeline that can support large populations without requiring dedicated hardware or continuous server maintenance. The proposed system brings together distributed data ingestion, event-driven model execution, and dynamic resource allocation to ensure rapid inference and seamless integration with existing health frameworks. Its novelty is based on the combination of serverless orchestration with a specialized deep learning architecture, optimized for low-latency medical prediction and providing a lightweight yet dependable tool for global tuberculosis screening while supporting broader initiatives for equitable, technology-enabled healthcare delivery.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Magnetics

title: High-Accuracy and Robust Non-Cooperative UAV Localization: RSS-Based Framework with Unknown Transmission Power
authors: Fasong Wang, Qilong Li, Xingwang Li, Jiankang Zhang, Ming Zeng, Dusit Niyato, Arumugam Nallanathan, Chau Yuen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Communications
abstract: This paper proposes a robust received signal strength (RSS)-based localization framework for non-cooperative unmanned aerial vehicles. Conventional RSS methods face three fundamental obstacles: susceptibility to heavy-tailed measurement noise, intractable non-convexity, and severe accuracy degradation when target transmission power is unknown. These vulnerabilities present critical security risks to emerging low-altitude economy networks. To overcome these limitations, we propose an integrated joint-estimation architecture. First, a cascaded preprocessing pipeline, combining Gaussian outlier suppression and statistical median weighting, is developed to mitigate multipath-induced biases and minimize variance. Second, an information-theoretic base station (BS) selection mechanism is designed to identify geometrically optimal BSs, thereby exponentially reducing computational overhead in both uniform and random deployment scenarios. Third, the power-unknown problem is reformulated via semidefinite programming, absorbing the unknown parameter into a higher-dimensional convex cone to guarantee global convergence without relying on initial guesses. Extensive Monte Carlo simulations demonstrate that under uniform BS deployment, our strategy achieves sub-10-meter accuracy (approximately 5 m root mean square error) using only 5 selected BSs in typical urban conditions with a path loss exponent of 3. Consequently, this approach delivers a highly accurate and computationally efficient solution for real-time target tracking in complex environments.

title:
Polymer Electret Based WSe
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Transistor for Reconfigurable Logic-in-Memory
authors: Long-Xin Ren, Xu Gao, Jian-Long Xu, Yuan-Zhe Shao, Hang-Fan Mu, Zi-Yi Yin, Zhong-Da Zhang, Ya-Nan Zhong, Sui-Dong Wang
publish: IEEE Electron Device Letters
abstract: Two-dimensional (2D) semiconductors have emerged as promising channel materials for transistor memories, however, achieving high reliability at low programming voltage remains challenging. We report a WSe<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> field-effect transistor with an embedded polymer electret (poly(2-vinylnaphthalene), PVN) as the charge-trapping layer, empowering nonvolatile memory function with excellent performance. The PVN layer provides deep charge traps, yielding a large memory window (~ 5 V) under gate biases of ±6 V. The transistor exhibits a high ON/OFF current ratio (~ 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">7</sup>) and retains the programmed states over 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sup> s. Endurance up to 2000 program-erase cycles is also demonstrated. We further achieve multilevel storage by tuning duration of gate pulses, and show the state update with programming pulses as short as 100 ns. Finally, a pre-programmable circuit is implemented with the WSe<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> memory, demonstrating the reconfigurable logic behavior. This work introduces the polymer-electret-based 2D transistor that may be well-suited for flexible electronics and logic-in-memory applications.

title:
Oxygen-Stoichiometry-Controlled Linearity and Uniformity of W/WO
                    <sub>x</sub>
                    /W Fixed-Resistance Arrays for Edge-AI Inference
authors: Hoesung Ha, Dongmin Kim, Ohhyuk Kwon, Yoori Seo, Hyunsang Hwang
publish: IEEE Electron Device Letters
abstract: We present W/WO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</sub>/W fixed-resistance synaptic devices that enable inference-only weight mapping without iterative programming. By establishing an oxygen-stoichiometry-dependent operating window in WO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</sub> that provides transport characteristics suitable for inference, near-ohmic read behavior (linearity factor ≤ 1.01) and low device-to-device variation (~ 0.01) are simultaneously achieved. Within this window, conductance is determined by the defined device area, enabling 5-bit weight representation. These characteristics are preserved in a packaged 32 × 32 array, where EMG-based neural inference reaches 85.4% accuracy. This work establishes an inference-suitable material regime for fixed-resistance WO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</sub> synapses and provides a practical pathway toward low-variation edge-AI hardware.

title: EquiLink Bridge: A Semi-Custodial Approach to Cross-Chain Transactions via TEE
authors: Tingda Shen, Yebo Feng, Jin Dong, Konglin Zhu, Lei Jiao, Lin Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Services Computing
abstract: The rising demand for blockchain interoperability is accelerating advancements in cross-chain bridge technologies, which are crucial for a seamless information transfer in multi-blockchain ecosystems. Existing blockchain bridges are typically classified into two categories: custodial and non-custodial. Custodial bridges use a trusted third party for easier and faster transactions but depend on custodian trust, while non-custodial bridges enhance transparency and control with smart contracts but increased complexity and latency. Currently, no bridge design successfully combines the benefits of both while avoiding their drawbacks. This paper presents EquiLink, a semi-custodial bridge that combines the benefits of both custodial and non-custodial methods. EquiLink employs a smart contract, known as the EquiLink Service, to initiate cross-chain transfers. It then uses the EquiLink Network, a system composed of remote-attested Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs), to verify and issue these transfers between two blockchains. Any eligible participants validated through remote attestation can join the EquiLink Network and contribute to the bridge's functionality. Additionally, participants are regulated by an economic model, providing an extra layer of security through economic incentives. This semi-custodial bridge enhances transparency and control for users. Meanwhile, it mitigates the risks associated with centralized custody and decentralization. In the evaluation, EquiLink is resilient against both replay and physical attacks. Additionally, it operates efficiently, reducing transaction costs by 14.1% and latency by 18.9%.

title: HMEPR: A Hierarchical Multimodal Emotion Recognition Framework with Redundancy-Aware Feature Refinement
authors: Bo Zhong, Jia Li, Yeling Xu, Aoduo Li, Rongpei Zhang
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Multimodal emotion recognition (MER) aims to infer affective states from heterogeneous inputs such as text, audio, and visual signals. In practice, however, redundant and conflicting information across modalities can weaken cross-modal alignment and impair downstream fusion, especially when emotionally irrelevant tokens or noisy modality-specific patterns are present. To address this problem, we propose HMEPR, a hierarchical multimodal emotion recognition framework built on an alignment-refinement-fusion paradigm. The framework first employs a Cross-Modal Interaction Alignment (CMIA) module to enhance semantic consistency through bidirectional interaction and gated alignment. It then applies a Redundancy-Aware Feature Refinement (RFR) module based on Cross-Modal Native Sparse Attention (CNSA) to suppress redundant non-text information by means of text-guided sparse connectivity, semantic hash grouping, and dynamic routing, while preserving emotionally salient cues. Finally, a Multi-Scale Multimodal Feature Fusion (MMFF) module aggregates the refined representations through multi-scale context modeling and adaptive channel-spatial weighting. Experiments on CMU-MOSI and CMU-MOSEI show that HMEPR achieves competitive performance against representative baselines. Ablation studies and additional analyses confirm the contribution of each component, particularly the role of the redundancy-aware refinement strategy in improving alignment quality and fine-grained affect modeling. Overall, the results indicate that explicitly inserting a refinement stage between alignment and fusion improves robustness to redundant multimodal cues.

title: Efficient Data Pruning via Early-Stage Prediction Stability and Balanced Sampling
authors: Chih-Yao Hu, Po-Yung Chou, Cheng-Hung Lin
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Recently, deep learning has achieved remarkable performance gains across a wide range of vision tasks, largely driven by increasingly large-scale training datasets. However, recent studies have shown that many datasets contain redundant or even mislabeled samples, which not only provide limited benefit for model learning but also incur substantial computational costs. Data pruning has therefore emerged as an effective strategy for improving training efficiency by selecting representative subsets of training data. A major limitation of existing data pruning methods is the high computational overhead required to compute sample scores, which contradicts the original goal of reducing overall training cost. To address this issue, we propose an efficient data pruning framework called Early-Stage Prediction Stability, which evaluates sample scores based on prediction stability during the early stages of training. In addition, we introduce a Balanced Sampling Strategy to prevent performance degradation under high pruning ratios. On CIFAR-10, under a 90% pruning ratio, the proposed method reduces the sample scoring time by approximately 66% while achieving a +4.19% accuracy improvement over the previous state-of-the-art method. Extensive experiments on CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100 demonstrate that the proposed method consistently outperforms existing state-of-the-art pruning approaches across multiple pruning ratios. Furthermore, under 20% label noise on Tiny-ImageNet, the proposed method effectively removes noisy samples and reduces overall training time while achieving state-of-the-art accuracy. The source code is publicly available at https://github.com/Hu210/esps-data-pruning.

title: PolyMerge: Compressing 3D Gaussian Splats with Polytope Coverings for Provably Safe Resource-Constrained Navigation
authors: Jihoon Hong, Chih-Yuan Chiu, Sara Fridovich-Keil, Glen Chou
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Obstacle avoidance is essential for safe navigation and motion planning. Recent radiance field reconstruction methods enable object detection and modeling with high fidelity, but remain too memory- and compute-intensive for on-board perception-based path planning. To address these limitations, we propose PolyMerge to convert a large, photorealistic 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) model of a scene into a lightweight representation of convex polytopes whose union provably over-approximates all obstacles in the original 3DGS model. PolyMerge tunes the polytope count to trade off conservativeness and compute cost, and integrates with control barrier functions (CBFs) to plan collision-free paths. We showcase PolyMerge in simulation and hardware experiments on a Crazyflie drone, which uses PolyMerge to compute and follow safe trajectories in real time under severe onboard compute constraints, outperforming baselines in speed while guaranteeing safety. For our code and videos, visit <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://athlon76.github.io/PolyMerge-website/</uri>.

title: Task-Adaptive Admittance Control for Human-Quadrotor Cooperative Load Transportation with Dynamic Cable-Length Regulation
authors: Shuai Li, Ton T. H. Duong, Damiano Zanotto
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: The collaboration between humans and robots is critical in many robotic applications, especially in those requiring physical human-robot interaction (pHRI). Previous research in pHRI has largely focused on robotic manipulators, employing impedance or admittance control to maintain operational safety. Conversely, research in human-quadrotor cooperative load transportation (CLT) is still in its infancy. This letter introduces a novel admittance controller designed for safe and effective human-quadrotor CLT using a quadrotor equipped with an actively-controlled winch. The proposed method accounts for the system's coupled dynamics, allowing the quadrotor and its cable to dynamically adapt to contact forces during CLT tasks, thereby enhancing responsiveness. We experimentally validated the task-adaptive capability of the controller across the entire CLT process, including in-place loading/unloading and load transporting tasks. To this end, we compared the system performances against a conventional approach, using both variable and fixed cable lengths under low- and high-stiffness conditions. Results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms the conventional approach in terms of system responsiveness and motion smoothness, leading to improved CLT capabilities.

title: Enhancing Multi-Step Solar Irradiance Forecasting through TVF-EMD-AWNN: a Time-Varying Hybrid Framework for PV Power Plant_supp1-3688713.pdf
authors: Sumanta Pasari
publish: N/A
abstract: Accurate solar irradiance prediction is inevitable for estimating photovoltaic energy generation and meeting utility demands. This paper presents a novel TVF-EMD-AWNN hybrid framework that integrates time-varying filter empirical mode decomposition (TVF-EMD) with an adaptive wavelet neural network (AWNN) for enhanced prediction accuracy at an hourly timescale. The proposed framework encompasses a sophisticated three-fold practically relevant integration architecture. First, the adaptive filtering mechanism of TVF enables dynamic frequency time optimization while maintaining non-stationary signal characteristics through its time-varying filtering process. Then, the EMD decomposes these enhanced signals into intrinsic mode functions, exhibiting unique temporal-spectral signatures and facilitating comprehensive multi-resolution analysis. Finally, the adaptive wavelet parameters in AWNN synergistically represent these decomposed components through dynamic scale-translation optimization in the wavelet space. Additionally, the proposed TVF-EMD-AWNN model is also validated through the expanding window cross-validation approach. The theory is illustrated on a long hourly solar irradiance data (2015–2021) at four diverse climatic regions of India. Results demonstrate noteworthy accuracy improvement in terms of relative RMSE reduction in comparison to established standalone and hybrid models, such as MLP (83%), LSTM (85%), Bi-LSTM (82%), EDBi-LSTM (80%), AWNN (78%), CEEMDAN-LSTM (83%), CEEMDAN EDBi-LSTM (80%), TVF-EMD-LSTM (65%), and TVF-EMD BiLSTM (54%). Besides, the performance of TVF-EMD-AWNN remains consistent even at multi-step hourly predictions. Therefore, the proposed hybrid framework with additional benchmarking, ablation studies, and time series validation offers robustness towards practical power system operation.

title: FedFEC: Tailored Learning via Multi-Level Knowledge Standardization and Fusion to Align Knowledge in Mobile Edge Computing_supp1-3691382.pdf
authors: Zhenyu Wang
publish: N/A
abstract: As awareness of privacy protection for mobile devices increases, the application of federated learning in Mobile Edge Computing (MEC) has attracted widespread attention. Federated learning enables the cooperative training of a robust and capable global model across mobile devices without accessing private data. However, the communication overhead and model heterogeneity issues pose significant challenges for federated learning in MEC. Although federated distillation can serve as a viable approach to reduce communication overhead through knowledge distillation and facilitate knowledge transfer among heterogeneous models, the mismatch in knowledge amplitude due to differing parameter scales between local and global models can severely impact its effectiveness. To address these challenges, we propose a flexible knowledge standardization method to align the knowledge amplitude between teacher and student models. Additionally, we introduce a multi-level knowledge fusion federated distillation approach, FedFEC, designed to enhance the performance of federated distillation for MEC. FedFEC selects clients using the Wasserstein distance, aligns knowledge via a standardization function based on model size, and fuses multi-granularity knowledge for effective server-side distillation. Clients then update local personalized model based on multi-level global knowledge. Extensive experiments conducted on four datasets demonstrate that FedFEC outperforms all benchmarks across various scenarios of data heterogeneity and model architectures.

title: Spatial Equalization with Adjustable-Delay RIS
authors: Kento Kurashima, Tomonari Kurayama, Teruyuki Miyajima
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: This letter investigates the application of an adjustable-delay reconfigurable intelligent surface (AD-RIS) capable of controlling the delay and phase shift at each element to spatial equalization in wideband single-carrier transmission. We derive expressions for the signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) and show that AD-RIS achieves a higher SINR than fixed-delay (FD) RIS. A low-complexity AD-RIS design method is proposed, where the element delays are determined by focusing on only the significant paths of the composite channel, and the phase shifts are optimized by a coordinate-wise successive convex approximation-based method. Numerical simulations demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms conventional methods for AD-RIS and FD-RIS.

title: Scaled Output Consensus for Euler-Bernoulli Beam Networks under Dual Communication Graphs
authors: Bao-Zhu Guo, Qing-Qing Hu, Feng-Fei Jin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control of Network Systems
abstract: This paper studies scaled output consensus for a network of Euler-Bernoulli beam systems subject to external disturbances acting through all channels. The boundary feedback in slope and angular velocity is transmitted over two distinct communication graphs. A virtual disturbance aggregator and an active disturbance rejection control (ADRC)-based scheme are developed to estimate and reject the total disturbance entering the control channel, while a virtual leader reformulates the objective as leader-follower synchronization. Exponential stability of the closed-loop error system is proved mainly using the Huang-Prüss criterion, yielding scaled output consensus. Numerical experiments validate the proposed approach.

title: Strategies in Managing Online Learning Assignments
authors: Shiyrah Theosebes Sela, Christine Paulina, Djuria Suprato
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 9th International Conference on Education and E-Learning
abstract: None

title: Optuna-Tuned Hybrid A*–DWA Planner for 3D Quadcopter Navigation in Dynamic Environments
authors: Nada Braiek, Imen Saidi
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: Hybrid global-local planning architectures are commonly used for quadcopter navigation to balance global path efficiency with local reactivity in the presence of obstacles. However, the performance of such hybrid planners is strongly influenced by the selection of internal parameters, which are often tuned manually and in an ad hoc manner. This paper presents a hybrid 3D path planning framework that combines an energyaware A* global planner with a 3D Dynamic Window Approach (DWA) local controller for quadcopter navigation in environments containing static and dynamic obstacles. Energy considerations are incorporated through a configurable energy proxy that penalizes altitude changes and aggressive velocity profiles. To improve planner performance and robustness, Bayesian optimization using Optuna is employed offline to tune key planning parameters. The proposed approach is evaluated through a comparative study between a default hybrid planner and its Optuna-tuned counterpart across multiple randomized scenarios. Experimental results show that parameter tuning leads to higher success rates and improved trajectory efficiency, highlighting the practical benefits of systematic parameter optimization for hybrid planning architectures in aerial robotics.

title: On-chip data-path equalization using distributed and interleaved inverted feedback
authors: Timothy M Hollis, Chulkyu “Ck” Lee, Chris Holub
publish: 2026 IEEE Workshop on Microelectronics and Electron Devices (WMED)
abstract: Signal equalization is often relied upon to enable offchip communication over bandwidth-limited channels, but similar principles may also benefit on-chip signaling. This paper introduces a design methodology combining transimpedance amplifier stages with distributed and interleaved inverted feedback to significantly enhance circuit performance in speed, energy-efficiency, area and robustness. The approach is demonstrated through two examples: 1) optimizing inverter-chain tapering for driving heavy loads at high speed, and 2) applying the framework to a novel high-speed delayline architecture.

title: Design of a Dynamic Correlation-Aware Prefetching Mechanism for Back-End Parallelism
authors: Meng Luo, Xiaokun Zhu, Yang Zhang, Guojun Han
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Although solid state drive (SSD) caching can effectively mitigate the flash access bottleneck, further improving read performance under highly concurrent and fragmented workloads remains challenging. Most existing prefetching strategies rely on short-term statistics or instantaneous correlations for prediction. When workload phases shift or noise is present, they struggle to balance prediction accuracy against system overhead. In addition, traditional prefetching schemes rarely consider SSD back end parallelism explicitly, which limits read performance gains. To address these issues, we propose Back-end Parallelism-Aware Dynamic Correlation Prefetching (BPDCP). With a fixed request window, BPDCP learns correlation patterns and preserves a stable model across windows via two-level filtering and recency-aware decay. On this model, it issues request level predictions and progressively weakens outdated correlations. At the execution level, BPDCP incorporates back-end resource status and applies a parallelism aware scheduling strategy, allowing prefetch requests to utilize idle parallelism across channels, LUNs, and planes, reducing interference with foreground I/O. Simulation results on FEMU with real world workloads show that, compared with representative prefetching methods, BPDCP reduces total I/O latency and average read response time while keeping the overhead on the write path under control.

title: Modeling, Design, and Enhanced Control of Hybrid Energy Storage for Standalone PV Systems
authors: Heba Mohammed Saeed Haji, Mohammed Obaid Mustafa
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: This paper investigates the effects of integrating a hybrid energy storage system (HESS) consisting of batteries and supercapacitors (SCs) into an off-grid photovoltaic (PV) system. The hybrid approach exploits the high energy density of batteries and the fast dynamic response of SCs, which reduces solar supply variations. Hybridization also helps reduce battery stress and improve system reliability. A power management strategy was developed to ensure that a 1 kW load would be continuously supplied by the PV array. The method coordinates power flow among the array, storage units, and the load. The system is initially controlled by a Proportional-Integral (PI) controller, which is later replaced by an Artificial Neural Network (ANN)-based controller that improves adaptability and dynamic performance across different operating conditions. Simulation results show that the HESS-based off-grid PV system proposed here effectively copes with changes in solar irradiance and load. The ANN controller provides better dynamic response and energy management capabilities than does the conventional <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{P I}$</tex> controller, leading to improved stability, efficiency, and suitability for various operating environments in standalone renewable energy systems.

title: Sleep-Wake Estimation using Frequency Analysis of Electroencephalogram Signals
authors: Effat Mahajabin Ahona, Afifa Halim Prapti, Maysha Rahman Trisha, Md. Bashir Uddin
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Diagnosis of sleep diseases like sleep apnea and insomnia requires a precise estimation of wake and sleep states. Using a single-channel electroencephalogram (EEG) signal, this work proposes an automated algorithm for sleep-wake segment estimation and determination of sleep parameters. 200 polysomnography (PSG) records along with the related demographics and manual scorings (annotations) from the sleep heart health study (SHHS) database were collected at random for this study. EEG signals were extracted from the PSG records and separated into development and validation. After preprocessing, EEG signal was divided into 30 -second epochs. The continuous wavelet transform (CWT) was used to estimate the frequency components from each epoch of EEG signal. To separate sleep segments from wake segments, a rule-based thresholding technique was used. The proposed technique produced the output in form of total sleep time (TST), total wake time (TWT), and sleep efficiency (SE). The total amount of time spent in each sleep stage is known as TST, whereas the sum of all wake episodes recorded during the total recording time (TRT) is defined as TWT. SE is defined as the percentage of bedtime (total hours in bed) that is spent asleep. From the epoch-based analysis, the algorithm obtained 87.79% accuracy, 91.67% sensitivity, 75.14% specificity, and an $F 1$ score of 0.91. Strong agreement between the algorithm and annotation was achieved by Bland-Altman plots. The mean bias for TST, TWT, and SE was resulted in -0.04 hours, -0.05 hours, and -0.32 %, respectively. The results indicate that the proposed automated technique has the potential to be incorporated into wearable and home-based sleep monitoring systems.

title: AIML-Enabled Body-Bias Management to Minimise Leakage and PDP in 65 nm Domino Logic
authors: C. Somasundar Reddy, Kathi Venkataramana, Seeniappan Kaliappan, Ramya M
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Domino logic is still popular in data paths that need latency, but at the 65nm node the increasing subthreshold leakage and narrowing energy-delay ratios makes it difficult to maintain performance and energy efficiency. This paper offered an AIML-enabled per-block body-bias management system which predicted and regulated both the uncertainty-aware surrogates and constrained optimisation predictions. The proposed system controls the static leakage, propagation delay and switching energy to permit safe, fine grained VB V tuning with PVT and aging. Models like XGBoost ensembles and Gaussian processes were trained on a reproducible PTM 65-nm SPICE dataset generated with Monte-Carlo PVT sweeps and aging perturbations. Constrained Bayesian optimisation and a reinforcement-learning variant were employed to propose bias settings that are subjected to probabilistic timing and SNM constraints. In SPICE-validated experiments the AIML controller achieved substantial gains over static baselines at an average leakage reduction of 42% and PDP improvement of 18% while keeping timing-violation probability within designer thresholds. This analysis additionally quantified sensor/ADC resolution, controller area/power overhead and interactions with DVFS and power-gating. Overall, the proposed framework offered a practical, low-overhead path to adaptive, reliability-aware power management for 65-nm domino logic.

title: Artificial Intelligence–Assisted Budget Forecasting and Operations Management for Strategic Decision-Making
authors: Halaswamy D, Lakshmi Kantha, Giridhara Naidu B, Rakesh N, K.Binith Muthukrishnan, Revanayya Kantayya
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Artificial intelligence has become a revolution in terms of improving financial and operational approaches within the corporate ecosystems. Financial forecasts and operations oversight that use state-of-the-art machines training and statistical analysis allow businesses to plan, minimize waste, and invest money where it will do the best. Better and more current knowledge on changes to budgets and operational risks might be made available to executives with the use of current data pipelines, machine learning, and adapting techniques. In addition to improving the cost framework and efficiency assessment, this approach may also make businesses less expensive and adaptable to changes in competition. Fiscal decisions that are in line with the company's strategic objectives will be consistent and transparent thanks to the combining of artificially intelligent forecasting apps with enterprise resource planning programs. Possible anomaly identification, operational streamlining, and data-based capital prioritization all add to the evident increase of correctness and robustness. Applying AI to spending planning and operational oversight procedures lays the groundwork for intentional decline, which in turn enables pre-emptive and long-range decision-making, delivers robust profitability, and offers sustained development in the dynamic corporate environment. With a Technical Accessibility Factor of 96.4%, the suggested solution outperformed all others.

title: Predictive Modeling of Malaria and Dengue Outbreaks Using Multi-Modal Machine Learning and Climate-Health Data Integration
authors: H Hima Bindu, Heeral Agarwal, Jaladhi T, Abdul Haq Nalband, Sugandha Saxena
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Malaria and dengue fever are still the main causes of illness in the tropics and subtropics, areas severely affected by these diseases, and their transmission has been greatly affected by climatic changes and the environment. Most traditional disease monitoring systems have delayed reports and are also limited in predicting situations. This article puts forward a holistic multimodal machine learning strategy that combines heterogeneous data sources such as past disease incidence, meteorological parameters, remote sensing data, and sociodemographic information for precise prediction of malaria and dengue outbreaks. Our method features a hybrid design that merges temporal models (LSTM, GRU) for sequential pattern recognition, ensemble methods (XGBoost, Random Forest) to deal with non-linear relationships, and attention mechanisms for feature importance weighting. The system is capable of processing climate variables (temperature, precipitation, humidity), vegetation indices (NDVI, LAI), and historical case data to predict outbreaks weekly at the district-level granularity. The exhaustive experiments carried out on real-world datasets from various Indian states testify to the framework’s power. It achieves 94.2% prediction accuracy and an F1-score of 0.927 for outbreak classification. The enacted system gives the public health department a tool in their hands to intervene before the situation gets out of control and also to allocate resources in the most efficient way, thus, reducing the disease burden through early warning capabilities.

title: Eye On Violence: Real-Time Violence Detection in Surveillance Video Using a CNN–LSTM Spatiotemporal Framework
authors: Mayuri Barapatre, Akshay Padia, Mayank Mokhere, Vaishvik Jaiswal, Pravin Sonsare
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Growing public safety concerns and the expansion of CCTV infrastructures have made violence detection in video surveillance streams an important area of study and research. When violent situations occur, manual monitoring of such systems is ineffective, error-prone, and often fails to deliver timely alerts. To address this challenge, this study proposes a hybrid Convolutional Neural Network–Long Short-Term Memory (CNN–LSTM) model capable of distinguishing violent from non-violent events by jointly capturing spatial cues from individual frames and temporal motion patterns across sequences. The pipeline includes frame extraction, fixed-length sequence generation, spatial feature encoding using a CNN backbone, and temporal modeling using LSTMs. The model is evaluated on the RWF-2000 dataset, a real-world collection of surveillance videos containing both violent and non-violent scenes. Experimental results demonstrate strong performance, achieving an overall accuracy of 68%, indicating the model’s suitability for near real-time, safety-critical surveillance applications. This study discusses dataset preparation, model design, training methodology, and evaluation, offering a practical framework for automated violence detection.

title: Ethical AI Audits: Preventing Algorithmic Discrimination in Substance Abuse Referrals
authors: Shalmali Joshi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The choices people make regarding health can be dictated by certain programs rather than by people. It can make some people not have good care. The EAIA framework proposed in this essay assesses the bias of clinical referral predictive machine learning models and also helps mitigate it. The framework uses 120,000 synthetic patient profiles and a public anonymized dataset of addiction-treatment intakes to compare differences across protected attributes of race, sex, income, age. Four different models were built, including a Logistic Regression, Random Forest and Gradient Boosting, and a Deep Neural Network to evaluate baseline gaps and fairness interventions. The findings indicate that the EAIA framework effectively diminishes discrepancies in false negative rates and statistical parity. It is able to achieve these results without sacrificing or with marginal sacrifices on the performance metrics. By visually inspecting the error distribution plots, ROC curves and calibration plots, it appears that our predictions are more stable as well as interpretable and more clinically trustworthy. The results indicate that audits based on monitoring and mediation can support the development of ethical, high-performance, responsible AI. The larger framework may enable a more generalized approach to the incorporation of fairness and accountability into healthcare ML pipeline.

title: Two-Stage Hierarchical Near-Field Beam Training for Extremely Large-Scale RIS
authors: Dingcheng Yang, Zhaoqi Zhang, Yu Xu, Fahui Wu, Changsheng You
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: The extremely large-scale reconfigurable intelligent surface (XL-RIS) is a highly promising technology for the sixth-generation (6G) wireless communications. However, the conventional near-field beam training (NFBT) for XL-RIS is intractable and incurs excessively high beam training overhead. To solve this problem, we propose a two-stage hierarchical NFBT scheme for XL-RIS. In the first stage, we generate a hierarchical codebook based on the proposed 3D binary-tree search strategy, which can iteratively and evenly divide the beam search region into sub-regions, wherein the sampling point pair formed by the center points of the BS sub-region and the user sub-region corresponds to a codeword in the codebook. Then, we perform NFBT on this hierarchical codebook to achieve coarse beam alignment in the downlink. In the second stage, we perform NFBT based on the globally optimal codeword of the first stage to achieve fine-grained beam alignment. Numerical results show that the proposed two-stage hierarchical NFBT scheme can significantly reduce the beam training overhead while achieving high rate.

title: A Stationary (And Therefore Compatible) Representation is All You Need
authors: Niccolò Biondi, Federico Pernici, Simone Ricci, Alberto Del Bimbo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Learning compatible representations aims to learn feature representations that can be used interchangeably over time whenever a model undergoes updates. In this paper, we demonstrate that stationary representations learned by <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$d$</tex-math></inline-formula>-Simplex fixed classifiers imply compatibility as in its formal definition. This result establishes a foundation for future works and can be directly exploited in practical learning scenarios. We address the challenge of learning compatibility using <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$d$</tex-math></inline-formula>-Simplex fixed classifiers when the model is sequentially fine-tuned. Learning according to a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$d$</tex-math></inline-formula>-Simplex fixed classifier with the cross-entropy loss aligns feature distributions at the first-order statistics. Consequently, it may not fully capture higher-order dependencies in the representation between model updates. To address this issue, we demonstrate that training the model using a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$d$</tex-math></inline-formula>-Simplex fixed classifier through a convex combination of the cross-entropy loss and a contrastive loss not only captures higher-order dependencies, but is also equivalent to learning with the cross-entropy under the compatibility constraints. We confirm our findings with extensive experiments also considering a new scenario where a pre-trained model is sequentially fine-tuned and occasionally replaced with an improved model. We show that stationary representations enable uninterrupted retrieval services (without reprocessing gallery images) while improving performance during model updates and replacements, achieving state-of-the-art.

title: Integrated Motor Drives: A Review of Recent Developments, Enabling Technologies and Future Research Directions
authors: Xu Deng, Barrie Mecrow, Thomas M. Jahns, Lee Empringham, Liliana De Lillo, Nick Simpson, Andrew Hopkins
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: The integration of electric machines, power electronics and controls into a single unit with common cooling and packaging is termed an Integrated Motor Drive (IMD). Integration with the load is also becoming increasingly common in many applications so, for example, the fan blades in a fan drive become integrated with the electric drive. Research into integrated drives and enabling technologies is both extensive and growing. Six years have passed since the last major review paper on the topic was published, and many significant new products have been introduced during this time in a wide variety of fields incorporating IMD-based configurations. Simultaneously, there have been major advances in materials, semiconductor technology, manufacturing methods, and cooling technologies, which have opened up many new opportunities for future IMD improvements. This paper reviews these advances and discusses the impact they will have upon new IMD systems. It also looks forward and presents a longer-term vision of how IMDs will further evolve during coming decades.

title: ARGUS-Net: An Uncertainty-Aware Multi-Agent Framework for Multimodal Deepfake Detection with Probabilistic Evidence Fusion
authors: Yasser Hmimou, Mohamed Tabaa, Azeddine Khiat, Zineb Hidila
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The rapid proliferation of generative models has made deepfake media increasingly realistic and accessible, posing critical threats to digital forensics and multimedia trust. Existing detection methods predominantly rely on single-modality analysis and deterministic predictions, yielding overconfident outputs and limited robustness under ambiguous and cross-dataset conditions. We introduce ARGUS-Net, an uncertainty-aware multi-agent framework for multimodal deepfake detection based on probabilistic evidence fusion. Four independent forensic agents analyzing physiological signals (rPPG), audio-visual synchronization, spectral artifacts, and CNN-based facial manipulation produce modality-specific suspicion and confidence scores. A probabilistic reasoning module fuses this multimodal evidence to estimate deepfake probability and quantify decision uncertainty via Shannon entropy. When uncertainty is high, the ARGUS-AC mechanism triggers controlled perturbations (illumination, motion, and occlusion) to re-evaluate ambiguous cases, reducing mean predictive entropy from 0.985 to 0.935. Decision-making is strictly separated from a post-hoc LLM-based forensic explanation module, ensuring interpretability without affecting the integrity of the detection process. Evaluated on Celeb-DF v2 (AUC = 0.793) and FaceForensics++ (AUC = 0.633), and further assessed on WildDeepfake under a restricted-modality external protocol, ARGUS-Net demonstrates that probabilistic multimodal reasoning combined with active uncertainty resolution improves the reliability of deepfake detection systems while remaining strongly sensitive to domain shift.

title: Environment-Aware IRS Deployment Via Channel Knowledge Map: Joint Sensing-Communications Coverage Optimization
authors: Yilong Chen, Zixiang Ren, Jie Xu, Rui Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: This paper studies the intelligent reflecting surface (IRS) deployment optimization problem for IRS-enabled integrated sensing and communications (ISAC) systems, in which multiple IRSs are strategically deployed at candidate locations to assist a base station (BS) to enhance the coverage of both sensing and communications. We present an environment-aware IRS deployment design via exploiting the channel knowledge map (CKM), which provides a priori channel state information (CSI) between each candidate IRS location and BS or targeted sensing/communication points. Based on CKM, we optimize the deployment of IRSs, jointly with the BS's transmit beamforming and IRSs' reflective beamforming during operation, with the objective of minimizing the system cost, while guaranteeing the minimum signal-to-clutter-plus-noise ratio (SCNR) requirements at sensing areas and the minimum signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) requirements at communication areas. In particular, we consider two cases when the IRSs' reflective beamforming optimization can be implemented dynamically in real time and quasi-stationarily over the whole operation period, respectively. For both cases, the joint IRS deployment and transmit/reflective beamforming designs are formulated as mixed-integer non-convex optimization problems, which are solved via the successive convex approximation (SCA)-based relax-and-bound method. Specifically, we first relax the binary IRS deployment indicators into continuous variables, then find converged solutions via SCA, and finally round relaxed indicators back to binary values. Numerical results demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed algorithms in reducing the system cost while meeting the sensing and communication requirements.

title: Push Smarter, Not Harder: Hierarchical RL-Diffusion Policy for Efficient Nonprehensile Manipulation
authors: Steven Caro, Stephen L. Smith
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Nonprehensile manipulation, such as pushing objects across cluttered environments, presents a challenging control problem due to complex contact dynamics and long-horizon planning requirements. In this work, we propose HeRD, a hierarchical reinforcement learning-diffusion policy that decomposes pushing tasks into two levels: high-level goal selection and low-level trajectory generation. We employ a high-level reinforcement learning (RL) agent to select intermediate spatial goals, and a low-level goal-conditioned diffusion model to generate feasible, efficient trajectories to reach them. This architecture combines the long-term reward maximizing behaviour of RL with the generative capabilities of diffusion models. We evaluate our method in a 2D simulation environment and show that it outperforms the state-of-the-art baseline in success rate, path efficiency, and generalization across multiple environment configurations. Our results suggest that hierarchical control with generative low-level planning is a promising direction for scalable, goal-directed nonprehensile manipulation. Code, documentation, and trained models are available: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/carosteven/HeRD</uri>.

title: A Magnetic-Sensitive Sensor Network for Monitoring Concurrent Mode of Multi-Layer Fatigue Cracks
authors: Chengjie Ping, Tao Chen, Yuting He, Xianghong Fan, Tao An
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Cracks may occur simultaneously in the outer and inner layers of the multi-layer metal structure of an aircraft. Fatigue cracks that are not detected in time may lead to disastrous consequences and the importance of monitoring structural cracks in aging aircraft is increasing day by day. The complexity of the multi-layer structure and the fatigue environment make real-time crack monitoring difficult. This paper has established a multi-layer cracks concurrent modal magnetic sensitive sensing network using flexible eddy current array technology and TMR magnetic sensitive chips, initially forming a distributed sensor monitoring system. It has been discovered that the impedance characteristic signals of the magnetic-sensitive sensing network have a strong correlation with the crack propagation process. Thus, it can conduct real-time comprehensive monitoring of surface and inner layer cracks at multiple key parts of multi-layer metal load-bearing structures of aircraft under fatigue conditions. It can determine the location and length of fatigue cracks, and the preliminary positioning and quantitative measurement of fatigue cracks can be achieved. Surface cracks can be quantitatively monitored with a resolution of 1 mm, and inner layer cracks at a depth of 2 mm can be preliminarily quantitatively monitored, with inner layer cracks of 5.78 mm and 13.83 mm being detected. It can provide effective assistance for the online monitoring of fatigue cracks in multi-layer metal structures of aerospace equipment.

title: Ranging Technology for UAV Nodes Based on Integration of Communication and Navigation
authors: Zhengdong Wang, Haoyu Nie, Wei Cao, Jiamin Qi, Yihui Wang, Hangcheng Han
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: UAV swarms demand reliable inter-node ranging, yet GNSS-based methods are interference-prone, and independent communication-navigation systems suffer from spectrum conflicts and inflexibility. This paper proposes a communication-navigation integrated ranging technique that enables relative distance measurement by reusing communication link data, decoupling high-speed data transmission from precision ranging. A TOA estimation method combining pseudo-code regeneration and sinc operator is introduced: the sinc operator models the band-limited distorted correlation function, and Gauss-Newton iteration performs high-precision peak localization. Simulations show the ranging accuracy asymptotically approaches the CRLB, remains independent of specific modulation/coding, and offers strong versatility for inter-UAV ranging.

title: Estimation of Lower Limb Trajectory From Foot Measurements
authors: Vijay Bhaskar Semwal, Ruchika Sharma, Neha Gaud, Yadunath Pathak, Abhishek Sharma, Pranay Yadav
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: In this research we develop a deep neural network model to forecast lower limb movements based on foot measurements. The model uses autoencoder and decoders to learn the relationship between foot morphology and lower limb kinematics, which includes joint angles and segment orientations. The model takes in a set of foot measurements as input, including foot length, width, arch height, and other parameters. These measurements are preprocessed , extract features from the input data and learn to model the relationship between foot morphology and lower limb kinematics. The output of the model is a set of predicted joint angles (hip, knee and ankle) and segment orientations for the lower limb, which can be used to analyze gait patterns, diagnose musculoskeletal disorders, and inform treatment and rehabilitation plans. During the experiment, the Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) consistently falls within a certain range of (1.48<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">◦</sup>, 1.38<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">◦</sup>). The development of the model represents an important step towards improving our understanding of the complex relationship between foot morphology and lower limb kinematics, and could have significant implications for the diagnosis and treatment of lower limb injuries and disorders.

title: A Dynamic Multi-Objective Cost Function for Personalized and Accessible Urban Pathfinding
authors: Aayam Bajaj, Atharva Dhokte, Yuvrajsing Bahure, Hitendra Chavan
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Standard urban navigation systems often fail to meet the needs of individuals with mobility impairments. These systems focus on optimizing the shortest path, ignoring critical accessibility factors like surface quality, dynamic real-time obstacles, and sidewalk slopes, thereby creating an informational gap for users. In order to address these shortcomings, this paper introduces a pathfinding framework, by enhancing the A* search algorithm using a dynamic, multi-objective cost function. With calculation of the traversal cost for every path segment, our methodology provides personalized and context-aware routing. The derivation of this cost happens through the integration of multiple layers of data, including: (1) Personalized user profiles, (2) Community-sourced data on obstructions and temporary obstacles, (3) Machine learning model for predictive risk assessment, and (4) Live weather updates. This results in generation of routes that consider user safety and comfort instead of just distance. Hence this system offers a reliable and accessible navigation experience.

title: Adaptive IoT Framework: A Modular Approach for Smart Device Integration
authors: Aravind Narayanan
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: This paper presents a modular, open-source IoT framework designed to address interoperability, scalability, and security challenges in heterogeneous smart device environments. The framework comprises three core components: a Rust-based device library for performance and reliability, a microservices backend for scalable device management, and a web-based dashboard for real-time monitoring. Emphasis is placed on modularity to enable seamless integration of new devices without system disruption. Security is implemented through layered mechanisms to ensure data protection and regulatory compliance. Experimental evaluation demonstrates 18% reduction in communication latency, support for 50,000 concurrent device connections with less than 5% performance degradation, and 27% lower response time compared to monolithic architectures. Case studies in insurance applications show improved risk assessment accuracy and fraud detection capabilities. The framework provides a foundation for adaptive IoT deployments across various domains.

title: Hybrid Reputation Routing for Decentralized Networks: A Blockchain-Enabled Secure and Scalable Protocol with On-Chain/Off-Chain Partitioning
authors: Ramkinker Singh, Om Narayan, Praveen Baskar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Our suggestion is a hybrid reputation-based routing protocol in the context of decentralized routing. networks which partitions trust measurements to on-chain immutable logs and off-chain dynamic computations to attain security and scalability. The protocol incorporates a layer of sharded blockchain to store critical. trust data and local reputation model to do real-time updates, enabling routing choices that cannot be tampered with. performance. The trust ledger which operated on-chain kept the records of the impartiality of. Historical performance and node identities, whereas the off-chain reputation engine uses graph neural network to calculate dynamic. real-time scores like packet delivery rate to be trusted. and latency. Additionally, it uses rollup-based batches of synchronization off-chain. optimistic updates to zero-knowledge proofs, making on-chain efficient. checking with minimum overhead. The suggested approach replaces conventional network discovery and forwarding modules using trust-based adjacency lists and trust-based pathing, and thus enhancing routing consistency over adversarial settings. Moreover, the structure integrates Hyperledger Fabric with. Graph Attention Network-based high-throughput sharded ledger operations. to update reputation in a privacy-preserving manner, proving to be linear. network size scalability. The experimental findings indicate that the system supports 10,000 transactions per shard and produces. Under 100 ms per ZK-Rollup proof, which is appropriate to large-scale IoT. and DeFi deployments. This publication fills the gap between pure on-chain. and off-chain reputation systems, which provides a viable solution to scalable and secure decentralized routing.
