We extract Mel-Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCCs), chroma, spectral contrast, and energy-based descriptors from preprocessed speech signals, aggregated into fixed-length vectors for classical classifiers such as Support Vector Machines (SVM) and Multilayer Perceptrons (MLP). For deep learning, sequential and spatial representations are captured using Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks and 2D Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) trained on log-mel spectrograms. Experiments on the Ryerson Audio-Visual Database of Emotional Speech and Song (RAVDESS) and additional benchmark corpora show that the spectrogram-based 2D CNN achieves the highest accuracy of 82.3% on a seven-class task where calm and neutral emotions are merged. The results demonstrate the importance of robust feature representation and lightweight neural architectures for reliable SER, and we provide reproducible protocols to facilitate future research.

title: A Unified Multi-Task 2D Perception Pipeline for Enhancing Single-Image 3D Modeling on Pix3D
authors: Mahammadismail Y Quadri, Shubham Karangale, Sudarshan Honnappanavar, Kushal Itnal, Niranjan Muchandi, Salma Shahpur
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Single-image 3D modeling critically depends on the quality of 2D cues extracted from the input image. However, many existing reconstruction pipelines implicitly assume clean object silhouettes and fully visible instances, assumptions that rarely hold in real-world scenarios. To address this limitation, we propose a multi-task 2D perception suite designed to operate as a dedicated preprocessing layer for single-image 3D reconstruction systems. The proposed framework consists of four independently trained deep networks: a ResNet50-based classifier for predicting nine object categories from the Pix3D dataset, a DeepLabV3 model with a ResNet101 backbone for binary silhouette segmentation, and two additional ResNet50 networks for occlusion and truncation detectionAll models are trained using a unified strategy that incorporates strong regularization techniques and curriculum-style staged layer unfreezing to improve generalization. The independently optimized networks are integrated into a single inference pipeline that produces structured outputs including object class labels, pixel-accurate segmentation masks, occlusion and truncation probabilities, geometric border-touch indicators, and visualization overlays. The final output is a compact CSV/JSON representation that can be directly consumed by Pix3D-based 3D reconstruction methods, providing a reliable and interpretable 2D perception foundation for downstream 3D modeling tasks.

title: Hybridisation of Quantum computing Methods in logistics optimization through Machine learning
authors: Ritu Aggarwal, Eshaan Aggarwal
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The intersection of quantum computing with machine learning transforms the various effective technologies. It works with the integration of quantum computing into logistics through supply chain management to solve and use optimization problems. These technologies have the capability of data pre-processing and analysing logistics through various machine learning models. This chapter explores the various quantum computing principles that are used to solve complex problems. This chapter discussed quantum annealing, quantum applications, and quantum optimisation algorithms by key factor algorithms based on the different ML approaches used. It is based on fleet maintenance, loading cargo, and prediction with scheduling algorithms. Quantum computing, when hybridized with ML and logistic optimisation, could solve the problems related to classical computing problems in this chapter. In this proposed work, the hybrid approach with ML and quantum computing with enhanced industry-specific solutions in different aspects of quantum computing in sectors such as cybersecurity. In logistics, the optimization concepts help to define the problems through a hybridization method with their applications.

title: FocusImb-YOLO11: Small Object Focusing and Class Imbalance Adaptive Learning Network for Sonar Imagery
authors: Fangxu Guo, Peicheng Sun, Weishuo Wang, Gang Hao
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Sonar imagery-based underwater target detection plays a critical role in marine resource exploration, underwater security, and seabed mapping. However, traditional object detection models face significant challenges in this domain due to the dominance of small targets, severe class imbalance, and low signal-to-noise ratios, which substantially degrade detection performance. To address these issues, we propose FocusImb-YOLO11, a small-object focusing and class-imbalance adaptive learning network built on YOLO11n. FocusImb-YOLO11 incorporates a Small Object Focus Module (SOFM) to strengthen small-target feature representation via adaptive multi-scale feature fusion and anchor refinement, and a Class Imbalance Adaptive Learning Module (CIALM) to dynamically adjust loss weights based on category sample distribution, thereby reducing the bias toward majority classes. Experiments on the SeabedObjects-KLSG dataset demonstrate that FocusImb-YOLO11 achieves 0.532 mAP50 and 0.333 mAP50-95, with notable gains on minority classes such as fish and other. Compared with the baseline YOLO11n, the proposed method improves the mAP50 of small and imbalanced classes by an average of 12.7%, indicating its effectiveness for underwater sonar target detection.

title: Adaptive Spectrum Sensing in Cognitive Radio Networks Using Reinforcement Learning
authors: Sachin B, Safia Banu M, S Asha
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Cognitive Radio Networks (CRNs) are potential solutions to the problem of spectrum scarcity through opportunistic access to unutilized frequency bands. But the traditional methods of spectrum sensing like energy detection, matched filtering, and cyclostationary detection usually exhibit constraints in low signal-noise ratio (SNR), noise uncertainty, and multipath fading conditions. In this paper, a proposal is made of an adaptive spectrum sensing framework, which utilizes the reinforcement learning (RL) and deep reinforcement learning (DRL) models to make an intelligent spectrum decision. This framework combines the spectral feature-extraction techniques of convolutional neural networks (CNNs), the channel-optimization techniques of Deep Q-Networks (DQN), and the multi-agent RL approach that utilizes cooperative sensing between distributed cognitive radios. Additionally, there is also a transfer learning mechanism to allow fast adaptation in the heterogeneous environment. Evaluations through simulations-based datasets and benchmark of the proposed approach have shown that it is capable of significantly enhancing the detection probability, the efficiency of spectrum utilization, and the resilience in the presence of uncertainty and outperforms classical baselines. The findings make the presented framework a scalable and effective solution to large-scale CRNs, which provides a better reliability and efficiency in the ever-changing wireless communication conditions.

title: Slice-Specific Intrusion Detection Decision Support System in 5G Networks with Interpretable Ensemble Learning
authors: Mst. Fatematuj Johora, Md. Abid Hasan Rafi, Mohima Binte Rasel, Pankaj Bhowmik, Md. Dulal Haque
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: The rapid adoption of 5 G network slicing introduces unique security challenges that demand specialized intrusion detection solutions for different service classes. This work presents a novel slice-aware intrusion detection system (IDS) that addresses the distinct requirements of enhanced Mobile Broadband (eMBB), Ultra-Reliable Low-Latency Communications (URLLC), and massive Machine-Type Communications (mMTC) through optimized machine learning ensembles. Our approach combines XGBoost, Random Forest, SVM, and MLP classifiers with Isolation Forest anomaly detection, enhanced by differential evolution for weight optimization and tailored feature engineering pipelines. The system achieves exceptional detection accuracy (99.28 % for eMBB, 99.46 % for mMTC, and 99.86% for URLLC) while maintaining interpretability through SHAP analysis and operational practicality through productionready implementations. Comprehensive evaluation across 12 security metrics demonstrates the framework’s effectiveness in handling high-throughput eMBB traffic, low-latency URLLC scenarios, and complex mMTC IoT environments, and cloud infrastructures. The solution advances 5 G security by providing an adaptable, explainable detection framework that respects slicespecific characteristics and operational constraints.

title: Comparative Study of Impedance Source Converters for Wide Input Range Power Applications
authors: Amit Kumar Singh, M.Venkatesh Naik, Rahul Kumar
publish: 2026 4th Odisha International Conference on Electrical Power Engineering, Communication and Computing Technology (ODICON)
abstract: This paper presents a comparative study of impedance-source DC-DC converter topologies for wide input voltage applications. Conventional Z-source (ZS), quasi-Z-source (ꟼZS), and beta-quasi-Z-source (β-ꟼZS) converters are evaluated in terms of voltage gain, duty-cycle range, efficiency, and component stress under boost operation. The limitations of ZS and ꟼZS converters at high gain are identified, including restricted duty-cycle operation and increased device stress. The β-IZS topology is shown to overcome these issues by enabling unrestricted duty-cycle operation, higher voltage gain, and reduced switch and capacitor stress. Steady-state analysis and MATLAB/Simulink simulations are used to validate the comparative performance. Results demonstrate that the β-IZS converter provides improved efficiency and reliability over a wide input voltage range, making it suitable for renewable energy and electric vehicle applications.

title: Dynamics and Trends of Quality of Life Among the Elderly in Northeast Thailand: Multi-Year Repeated Cross-Sectional Study (2019-2025)
authors: Uraiwan Runghairun, Karittha Onkeaw, Yuwadee Wongweeraprasert
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Cybernetics and Innovations (ICCI)
abstract: This research aims to analyze the dynamics and trends of Quality of Life (QoL) among the elderly in Northeast Thailand during 2019, 2021, and 2025. The sample comprised 957 participants aged 60 and older. The WHOQOL-BREF assessment was employed as the primary research instrument, and data were analyzed using descriptive statistics, multiple regression analysis, and cluster analysis. The findings revealed an inverted U-shaped pattern in overall QoL scores, peaking in 2021 (Mean <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=93.21$</tex>) before experiencing a slight decline in 2025 (Mean <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=91.60$</tex>). Dimensional analysis indicated continuous improvements in the environmental and social relationship domains, whereas the physical health domain exhibited the highest volatility. Predictor analysis confirmed that “underlying medical conditions” was the most significant negative determinant of QoL (Beta <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=-5.98, \mathrm{p}<.001$</tex>). Furthermore, cluster dynamics highlighted a “Hollow Middle” phenomenon in 2025, characterized by a shrinking moderate-QoL group and a simultaneous increase in both high- and low-QoL groups. This suggests an intensifying polarization of quality of life among the elderly population.

title: Control and Detection of Epileptic Seizure Using Machine Learning Techniques: K-Means Clustering Application
authors: Houda Ghidhaoui, Wahid Souhail, Hedi Khammari
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: In this study, a new method for real-time prediction of epileptic seizures is proposed that uses single-channel EEG recordings from FP1-F7 electrode using spectral analysis in sliding windows to detect preictal changes in the EEG as an early warning signs of seizures, and uses empirical mode decomposition (IMF1 and IMF3) to capture fine frequency and amplitude variations. Relevant features, such as amplitude fluctuations, frequency shifts, and spike counts are extracted, and a Support Vector Machine (SVM) classifier is employed to differentiate seizure-related and non-seizure states, which shows the efficacy of combining sophisticated signal processing with machine learning for seizure forecasting.

title: Predictive Control of a Hybrid PEM Electrolyzer-Fuel Cell System for Frequency and Voltage Regulation in High PV Penetration Grids
authors: El Manaa Barhoumi, Sulaiman Z. Almutairi, Kamel Sayahi, Abdel Aziz Raslan, Slah Farhani, Faouzi Bacha
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: The widespread development of grid-connected electrolyzers for green hydrogen production has brought new opportunities and challenges to modern power systems. Electrolyzers facilitate flexible usage of excess renewable power, however their dynamic interaction with the power grid can have significant impacts on voltage stability, frequency support and power quality. In this paper, the role of PEM electrolyzers and FCs in improving voltage and frequency stability in PV integrated energy systems is analyzed. The surplus electricity of PV system is fed to a 50 MW PEM electrolyzer to produce green hydrogen, which can be recirculated to fuel cells as an energy vector during periods with low solar radiation and high load demand. An energy management system formulation is adopted to ensure system-level coordination. The optimization is performed every 5 minutes using a prediction horizon of 1 hour. Optimization variables are the Electrolyzer and fuel cell power trajectories. The objective is the minimization of the overall cost of grid import, frequency error, hydrogen deficit and actuation, subject to operating limits and rate bounds. The simulations show that in a system combining 100 MWp of PV with the 50 MW Electrolyser, grid stability is enhanced, and the effects of PV intermittency are dampened. The results demonstrate the capability of predictive control-based coordination for increasing reliability and enabling hydrogen-based flexibility in future smart and sustainable energy systems.

title: Speaker Identification from Bone-Conducted Speech via Dual Attention-Transformer Model: A Study with EmoBone Dataset
authors: Md. Ibne ShihabShad, Sayeeda Khan, Md. Sarwar Hosain, Akif Mahdi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Speaker identification using bone conducted (BC) speech is challenging due to limited data availability. A dual attention based CNN Transformer hybrid model is proposed and evaluated on the EmoBone dataset, which comprises 14,354 samples from 28 speakers across different regions. Experiments were conducted in python within a GPU-accelerated setup. The feature extraction process fused local acoustic descriptors, such as MFCCs and mel spectrograms, with global prosodic cues to characterize speaker identity under varying emotional conditions. The model architecture combines convolutional layers for capturing fine grained temporal frequency patterns with transformer layers employing attention to model long range contextual dependencies. The model was trained using the adam optimizer, incorporating early stopping and learning rate scheduling to promote stable convergence, without employing class weights. It attained a final training accuracy of 99% with a loss of 0.8272, and a validation accuracy of 99.36% with a loss of 0.8026. The model exhibited excellent generalization and robustness, as reflected by classwise precision, recall, and f1-scores mostly above 0.98, an IoU of 0.9717, balanced accuracy of 0.9985, and strong AUC metrics. These results highlight the potential of BC speech for speaker identification and provide a reliable foundation for future studies and practical implementations.

title: Enhancing Zero Trust Network Security with AI-Powered Analytics and Blockchain Verification
authors: Venkateswaran Petchiappan, Vinitha Vadivel
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Despite protections, complex assaults threaten corporate networks, data, and infrastructure. The article employs ZTA for business network security. Using the “never trust, always verify” principle, Zero Trust secures all network traffic. AI and blockchain enable dynamic, tamper-proof Zero Trust. AI-based real-time threat, anomaly, and adaptive authentication enable networks to respond more quickly and effectively. Identity management, audit trails, and network transparency are secure thanks to blockchain’s decentralisation and immutability. This white paper analyses how AI and blockchain enhance Zero Trust’s microsegmentation, identity verification, and least-privilege access. Traditional security designs and Zero Trust are compared to prevent data breaches, insider attacks, and APTs. Case studies on adopting AI and blockchain with Zero Trust illustrate how organisations across sectors employ them, highlighting their benefits and drawbacks. AI-based threat detection and blockchain technology strengthen company networks and reduce unlawful access. The report finishes with business Zero Trust Architecture best practices and AI and blockchain cybersecurity predictions.

title: A Hybrid Deep Learning Framework Combining Swin Transformer and Mask R-CNN for Dismantled Weapon Part Detection
authors: P Shanthi, B Yamini, A Vishnupriya, S Hariprabha, K Vidhya, R Panneer Selvi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: The growing prevalence of dangerous weapons being misused in public spaces presents a major danger to the public. As a result, society needs intelligent surveillance systems to enable early identification of threats. The challenge with identifying dismantled weapon parts is based on their small size, ability to be hidden, and resemblance to other non-threatening objects. The current methods of detecting weapons primarily rely on identifying assembled weapons and are limited by conventional CNN-based architectures. These limitations of current solutions result in a lack of contextual understanding of the surrounding environment and limited effectiveness in identifying threats that are occluded or surrounded by clutter. This paper proposes a framework for identifying dismantled weapon parts using a Mask R-CNN architecture with a Swin Transformer as its backbone. The Swin Transformer enables the extraction of hierarchical, multi-scale features using shifted-window self-attention. With the Mask R-CNN architecture, instance-level classification, bounding-box regression, and accurate segmentation of each weapon component are possible. The results of the experiments demonstrate that this system performs exceptionally well: it has demonstrated a 96.8% accuracy, 95.9% precision, and 96.3% recall. Moreover, this system supports proactive threat identification, enabling police to take more effective steps to keep the public safe.

title: Data Accounting in Agriculture: Satellite, Station & IoT Fusion for Reliable Forecasts
authors: Saksham Mittal, Santhosh B, B Namasivayam, Shakhnoza Muzrapova, Barno Matchanova, Artiqova O'g'iljon
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: A structured assessment and weighting of heterogeneous sensing streams of data accounting enable more reliable agricultural forecasts by exposing and correcting biases, gaps and redundancy across satellite, station and IoT inputs. This paper presents a reproducible pipeline that integrates an explicit data-accounting layer with a physically consistent fusion backbone (ESM–GAMS) and a learned ensemble-weight (EW) operator implemented as an LSTM. This study defines a Data-Value Index (DVI) and a Data-Value Ratio (DVR) to quantify per-source contribution and used those diagnostics inside both the fusion step and a crop-model ensemble merge. Weather-map forecasts were generated with a physics-led downscaling ensemble and a convolutional residual corrector. Crop yields were simulated with a process model and weighted by LSTM-derived EWs that depend on historical and recent data-quality diagnostics. Experiments over three southern India districts for 2010–2022 showed that data accounting reduced precipitation RMSE by 8–16% and decreased crop-model yield bias by 6– 12% relative to naïve fusion.

title: Thai One-Stage Fingerspelling Image Dataset for Hand Detection and Static Gesture Recognition
authors: Siriwiwat Lata, Emmanuel Okafor, Sirawan Phiphitphatphaisit, Olarik Surinta
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Cybernetics and Innovations (ICCI)
abstract: One-stage fingerspelling and includes 15 Thai consonants, each represented by a single static hand sign. Data collection was conducted in collaboration with an experienced TSL instructor from the Special Education Department at Rajabhat Maha Sarakham University, Thailand. A total of 14 students proficient in sign language and 40 non-signers participated in the collection process. Images were captured using a DSLR camera in typical indoor environments without controlled conditions for clothing, lighting, or background to ensure high visual diversity and environmental complexity. The dataset comprises 7,200 RGB images, each accompanied by XML-formatted annotations providing ground-truth hand localizations and gesture labels. To establish a performance baseline, a two-stage pipeline was evaluated. For the detection task, the YOLOv3 model achieved a mean Average Precision (mAP) of 97.87 %, indicating reliable hand localization. In the classification phase, various convolutional neural network architectures were tested; DenseNet121 and MobileNetV2 demonstrated the highest accuracy at 98 %. To promote reproducibility and standardized evaluation, the dataset is publicly accessible via the Mendeley Data repository, providing a foundational resource for the TSL research community.

title: MISTY: Multidimensional Identification of Synthetic Text Yield
authors: Md. Siam Ansary
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: The proliferation of large language models has made distinguishing between human-written and AI-generated text a critical challenge across academic, journalistic, and regulatory domains. In this work, we propose MISTY (Multidimensional Identification of Synthetic Text Yield), a novel hybrid detection framework that integrates supervised contrastive learning with rich linguistic feature engineering to robustly identify AI-generated content. MISTY first encodes input texts using the all-MiniLM-L6-v2 sentence transformer, then optimizes a supervised contrastive projection head to learn semantically separable embeddings. These embeddings are further fused with a comprehensive set of stylistic, syntactic, discourse, and entropy-based features-including perplexity from GPT-2, readability scores, part-of-speech distributions, and lexical entropy-yielding a $\mathbf{1 4 6}$-dimensional feature vector. A neural classifier trained on this enriched representation distinguishes human and AI text with high precision. Our results demonstrate that MISTY is able to achieve excellent outcomes. This framework offers a lightweight, extensible, and interpretable approach for the detection of synthetic text in practical applications.

title: ECG Signal Acquisition Module:Innovative Solutions for Cardiac Monitoring
authors: Asif Ahmed Rafi, Rahul Biswas, Md Moznuzzaman
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Electrocardiogram (ECG) signal acquisition is fundamental to cardiac health monitoring and diagnosis. This paper presents the design and implementation of an innovative ECG signal acquisition module optimized for real-time, low-noise, and high-fidelity cardiac monitoring applications. The proposed system integrates advanced analog front-end circuitry with efficient digital signal processing techniques to ensure accurate detection of subtle cardiac events. A low-power microcontroller based interface that enables portability and seamless data transmission for remote healthcare solutions. Extensive testing on simulated and real patient data demonstrates the module’s reliability, noise immunity and compliance with biomedical signal standards. This work aims to contribute to the development of compact, cost-effective and scalable ECG monitoring systems for modern healthcare environments.

title: Improve Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) Brain-Computer Interfaces: Wearable Sensor and Deep Network Integration for Better EEG Signal Classification
authors: J. Bala Samuvel, Hebatullah Awwad, M Monisha, K. Saranya, Ramesh Banoth, L Manimegalai
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: One exciting development in brain-to-device communication is the use of brain-computer interfaces (BCIs) based on electroencephalogram (EEG) signals. Classifying EEG signals is challenging due to the non-stationarity of brain waves, poor signal-to-noise ratio, and large inter-subject variability. The automatic extraction of discriminative features from raw EEG data using Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) has demonstrated promising results; nevertheless, their efficiency can be hindered by sensor constraints and a lack of communication between wearable devices and deep learning architectures. To enhance the accuracy and robustness of EEG signal classification, this research proposes a CNN-based BCI framework that integrates state-of-the-art wearable EEG sensor designs with optimal deep neural network topologies. The suggested approach processes spatiotemporal EEG patterns captured by high-fidelity wearable sensors using specialised convolutional neural network (CNN) layers, preserving physiological signal properties, all via end-to-end learning. Sensor-aware preprocessing, adaptive convolution kernels, and attentionbased feature fusion all work together to make the model less noise sensitive and more generalizable. This technology is perfect for practical BCI applications, as it employs small, wearable sensors to capture data in real time without restricting the user’s movement or comfort. Compared with BCI models that rely on convolutional neural networks (CNNs), the integrated design shows markedly improved performance in classification tests. The results show that scalable, user-friendly, and reliable brain-computer interface (BCI) systems for healthcare, rehabilitation, and HMI use wearable electroencephalogram (EEG) hardware in conjunction with deep learning architectures.

title: Real-Time Computer Vision for Adaptive Traffic Control in a Digital Twin
authors: Patiyuth Pramkeaw, Warit Leangruttananon, Pimyada Suksomboon
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Cybernetics and Innovations (ICCI)
abstract: Traditional traffic lights that stay on for a set amount of time don't work well in modern cities where things are always changing, which makes traffic worse. This paper presents an adaptive traffic light optimization system created in a high-fidelity Digital Twin setting utilizing Unity3D. The system uses a YOLOv8n object detection model to process real-time visual data from virtual CCTV feeds through a low-latency UDP pipeline. This helps to close the gap between experimental theory and real-world deployment. A dynamic timing algorithm figures out the best green light durations and uses green-wave synchronization to coordinate adjacent intersections by counting the number of cars in a certain Region of Interest (ROI). Results from 20 simulation runs show that the AI-powered system cut the average time it took to clear traffic from 134.52 seconds to 99.45 seconds, which is a 26.1% improvement in efficiency over traditional methods. The study shows that Digital Twin technology can be used as a scalable, risk-free testbed to speed up the rollout of vision-based Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS).

title: A Multilingual Transformer Based Framework for Phishing Email Detection and Deployment in Real-World Pipelines
authors: Mouhamadou Mame Diop, Mamadou BA, Khalifa Sylla, Samuel Ouya
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Phishing emails remain a persistent cybersecurity threat, relying on linguistically and emotionally crafted social engineering strategies. While Transformer based models have demonstrated strong capabilities in capturing semantic and affective cues embedded in such messages, existing studies largely focus on offline evaluations and often overlook deployment constraints and interpretability issues. This paper proposes a multilingual phishing detection framework based on Transformer architectures (mBERT, DistilBERT, and XLM-R), trained and comparatively evaluated on a consolidated corpus of 163,948 realworld emails. Experimental results show that XLM-R achieves the best overall performance (F1 = 0.996), whereas DistilBERT provides a competitive trade off under constrained computational settings. Beyond model evaluation, an end to end operational pipeline is presented, integrating real email analysis via the Gmail API, an interactive Streamlit interface, and LIME-based explainability. The proposed framework demonstrates the feasibility of deploying high performance and interpretable phishing detection systems under realistic operational conditions.

title: Develop Artificial Intelligence Systems for Space Exploration: Processing Images in Real Time and Identifying Anomalies from Radio Frequency Sensor Data from Satellites and Rovers
authors: Kanne Naveen, Shazia Kausar, N. Arockia Rosy, D. Kirubakaran, D Shobana, A. Rajalingam
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is now a significant tool for helping people make decisions on their own, processing data in real time, and swiftly finding problems in these complicated circumstances. This study primarily focuses on developing advanced AI frameworks that use radio-frequency (RF) sensor data to evaluate images in real time and detect anomalies. The suggested method uses machine learning, deep learning, and signal processing techniques directly on the onboard computer units. This is different from earlier systems, which mostly used land-based technology for interpretation. This makes it easier to talk to each other, makes people more receptive, and lowers the chances of problems that could get in the way of mission goals. There are two key parts of the AI system: a computer vision framework for analysing images and a signal-processing module for decoding RF signals. The vision-based module uses both attention processes and convolutional neural networks (CNNs) to find surface flaws, geological features, and barriers. Scientists can make discoveries more easily because they can travel more easily. This study examines real-time edge computing, which enables AI to operate even when rovers and satellites have limited processing power. The simulation results show that the rates of discovering abnormalities, latency, and accuracy have all improved a lot compared to previous methods. In conclusion, building AI systems capable of processing RF and image data in real time is a significant step forward for space exploration. This approach addresses the growing need for autonomous operations in remote areas and makes it easier to use AI more widely in future planetary exploration missions.

title: A Predictive IoT Framework for Continuous Vehicle Emission Monitoring Using Ensemble CatBoost and XGBoost Learning
authors: Sk Kamal Sundar, Ra Kaushik, Akhilesh Shukla, R Srinikesh, P Jayanthi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Traditional vehicle emission monitoring systems are limited by their reactive, single-point approach, which does not account for real-world factors like weather and traffic that affect vehicle emissions and undermine proactive policy and maintenance. In this paper, we propose GreenPulse AI, an IoT-based Smart Emission Monitoring System that uses predictive Machine Learning (ML) methods to mitigate those limitations. GreenPulse AI continuously collects data to compute an adaptable a multifactor Green Emission Score and leverages accurate ML model predictions to forecast emission levels for the next hour. The significant contributions of this work include a multi-factor scoring algorithm that incorporates all emissions contributors, ML to enable accuracy in short-term predictions, and a timely, multi-factor framework that collects data continuously for further preventive maintenance and proactive environmental policy reasoning.

title: CarryMe: A Mobile-Based Structured Purchase-Request Management System for Enhancing Usability and Digital Consumer Experience
authors: Pisit Chansuek, Sarinthip Phaenthong, May Boondee, Thanakrit Janchidfah, Pirat Worasiri
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Cybernetics and Innovations (ICCI)
abstract: In the era of social commerce, informal purchase-request activities are commonly managed through chat-based platforms, leading to fragmented information, miscommunication, and inefficient coordination. This study proposes CarryMe, a mobile-based application designed to structure and manage purchase-request activities using a cloud-based architecture. The system enables real-time tracking, centralized data management, and standardized workflows. The application was developed and evaluated with 114 participants using a structured usability questionnaire. Descriptive statistics and inferential analysis were applied, including mean, standard deviation, and independent t-tests. The results indicate a high level of user satisfaction (Mean <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$= 4.25, \text{SD}=0.58$</tex>), particularly in ease of use and process clarity. Statistical analysis revealed no significant difference across gender groups <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathbf{p}>0.05)$</tex>, indicating consistent usability. The findings demonstrate that transforming informal coordination into structured systems significantly enhances usability and reduces operational ambiguity. This study contributes to mobile application design in social commerce environments.

title: AI as Human Amplification in Cybersecurity: An Intelligence-Centered Design Framework for Risk Management
authors: Stasija Raicevic, Chanakan Sriratanaban, Theerawut Tantiathimongkhon
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Cybernetics and Innovations (ICCI)
abstract: This paper presents a framework integrating design thinking, artificial intelligence, and cybersecurity risk management to address the human factor in security systems. Traditional cybersecurity approaches often overlook user behavior and needs, leading to security measures that users circumvent or misunderstand. This paper proposes leveraging AI to enhance the empathize phase of design thinking by analyzing user behavior patterns at scale, enabling security teams to design more effective and user-friendly controls. Concurrently, this paper addresses the critical need for robust risk management of AI systems themselves, as these tools become both security assets and potential vulnerabilities. This framework positions <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$A I$</tex> as a cognitive amplifier that elevates human capabilities rather than replacing human judgment in security decision-making. This paper demonstrates how AI handles scale and pattern recognition while humans provide contextual understanding, ethical reasoning, and strategic oversight. The bidirectional relationship between AI-enhanced security design and AI system security creates a comprehensive approach where design thinking principles apply to both userfacing security measures and the governance of AI tools. This human-AI partnership approach ensures that cybersecurity solutions remain both technically robust and aligned with human needs and capabilities.

title: A Unified Neural Ablation and Robustness Analysis Framework for Small-Sample Heterogeneous Tabular Prediction: A Physical Fitness Assessment Case
authors: Xuanxuan Li
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Physical fitness assessment data constitute a typical small-sample heterogeneous tabular prediction problem, where limited observations, mixed variable types, and nonlinear interfeature associations make reliable modeling and interpretation difficult. To address this issue, this study develops a unified neural analysis framework for small-sample structured data based on a multilayer perceptron under a controlled experimental protocol. The framework integrates group-wise input ablation, cross-task prediction, robustness evaluation under partial feature loss, and permutation-based contribution analysis, enabling systematic examination of feature-group utility, model stability, and indicator-level importance within a single computational pipeline. Using physical fitness assessment as a case study, five crossindicator prediction tasks are constructed by treating each performance variable as the target in turn. Different input group combinations are evaluated under identical training settings, and the best-performing configurations are further compared with conventional regression baselines. Results show that auxiliary performance indicators provide stronger predictive value than environmental variables in most tasks, while the proposed framework maintains competitive predictive accuracy and stable explanatory capability across multiple targets. More importantly, the study demonstrates that the proposed pipeline offers a reusable neural modeling and analysis strategy for small-sample heterogeneous tabular data beyond the specific application scenario.

title: Integrating Ensembled Vision-Based Techniques for Intelligent Path Planning in Agricultural UAVs
authors: Rohan Vishwakarma, Ram Kumar Sahu, Kamlesh Raghuwanshi, Surabhi Karsoliva, Rahul Das Vairagi
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) are transforming the area of precision agriculture by allowing effective crop monitoring, crop surveillance and autonomous flight over expansive farmlands. Nevertheless, the implementation of effective and intelligent path planning in dynamic agricultural settings is an important issue because of the uneven terrain, disorganized patterns of crops, and the changing environmental factors. In this paper, the authors suggest a unified platform that builds on ensembled vision-based methods to achieve precise scene perception and smart route planning in UAVs in the agricultural field. The ensemble model combines predictions of multiple deep learning models such as convolutional neural networks and transformer-based segmentation models to improve the accuracy and precision of detecting crop rows, obstacles, and classifying the terrain. After that, the field maps divided into segments are applied to create optimal flight paths through a hybrid planning process that involves heuristic algorithms (A search and Genetic Algorithms) that are combined with Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) solutions. The simulations and real-world data justify the proposed system and prove that the accuracy of the navigation, the efficiency of the coverage, and the effectiveness of the obstacle avoidance is much higher than those achieved using the single-model or conventional planning methods. The findings support the possibility of vision-based ensemble learning and hybrid planning to enhance the intelligent and autonomous UAV operations to support precision agriculture.

title: Multimodal Interest-Tree Based Negative Enhancement for Recommendation
authors: Doudou Si, Lexin Song
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Multimodal recommender systems often suffer from “semantic distortion” when constructing item graphs via modality similarity, misaligning feature proximity with user intent. Existing behavior-based denoising methods remain limited by shallow high-order modeling or noise introduction. We propose MINERec, which introduces Interest-Tree-Guided Representation Purification. MINERec first constructs a Behavior-Aware Interest Tree (BAIT) based on item co-occurrence in user interaction histories to anchor authentic user intents. By cross-validating modality similarity against this co-occurrence structure, we identify items with “high modality similarity but zero co-occurrence” as hard negatives that represent semantic traps. Coupled with Structural-Semantic Adaptive Contrastive Learning (SACL), our method dynamically repels these negatives to filter noise at the representation level prior to graph reconstruction. Experiments on three datasets show MINERec outperforms state-of-the-art baselines, achieving an average improvement of 3.16%.

title: ACAR-YOLO: Adaptive Context Aggregation and Channel Recalibration for Efficient Object Detection
authors: Junzhe Xu
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: This paper proposes ACAR-YOLOv8s, a lightweight object detection model that combines three different enhancement strategies: adaptive sampling, multi dilation context aggregation, and channel-wise recalibration. To address the issue of information loss due to strided downsampling, a light-weight adaptive extraction (LAE) module is added to the model to extract fine-grained local details. A MultiDilation (MD) module is used to extract multi-scale contextual information by performing parallel dilated convolutions with different receptive fields. Additionally, a channel attention (CA) mechanism is added to the model to recalibrate the feature responses and highlight the defect-related cues. Experiment results on the Fabric Defect Computer Vision Dataset show that the proposed model achieves 93.8% mAP@0.5, 50.5% mAP@0.5:0.95, 93.5% Precision, and 83.9% Recall, respectively, which outperforms the YOLOv8s baseline model by 7.3 percentage points in mAP@0.5. Ablation experiments also validate the complementary roles of the three modules, which consistently improve the model performance when combined together.

title: Bridging Fiber Optic and Free-Space Optical Communication: A Comparative Study and Hybrid Framework using Artificial Intelligence
authors: Aanchal Vij, Priyanka Gaba, Sourav Vij
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The fast development of smart systems, 5G/6G networks, and data-sensitive applications has created severe demands on contemporary communication systems with respect to bandwidth, stability, and flexibility. Though fiber optic communication has high capacity and low attenuation, its implementation is usually limited by cost and topography as well as physical infrastructure. On the contrary, free-space optical (FSO) communication offers flexibility and cable-free communication, which has weaknesses in atmospheric sensitivity as well as alignment difficulties. The current paper is a brief comparative analysis of the fiber optic and FSO communication systems, their strengths, limitations, and applicability. To address the fundamental weaknesses of single technologies, a hybrid optical communication framework with an AI is offered. The hybrid network ensures intelligent switching of links, beam adjustment, prediction of traffic, fault identification, and provision of enhanced security due to the integration of artificial intelligence. The suggested strategy will enhance the resilience of the network, the efficient use of the resources, and the maintenance of the stability of communication in the conditions of the dynamic environment. This paper has shown that the convergence of fiber optics, FSO, and artificial intelligence can provide a bright future of smart and futuristic communication networks.

title: On Amount of Fading and Ergodic Channel Capacity of Terahertz Communications Using Compound Channel Model
authors: Tingguang Gan, Shuping Dang, Xiangcheng Li, Hongliu Wei, Zhenrong Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: In this paper, we study terahertz (THz) communication systems using a compound channel model that captures the effects of multi-path fading, dynamic shadowing, molecular absorption loss, and propagation loss. Based on the estimates of the channel parameters under consideration, we derive the exact expressions of a probability density function (PDF) and a cumulative distribution function (CDF) for the end-to-end signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) of the THz communication system. Afterwards, we use the obtained PDF and CDF to derive closedform expressions of amount of fading (AoF) and ergodic channel capacity (ECC). Moreover, the asymptotic analytical expression of ECC is also derived to discover more useful information. The correctness of the derived expressions is verified by numerical simulations through Monte Carlo simulations. Through the analytical and numerical results presented in this paper, we provide different perspectives on analyzing THz communication systems using the compound channel model, which are expected to expedite the theoretic research of THz wireless communications.

title: Research on LiDAR and GNSS Coordinate System Calibration for AGV Based on SVD in Hybrid Environments
authors: Qian Wei
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: In indoor-outdoor hybrid scenarios, Automated Guided Vehicles (AGVs) usually fuse LiDAR and GNSS for seamless localization. However, inconsistent coordinate frames between the two sensors impede direct data fusion. This paper presents a systematic calibration method for unifying LiDAR and GNSS coordinates in indoor-outdoor hybrid environments. By leveraging the classic Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) algorithm for point set registration, the proposed approach addresses the practical challenges of spatial-temporal asynchrony and abrupt observation changes in transition zones. Without extra calibration targets, the proposed method is highly automatic and effectively improves localization consistency in transition zones. It offers a reliable solution for multi-sensor fusion localization in mixed indoor-outdoor environments.

title: Dual-Channel Contrastive Convolutional Network for Respiratory Disease Prediction and Classification
authors: Laxmibai, Lakshmi Patil, Vinita Patil
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Respiratory diseases such as COVID-19, pneumonia, and tuberculosis continue to pose major global health challenges, demanding rapid and precise diagnosis. Although chest X-rays (CXRs) are widely used for screening, traditional machine learning approaches often fail to handle noisy, low-contrast images, leading to suboptimal classification accuracy. To address this research gap, this study proposes the Dual-Channel Contrastive Convolutional Network (DCC-Net), designed to enhance CXR quality, extract multi-scale features, and provide accurate respiratory disease detection and classification. The objectives include noise removal, contrast improvement, attention-based feature refinement, and efficient prediction using a hybrid classifier. DCC-Net’s architecture integrates a Noise-Contrast Removal Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) (NC-RCNN) for preprocessing, an Enhanced Multi-Scale CNN (EMSC-Net) for feature extraction, and a hybrid classifier for prediction/classification. Extensive experiments on COVID-19 Radiography, Lung Disease, and CXR Image datasets demonstrate high performance, achieving accuracies of 99.5%, 99.95%, and 99.96%, respectively. The results validate the robustness and efficiency of DCC-Net, highlighting its potential for real-world clinical applications.

title: Artificial Intelligence for Interpreting Cognitive and Emotional States in Psychoanalytic Contexts
authors: Kumari Priyanka Sinha, T. Hashni, Ch Madhava Rao, Ramraj B, K.Rani, Ch. Janakamma
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Artificial intelligence has progressed markedly in simulating cognitive and emotional processes; nonetheless, its utilization in psychoanalysis settings is still constrained. This research presents a Hybrid Deep Neuro-Symbolic Psychoanalytic Interpretation Model (HDNS-PI) that amalgamates heterogeneous neural networks with symbolism rationale models to analyse cognition and mental conditions in therapeutic contexts. The technique employs vocal prosody, subtle facial movements, text-independent associations, and physical cues to establish an integrated latent psychoanalysis paradigm that links observed behaviours with core psychoanalysis principles. A transformer-based video encoder utilises methods augmented by semi-supervised contrastive learning, enabling the recognition of defensive mechanisms, emotional fluctuations, and cognitive distortions. The embedded elements are incorporated into a neural processing layer informed by psychoanalytic theory, allowing the model to infer fundamental trends and dynamic conflicts. The system further replicates behavioural state transitions between sessions, offering a comprehensible trajectory regarding mental variations and resistance patterns. The outcome is structured as clinically coherent narratives, providing counsellors with pragmatic insights while preserving interpretive richness. This methodology amalgamates psychoanalytic concepts with AI analysis, linking smart machines and therapeutic practice, so augmenting the precision and ethical oversight of assessments of unconscious processes in medical psychoanalysis. The proposed HDNS-PI method achieved an overall accuracy of 98% in interpreting cognition and emotional conditions.

title: Projection of epigenetic markers of Type 2 Diabetes Reveal Pancreatic Cancer Prognostic Tumour States
authors: Subhradyuti Basu, Bibekanand Mallick, Amrita Singh, Khalid Baig Mirza
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)

title: Context-Aware Transformer Optimization for Improved Generalization in Large Language Models
authors: Santosh Datta Bompally
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The study presents a new framework called Context-Aware Transformer Optimization (CATO), designed to enhance the contextual reasoning and generalization abilities of large language models (LLMs). Conventional Transformers are usually based on one focus mechanism and a set of predetermined optimization procedures, which do not offer much capacity in terms of managing a range of linguistic situations and long-term relations. The suggested CATO structure integrates three components which operate in a synergistic manner: Context Importance Estimation (CIS), Context-Aware Attention Refinement and an Adaptive Optimization Mechanism. Together, these modules enable dynamic adjustment of attention weights and learning rates based on contextual relevance, resulting in improved interpretability and efficiency. Experiments under the SST-2 benchmark reveal that CATO has a better accuracy92.71, which outperforms the fine-tuned Baseline Transformer by 2.19 points and lower perplexity and convergence rate. The visualizations confirm that the model is able to identify semantically significant tokens, resulting into context-sensitive and reliable predictions. The low-rank adaptation strategy ensures scalability with minimal computational costs. Overall, CATO advances the development of adaptive, context-sensitive Transformers, paving the way for well-constructed, generalizable, and efficient large-scale language models suitable for real-world applications.

title: Filtered CE-OFDM for Spectral Efficiency in Visible Light Communications
authors: B. M. S. R. Rosmaninho, B. M. Oliveira, V. N. H. Silva, M. C. R. Medeiros, P. P. Monteiro
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Constant Envelope OFDM (CE-OFDM) offers excellent robustness to nonlinear distortion and noise, but at the cost of poor spectral efficiency (SE). This paper proposes and experimentally validates a filtered variant, Filtered CE-OFDM (F-CE-OFDM), designed to improve SE while preserving the intrinsic robustness of CE-OFDM. A flexible optimization framework is introduced, enabling dynamic adjustment of the filtering parameter α according to different system requirements. Experimental results in a visible light communication (VLC) setup demonstrate that F-CE-OFDM can consistently outperform conventional CE-OFDM in terms of SE, achieving gains up to 3 bits/s/Hz when using α=1 and 2πh=1.6 at the highest average received power level with only a small EVM penalty. Moreover, compared to conventional OFDM, F-CE-OFDM achieves an EVM improvement of up to approximately 10 dB under low-SNR conditions. These results highlight F-CE-OFDM as a compelling compromise between SE and EVM, delivering substantial spectral efficiency enhancement while maintaining strong robustness against noise and nonlinearities. The proposed scheme introduces only a low-complexity digital filtering stage, implying negligible impact on implementation cost while largely preserving the power-efficiency benefits inherent to constant-envelope modulation. Its tunable filtering parameter α enables adaptive operation, allowing the system to prioritize either low EVM or enhanced SE depending on channel conditions and design objectives. This versatility makes F-CE-OFDM particularly attractive for communication scenarios constrained by noise or bandwidth, where maintaining signal integrity and efficient spectrum use are equally critical.

title: Denoise2Seg: A Deep Denoising-Segmentation Model for Interference-Resilient Spectrum Sensing
authors: Thien Huynh-The, Ngoc-Hoang Mai, Quoc-Viet Pham, Hai-Trang Phuoc Dang
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: Existing deep learning models for spectrum sensing in 5G/6G radar-communication environments face a critical accuracy-efficiency trade-off. To this end, the paper introduces Denoise2Seg, an efficient model that synergistically denoises and segments coexisting 5G, LTE, and radar signals. The core innovation is a multi-task paradigm where an auxiliary denoising task acts as a powerful regularization mechanism, forcing a shared encoder to learn robust, noise-invariant features. This collaborative mechanism is realized through novel architectural modules engineered to perform robust multi-scale analysis and to intelligently arbitrate between clean and noisy feature streams.Through comprehensive experiments, Denoise2Seg demonstrates superior performance, achieving a state-of-the-art mean accuracy of 97.94%, a mean Intersection-over-Union of 96.08%, and a mean F1-score of 98.00% with a compact design of only 4.34M parameters. This represents a significant reduction in complexity compared to other high-performance models, establishing Denoise2Seg as a robust and practical solution for intelligent spectrum sensing in next-generation wireless networks.

title: Remote Magnetic Levitation Using Reduced Attitude Control and Parametric Field Models
authors: Neelaksh Singh, Jasan Zughaibi, Denis von Arx, Bradley J. Nelson, Michael Muehlebach
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Electromagnetic navigation systems (eMNS) are increasingly used in minimally invasive procedures such as endovascular interventions and targeted drug delivery due to their ability to generate fast and precise magnetic fields. In this paper, we utilize the OctoMag and a custom 13-coil eMNS to achieve remote levitation and control of multiple rigid bodies across large air gaps, showcasing the dynamic capabilities of such systems. A compact parametric analytical model maps coil currents to the forces and torques acting on the levitating object, eliminating the need for computationally expensive simulations or lookup tables and establishing a levitator- and platform-agnostic control framework. Translational motion is stabilized using linear quadratic regulators. A nonlinear time-invariant controller is used to regulate the reduced attitude accounting for the inherent uncontrollability of rotations about the dipole axis and stabilizing the full five degrees of freedom controllable pose subspace. We analyze key design limitations and evaluate the approach through trajectory tracking experiments across different objects and actuation platforms. Notably, our proposed controller demonstrates superiority over an equivalent baseline PID formulation, reliably tracking large spatial angles up to 65<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^\circ$</tex-math></inline-formula>. This work demonstrates the dynamic capabilities and potential of feedback control in electromagnetic navigation, which is likely to open up new medical applications.

title: PredVC: Predictive Diffusion Models for Video Compression
authors: Siddharath Narayan Shakya, Parimala Kancharla
publish: IEEE Signal Processing Letters
abstract: Video data demands substantial storage and trans mission bandwidth, posing challenges for applications such as streaming, conferencing, and robotic tasks. In recent years, generative AI models have demonstrated remarkable efficacy in learning spatial signatures and exploiting temporal redundancy, positioning them as a powerful tool for next-generation video compression. Conventional codecs like H.264 and H.265 rely heavily on motion estimation, but they struggle to scale efficiently with modern bitrate requirements. This work proposes PredVC, a diffusion-based video compression framework that combines generative frame prediction with learned compression. It predicts future frames from a small set of reference frames, encodes only those references and their residual errors, and reconstructs the missing frames at the receiver by adding decoded residuals to the predicted frames, preserving visual fidelity. Experimental results show that PredVC significantly outperforms both traditional codecs and state-of-the-art learned compression models, particularly at low bitrates, making it highly effective in bandwidth constrained scenarios. This work highlights the growing potential of generative diffusion models as a new paradigm for efficient video compression.

title: A Hybrid Modulation Strategy for Enhancing the Reactive Power Output Capability of Three-Phase Isolated DC-AC Matrix Converter
authors: Chaofan Wei, Jiatao Yang, Rui Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: The three-phase isolated DC–AC matrix converter is a promising isolated converter topology featuring high efficiency and high power density. However, existing studies mainly focus on unity power factor operation, while the reactive power capability of this converter has not been systematically investigated. This paper first analyzes the reactive power limitation of the converter from the perspective of current synthesis. The maximum achievable power factor angle under different DC voltages is determined through numerical analysis. Based on this observation, analytical expressions for the DC voltage and the maximum AC-side power factor angle under conventional two-line voltage modulation are derived. To overcome this reactive power limitation, a hybrid modulation strategy is proposed. By introducing an additional line-to-line voltage within the switching period, the proposed strategy enables the generation of the required reactive current when the conventional two-line voltage modulation reaches its limit. The converter automatically transitions between the two-line and three-line voltage modulation modes according to predefined conditions. Experimental results at multiple DC voltages verify the effectiveness of the proposed strategy and demonstrate that a power factor of 0.8 can be maintained over a wide DC voltage range with current THD below 3%.

title: Reddit as a Semantic Sensor: Forecasting Forex Movements via Emotionally Weighted Signals in Computational Social Systems
authors: Lersak Phothong, Anupong Sukprasert, Prapassorn Wareesri, Nantana Ngamtampong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: This study presents an empirical framework for forecasting directional movements of the EUR/USD foreign exchange market using sentiment signals extracted from Reddit. Sentiment is classified with FinBERT (a financial-domain adaptation of Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers, BERT) and transformed through an engagement-weighted formulation to capture social amplification effects, with a two-day behavioral lag applied to align sentiment with market responses. Feature engineering incorporates linguistic attributes, interaction terms, and rolling sentiment averages. Four machine learning models—Logistic Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost (XGB), and Neural Networks—are evaluated using walk-forward time-aware cross-validation. Results indicate that XGB achieves the highest average predictive performance, with accuracy around 0.78 and consistently high AUC under walk-forward evaluation, relative to standard price-based and naive baseline models. Model interpretability is examined using SHapley Additive Explanations, while a posthoc semantic analysis employing BERTopic, WordClouds, and uniform manifold approximation and projection (UMAP) shows that correct predictions are associated with coherent and recurring discourse patterns related to market conditions and sentiment expression. Overall, the findings demonstrate that engagement-weighted Reddit sentiment provides a robust and interpretable signal for short-horizon Forex forecasting.

title: Enhancing Mouth Stick Usability for People with Tetraplegia: A Quantitative Evaluation of UI Target Size and Placement
authors: Izumi Mizoguchi, Hiroyuki Kajimoto
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: For individuals with tetraplegia due to spinal cord injury, a mouth stick is a low-cost and practical assistive technology for operating smartphones. However, established UI design guidelines that consider its unique operational characteristics are lacking, and existing guidelines (e.g., a minimum target size of 44 iOS pt) can significantly impair their usability. This study aims to quantitatively determine the optimal target size and a recommended placement area that minimizes interaction errors in smartphone UIs, thereby improving accessibility for mouth stick users. We created an experimental environment where 10 able-bodied participants experienced simulated tetraplegia by physically restricting their trunk and neck movements. Participants performed a tapping task that combined five target sizes (44 iOS pt to 132 iOS pt) and 25 screen locations under two levels of simulated impairment. Task completion time and the number of mis-taps were recorded and analyzed as performance metrics. Notably, the minimum sizes recommended by existing guidelines (e.g., 44 iOS pt or 48 iOS pt) were found to be impractical, leading to frequent mis-taps. To significantly reduce interaction errors and time, a target size of 88 iOS pt or larger is strongly recommended. Furthermore, the optimal placement area for buttons was identified as the lower-central region of the screen, while the screen edges, especially the top, should be avoided. This study is the first to provide specific UI design guidelines based on quantitative data specialized for mouth stick interaction. The target size and layout recommendations proposed herein are an indispensable contribution for UI designers aiming to develop truly accessible applications for users with tetraplegia.

title: Meta relation-aware Metric Learning Framework for Few-Shot Uncertain Knowledge Graph Completion
authors: Xinyan Wang, Kuo Yang, Jian Liu, Xianan Li, Rui Hua, Kuan Xu, Zixin Shu, Xuezhong Zhou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering
abstract: Uncertainty is a fundamental aspect of human knowledge, widely present in various domains such as medicine, biology, and finance. Uncertain Knowledge Graph Completion (UKGC) tasks encompass both link prediction and confidence prediction. However, the inaccessibility of high-quality knowledge results in sparse knowledge graphs, where a large number of entities exhibit relatively sparse neighborhoods. This sparsity severely hinders the performance of few-shot UKGC models, which generally rely on neighborhood aggregation to enhance entity representations. In this study, we propose a Meta relation-aware Metric learning framework designed for few-shot UKGC, named MMUC. To mitigate neighbor sparsity, MMUC introduces a relation-centric paradigm that decouples representation enhancement from background graphs, integrating an uncertainty-driven similarity measure through a learnable confidence learner to achieve robust, non-linear fusion for joint link and confidence prediction. Extensive evaluations across multiple benchmarks demonstrate MMUC's superior performance through rigorous link and confidence prediction, ablation studies, and sparsity analysis, highlighting its particular resilience to neighbor sparsity in few-shot uncertain KGs. The source code can be accessed in <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/sienna-wxy/MMUC</uri>.

title: Smart Retrieval of Industrial Standards with LLM and Elastic search
authors: Poornima Jayaraman, Pachaivannan Partheeban, Kothalam Krishnan Nagarajan, N Koushik, G Nikil Manikandan, J.Jerauld Alwin
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Manufacturing, energy, and construction industries significantly use the services of ASME, API, and NFPA standards. But the retrieval of precise information from these large code libraries can be a slow process. For these reasons, a new AI-based hybrid document retrieval system is discussed in this paper. The new system will not only use keyword searches using Elasticsearch with traditional AI methods, but it will also use transformer-based large language models (LLMs) for providing precise information related to industrial standards. The system will use tokenaware preprocessing techniques, fall-back approaches for large queries, or a summary to ensure clarity. In contrast to conventional Retrieval-Augmentation-Generation (RAG) approaches, it will divide the two processes for faster execution. The system will demonstrate sub-two-second latency measurements with precise alignment of source documents.

title: Aware of Latency Moving to Edge–Cloud Computing Collaborative Mobile Computing: Real-Time Decision-Making Algorithms
authors: Muruganantham Angamuthu, Yousuf Hazaimeh, Chandra Doddapalli Ramachandra Reddy, R. Ramesh, G. Ramkumar, G Karuna
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Problems with computing latency, energy economy, and service quality have arisen as a result of the explosion of data-intensive apps and mobile devices. Due to network congestion and high communication latency, traditional mobile cloud computing architectures cannot meet real-time demands. Edge computing might cause a major shakeup by bringing data storage and processing closer to the people who use it. It is not advisable to rely solely on edge resources to support complex and large-scale applications due to their inherent constraints. Presenting real-time decision-making approaches and investigating collaborative, latency-aware mobile computing, this work aims to maximise job execution across distributed computing types. Intelligent compute-offloading algorithms that can determine in real time whether mobile tasks are better handled in the cloud, on the edge, or locally are the primary focus of this research. Application delay limits, device energy levels, workload considerations, and network conditions are all evaluated in real time to help us make these determinations. Latency-aware decision algorithms optimise the distribution of computational load across diverse resources and reduce end-to-end response time. The proposed system uses adaptive scheduling, resource allocation, and real-time feedback to adapt to changing mobile environments. A multi-dimensional latency model that accounts for migration overhead, processing time, transmission delay, and queuing delay is this study’s key contribution. Real-time monitoring and predictive analytics allow decision-making algorithms to anticipate network changes and alter offloading strategies.

title: A Multi-Modal Architecture for Document Forgery Detection
authors: Amrit Koul, Henny Prajapati, Heer Shah, Harshita Swali, Sudhir Dehkane
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Document fraud has evolved into a technically sophisticated threat, involving high-resolution print–scan recapture, selective region manipulation, and adversarial rewriting. This work presents a multimodal deep architecture for robust forgery detection that integrates visual texture cues, structural edge patterns, and textual semantics. The system employs an MobileNet-based visual backbone augmented with CBAM and a lightweight ASPP module to capture fine-grained manipulation artifacts. Parallel pathways model structural inconsistencies via edge analysis and linguistic deviations through OCR embeddings, targeting splicing and font substitution attacks respectively. The three modalities are fused through a normalized integration block and classified using an MLP head. Experiments conducted on a 10k-image IDNet-Estonia-derived corpus demonstrate an accuracy of 97.8 and an AUC of 0.9992 across seven fraud categories. Qualitative visualizations further confirm the model’s ability to localize manipulation artifacts, significantly outperforming unimodal baselines in complex attack scenarios.

title: EVM Testing Method for Programmable RF Transceivers
authors: Shuheng Lin, Xuewu Li, Cenyue Zhang, Fan Zhang, Lang Zeng
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Error Vector Magnitude (EVM) is a pivotal metric for evaluating the modulation quality and comprehensive performance of digital receivers. As programmable Radio Frequency (RF) transceivers evolve toward System-on-Chip (SoC) architectures-where RF analog front-ends and digital basebands are deeply integrated-traditional testing methods relying on external Vector Signal Analyzers (VSA) face significant challenges, such as inaccessible digital interfaces and fragmented testing links. This paper proposes an FPGA-based on-chip EVM selftest method. By constructing a virtual VSA and Vector Signal Generator (VSG) within the system, a complete workflow for QAM modulation/demodulation and EVM calculation is established. Through the optimization of shaping filter parameters and improvements in timing and carrier synchronization algorithms, the proposed method achieves high-precision measurement while maintaining hardware resource efficiency. Experimental results demonstrate that this method offers excellent stability and engineering feasibility, providing a low-cost, deployable solution for the performance verification of highly integrated RFSoCs.

title: DelowlightSplat: Feed-Forward Gaussian Splatting for Lowlight 3D Scene Reconstruction
authors: Fuzhen Jiang, Zengtian Xie, Zhuoran Li
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Novel-view synthesis and 3D reconstruction from sparse posed images are central to robotics and AR/VR. Yet, feed-forward 3D Gaussian reconstruction fails under lowlight due to noise, color shifts, and unreliable correspondence. We propose DelowlightSplat, a lowlight-aware feed-forward Gaussian splatting framework for clean novel-view rendering. We build a controllable multi-view lowlight benchmark by degrading only context views while keeping target views clean. We introduce a lightweight Lowlight Adapter for residual enhancement to improve matchability, and couple it with cost-volume-based multi-view inference to directly predict clean 3D Gaussians. Experiments show that DelowlightSplat significantly outperforms previous feed-forward method and two-stage pipeline under lowlight conditions.

title: Multi-Objective Scheduling for Large Language Model Inference with Prompt-Level Cost Prediction and SLO Awareness
authors: Jiajing Liao, Feng Chang, Yihan Xue, Tianjian Xia, Zeyu Huang, Yuxiao Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Large language model (LLM) inference in multitenant clouds is becoming an increasingly important contributor to data-center carbon emissions, yet existing carbon-aware scheduling techniques target long-running training jobs and are illsuited for the short, bursty, SLO-sensitive nature of online serving. We propose CAPS (Carbon-Aware Prompt Scheduling), an online bi-objective scheduler that jointly optimizes goodput and perrequest carbon cost for multi-tenant LLM inference. CAPS first employs a lightweight prompt complexity predictor to estimate token generation cost and latency risk for each incoming request. It then combines real-time grid carbon intensity, GPU energy profiles, and per-tenant SLO tiers to route each request to one of three execution pools: a low-latency pool, a low-carbon pool, or a delay-tolerant batch pool. A composite reward function balances goodput, carbon emissions, and SLO violation rate. In trace-driven simulations using public conversation traces and regional carbon intensity data, CAPS reduces average carbon emissions per <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 K}$</tex> generated tokens by 26.8 % compared to round-robin scheduling while achieving an SLO attainment rate that matches or exceeds a dedicated SLO-aware baseline.

title: LangChain Orchestrated Ecosystem for Token-Aware LLM Routing, Hybrid Semantic Retrieval and Explainable Conversational Automation with Dynamic Data Handling
authors: Hanumanth Sastry Sistla, Bahubali Shiragapur, Vinay R, Keshav Sairam, Akshath Vijay Kumar, Karan A
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: With the increasing complexity of institutional workflows and information management, there is a growing need for intelligent platforms that can adapt to dynamic data requirements and ensure secure, scalable interactions. In our work, we have used FastAPI for high-performance backend services, replacing Flask to accommodate asynchronous operations and robust API handling. FastAPI leverages LangChain for orchestration of advanced chains and agent-based workflows, enabling Robotic Process Automation (RPA) in response generation using prioritized AI-Based hybrid semantic data retrieval System. A conditional logic pipeline has been implemented wherein the token size of retrieved context determines the processing route: if the token count is below a defined threshold, lightweight LLMs such as Llama 3 accessed via externally hosted APIs through a unified LLM wrapper are used for inference. For larger token contexts, data is first distilled through older models like GPT-2 or BART before being passed to newer LLMs, thus improving both efficiency and answer quality. Additionally, tools such as LangSmith have been integrated to streamline debugging, evaluation, and prompt optimization. Furthermore, the memory module within LangChain is leveraged to enable reinforcement learning-based adaptation and iterative performance improvements.Evaluations show the hybrid LLM pipeline achieved a BLEU score of 91.2, ROUGE score of 88.4, and precision of 90.1, with user upvote rates reaching 96% and fallback rates as low as 2%. The adoption of FastAPI contributed to significantly reduced response times under concurrent user loads. The LangSmith-integrated feedback loop accelerated prompt refinement and error reduction across sessions. This work contributes to the field by demonstrating a generalized framework for intelligent academic automation, integrating conditional LLM routing, explainable evaluation, and modular RPA pipelines for institutional applications.

title: On What GPT-Style Initialization Contributes to Sensor-Only Traffic Forecasting a Parameter-Matched Case Study on Two PEMS Benchmarks
authors: Hongbo Zhao
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Recent traffic forecasters initialized from GPT-style checkpoints report gains in low-label settings, but the source of those gains remains unclear. In the sensor-only setup, text is absent at inference time; a text-pretrained Transformer is used primarily as initialization for a numerical sequence model. We revisit this setting under a parameter-matched protocol where backbone, tokenizer, prediction head, optimizer, and compute budget are fixed, and only initialization varies: GPT-2, random Xavier, or self-supervised pretraining on unlabeled traffic windows. Across PEMS04 and PEMS08, covering full-shot forecasting, few-shot learning, cross-dataset adaptation, horizon-wise error analysis, and missing-observation stress tests, GPT-2 initialization consistently improves over random initialization. At the same time, traffic-domain pretraining is stronger in most settings and deteriorates more gradually under corruption. The results suggest that, within the sensor-only setting studied here, a substantial part of the gain associated with GPT-style initialization may come from pretraining itself-especially when the pretraining task is aligned with spatio-temporal structure-rather than from language-specific knowledge.
