To improve realism, the ground regions are identified, and the foreground is appropriately scaled down and placed correctly. Experimental results show that this approach improves narrative consistency across multiple backgrounds and also improves reusability in contrast to conventional image generation models. The proposed framework demonstrates the effectiveness of multimodal pipelines for applications such as screenplay visualization, digital storytelling, and visual content generation.

title: EV Battery Health Prediction and Personalized Recommendations using LightGBM and SHAP
authors: Aditya K, Arya Bhat, Samarth G Ghodke, Satish Chikkamath, Kaushik M B, Suneeta V Budihal
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Electric Vehicles (EVs) are the best application of Renewable energy. Lithium-ion batteries (LIBs) are the heart of EVs. The ability to predict battery health has great importance in the field of battery technology and is a challenging task. By taking EV battery parameters as input, we can calculate the battery health via Machine Learning (ML) techniques, and to take this a step further, we take inputs from EV users and give them personalized recommendations based on their driving style and the climate of the area where they reside, for reliable and proper battery management. The dataset was created synthetically, keeping the public dataset as a base for the ML model to function in the worst-case scenario in real-life situations. The performance of our model is meticulously tested, and we got an accuracy that is accurate enough for practical use cases. We also tested the model on a hardware device for its robustness in real life. Index Terms—EV battery, Lithium-ion battery, Machine Learning, SoH, LightGBM

title: Smart Healthcare Prediction Using Hybrid Machine Learning And Fuzzy Logic Systems
authors: R Ramachandran, Vinothkumar C, G.Manikandan, Shruthi B S, Vibhavi R N, Rajashree M Byalal
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: In smart healthcare systems, fuzzy logic and machine learning together provide a breakthrough approach for predictive analytics. This paper introduces a novel hybrid model merging Adaptive Neuro-Fuzzy Inference System (ANFIS) for enhanced decision-making under uncertainty with Random Forest for feature selection and classification. Combining expert-like thinking with data-driven learning, the model is meant to highly accurately and interpretably predict patient health issues. Testing the proposed system uses actual healthcare data including patient symptoms, lab results, and historical diagnoses. Early illness identification, risk prediction, and therapy recommendations all show better performance than more traditional models. By means of managing problems like unbalanced data, noisy inputs, and non-linear interactions, the hybrid system increases prediction accuracy and resilience. This method makes real-time, customized healthcare information feasible, which improves proactive patient treatment and resource management by letting the results show great potential for expanded implementation in systems of smart clinical decision assistance.

title: Zero-Trust Architecture for Hybrid Cloud Security in Critical Infrastructures
authors: Rachakonda Uma, Mounika Maggidi, Anurag Reddy Ekkati, Sharmila Bandlamudi, Chiranjeevisantosh Madugundi, Sai Kiran Taduri
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The extreme usage of hybrid cloud environment in very vital industry like energy, healthcare, transport has increased the complexity of cyber-attacks caused by distributed assets and inappropriate security controls. Older security models that rely on perimeter security models do not secure the contemporary attacks that make use of internal trust assumptions. This research is based on a Zero-Trust Architecture (ZTA) design applied to the hybrid cloud setup based on the combination of public (Microsoft Azure) and private (VMware vSphere) clouds. The proposed system incorporates identity-based access control, micro-segmentation, which is software-defined, and on-going monitoring with the help of SIEM and EDR. Quantitative performance of a six-month trial deployment in a simulated critical infrastructure setting demonstrates that an impressive security improvement took place: the Mean Time to Detect (MTTD) was cut down 8 hours to 45 minutes (82% reduction), attempted unauthorized access attempts dropped by 89% and NIST 800-53 compliance had improved by 63 to 91%. Also, lateral movement incidents were reduced by 90 by the system, which offered a high level of withholding of insider and external threats. These results prove that the zero-trust architecture can successfully enhance hybrid cloud resiliency, improve visibility, and implement dynamic access control in the most significant infrastructural processes.

title: IoT Based Smart Safety Mask for Real-Time Chemical Monitoring in Industrial Environments
authors: Dhana Sekar V, Dhayananth B, Kavin Kumar S, Hariharan R
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: In industrial workplaces exposure to hazardous chemicals is a major concern, posing risks to the health and safety of the workers. The traditional personal protective equipment (PPE) has limited protection for the workers and lacks real-time monitoring of air quality and emergency alerts. This paper introduces an IoT-enabled smart safety mask system designed to continuously monitor airborne toxins and environmental conditions for workers in industry. The developed system uses an ESP32 microcontroller for processing and the sensing unit is integrated with chemical and environmental sensors including MQ-135 and MQ-2 modules for detecting gases like ammonia, volatile organic compounds, carbon monoxide and methane, a PM2.5 sensor for monitoring particulate matter in the air and a BME680 sensor for temperature and relative humidity measurement. To alert the workers in emergencies, a buzzer and a vibration actuator are incorporated with the hardware. In addition to local alerts, data is continuously logged to a cloud platform for real-time remote monitoring and further detailed analysis. The proposed system offers a scalable and cost-effective way to enhance the industrial safety of workers.

title: Kubeflow for Healthcare: Zero-Touch Model Retraining in HIPAA-Compliant Clouds
authors: Shalmali Joshi, Ramkumar Raju Djeabalaradjou, Rama Krishna Kumar Lingamgunta
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Healthcare AI systems learn from previous data sets and models. This leads to a slow but continuous change in past data. These changes affect the accuracy and reliability of the model with time, which results in needing a retrain often. They require a lot of resource, even in HIPAA settings, and manually retraining them is lengthy and inconsistent. An architecture for a fully automated zero-touch model retraining framework using cloud-based HIPAA, Kubeflow and Kubernetes-native MLOps components is proposed in this paper. We propose an all-in-house solution that includes safe FHIR/DICOM uptake, statistical drift examinations, auto hyper parameter optimisation, robust governance check-ups on ML model performance and GitOps based deployment. Testing produced excellent AUROC improvement (up to 9.4%) on CT/MRI/CXR/EHR/large scale datasets and end to end training time 7x faster than before without loss of performance under varying patient loads. The system works accurately and reliably; that is, it has a 98.4% compliance success rate. The findings support that the proposed framework makes a clinically applicable solution for the continuous-learning AI systems in a safe and scalable manner.

title: GeomeTrace: Development of a Mobile Application for Teaching Geometric Figures to Learners with Visual Impairments in the Philippines
authors: Julius Christopher Lim Doolittle, Frederick Toralballa Talaue
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 9th International Conference on Education and E-Learning
abstract: None

title: Quantum-Aware Noise Modeling for Coherent ZR/ZR+ Optical Links Using QuTiP and OptiSystem: A Giant Step Toward Optical Digital Twins
authors: Praveen Kumar Pal
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Coherent pluggable transceivers such as 400ZR, 800ZR, and ZR+ have emerged as essential enablers for modern data-center interconnects (DCI), offering high-capacity transmission over metro and regional distances. Traditional simulation methods rely predominantly on classical stochastic models for fiber impairments, amplified spontaneous emission (ASE), phase noise, and Digital Signal Processor (DSP) distortions. However, as symbol rates exceed 100 Gbaud and OSNR margins continue to shrink, several noise processes begin exhibiting distinct quantum mechanical characteristics that classical approximations cannot accurately capture. This paper introduces a Quantum-Aware Noise Modeling Framework that integrates QuTiP (Quantum Toolbox in Python) with OptiSystem to simulate coherent ZR/ZR+(ZR Standards) optical links under low-Optical Signal to Noise Ratio (OSNR) and high-order modulation regimes. The hybrid simulation approach incorporates density-matrix evolution, quantum-limited amplification, operator-based phase diffusion, vacuum fluctuations, and photon-number uncertainty into a classical coherent transmission workflow. Experimental analysis demonstrates that quantum-aware modeling yields significantly improved accuracy in Bit Error Rate (BER), Quality(Q)-factor, and constellation behavior prediction, especially in quantum-dominant noise regimes. The proposed methodology provides a foundation for next-generation transceiver co-design and quantum-enhanced coherent DSP algorithms. This approach is the first giant step towards creating the next-generation ultrahigh-speed digital twins for faster Optical product development.

title: Multi-Task Graph Attention Network for Kinematic Rehabilitation Exercise Analysis
authors: Pragati Priyadarshinee, Vinith Reddy Banda, Shafaat Siddiqui, Kunal Amgire
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Physical rehabilitation assessment faces critical challenges, including therapist shortages, subjective evaluations, and inability to detect early disease indicators from movement patterns. Spatial-temporal motion is effectively modelled by contemporary deep models, especially graph convolutional and recurrent architectures. However, these models are primarily single-task and provide limited information about which joints or time segments affect predictions. On benchmarks such as Kinematic Assessment of Movement and Clinical Scores for Remote Monitoring of Physical Rehabilitation (KIMORE) and University of Idaho – Physical Rehabilitation Movement Dataset (UI-PRMD), this restricts clinical trust and cross-dataset generalization. This study proposes a Multi-Task Graph Attention Network and examines graph-based, attention-enabled, and hybrid spatial-temporal approaches for skeleton-driven rehabilitation evaluation. To concurrently perform disease classification and exercise quality estimation from KIMORE and UI-PRMD, Multi-Task Graph Attention Network (MT-GAT) combines lightweight geometric features, adaptive joint level attention, and a shared temporal encoder. Additionally, it supports the development of clinically beneficial automated evaluation tools by offering joint-time saliency visualizations to help clinicians interpret the data.

title: Edge-Intelligent Blockchain Framework for Federated Privacy-Preserving Medical Diagnostics
authors: M. Lakshmanan, Gaurav Londhe, Sriramkumar R, Mude Nagarjuna Naik, Joshuva Arockia Dhanraj, Neeraj Jain, Shreyas Rajendra Hole
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Rapid advancements in technology have had a significant impact on the world's health care system. Among these, the innovative devices of the Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) and the first ever artificially intelligent diagnostic systems have made it easier to keep up with the healthcare industry, with data privacy, latency, and trust remaining as the most critical challenges. In support of the aforementioned, the discussion below will describe the Edge Intelligent Blockchain Framework (EIBF), which is considered to form a unique solution formed by federated learning (FL), edge computing, and a purpose-built blockchain. Here, the hospital edge nodes join to create an AI model through the background electronic health record (EHR) data that is only for their own usage without keeping or sharing the original and sensitive data. Then, once the user’s data has been protected and only the AI model has been passed on, smart contracts on the blockchain will validate the encrypted model updates kept on the immutably secured ledger. The framework will come up with a POA consensus mechanism optimized by energy consumption (A PBFT) that can agree on and execute messages in a timely, reliable, and fault-free way even at a fraction of the cost compared to other systems that are not energy-optimized. The final application outcome of the investigation using the MIMIC III and PhysioNet databases was that the edge-accelerated EIBF approach is at the 96.4% classification accuracy level and is able to offer a more reliable and faster model convergence time by 35% as well as a 40% renewable energy consumption than the conventional FL systems. Thus, these findings provide evidence for the establishment of EIBF as a new and improved secure and intelligent healthcare network by protecting the data, enabling its transparent audit, and guaranteeing the provision of the real-time diagnostics.

title: Blockchain-Based Cybersecurity Solutions for Decentralized Network Management
authors: Bharathi Ravishankar, S. Sivagami, M. Vijaya Maheswari, V Sujatha, Pappala Balaakrishna, Dinesh Babu M
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Cybersecurity solutions that are based on blockchain are developing into a revolutionary method of an increase in safeguarding of a decentralized network management. Conventional centralised security systems are not always suitable in distributed infrastructures because of the single point of failure, data manipulation, lack of transparency and much more. Due to its irreversible records, consensus systems, and distributed confirmation, cryptocurrency will provide a higher degree of credibility and resilience against an ever-evolving array of online dangers. The use of smart agreements for remote management and artificially intelligent identification of anomalies strengthen privacy by making auditing data patterns and storage immutable. Additionally, cryptography software provides security for personal information in dissimilar settings, and decentralised management of identities provide identification that is favourable to confidentiality. It becomes even more impervious to distributed denial of service (DDoS) and info quality intrusions when cryptocurrency is coupled with advanced protection technologies, which removes the possibility of insider’s assaults. Moreover, bitcoin's democratic, accessible, and verified protections might enable distributed platforms to safely self-regulate, fostering responsibility and trustworthiness in vital sectors like medical care, insurance, and logistics systems. This combination is a major step towards building contemporary safety systems that can withstand and react to new threats. Under normal circumstances, the suggested system had a detection accuracy of 93% and under stress, it had an audit trust score of 90%.

title: Early Risk Prediction and Insurance Plan-Aware Referral for Diabetic Readmissions using Machine Learning
authors: B.Rajalakshmi, Apurva P V, Annapoorni M, Bavatharini R
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Diabetes mellitus is a common cause of frequent hospital readmissions due to chronic sequelae and progressive metabolic challenges, causing both clinical burdens and economic impact on the health care system. Traditional prediction frameworks with rules tend to be both inaccurate and do not accounting for the economic aspect of patient management based on different types of insurance plans. The current work proposes a machine-learning based approach for predictive identification of diabetic readmissions, while also preserving a novel insurance-aware guidance for effective healthcare delivery. An analytical foundation of integrated Electronic Health Records, inclusive of demographic, pathological, comorbidity, and treatment regimens, previous admissions, and insurance characteristics. An ensemble approach that unites Random Forest and XGBoost displays commendable prediction performance, achieving almost 89% for risk assessment. The framework provides recommendations for personalized treatment pathways based on medical susceptibility and financial risk, including teleconsultations, outpatient follow-up, structured inpatient coordination or insurance plan modifications (prediction: at an individual level). The output interface for clinicians and beneficiaries is streamlined to facilitate intuitive interpretation of risk severity, recommended clinical actions, and cost impact. The study puts forth a dual-purpose strategy targeting both health outcomes and the cost, by integrating predictive modeling with insurance-aware advisory mechanisms. This proposed system shows a contribution to the future of proactive diabetic care by exercising informed decision-making, avoiding unnecessary admissions, and emphasizing the value of health care management.

title: Development of STM32 based controller board for Power Electronics Converters
authors: Ritika Asati, Vedashree Mahajan, Aary Angre
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The presented methodology describes the design and implementation of a controller board generating six pulse width modulation (PWM) signals using an STM32 microcontroller. The system provides a low-cost and flexible alternative to dedicated PWM driver ICs, particularly for applications requiring precise phase-shifted gating signals. The STM32 microcontroller is employed to generate four hardware PWM outputs using its internal timers, while the remaining two phase-shifted signals are derived through a combinational logic circuit that compensates for the limited number of available PWM channels. The proposed system allows real-time adjustment of PWM frequency and duty cycle through an analog interface using a potentiometer. The design is validated using both simulation and hardware implementation, with results confirming accurate phase alignment, stable duty-cycle control, and reliable waveform generation across the operating range. The methodology demonstrates an efficient and scalable approach for producing multi-phase PWM signals using minimal external circuitry, making it suitable for prototyping and various low-power power electronics converter applications.

title: Online Learning-Based Beamforming for Time-Varying Near-Field XL-MIMO Channels
authors: Jinsong Ruan, Hongxia Miao
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Deep learning beamforming for near-field XLMIMO is typically trained offline and degrades under time-varying channels. We propose a supervised online adaptation framework with an experience replay buffer to update the beamforming network stably without exploration overhead. Simulations show consistent gains over a fixed offline model under abrupt channel distribution changes.

title: A Comparative Analysis of Business-Driven Variational Autoencoder Models for Stable Customer Segmentation in E-Commerce
authors: Rakshita Janadri, Srushti Savadi, Devika Desai, Vinay Talekar, Guruprasad S Konnurmath, Channabasappa Muttal
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: E-commerce platforms generate huge streams of behavioral and transactional data. Therefore, sharp customer segmentation becomes of critical importance in personalizing and guiding marketing efforts. However, conventional methods of clustering are likely to fail when capturing nonlinear behavioral patterns and provide unstable fragile segments. In this regard, the present work contrasts three VAE-based architectures for unsupervised customer segmentation: lean, optimized baseline VAE; business-oriented, enriched with domain-specific features VAE; and a more advanced VAE that combines feature prioritization with stability-aware clustering. All these models will be assessed within one unique experimental framework regarding silhouette score, Davies–Bouldin index, Calinski–Harabasz score, and Adjusted Rand Index. The results point out that enhanced VAE gives the most coherent and repeatable clusters, outperforming baseline models in terms of separation quality and stability. That is, fusing business-driven features with deep representation learning secures actionable and more reliable customer segments for real-world e-commerce personalization.

title: TabNet-Driven Efficient DDoS Detection for High-Dimensional Network Traffic
authors: G. Kirubavathi, Bharat Kesav, Venkateswaran Radhakrishnan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The increasing complexity of Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks poses a significant problem to protecting Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) systems, which are still vulnerable due to limited computational resources and different communication protocols. This article presents a TabNet-based DDoS detection framework that uses the CICDDoS2019 dataset for training and evaluation. The system uses extensive preprocessing, feature engineering, and time-series analysis, as well as TabNet’s dynamic feature selection and interpretability, to improve anomaly identification. Experimental results show that the suggested system outperforms state-of-the-art approaches, with a 99% detection accuracy, fewer false positives, and greater computational efficiency. The system’s low latency and excellent precision make it ideal for real-time deployment in IoMT situations. By combining quantitative and qualitative studies, this study emphasizes the framework’s adaptability to developing cybersecurity risks. Future research seeks to use hybrid techniques to increase detection accuracy and scalability in dynamic and resource-constrained IoMT environments.

title: Smart Complaint Routing Systems for Public Issues Using Multimodal Artificial Intelligence
authors: Mary Snowfa A, Shrimathi K, Nithya K
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Manual complaint categorization and wrong routing to the municipal department causes delay in public grievance redressal mechanisms in the administration of urban local bodies. Inhabitants are encouraged to use online applications for potholes, waste collection or water and power outages, but inefficient management hampers the effectiveness of such systems. In this paper the Smart Complaint Routing System for Public Issues using multimodal AI is being implemented. Thev proposed system scrutinizes textual complaints as well as the input images to determine the nature of complaint automatically. An image classification is performed with the help of Convolutional Neural Network and semantic features are derived from complaint text using natural language processing model. The two model outputs are then integrated using the popular multimodal fusion technique to enhance classification performance. Depending on the predictor category, complaints are then automatically sent to relevant government department. Experimental results demonstrate that it has highly improved the performance of both single-modal methods, and reduce much misrouting as well as much CPU time. The system improves efficiency, scalability, and user satisfaction for smart city governance.

title: Comprehensive Approach to Cloud Security Using Trusted Mobile Cloud and Computing Technologies
authors: Vishal Mehra, M. Prashanthi, Amit Sahu, M Ramesh Naik, Seema Raj, Anup Singh Negi
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The limitations of cell phones can be addressed by mobile cloud computing (MCC), but it does not provide the requisite degree of security and processing performance assurances. In this part, we offer a hybrid approach to trustworthy MCC by integrating security precautions with efficient probabilities monogrammed computing. The proposed approach enhances computing performance with a better keyword searching technique and strengthens MCC security with authorization and authentication. An innovative stochastic monogrammed computing approach is developed for achieving efficient and accurate retrieval of information by utilizing indexed synonyms searching using weighted terms and rankings for keywords. Using a password-based authorised exchange of keys mechanism using Chaotic Maps additionally offers safe authentication and access control. Relevant multi-keyword-based and fuzzy keyword-based search methods were found to be inferior to the proposed Efficient Computation along with Authentication and Authorization in Mobile Cloud Computing (ECAAMC) model in terms of time to computation, efficiency, and performance. The results show that ECAAMC provides a safer computing environment, which enhances data security, boosts search performance, and decreases destructive behaviour. This hybrid approach provides a solid framework for safeguarding and improving MCC, making it a viable choice for scenarios involving mobility and limited resources. Outperforming FKWBS and MKWBS, the suggested ECAAMC method achieves the best accuracy at 93.2%.

title: An Optimized Framework for E-catalog Categorization in Large-Scale Heterogeneous Datasets
authors: Meenal N. Pande, Sanjay E. Yedey
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: In the realm of digital commerce, product classification stands as a pivotal determinant of organizational success, particularly given the escalating volume of data, imperative for enhanced customer experience, and optimized operational efficiencies. Persistent challenges to efficient classification stem from the inherent complexity of product-associated data and the dynamic nature of market environments. This research provides a comparative analysis of these classificatory impediments, detailing methodologies and technologies employed to address issues such as data inconsistencies, high-dimensional data, and dynamic attribute variability. Furthermore, the study investigates the impact of data integration and real-time processing on product classification outcomes, quantifying the correlative strength between these factors and classification efficacy. The proposed approach leverages Python software, integrating K-Means clustering to enhance classification by grouping products based on salient features like price and ratings. A Hybrid Feedforward-Recurrent Network (HFRN) model is introduced, demonstrating superior accuracy and reliability in categorizing products (e.g., 'Premium Electronics,' 'Trendy Fashion Items') through feature extraction and sequence modeling applied to pre-clustered data. This hybrid methodology achieves a 99% accuracy rate, outperforming conventional models such as Fully Connected Neural Networks, Support Vector Machines, and Logistic Regression by 3.39%.

title: Generative Augmentation via Balanced Subset Ensembles for Imbalanced Tabular Data
authors: Peng Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Imbalanced tabular data is a common challenge in many real-world applications, where minority classes are severely underrepresented. Existing generative models such as CTGAN and TVAE often struggle in these scenarios, as training on highly imbalanced datasets leads to majority-dominated distributions and poor modeling of minority patterns. To address this issue, we propose a Balanced Subset Generative Ensemble (BSGE) framework that constructs multiple balanced training subsets by undersampling the majority class and combining them with the complete minority set. Independent generative models are trained on each subset, and their outputs are aggregated to generate diverse and informative minority samples. Experiments on eight imbalanced tabular datasets demonstrate that BSGE effectively enhances minority representation and significantly improves downstream classification performance.

title: Swarm Robotics Intelligence for Autonomous and Adaptive Infrastructure Monitoring and Optimization in Artificial Intelligence Powered Smart Urban Cities
authors: Vivekanand Kuriyal, Poonam Pandey, Girija Shankar Dewangan, Anand Goswami, Ravi G, Anshu Malhotra
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: A notion that has recently gained traction in contemporary urban studies is the "smart city," a kind of urban planning that is autonomous, fully networked, and able to adapt to its environment. Here, swarm robotics offers a highly rich perspective from which to examine the optimization and monitoring of autonomous and adaptable infrastructure. Our approach uses decentralized cooperative robotic agents to examine and repair key infrastructure including bridges, highways, and energy networks in real time using our collective innate intelligences. By integrating machine learning with multi-agent systems, swarms may self-organize to respond to abnormalities in infrastructure and environmental changes, ensuring continuous and robust operations. We self-organize, are fault-tolerant, and learn continually using sensory data and AI. Innovative ideas include an adaptive swarm coordination protocol for complicated urban environments and a resource and task optimization model. Together, they improve system efficiency, save maintenance costs, and strengthen the city. Smart cities' scalable, sustainable, and proactive urban infrastructures are modeled in the present model using AI and swarm robots.

title: Prediction of Energy Consumption Patterns of Smart Buildings Based on Data Mining Algorithm
authors: Xinpei Hu
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: With escalating global energy demand and the pressing need for sustainable development, optimizing smart building energy management—a critical domain of energy consumption—has become imperative.However, traditional energy management methods are difficult to accurately grasp the complex patterns and changing trends of building energy consumption, resulting in low energy utilization efficiency. Leveraging data mining algorithms, this study first transforms and normalizes smart building energy consumption data, followed by an in-depth analysis of energy usage patterns to construct a predictive model for energy demand. Through experimental analysis of actual smart building energy data, the determination coefficient (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) of the adopted XGBoost model is 0.95, which is better than the R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>=0.82 of the traditional ARIMA model. The research results provide effective technical support for smart buildings to achieve efficient energy management and precise regulation.

title: Study of Access Region Sheet Resistance in Superlattice U-GaN HEMT and its Compact Modeling
authors: Vikram Magendar, Hiu Yung Wong
publish: 2026 IEEE Workshop on Microelectronics and Electron Devices (WMED)
abstract: Superlattice GaN structures are expected to have reduced on-state resistance due to the formation of multiple twodimensional electron gas (2DEG) channels in the access regions. The U-GaN structure, which has one channel in the gate region for normally-off operation, employs regrown Si-doped vertical GaN sidewalls to electrically connect multiple channels in the access regions. In this paper, an access region with thin superlattice layers, which is expected to have less relaxation and dislocations than the thicker one, is studied. Technology Computer-Aided Design (TCAD) simulations confirm the formation of multiple polarization-induced 2DEG channels. It is found that the equivalent access-region sheet resistance remains nearly constant for different numbers of channels, resulting in similar drain currents at linear regime, while enhanced current is only observed in multiple channels in the saturation regime. This is due to strong electrostatic coupling between the layers. A compact model is developed to model the behaviors based on the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) Virtual Source GaNFET HighVoltage (MVSG-HV) model. To fit the TCAD data, a drain-voltage-dependent threshold-voltage shift needs to be introduced in the access-region transistors due to the nature of the model, in which the access region’s effective “gate voltage” is calculated by the 2DEG sheet resistance. Moreover, it is also found that hole tunneling needs to be turned on in the source/drain Schottky barriers to allow accurate TCAD simulations, likely to avoid isolated holes.

title: A Dual-Mode Cryptosystem Based on AES-256 a Key Generated by GEFFE to Secure Images.
authors: Riad Saidi, Nada Cherrid, Tarek Bentahar
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: The increasing development of telecommunications and the need for secure transfer of multimedia data have led to the utilization of a multitude of encryption algorithms to ensure data security. To meet security criteria such as confidentiality, data integrity, and prevention of unauthorized data usage across commercial, scientific, and military contexts, numerous encryption algorithms have been developed in recent years. Given the heavy utilization of image data in various fields (medical, industrial, etc.), it is imperative to employ and adapt these security techniques to such data types.

title: AI-Driven Traffic Congestion Prediction and Adaptive Signal Control Using Spatial–Temporal Graph Neural Networks
authors: G N Karthik Rajan, S Kabilan, K Nithya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Traffic congestion forecast and adaptive signal control have been a significant problem in modern intelligent transportation systems due to the high rate of urbanisation and road congestion. The current paper presents an AI-based approach that integrates Spatial-Temporal Graph Neural Networks (ST-GNN) with reinforcement-learning-managed adaptive signal control to improve traffic management performance. The road network is modelled as a graph to capture spatial correlations among interconnected intersections, and temporal information is derived from past and present traffic data. The model is compared against benchmark datasets, such as METR-LA, PeMSD7, and PeMS-Bay, and validated in the SUMO traffic simulation environment. The experimental findings demonstrate that the ST-GNN model achieves superior predictive performance, with Mean Absolute Error (MAE) and Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) of 2.31 and 4.08, respectively, outperforming traditional models such as ARIMA, Random Forest, and LSTM. Further, the adaptive signal control module reduces the average time vehicles wait by 32%, the queue length by 27%, and the intersection throughput by 18% compared to fixed-time control strategies. The findings show that the suggested framework offers an effective, scalable and practical solution to real-time urban traffic congestion management.

title: Remote Sensing-Based LULC Classification of Bangladesh Using Google Earth Engine and Machine Learning
authors: Anika Tahsin, Muntasir Mohammad Shoeb, Shahriar Hossain, Md. Rashid Awal Chowdhury, Jannatul Ferdous, Dil Afroza Karim, Arafat Uddin
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Land Use and Land Cover (LULC) classification in developing nations such as Bangladesh presents significant challenges, including limited data quality, constrained computational resources, and the complexity of processing extensive datasets. Despite the importance of this task, effective methodologies for classifying the vast geographic expanse of Bangladesh remain insufficiently explored, resulting in a knowledge gap regarding its landscape. This study addresses this gap by identifying the most suitable classification technique for nationwide application in Bangladesh. We propose a methodological framework that enables efficient processing of Sentinel-2 imagery within Google Earth Engine (GEE) by integrating a novel combination of spectral and topographic variables specific to Bangladesh. The performance of Gradient Tree Boost (GTB) was assessed alongside commonly employed algorithms, including Random Forest (RF), Support Vector Machine (SVM), and Classification and Regression Trees (CART), using a limited training dataset. The study classified the landscape into six categories: waterbody, wetland, agriculture, forest, bareland, and built-up areas. Evaluation of classifier performance employed metrics such as Overall Accuracy (OA), Kappa coefficient, Producer’s Accuracy (PA), and User’s Accuracy (UA). The Random Forest algorithm achieved superior results, with an OA of 93.8% and a Kappa coefficient of 0.92. These results highlight the effectiveness of integrating machine learning algorithms with GEE to address the inherent challenges of large-scale LULC classification in Bangladesh.

title: Stacking Ensemble Classification Model for Network Intrusion Detection
authors: Md. Taukir Ahmed, Md. Showrov Hossen
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: The rapid evolution of technology and the increasing need for interconnected systems have brought significant challenges in securing digital infrastructures. To mitigate security risks and prevent damage to information systems, Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) have become essential tools. These systems help identify malicious activities and vulnerabilities within network environments. As network communication continues to grow, so does the frequency and complexity of network intrusions, making effective intrusion detection a critical concern. Network intrusions are typically characterized by unusual or suspicious behavior in data traffic. In recent years, machine learning techniques have been widely explored for their ability to detect such anomalies. Among them, ensemble learning models have shown promising results due to their improved prediction capabilities. This research focuses on developing an efficient IDS using a Stacking ensemble method, which combines multiple machine learning algorithms to enhance detection accuracy. The proposed model integrates several base classifiers, including Logistic Regression (LR), KNearest Neighbors (KNN), Support Vector Machine (SVM), Decision Tree (DT), Random Forest (RF), and Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP). Experiments were carried out on the NSLKDD dataset, and the performance of the ensemble model was compared with individual classifiers. The findings indicate that the Stacking-based IDS achieves superior results, with a high accuracy of 98.73% and a low false alarm rate of 0.021, demonstrating its effectiveness in identifying network intrusions more accurately than traditional approaches.

title: Optimized Elevator Systems Group Control Using Genetic Algorithms
authors: Zaid Jaber, Awos Kanan
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: Nearly every standing building has at least one elevator functioning around the clock, excessively used by people going into a building, transporting between floors, and going out. Many round trips per hour are accomplished by elevators, and many loading and unloading are made to these systems. For that, a huge field of research and continuous developments are carried out in elevator traffic design. The challenge in making elevator systems more efficient and reliable in the up-peak (the traffic at its maximum) is to manipulate the dominant parameters that affect the performance, such as the round trip time, car capacity, speed, acceleration, and jerk. The adjustments and the designs of these variables, whether by statistics, probability, or other engineering methods, are the key to solving elevator traffic problems and making them more efficient and reliable. In this paper, a genetic algorithm optimization approach is proposed for the Elevator Group Control problem. Experimental results show that the proposed metaheuristic approach achieves enhanced performance compared to existing heuristic approaches that are developed by experience, and trial and error, and analytical modeling.

title: Robust Off-Grid DOA Estimation Algorithm in Complex Non-Gaussian Environment
authors: Zhiye Han
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Aiming at the two inherent limitations of traditional sparse Bayesian learning (SBL) in DOA estimation-Gaussian noise assumption and grid mismatch, this paper proposes a joint optimization framework that integrates off-grid modeling and robust maximum likelihood estimation. Firstly, it is hypothesized that the array data are assumed to follow the complex elliptic symmetry (CES) distribution family, and a generalized maximum likelihood objective function is constructed by introducing a parameterized loss function to realize the adaptive weight reduction of abnormal snapshots. Secondly, the first-order Taylor expansion is used to parameterize the steering vector off-grid, and the grid deviation <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\beta$</tex> is used as a continuous variable for joint optimization. On this basis, an alternating iterative algorithm is derived to update signal power <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\gamma$</tex>, grid deviation <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\beta$</tex> and noise variance <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${\sigma}^{\mathrm{2}}$</tex> simultaneously. The simulation results show that the root mean square error (RMSE) of the proposed method can closely approximate the Cramer-Rao lower bound (CRB) under the conditions of heavy-tailed noise and coarse grid, which is significantly better than the traditional on-grid method and the conventional off-grid method.

title: Super-Twisting Sliding Mode Control of an ARVIN Bidirectional DC–DC Converter for V2G/G2V Applications
authors: MARZOUG Marouan, GHAFFARI Fakhreddine, JOUILI Mabrouk, BACHA Faouzi
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: The widespread deployment of electric vehicles (EVs) and the increasing penetration of renewable energy sources are driving the development of advanced power electronic interfaces capable of bidirectional energy exchange. Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) and Grid-to-Vehicle (G2V) technologies allow EV batteries to operate as distributed energy storage units, contributing to grid stability, peak shaving, and ancillary services. In such applications, bidirectional DC–DC converters must provide high voltage conversion ratios, fast transient response, and reliable DC-bus voltage regulation under highly variable operating conditions. This paper proposes a Super-Twisting Sliding Mode Control (STA) strategy for DC-bus voltage regulation of an ARVIN bidirectional DC–DC converter. The proposed approach combines an analytical feed forward duty-cycle computed from the steady-state voltage conversion ratios with a STA-based feedback term, resulting in a hybrid feed forward-feedback control structure. The performance of the proposed controller is evaluated through MATLAB/Simulink simulations under step changes in power demand and bidirectional operating-mode transitions. The obtained results demonstrate fast transient response, reduced voltage ripple, and strong robustness against disturbances, confirming the suitability of the proposed control strategy for V2G/G2V applications.

title: FAST-RRG: Efficient and Effective Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Chest X-Ray Report Generation
authors: Yichi Cai
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) enhances the factual consistency of radiology report generation by retrieving fine-grained diagnostic phrases from medical images. However, existing frameworks encounter substantial limitations in feature extraction efficiency, multi-view feature disentanglement, and training convergence stability for chest X-ray analysis. We present FAST-RRG, our efficient retrieval-augmented framework featuring a novel retrieval architecture that integrates lightweight multi-scale feature extraction, adaptive sampling with view-specific disentanglement, and semantic denoising for accelerated convergence. By unifying these advances into a cohesive retrieval pipeline, FAST-RRG achieves both precise diagnostic phrase retrieval and improved training efficiency. Comprehensive evaluation on the MIMIC-CXR dataset demonstrates state-of-the-art clinical efficacy and factual completeness, alongside an over 50% reduction in training epochs relative to previous standard architectures.

title: Improved Estimation of Wind Turbine Bearing Lifespan via Advanced Machine Learning Models
authors: Ameni JELLALI, Hamida MAATALLAH, Kaïs OUNI, Lilia EL AMRAOUI
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: Accurately predicting the Remaining Useful Life (RUL) of wind turbine bearings is essential for enhancing system reliability and minimizing maintenance costs in renewable energy infrastructures. Building on our earlier work where Linear Regression unexpectedly yielded highly precise RUL estimations, this study explores whether more advanced machine learning models can deliver equal or superior performance. We conduct an extensive comparative analysis involving both traditional and cutting-edge methods, including Random Forest, XGBoost, and LightGBM. Leveraging features such as temperature, viscosity related damage, and dynamic load, the models are assessed using RMSE and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> metrics. The results reveal that ensemble learning techniques substantially outperform linear models, achieving near-perfect predictive accuracy. These findings underscore the effectiveness and scalability of modern machine learning approaches for predictive maintenance in wind turbine applications. keywords: Remaining Useful Life, Wind Turbine, Machine Learning, Random Forest, XGBoost, LightGBM, Predictive Maintenance.

title: Keyword Index
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)

title: A Systematic Analysis of Image Pre-processing Pipelines for Deep Learning-Based Grape Leaf Disease Classification
authors: Md. Ashikuzzaman Ashik, Sakib Imtiaz, Md. Efat Al Khalid Shahadout, Zahara Tasnim
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Accurate detection of grape leaf diseases is essential for precision viticulture and crop health management. While deep convolutional neural networks (CNNs) have substantially advanced automated disease recognition, their performance remains highly sensitive to the chosen image preprocessing strategies. This study presents a comprehensive evaluation of multiple preprocessing workflows for the classification of four grape leaf conditions-Black Rot, Esca, Leaf Blight, and Healthy-using PlantVillage dataset available on Kaggle. We systematically assess the impact of diverse pre-processing techniques, including contrast enhancement (CLAHE), Gaussian blur, and various filter combinations, across a suite of state-of-the-art pretrained CNNs (e.g., VGG19, ResNet50, Xception, MobileNetV2, DenseNet121), a custom-designed CNN, and several ensemble models. Images were standardized through resizing, normalization, and augmentation before being subjected to alternative pre-processing pipelines, as detailed in our unified workflow. Our experiment shows that Custom CNN with a certain pre-processing pipeline (RGB, Gaussian Blur, Histogram Equalization, Sobel Filter, Canny Filter) performs best (accuracy, precision, recall, f1-score: $\mathbf{9 9 \%)}$. Evaluation metrics including confusion matrices, ROC and precision-recall curves, t-SNE/UMAP feature projections, and Grad-CAM analysis were employed to interpret both overall robustness and class-specific discriminative ability. These findings underscore the importance of customized, multi-stage preprocessing for maximizing the reliability and field-deployment potential of AI-based grape disease detection systems, and provide actionable guidelines for developing scalable diagnostic tools in viticulture.

title: A Utility-Aware Probabilistic Digital Twin Framework With CC-MPC for Hard-Compliance Real-Time Decision Support in Continuous-Flow Environmental Treatment
authors: Keqiang Pan, Xin Shen, Shuwen Li
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Compliance-critical environmental treatment units must satisfy hard discharge limits while operating under time-varying disturbances and model uncertainty. This paper presents a utility-aware probabilistic digital twin (PDT) framework that couples online Bayesian uncertainty maintenance with chance-constrained model predictive control (CC-MPC) for minute-level decision support. The PDT maintains a posterior over uncertain process parameters using sequential Bayesian filtering, which induces calibrated predictive distributions for compliance-relevant variables. CC-MPC then selects dosing actions by enforcing chance constraints on the predicted discharge quality while minimizing expected operating cost. To enable reproducible and decision-relevant comparisons under practical data-confidentiality constraints, we introduce a compliance-gated utility-aware evaluation protocol: implementations must first pass explicit compliance gates before utility (cost) is compared, and calibration is reported as a necessary diagnostic. A numerical study based on anonymized/simulated minute-level signals, including an injected drift event, demonstrates the end-to-end workflow, reports compliance and cost outcomes, and quantifies computational load for real-time feasibility.

title: Dynamic Sphere Envelopes and SFF-DMP for Real-Time Obstacle Avoidance in Space Cable Assembly
authors: Xumeng Cheng, Gangfeng Liu, Jie Zhao
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: This paper addresses real-time obstacle avoidance and precision manipulation in on-orbit cable disassembly tasks involving flexible, vibrating cables. We propose a control framework that combines dynamic sphere envelopes with a Steering Force Field enhanced Dynamic Movement Primitive (SFF-DMP) formulation. The dynamic sphere envelopes approximate oscillating cables with radius adaptive virtual obstacles, enabling compact geometric representations of flexible dynamics. The proposed SFF-DMP integrates a Cartesian-space DMP with a steering force field, enabling smooth and reliable obstacle avoidance with low free-space loss, while the joint-space DMP preserves demonstration similarity and tracking accuracy through null-space optimization. Cable dynamics modeling and simulation studies validate the method's capability to avoid time-varying obstacles, and experiments on electrical connector disassembly demonstrate that a single cable free demonstration can generalize to multiple disturbed configurations. Results show consistent collision-free execution with position error below 0.7 mm and orientation error below 0.011 rad. The proposed approach offers a low-demonstration-cost, high-space-efficiency solution for safe manipulation in dynamic and constrained space environments.

title: Deep Learning–based RF Fingerprinting for Secure Physical-Layer Device Authentication
authors: Pranav Arakkal, Sai Srevarshan Suresh, Devapriya Sreehari, Neeraj Kulappuram Anand, Neha Krishna Vinod, S S Poorna, K Anuraj
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Radio Frequency Fingerprinting (RFF) has emerged as a promising physical layer authentication technique that is capable of exploiting the inherent hardware impairments for uniquely identifying the wireless transmitters. In this study, a complete end-to-end RFF framework is proposed that synthesizes highly realistic device signals, constructs hybrid time and frequency feature representations, and also evaluates multiple deep learning architectures for the multi-device classification. Using QPSK and BPSK base-band modulation schemes, a dataset is generated for 20 RF devices, each modeled with distinct impairments that include Sampling Clock Offset, Carrier-Frequency Offset, Phase Noise, Static Phase Rotation, Non-linear Power Amplifier distortion, DC Offset, Stochastic Phase Noise, and I/Q imbalance. These signals are then segmented into a fixed length and normalized I/Q samples, and FFT-based spectral magnitudes are also extracted. The experimental result achieved an accuracy of 94.32% for the Deep Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) model, with BPSK, significantly outperforming other CNN variants. The ROC analysis further validates the superior discriminative power of Deep CNN. The proposed framework highlights the potential of a Deep CNN for reliable RF device identification, and it also establishes a robust foundation for future studies involving real-time RF captures and dynamic channel conditions for secure wireless authentication.

title: AI-Driven Neural Architecture Search for Scalable and Accurate Big Data Analytics in Cloud Environments
authors: D Shobana
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Designing the new deep learning architectures requires highly demanding skills and time. Hence, Neural Architecture Search (NAS) is used to identify deep learning architectures suitable for the particular task. To design deep learning methods for big data analytics in the cloud, we propose a hardware-aware NAS called CloudNAS. By employing multi-objective optimisation, we have observed that our proposed CloudNAS architecture is 4 2% faster in search and 38% faster in training the model. For large datasets, the accuracy-tolatency ratio improves by 35%. In our proposed model, for the task transfer process, we employed an evolutionary algorithm along with meta-learning. To identify scalable architectures, we used TensorFlow, Spark, and Kubernetes, and for efficient computation, a weight-sharing method was used. To clarify our model’s effectiveness, we have used datasets from ImageNet, Text Classification Time-series, Tabular Data, and CIFAR-100 for testing.

title: Miniaturized Multi-Band THz Microstrip Patch Antenna for Next-Generation 6G Mobile Networks and Spectroscopic Applications
authors: Mohammed Rakibul Hoque, Nirzar Barua, Mohammad Abid, Most. Tajrima Akter Oishee, Chandan Saha
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: This paper presents a miniaturized, highperformance microstrip patch antenna designed for narrowband applications in the terahertz (THz) frequency range. The proposed rectangular patch is fabricated on a $4.6 \mu \mathrm{~m}$ quartz substrate with a thin copper layer, achieving a compact footprint of $97.27 \mu \mathrm{~m} \times 76.07 \mu \mathrm{~m}$. The antenna resonates at 1 THz, $1.5 \mathrm{THz}, 1.89 \mathrm{THz}$, and 2.13 THz, with return loss values of $-29.4 \mathrm{~dB},-38.4 \mathrm{~dB},-31.2 \mathrm{~dB}$, and -26.4 dB, demonstrating excellent impedance matching and efficient radiation. Optimized over the $\mathbf{1 - 2. 4} \mathbf{~ T H z}$ spectrum, it enables high-resolution spectroscopy and chemical sensing for precise identification of gases, explosives, and pharmaceuticals. In imaging applications, it supports non-destructive and through-barrier inspection, benefiting security screening, semiconductor diagnostics, and packaging analysis. Moreover, its performance in the $\mathbf{1 - 3 ~ T H z}$ range allows ultra-fast, short-range wireless communications with terabit-per-second data rates, making it suitable for future 6 G and beyond networks. The combination of compact size, multi-resonant operation, and high efficiency positions this antenna as a versatile solution for sensing, imaging, and next-generation THz wireless applications.

title: Attitudes, Behaviors and Expectations Toward Filial Piety: An Analysis of Opinions Among Generation X and Generation Y in Thailand
authors: Yuranun Tamkarn, Yuwadee Wongweeraprasert, Karittha Onkeaw
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Cybernetics and Innovations (ICCI)
abstract: This research article aims to examine and compare the attitudes, behaviors, and expectations of Thai people in Generation <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$X$</tex> and Generation <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$Y$</tex> toward filial piety within the context of an aging society. Data were collected through a questionnaire survey administered to Thai respondents from Generation <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$X$</tex> and Generation <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$Y$</tex>. The sample size was calculated using Taro Yamane's formula. Quota sampling was employed to allocate respondents proportionally across 5 Thailand's major regions, followed by accidental sampling in the final stage. A total of 406 respondents participated. The findings, derived from descriptive statistical analysis and inferential statistics including ANOVA, Tukey's HSD, and MANOVA, reveal that both Generation <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$X$</tex> and Generation <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$Y$</tex> continue to demonstrate high levels of filial piety across the dimensions of attitudes, behaviors, an expectations. Overall, Generation <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$X$</tex> exhibited higher levels of filial piety than Generation Y. Notably, the behavioral dimension of filial piety among Generation <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$Y$</tex> recorded the lowest mean score among the three dimensions, although it remained at a moderate level. Furthermore, analysis of group differences indicates that region of residence was the most significant factor contributing to differences in perspectives on filial piety between the two generations.

title: Disaster-Proof File Management Using Blockchain and IPFS for Data Security
authors: Shakil Ahmed, Md. Farhan Yousuf, Shamim Ahmad, Istiaque Ahmed
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: In the digital era, ensuring the security, transparency, and resilience of government and enterprise data has become a major challenge, especially in the face of disasters and cyber threats. This paper proposes a Disaster-Proof File Management System that integrates Blockchain technology and the InterPlanetary File System (IPFS) to provide a decentralized, tamper-proof, and transparent file management solution. The system enables secure file storage and retrieval by utilizing IPFS for distributed data storage and Blockchain for immutable record-keeping of file metadata, timestamps, and content identifiers (CIDs). Furthermore, Verifiable Credentials (VCs) are incorporated to enhance user authentication and data integrity within the network. The proposed model eliminates single points of failure, ensures data availability even during network disruptions, and strengthens trust in government file operations. Experimental results demonstrate that the system effectively maintains transparency, reliability, and security while significantly reducing the risk of data loss during disasters. This approach contributes to the development of a sustainable and trustworthy digital governance infrastructure.

title: A Stacked Ensemble Framework for Accurate Breast Cancer Classification Using Stepwise LDA
authors: Machiraju Adithya Vaibhav, Majji Harsha Vardhan, Jitisha Khede, Rahul Kumar Chaurasiya, Mohit Choubey
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Breast cancer remains one of the leading causes of cancer-related deaths and detecting it early can make a major difference in treatment outcomes. In this project, we worked with the Wisconsin Diagnostic Breast Cancer dataset to test how well classical Machine learning models can classify tumors. After cleaning the data and removing irrelevant fields, we converted diagnosis labels into binary form and selected sixteen meaningful features using stepwise LDA. We applied Z-score normalization to scale the features and used stratified sampling to split the data while preserving class balance. Six models were trained—Logistic Regression, Random Forest, LightGBM, CatBoost and XGBoost. Finally, we combined them using a stacking ensemble approach. This stacked model outperformed all individual classifiers and achieved a test accuracy of 98.25%, showing a slight but meaningful improvement over the best standalone models. The results suggest that blending even relatively simple models, when supported by solid preprocessing and thoughtful feature selection, can yield strong predictive performance. This approach offers a practical balance of accuracy and interpretability, making it suitable for real-world diagnostic applications.

title: Construction of a Digital Twin System for Intelligent Welding Production Line Based on MES
authors: Xin Xin, Zongjun Xin, Richang Xian, Shaopu Li, Yang Yang, Yang Liu, Zhen Zhao
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Against the backdrop of Industry 4.0, the development of smart manufacturing technologies is profoundly transforming the landscape of traditional manufacturing. This paper focuses on an innovative intelligent welding system based on digital twin technology, specifically addressing the requirements for efficiency and quality in transformer enclosure welding processes. The system integrates critical components such as gantry systems, robotic arms, Automated Guided Vehicles (AGVs), fixtures and specialized pallets, laser marking, and Manufacturing Execution System (MES) information upload platforms. By establishing real-time interaction mechanisms between physical entities and their digital models, it enables comprehensive monitoring and optimization of production processes, enhances product traceability, and provides robust data support for quality management and production scheduling. Furthermore, by conducting full-process digital twin modeling of transformer enclosure welding, bidirectional data exchange between the physical world and the digital twin is achieved, allowing dynamic adjustments and optimizations of actual production processes to precisely control welding parameters. Additionally, based on big data analysis results, a fault diagnosis algorithm combining Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) with Long Short-Term Memory networks (LSTM) was designed to accurately diagnose and predict equipment failures, effectively reducing failure rates and enhancing overall system stability.

title: Research on Techniques for Enhancing Network Security Threat Detection and Intelligent Decision-Making Based on Artificial Intelligence
authors: Cong Hou, Yuyue Su, Zhenghan Tang, Yue Sun
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: With the rapid evolution of internet technology, network attack techniques have become increasingly automated and intelligent. Traditional Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) often face challenges such as detection response latency, high false alarm rates, and difficulty in capturing deep-level attack features. To address this, we propose a novel network intrusion detection method that integrates big data technology with deep learning algorithms. The underlying system utilizes a distributed architecture based on Hadoop and Spark. The core detection engine integrates a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), a Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory network (BiLSTM), and a Multi-Head Attention (MHA) mechanism.

title: Intelligent Monitoring and Auto-healing System using Kubernetes in e-commerce
authors: M Krishnaraj, Sam J Prakash Roy, M S Sanjay Jakathees
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: E-commerce apps run on Kubernetes, staying up all the time matters as much as fixing problems fast - like when pods freeze or services go down. A monitoring setup shaped by real-time insight was built, not only for tracking but also for automatically recovering Kubernetes-powered online stores. Different tools perform independent functions: Prometheus gathers data, Alertmanager sends alerts only when needed, and Grafana helps people see what’s happening without getting lost. On top of these custom layers, built-in Kubernetes features take over by repairing themselves when issues pop up - turning trouble into smoother operation. What stands out is how the method uses real-time checks on pods, their reboots, and which nodes stay online - no guesses needed from algorithms. Rather than forecasting failures, it reacts directly when problems happen. In a live Kubernetes setup, tests simulated crashes by intentionally killing containers. When replicas kicked back into action, services kept running without major hiccups. During failures, e-commerce systems showed better stability by combining observability with Kubernetes recovery tools. Tests were run on a locally set up Kubernetes environment, where pod crashes were manually triggered multiple times.

title: Transfer Learning Based-Detection of Mango Leaf Diseases: A Case Study from Gajwel, Telangana
authors: M.S.S. Bhargav, Sania Begum
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: In India the mango is a crucial horticultural crop that is prized for both its nutritional value and economic significance. Mango trees are particularly susceptible to a number of leaf diseases including sooty mold, anthracnose, powdery mildew, bacterial canker, cutting weevil, dieback and gall midge. Both the amount and quality of fruit produced can be significantly decreased by these infections. Thus, maintaining sustainable mango cultivation depends on the early and accurate detection of such diseases. This study focuses on small-scale mango growers in Gajwel Telangana where 394 acres of mango orchards are grown by 98 farmers. We introduce a lightweight deep convolutional neural network called MobileNetV2 which is used in a transfer learning-based approach to automatically identify diseases of mango leaves. 70% of the 4000 mango leaf photos in the dataset were used for training 15% were used for validation and 15% were used for testing. The dataset was obtained from Kaggle. More photos were taken straight from mango orchards in Gajwel Telangana to improve practical applicability. A web-based application that enables farmers to upload leaf images and instantly receive disease predictions was implemented using the trained model. The system maintained low computational requirements while achieving an overall accuracy of 94%. These findings demonstrate the models dependability and efficiency establishing it as a workable option for helping Gajwels small-scale farmers manage mango leaf disease in a timely and precise manner.

title: Interpretable AI for 3D Structural Recognition: A Lightweight Approach to Point Cloud Segmentation
authors: Sourabh Subhash Rajput
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Understanding complex structures in 3D environments is crucial for various real-world applications, from automated inspection systems to geospatial analysis. This paper introduces a novel lightweight AI-driven approach for segmenting structural elements in large-scale 3D point clouds. The method leverages a mathematically grounded framework based on interpretable operators, ensuring robustness against noise and data imbalance while maintaining computational efficiency. Through extensive evaluation on real-world datasets, the model demonstrates superior precision and resource efficiency compared to conventional deep learning architectures. The proposed approach paves the way for scalable, transparent, and adaptable solutions in automated structure recognition and analysis.

title: Precision Optimization of Personnel Localization Based on a Grey Prediction Model in Complex Underground Scenarios
authors: Lijun Tang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: In order to solve the issue of invalid zero-value location data in underground coal mine UWB personnel positioning systems under long-range coverage scenarios, which is caused by complex environmental interference and communication link interruptions. The paper proposes a positioning accuracy optimization method based on a grey prediction model. This method employs a two-stage logic of pre-filtering of data validity and grey model prediction. First, invalid data is filtered out, and then the grey model' s predicted values are used to compensate for missing data that is abnormal. Experiments show that this method effectively handles invalid zero-value data. Compared to the Kalman filtering and gray prediction models without data filtering, this method can control the error of invalid data within 1 meter, significantly improving the stability and accuracy of the positioning system and providing technical support for personnel safety management in underground coal mines.

title: DiaGen: An Explainable Multi-Agent Framework for Automated Diabetes Decision Support
authors: Vivekananda Reddy Ranabothu, Y C A Padmanabha Reddy, Kondapur Mohammed Jawaad Safe, Nageswara Rao Sirisala
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Managing type 2 diabetes (T2D) is no easy feat; more often than not, proper T2D treatment demands strict adherence to growing and complex clinical recommendations. Clinical decision support systems (CDSS) in use today are useful in practice yet too often behave like mysterious "black boxes," resulting in dwindling trust among the medical community, hence the puzzlement of problems such as alert exhaustion. The purpose of this study is to outline the concept of DiaGen, the new solution that is transparent and multi-agent for totally automated decision support in T2D management. DiaGen is comprised of multiple agents working in harmony towards decision support. It has a specialized agent for the organized structuring of T2D patients. Yet another specialized agent is in use for evidence-based decision-making. There is yet another specialized agent in this multi-agent system that aids in the planning of treatment for T2D patients. There is no need for elaborate explanations in the working of the Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) engine since its primary purpose is the reliance of recommendations on its comprehensive vectorized medical database; indeed, complete transparency is implied here. The efficacy of the DiaGen method is well-demonstrated in the case study that follows. The case study illustrates the ability of the systematic method in extracting data from unstructured medical reports. From the case study, DiaGen is able to provide substantial evidence for the diagnosis of T2D in patients. It has also been able to provide evidence-based recommendations for the treatment of T2D patients. Indeed, DiaGen is able to provide recommendations for T2D treatment in multiple steps that are totally evidence-based. Moreover, recommendations for treatment in T2D are well-extracted in the case study with supporting quotes in the references.

title: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
authors: N/A
publish: N/A

title: Eld-Era: A Hybrid Edge–Cloud Intelligent IoT Audio System for Medication Assistance and Family Interaction
authors: P. Dhanavanthini, Pattan Munwar Ali Khan, Pamula Jaswitha, Gandla Sai Santhosh
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: In the context of rapidly aging population Medication adherence has been a critical healthcare concern, particularly in the group of socially isolated elderly individuals and those in elderly care homes who experience cognitive problems such as loss of memory. Lack of medication. or poor timing, can cause severe well-being issues, worsening health, and emotional instability. In an endeavor to address this dilemma, we came up with Eld-Era which is an intelligent and fully integrated system of support which will not only help enhance medication adherence but also increase family-to-elderly adult communication. It is based on a system consisting of the Internet of Thing(IoT)hardware and a machine learning predictive engine that is targeted towards proactive healthcare intervention. It has ESP32-S3 microcontroller that is connected to a MAX98357A digital audio amplifier, which has customized, multilingual text-to-speech(TTS)drug reminders. This saves a lot of cognitive load by the aged users. The cases of each medication are verified by simple push-button confirmation and stored securely to a MongoDB cloud backend via RESTful APIs, which forms a compliance history that is resistant to tampering. Its primarily renewal is the Medicine Miss Prediction System that is driven by the LightGBM classification algorithm. This system analyzes behavioral patterns and past data of historical recognition to determine the time that it is most likely to miss doses. Upon identifying a high risk scenario will give the caregivers immediate notifications. Furthermore Eld-Era has a role-based React.JS monitoring dashboard that allows caregivers and family members constant access. It promotes interaction with elderly users and acts as a source of emotional support. The experiment confirms the usefulness of the system with an F1-Score of 0.83%,predictive accuracy of 82.2 and a ROC-AUC of 0.85, demonstrating strong capability in distinguishing between likely taken and likely missed dosses. Overall Eld-Era ensures, supports healthy and independent aging, reduces burden of caregivers, improvement of emotional health and a scalable option that is ready to be used in any modern geriatric healthcare issues.

title: Enhancing Alzheimer’s Detection Through Hybrid CNN–GCN Model of MRI Brain Scans
authors: Samarth Kattimani, Sanjana Biradar, Vinod Nalavade, Abhilash Basarikatti, Santosh Pattar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Alzheimer’s disease is a progressive, irreversible illness that gradually impairs thinking, memory, and behaviour. Eventually, it makes daily tasks challenging and diminishes an individual’s independence. Early disease detection is crucial because treatment and care plans work better when initiated earlier. In this study, we explored a hybrid deep-learning model that combines a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) with a Graph Convolutional Network (GCN) to analyze MRI scans and classify the stages of Alzheimer’s. While the GCN uses a k-nearest neighbour graph to learn relationships between similar scans, the CNN extracts significant structural details from the brain images. This combination enables the model to discover deeper connections within the data as well as visual patterns. The model demonstrated strong performance, particularly in early-stage detection, with an accuracy of roughly 90.15% following implementation and testing in Python. These results imply that combining relational and spatial learning can enhance diagnostic precision and offer useful assistance for clinical decision-making.

title: Adaptive Reinforcement Learning Defense Against Jamming in Intelligent Wireless Edge Systems
authors: Poojitha D, Syed Abrar C, Ishvarya G, U Kumaran
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Wireless edge networks, crucial for real-time intelligent device communication, are highly susceptible to sophisticated jamming attacks that severely reduce reliability; traditional, rule-based defenses fail against dynamic jammers. To provide an intelligent solution, we propose a novel reinforcement learning-based adaptive defense framework utilizing the Soft Actor-Critic (SAC) algorithm, enhanced by curriculum-guided training, which models the defense as a continuous decision-making process to dynamically optimize power control and channel selection based on observed interference and signal strength. Experimental results in a simulated IEEE 802.11 environment confirm the framework’s superior performance, achieving a 250% improvement in SNR and a 70% reduction in packet loss (more than doubling throughput) compared to baseline methods, demonstrating robust resilience against both random and reactive jammers and confirming its effectiveness as a scalable foundation for autonomous anti-jamming protection

title: Remote Sensing Image Object Detection Method Based on RT-DETR and Super-Resolution Technology
authors: Wang Zhou, Zongmei Li
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
