abstract: In response to the detection difficulties such as diverse target scales and dense small targets in remote sensing images, this paper constructs an improved detection framework RS-RTDETR based on the real-time detector RT-DETR architecture, which integrates super-resolution preprocessing and multi-scale feature enhancement. Firstly, in the preprocessing stage, this article uses EDSR image super-resolution network for data enhancement, which can increase the absolute pixel count of small targets. Secondly, dual path lightweight convolution is introduced in the backbone network to reduce computational overhead, and deformable convolution is embedded to adaptively sense field deformation, improving the geometric modeling ability for irregular targets. Integrating EMA attention module into the neck network to enhance the detection capability of remote sensing small targets. Finally, the S2 feature layer is introduced into the neck network to enhance multi-scale feature fusion and improve the discriminability and expressive power between features at different levels. Experiments on the DIOR dataset showed that RS-RTDETR improved accuracy and mAP by 3.2% and 4.5% respectively compared to RT-DETR, and also significantly improved the problem of missed detection of small targets in remote sensing images.

title: Predicting Software Faults Using Machine Learning Models for Quality Improvement: An Empirical Study
authors: Pooja Chandrashekar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Software fault prediction is a crucial factor that improves the reliability of software, minimizes maintenance work and also advances the general quality of the software products. This paper proposes an effective machine learning (ML)-based model for predicting software failures using the JM1 dataset from the NASA Metrics Data Program. The data is extensively preprocessed, normalized, and encoded using one-hot encoding, and data balancing is performed using the ADASYN technique for class imbalance. Two powerful models, Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) and Support Vector Machine (SVM), were used and tested on the performance metrics of accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and ROC-AUC. The experimental findings indicate that the XGBoost model performs better, with an accuracy of 95.70%, precision 95.70%, recall 95.73%, F1-score 95.73%, and AUC 0.98%, which is better than SVM, which has an accuracy of 94.20 and an AUC of 0.97. The effectiveness of the proposed approach is demonstrated by comparison with existing techniques, such as AdaBoost, Random Forests, and NaiveBayes. The authors find that a combination of strong preprocessing and sophisticated machine-learning algorithms can greatly increase the predictive accuracy of software fault-detection systems, offering useful insights for improving software quality assurance and defect control in real-world scenarios.

title: Cyber Sentry - Protecting Users from Zero-Day Phishing
authors: S Raja, C Jackulin, Keerthivaasan N, J Liyakath, S Kaviyarasu, D Jonathan Ryen Joseph
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: A lightweight client-side framework, called Cyber Sentry, which aims to identify phishing IP addresses and prevent the use of malicious applications by the end user. It also performs on-device analysis, which protects user privacy, unlike traditional blocklist- or cloud-based detection systems that offer real-time results. Cyber Sentry incorporates a variety of security functions, including IP reputation rating, DNS resolution habits, WHOIS registration trends, TLS/SSL certificate anomalies and lexical URL features. Its hybrid engine that uses BiLSTM as a sequential behaviour analyser and ELECTRA as a semantic representation provides high-quality phishing detection at a low computational cost. In addition to identifying known threats, the system can counter zero-day phishing attacks by detecting suspicious patterns in the initial stages of an attack. Large-scale experimental tests point to its strength, high precision, low false-positive rate, and ability to work in other settings. Cyber Sentry is an extension that not only provides a degree of browser security but also enhances adversarial resistance and privacy-preserving identification, rendering it a robust solution for real-world effectiveness and phishing protection in the long run.

title: Quantum-Edge AI Framework for Real-Time Vehicle-Cargo Matching in Intelligent Transportation Systems
authors: P. Velmurugadass, P. Anusha, T. Navya Sri, K. Chaitanya Lakshmi, Y. Charvitha
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Efficient and privacy-preserving vehicle–cargo matching remains a significant challenge in intelligent transportation systems (ITS) due to dynamic network conditions, decentralized data sources, and limited computational resources at the edge. This paper proposes a Quantum-Edge AI Framework that integrates quantum optimization, edge artificial intelligence (AI), and federated learning to achieve secure, low-latency, and scalable decision-making for real-time logistics. The framework employs a Quantum Approximate Optimization Algorithm (QAOA) to solve a multi-objective cost function encompassing distance, time, and environmental impact, while Neural Architecture Search (NAS)-optimized edge models enable rapid inference under resource constraints. Privacy and trust are preserved through a hierarchical federated learning scheme supported by secure aggregation and anomaly detection mechanisms. A digital twin environment is developed to validate the proposed architecture under realistic transportation conditions, demonstrating enhanced adaptability and resilience to network fluctuations. The framework represents a unified approach toward sustainable, intelligent, and privacy-aware transportation, paving the way for next-generation urban mobility solutions aligned with global sustainability goals.

title: Deep Learning for Diabetic Retinopathy Detection: An Elaborative Study Using Fundus Imaging and Generative Approaches
authors: Vinothkumar Kolluru, Seema S Reshmi, Shreekant Salotagi, Shreyas Rajendra Hole, Thirunaavukkarasu Murugesan, Ajeeb Sagar
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) remains one of the highest contributors to blindness globally, making the need for early Automated screenings a prioritised focus within the field of medical imaging. The standard diagnostic approach consists of the labour-intensive manual analysis of retinal fundus images by an ophthalmologist, a process laden with errors and variability due to the subjective nature of the assessment. The development of deep learning technologies, especially convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and attention-based methods, permits rapid and reliable analyses of retinal images, allowing for the accurate classification of images and staging of the disease. This work outlines the construction and assessment of a deep learning pipeline for the detection of DR using Kaggle’s Diabetic Retinopathy dataset. This work describes how image resizing, histogram equalisation, and contrast enhancement as preprocessing steps set the standard for work performed with leading CNNs, including Inception V3 and U-Net-based models. From the experiments, we are able to report a state-of-the-art benchmark score of 88.7% accuracy and a 0.82 quadratic weighted kappa score while surpassing foundational methods implemented within Kaggle. To increase the understanding of the model, we use LayerCAM to provide visual evidence and interpret the working of the model. This identifies and segments microaneurysms and exudates, which forms the basis of disease to improve the confidence of medical practitioners. Finally, we introduce the use of Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) for image colourisation and augmentation to demonstrate the use of generative methods on dataset diversification and class imbalance. The developed approach that integrates generative synthesis, explainable AI, and discriminative classification provides a holistic approach to fully automate the detection of diabetic retinopathy (DR). Our research demonstrates the effectiveness of deep learning in diabetic retinopathy screening and suggests that using interpretability and augmentation techniques, deep learning will almost certainly help to diminish the burden on ophthalmologists, allowing for earlier interventions to avert vision loss.

title: OTFS-IM-Assisted Non-Terrestrial Networks Relying on Autoencoder-Aided Soft-Decision Detection
authors: Xinyu Feng, Chao Zhang, Mohammed EL-Hajjar, Chao Xu, Lajos Hanzo
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Vehicular Technology
abstract: Orthogonal Time Frequency Space (OTFS) modulation offers significant advantages over Orthogonal Frequency Division Multiplexing (OFDM), particularly in high speed environments. Hence, we consider OTFS transmission over high-Doppler Non-Terrestrial Networks (NTN). However, OTFS-based systems inherit some deficiencies from OFDM, such as its high peak to average power ratio, the bandwidth efficiency loss due to the cyclic prefix, and the sensitivity to the carrier frequency offset. Against this background, we harness both Multi-Band Discrete Fourier Transform-based Spreading (MB-DFT-S) and Index Modulation (IM) in our OTFS system, termed as MB-DFT-S-OTFS-IM. More explicitly, 1) DFT-S has been shown to reduce the PAPR; 2) IM is capable of improving the throughput by harnessing it in the Delay and Doppler (DD) domain; and 3) MB-DFT-S-OTFS-IM provides frequency diversity gain, which benefits the tolerance to carrier frequency offset. Furthermore, we propose a PAPR reduction method based on a Deep Learning (DL) Autoencoder (AE) architecture for both hard- and soft-decision detection, where the encoder is specifically trained for minimizing PAPR and the decoder is conceived for accurately reconstructing the transmitted signal. Finally, we extend the proposed AE-aided OTFS-IM scheme constructed for a practical NTN channel model, representing a variety of satellite-to-ground schemes.

title: EduGen: A Multi-Model Generative AI Framework for Dynamic Educational Resource Synthesis
authors: Savita Mane, Aryan Tamboli, Sachin Jadhav, Om Bhutkar, Sahil Karne, Yash Gunjal
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The exponential growth of digital learning has created an urgent need for intelligent systems capable of automatically generating personalized, multimodal educational content. Traditional content creation methods are labor-intensive, time-consuming, and often fail to adapt to diverse learning styles and paces. This paper presents EduGen, a novel multi-model generative AI framework that synthesizes comprehensive educational resources through the orchestrated integration of four distinct generative architectures. The system employs Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) for intelligent question generation, Variational Autoencoders (VAEs) for efficient diagram compression and reconstruction, Transformer-based models with Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) for contextual summarization and note generation, and Diffusion Models for scientifically accurate illustration synthesis. Trained and evaluated on the ScienceQA dataset comprising a large-scale collection of question-answer pairs across K-12 science curricula, EduGen achieved outstanding performance metrics across text generation, image reconstruction, and visual synthesis tasks. Human evaluation by educators and students yielded strongly positive ratings for content relevance and overall system usability. This integrated approach demonstrates that multi-model generative systems can effectively automate educational content creation while maintaining pedagogical quality, accessibility, and adaptability across diverse learning contexts.

title: Bi3D++: Hybrid Bi-domain Active Learning for Cross-domain 3D Object Detection_supp1-3688543.pdf
authors: Tao Chen
publish: N/A
abstract: Domain adaptation has been widely explored in 3D detection tasks recently. Previous works mainly use unsupervised domain adaptation (UDA) techniques to deal with domain discrepancies. Despite large improvements, the performance still largely falls behind the model trained on the fully annotated target domain which is because of the larger domain discrepancies caused by different sensors and changing surrounding environments. In this paper, we fully exploit the characteristics in autonomous driving scenarios (i.e., similar scenes and class-imbalance distribution) and explore a new task, named active domain adaptation (ADA) in 3D object detection which selects partial-yet-important target data and annotates them to further improve the performance on the target domain. To this end, we propose a hybrid bi-domain active learning strategy, namely Bi3D++, to sample valuable data from both source and target domains and transfer the knowledge learned from the source domain to the target domain. Bi3D++ first samples target-like source data by measuring scene-level and instance-level similarity between the source and target domains to avoid interference with irrelevant source data. Then a hybrid active target sampling strategy is introduced to sample target data that comprehensively considers rare-class similarity, intra-frame, and inter-frame diversity to sample diverse frames with diverse instances and focus more on rare classes in autonomous driving scenarios at the same time. Experiments on multiple cross-domain settings including cross-beam and cross-location demonstrate that our Bi3D++ can outperform existing state-of-the-art UDA methods with only 1% labeled target and consistently improve the performance with the increase of annotated target data.

title: Message from the Editor-in-Chief
authors: Rose Qingyang Hu
publish: IEEE Communications Magazine
abstract: As we present the May 2026 issue of IEEE Communications Magazine, it is clear that our field is in the midst of a fundamental transformation. Communication systems and networks are no longer simply infrastructures for moving data. They are increasingly becoming intelligent and adaptive systems that can sense, learn, and make decisions. This evolution is being driven by the convergence of communications with advances in artificial intelligence, distributed computing, and large-scale connectivity. Together, these forces are reshaping how we think about, design, and operate networks, opening the door to a new generation of systems that are far more dynamic, responsive, and capable than ever before.

title: A Window to the World of Negative Refraction_supp1-3687512.pdf
authors: Scott S. Steinfield
publish: N/A

title: Crop And Fertilizer Recommendation System Using ML
authors: Rajvansh Vashishtha, Varun, Shivam Kumar, Shivam Goyal, Swati Sharma, Mukesh Rawat
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Agriculture is the foundation of many economies across the world, especially in developing nations where a considerable proportion of citizens rely on it for their sustenance. However, despite this critical role, in recent years agricultural productivity has been facing a number of constraints including climate change-induced soil degradation, inappropriate use of fertilizer and limited accessibility to modern farming information. What is a huge problem in farmers’ lives these days, is they can’t seem to figure out exactly what the best crop for their soil might be, or what type of fertilizer (in terms of kind and amount) will enable them achieve maximum yield. Traditionally, farmers have based their crop management and fertilization decisions on intuition or personal experience(applied agriculture).

title: Physics-Informed Neural Network Based Noninvasive LC-Filter Parameter Estimation in Inverters
authors: Jie Kong, Dao Zhou, Xing Wei, Shuai Zhao, Zhao Yuan, Yan Xu, Huai Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: This article proposes a novel physics-informed neural network (PINN) parameter estimation method that integrates circuit physical models with deep learning for three-phase inverter in uninterruptible power supply (UPS) system. Due to manufacturing tolerances and continuous operation, the AC-side LC filter of inverter in UPS is subject to degradation and parameter drift, which can affect service performance and equipment lifetime. The proposed noninvasive approach avoids additional hardware and demonstrates strong performance trained by small-scale dataset. Pretrained neural networks and transfer learning strategy are employed to enhance training efficiency, model generalization, and noise immunity, enabling reliable and faster convergence. Moreover, a novel trigger-based sampling and data selection strategy is proposed to improve efficiency in parameter identification. An experimental prototype was built for verification, and AC film capacitors are used in the prototype. The experiment results indicate that the estimation error for inductor remains within 2.5% and the error for capacitor is approximately 5% under normal operating conditions.

title: Deep Learning Approaches for an Integrated Wildfire Prediction using Multimodal Data Fusion
authors: M. Kishore, T. Gowtham Reddy, Charles Prabu V, P. Pandiaraja, S. Jagan, M. Arun
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The global climate, human settlements, and ecosystems are all seriously threatened by wildfires, which is why precise and prompt prediction is a top research priority. Current methods for predicting wildfires mostly use single-modality data, like vegetation indices or weather parameters, which restricts our ability to understand space and time, particularly in dynamic climatic conditions, these methods lead to decreased accuracy and delayed detection. The multimodal data fusion framework for integrated wildfire prediction presented in this paper is based on deep learning and integrates a variety of data sources, including vegetation indices, satellite imagery, meteorological data, and topographical information. The suggested system uses Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks to record temporal dependencies from weather and environmental data and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) to extract spatial features from multispectral satellite images. Spatial-temporal representations are integrated by a feature fusion layer to create probabilistic maps of wildfire risk. According to experimental results, the CNN-LSTM fusion model suggested performs noticeably better than conventional single-modality and shallow learning techniques, attaining an overall precision of 94.6% with fewer false alarms and improved early detection capabilities in a range of climatic zones. These results confirm the ability of the model to assist long-term mitigation plans, proactive wildfire management, and disaster preparedness.

title: Joint Beamforming and Position Optimization for FIRES-NOMA Assisted Wireless Communication Systems
authors: Yu Liu, Qu Luo, Gaojie Chen, Pei Xiao, Ahmed Elzanaty, Mohsen Khalily, Rahim Tafazolli
publish: IEEE Transactions on Communications
abstract: To address the limitations of conventional reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (RIS) in spatial control capability, this paper proposes a fluid integrated reflecting and emitting surface (FIRES) assisted non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) multi-user communication system. In this system, each FIRES element can continuously and flexibly adjust its position in response to environmental variations, enabling simultaneous service to users in both transmission and reflection zones. This significantly enhances the system’s spatial degrees of freedom (DoF) and service adaptability. To maximize the system’s total sum rate, we formulate a non-convex optimization problem that jointly optimizes the base station beamforming, the transmission/reflection coefficients of the FIRES, and the element positions. An alternating optimization (AO) algorithm is developed, incorporating successive convex approximation (SCA), semi-definite relaxation (SDR), and majorization-minimization (MM) techniques. In particular, to address the complex channel coupling introduced by the coexistence of direct and FIRES paths, the MM framework is employed in the element position optimization subproblem, enabling an efficient iterative solution strategy. Simulation results validate that the proposed system achieves up to a 27% increase in total sum rate compared to conventional STAR-RIS systems and requires approximately 50% fewer RIS elements to attain the same performance, highlighting its effectiveness for cost-efficient large-scale deployment.

title: Multistage Classification of Sarcopenia Using Ensemble Learning
authors: Sobhana Mummaneni, Teja Aravind Kumar Rayalavarapu, Raghunath Kadimi, Lal Basha Shaik, Chandrika Kanuru, D.N.V.S.L.S. Indira
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Sarcopenia is characterized by the progressive decline of skeletal muscle mass and quality, leading to reduced mobility, frailty, and increased clinical risk. Most of the previous studies are binary classification and primarily relied on the Skeletal Muscle Index (SMI) which is biased by patient height and body composition. To address these limitations, this study introduces a four-class sarcopenia staging model using the Relative Muscle Index (RMI), a height-and BMI-adjusted metric derived from L3 CT-based muscle measurements. A dataset of 2,346 abdominal CT scans was processed, and subjects were categorized as Normal, Mild, Moderate, or Severe using RMI thresholds. Multiple machine learning models including Random Forest, LightGBM, and XGBoost were trained and integrated through a soft-voting ensemble, achieving an accuracy of 92 percent and outperforming individual base classifiers. SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) based interpretability was incorporated to provide global and local insights, identifying Skeletal Muscle Area (SMA), weight, cohort, age, sex, and height as the dominant contributors influencing the model’s predictions. A Gradio-based interface was additionally developed to enable real-time patient-level staging. The proposed framework offers a robust, interpretable, and clinically aligned solution for automated sarcopenia classification.

title: 3DRailNet: A Multifocal Cameras Fusion Network for Long-Range 3-D Rail-Track Detection
authors: Wentao Liu, Zhangyu Wang, Guizhen Yu, Bin Zhou, Songyue Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: Accurate 3-D rail-track detection is vital to the perception of the railway environments for autonomous trains. However, existing methods based on monocular image cannot capture 3-D spatial features, facing challenges in detecting 3-D rail-track in turnouts and distant scenarios. This study introduces 3DRailNet, a long-range 3-D rail-track detection network using multifocal cameras. 3DRailNet consists of two modules: disparity-based feature extraction (DFE) module and long-short rail-track detection (LSRD) module. Specifically, the DFE module utilizes multifocal images to generate a disparity image and depth image, acquiring 3-D depth features to enhance the spatial information of rail-track. Based on the 3-D depth features, the LSRD module designs a detection head for long and short focal cameras to predict the 3-D position of rail-track. Experimental results demonstrate that the mean F1 score (mF1) of our proposed 3DRailNet is 83.2%, establishing it as the state-of-the-art method in this field. All these results indicate that 3DRailNet has the potential to be readily applicable in 3-D rail-track detection in railway environments.

title: Aging Behaviors of Ester-Insulated Oil–Paper Composite Systems under Combined Electrical and Thermal Stresses – Part II: Aging Characteristics and Microscopic Mechanisms of Oil-Impregnated Paper
authors: Jiaming Yan, Guangliang Liu, Wenyu Ye, Yixin He, Tianxiang Ding, Xinhan Qiao, Jianwen Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: To investigate the aging characteristics and differences of insulating paper in various ester-based insulating oils, this study conducted accelerated electro-thermal aging tests on soybean-based natural ester and palm fatty acid ester (PFAE) insulating oil-paper composite insulation systems. Molecular dynamics simulations were employed to reveal the underlying mechanisms of the aging characteristics of the insulating paper. The results showed that during the early stages of aging, cellulose with a higher degree of polymerization formed more hydrogen bonds with H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O and H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>O<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">+</sup>, leading to stronger binding energies, which accelerated the degradation of cellulose. After aging, the number of hydrogen bonds and binding energies between cellulose and H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O/H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>O<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">+</sup> decreased, slowing the aging rate. As a result, the aging process of the insulating paper exhibited a characteristic pattern of rapid aging initially, followed by a slower rate. Crystalline cellulose, which is less prone to forming hydrogen bonds with H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O and H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>O<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">+</sup>, is less susceptible to hydrolytic aging, leading to a limit on the degree of polymerization at the end of the aging process. Compared to soybean-based natural ester, PFAE insulating oil showed stronger net interaction energies with H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O and H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>O<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">+</sup>, which reduced the number of hydrogen bonds between cellulose and H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O/H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>O<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">+</sup>, thus delaying the hydrolysis of cellulose. Therefore, the aging degree of insulating paper in PFAE insulating oil is lower, and the final residual degree of polymerization in the insulating paper of PFAE insulating oil is approximately 140 higher than that in soybean-based natural ester. This paper provides theoretical insights into the aging characteristics of insulating paper in ester-based insulating oils under electro-thermal combined effects from both experimental and molecular scales.

title: Secure Fuzzy Tracking Control of Multiagent Systems: A Dynamic Sampled-Data-Based Event-Triggered Perspective
authors: Chenxi Song, Yuan Li, Yin Sheng, Zhigang Zeng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Fuzzy Systems
abstract: This paper develops a novel dynamic sampled-data-based event-triggered (DSET) secure fuzzy tracking control scheme for a category of unknown nonlinear heterogeneous multiagent systems (MASs). Asynchronous DSET mechanisms are developed, in which the triggering thresholds evolve dynamically with the system states. The proposed mechanisms only require discrete state sampling and triggering evaluation, thereby avoiding continuous monitoring and reducing triggering frequency. Moreover, the triggering interval is shown to admit a positive lower bound that is not solely determined by the sampling period. Under denial-of-service (DoS) attacks, distributed DSET observers are constructed to estimate the leader's system matrices and states, where only a subset of followers requires access to the leader information. A detection scheme is further designed to identify the termination of DoS attacks. Furthermore, a fuzzy fault-tolerant control scheme is proposed based solely on output measurements. The convergence of observations and tracking errors is rigorously established. Simulation results demonstrate the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed approach.

title: Reactive Motion Generation via Phase-varying Neural Potential Functions
authors: Ahmet Tekden, Dimitrios Kanoulas, Aude Billard, Yasemin Bekiroglu
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Dynamical systems (DS) methods for Learning-from-Demonstration (LfD) provide stable, continuous policies from few demonstrations. First-order dynamical systems (DS) are effective for many point-to-point and periodic tasks, as long as a unique velocity is defined for each state. For tasks with intersections (e.g., drawing an “8”), extensions such as second-order dynamics or phase variables are often used. However, by incorporating velocity, second-order models become sensitive to disturbances near intersections, as velocity is used to disambiguate motion direction. Moreover, this disambiguation may fail when nearly identical position–velocity pairs correspond to different onward motions. In contrast, phase-based methods rely on open-loop time or phase variables, which limit their ability to recover after perturbations. We introduce Phase-varying Neural Potential Functions (PNPF), an LfD framework that conditions a potential function on a phase variable which is estimated directly from state progression, rather than on open-loop temporal inputs. This phase variable allows the system to handle state revisits, while the learned potential function generates local vector fields for reactive and stable control. PNPF generalizes effectively across point-to-point, periodic, and full 6D motion tasks, outperforms existing baselines on trajectories with intersections, and demonstrates robust performance in real-time robotic manipulation under external disturbances.

title: Semantics-guided Reconstruction of Weak-Manhattan Indoor as-built BIMs from 3D Point Clouds
authors: Xuzhe Wang, Juntao Yang, Zhizhong Kang, Yutao Zhang, Jianghong Zhao, Wen Xiao, Xue Zhang, Qingtian Zeng
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: The reconstruction of indoor as-built BIMs from 3D point clouds has garnered considerable interest due to its significant potential in applications. However, the challenges posed by severely occluded and cluttered environments, along with the complexity of indoor building structures, complicate the automatic reconstruction task. To address these issues, we propose an effective pipeline for reconstructing weak-Manhattan indoor as-built BIMs directly from raw 3D point clouds. Based on fine-grained semantics derived from our previous studies, we first develop a semantic-guided room segmentation method without the down-sample operations for fixed-size input, thus preventing the loss of room boundary details, especially for large-scale indoor scenes. Following this, we present a novel room-wise vectorization method that does not rely on strict Manhattan assumptions. This process allows us to achieve regular room borders while preserving their original shapes as much as possible. Finally, the principal building structures and in-wall elements are converted into a popup 3D model, allowing for the subsequent assembly of comprehensive BIMs. To validate the reliability and robustness, our approach is conducted on the available S3DIS and Matterport3D datasets from both qualitative and quantitative perspectives. Results from our approach achieves higher performance than contemporary state-of-the-art methods, retaining finer boundary details and achieving decimeter-level modeling accuracy. Our code is publicly released at https://github.com/wangxuzhecode/FloorSG.

title: Trust and Interaction-Driven Resilient Distributed Secure Learning Algorithm and Its Application
authors: Fuxi Niu, Xiaohong Nian, Te Ma, Shiling Li, Yong Chen, Maolong Lv
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control of Network Systems
abstract: This paper investigates the problem of distributed non-convex secure learning for multi-agent systems in non-secure and non-trusted environments, where only local datasets are available. To enhance the system's security and privacy during information exchange, we propose a trust and interaction-driven robust adaptive learning algorithm. Theoretical analysis demonstrates that the proposed algorithm effectively protects agent privacy while mitigating the impact of various types and arbitrary numbers of non-cooperative agents, without incurring additional communication or computational overhead. Finally, experiments on the image recognition task validate the algorithm's performance, showing that it outperforms existing methods in terms of security and privacy.

title: Real-Time Continuous Object Segmentation Using High Temporal Resolution Event Cameras via Random-Coupled Empowering Neural Network Optimized by Chef Leader Algorithm
authors: C Selvan, Soumyalatha Naveen
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The concept of event-based cameras has emerged as possibly helpful solution to the problem of high-temporal and low-latency capturing dynamic scenes and is therefore extremely applicable to the continuous object segmentation problem. Scarcity and asynchronous nature of event information, though, pose severe issues in feature description and precision of delineation. The given work proposes a systematic deep learning model on continuous object segmentation in event-based vision that is composed of the preprocessing, feature extraction, attention modeling, and optimization strategies. The framework uses EfficientNet-B3 to achieve the spatial features extraction, the attention mechanism to boost the temporal representation, the Random Coupled Empowering Neural Network (RCENN), to boost the interaction of features, and Chef Leader Optimization (CLO) to carry out the stable and effective optimization of the parameters. The proposed model is evaluated on EOS event-based data with regards to classification and segmentation. The experimental results indicate that the framework has excellent segmentation performance with the Intersection over Union (IoU) of 0.82, Dice coefficient of 0.86 and mean Intersection over Union (mIoU) of 0.81 and the classification performance is excellent. Ablation experiments and comparison are also effective in demonstrating the contribution of each aspect towards the enhancement of performance. Although the results are promising, the performance relies on the properties of datasets and experimental conditions and is yet to be demonstrated on more diverse real-life situations. Generally, the framework presented is a scalable and efficient solution to the event-based segmentation of dynamic environments.

title: Joint-Level Intent Recognition of Upper Limb based on XGBoost and Viterbi Decoding
authors: Qi Zhang, Yong Tao, Zikang Xiao, Xingsong Wang
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: sEMG-based upper-limb motion intent recognition is challenging due to the high flexibility of upper-limb joints. Existing methods that classify overall actions often struggle to generalize to unseen movements, lack joint-level interpretability, and suffer from prediction jitter in real-time frame-level outputs.To address these issues, this study proposes an upper-limb intent recognition framework based on XGBoost and Viterbi decoding. Complex movements are decomposed into two functional sub-models for the shoulder and elbow joints. An automatic activation detection method based on time–frequency energy changes is introduced, and Viterbi decoding is applied to improve temporal continuity.Experimental results show that the proposed framework can effectively recognize unseen complex movements, achieving an average accuracy of 94% in typical daily activities. Compared with raw frame-level predictions, Viterbi-based temporal optimization significantly reduces label switching frequency and improves prediction stability.

title: The Role of AI-Related Micro-Credentials in Shaping Employment Designations of Bachelor of Science in Computer Engineering Graduates
authors: Pearl Stephanie De leon Ulep, James Ronald Opulencia Mesina, Ramachandra Castro Torres, Ivana Ruth Cunanan Vidal
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 9th International Conference on Education and E-Learning
abstract: None

title: AI-Based Python Virtual Lab Development
authors: Mery Noviyanti, Thesa Kandaga, Suci Nurhayati
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 9th International Conference on Education and E-Learning
abstract: None

title: Phishing Urls Detection Using Machine Learning Models
authors: Preksha Patel, Raunak Raj, Pooja Sapra
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Phishing attacks represent a major cybersecurity risk because they use false websites to acquire important user information, such as passwords and financial credentials. Conventional detection methods, including blacklist-based strategies, exhibit slow response times and low effectiveness against newly emerging phishing websites. This study introduces a detection framework that integrates machine learning (ML) methodologies to detect malicious URLs via extensive feature analysis. The proposed methodology examines various URL attributes, such as structural patterns, length metrics, and embedded keywords, employing a range of algorithms that include decision trees (DT), support vector machines (SVM), random forest (RF), XGBoost, and logistic regression (LoG). The proposed model has been trained and tested using a dataset of 5000 URL samples and shown exceptional performance. Results from experiments indicate that the integrated ML methodology much surpasses conventional detection techniques while preserving computing efficiency appropriate for practical implementation. This research provides a strong and scalable approach for proactive cybersecurity defense against advanced phishing threats.

title: Ensemble Deep Learning Framework for Automated Diabetic Retinopathy Detection using EfficientNet Models
authors: Srikanth Cherukuvada, Dipanwita Debnath, Sachin Mahesh Nalluri, Jethin Pokala, Hansika Sudula
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) is one of the most prevalent microvascular complications of diabetes and a major global cause of avoidable blindness. Effective clinical care and vision preservation depend on the early recognition and precise severity classification of DR. However, traditional screening techniques depend on skilled ophthalmologists manually analyzing retinal fundus images, which is laborious, subjective, and challenging to scale for large patient populations. An automated Deep Learning-based framework for multi-class severity classification and diabetic retinopathy identification is proposed in this study. To leverage complementary feature representations and improve classification resilience, the proposed system uses an ensemble of EfficientNet-B2 and EfficientNet-B3 models. To solve the class imbalance and enhance generalization, a large-scale retinal fundus image collection was created by merging the EyePACS, APTOS, and Messidor datasets with significant data augmentation. To successfully adapt the pretrained models to DR-specific features, a two-stage fine-tuning technique and standardized image preprocessing were used. Five DR severity levels were used by the ensemble framework to categorize images: No DR, Mild, Moderate, Severe, and Proliferative DR. According to the experimental results, the ensemble technique performed better than the individual models in terms of accuracy and stability, suggesting that it is appropriate for diabetic retinopathy screening applications in the real world.

title: Enhancing Deepfake Detection with an Ensemble of Specialized Neural Networks
authors: B. Rajesh, R. Tamilkodi, K.Suryakala, Parasa Lakshmi Sruthi, Bandaru Anusha, K M D S Pradeep
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The rise of deepfake technology poses a serious threat to information integrity, personal security, and public trust. Current detection methods often depend on one analytical model, which can be bypassed by more advanced generation techniques. This paper presents a strong, multi-modal ensemble system for detecting deepfake videos. Our proposed system combines three different, cutting-edge neural network models: Face X-ray, which identifies boundary artifacts; LipForensics, which looks for inconsistencies in lip movements; and a top-performing EfficientNet model from the Deepfake Detection Challenge (DFDC), which offers powerful general classification. By combining the predictive scores from these various models, the system achieves better and more consistent detection. The whole analysis process is handled through an easy-to-use web interface, making advanced deepfake detection accessible to those without expertise. We propose a model which works using ensemble approach with different detection methods, improving the ability to identify deepfake video content compared to single model system.

title: Cloud-Based Intelligent Inventory Tracking System
authors: Kaviya K, Jeshlin P V, Ezhil Dyana M V
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: In the fast-paced economic environment, precise prompt decision-making tools have become necessary for traders, investors, and analysts. This paper presents StockSense, an intelligent, cloud-based web application outlined to provide AI-powered analytics and real-time stock market insights. The system combines predictive models, statistical analysis, and a dynamic dashboard interface to provide users an all-inclusive view of stock performance and investment recommendations. Built using Flask, HTML, CSS, and JavaScript technologies, StockSense holds up multiple user roles—customers, managers, and administrators—enabling personalized functionalities for each. It exploites cloud services for safe data storage, live updates, and flawless access to external APIs. The key feature of the application is an AI-powered analytics engine potentially achieving up to 92% prediction accuracy, offering recent price performance based on rule-based scoring techniques and volatility metrics. Relative evaluation through graphical representation manifests notable performance improvements over conventional and basic cloud-based systems. The proposed solution not only increases inventory and stock management accuracy but also guarantees robust, scalable, and intelligent investment support. StockSense thus epitomizes the integration of cloud computing and artificial intelligence in evolving future generation financial services.

title: A Three-Stage Vision-Based Transformer for Channel Gain Map Construction
authors: Tongyi Wei, Kun Tang, Hao Wu, Wenjie Feng, Wenquan Che, Quan Xue
publish: IEEE Transactions on Communications
abstract: The channel knowledge map (CKM) is regarded as a digital twin of the wireless channel and provides location-specific channel information for environment-aware wireless communications. The channel gain map (CGM) is a typical form of CKM that stores spatially indexed channel gains over a target area, enabling fast link scheduling, coverage planning, and beam selection. Existing CGM construction methods mainly rely on propagation modeling or data-driven spatial completion, which can be computationally expensive or may inadequately capture site-specific propagation characteristics, thereby limiting prediction accuracy. To address this issue, we propose a three-stage Transformer-based CGM construction framework for predicting channel gains at arbitrary locations. In the first stage, the city map is discretized into grids, and the channel gain values within each grid are normalized to facilitate continuous spatial prediction. In the second stage, a Transformer-based coarse CGM prediction method is proposed to extract environmental features from the city map and generate a coarse CGM. In the third stage, a lightweight refinement network is designed, which leverages a small number of sparse measurements to refine the coarse predictions. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method significantly outperforms the state-of-the-art approaches across all performance metrics. In particular, compared with the existing optimal scheme, the proposed method achieves 32.81% and 20.63% reductions in normalized mean square error (NMSE) and root mean square error (RMSE), respectively, while improving the structural similarity index (SSIM) and peak signal-to-noise ratio (PSNR) by 2.08% and 3.71%, respectively.

title: Meta-CT: A Meta-Learning-Based Spectrum Sensing Scheme Using CNN-Transformer for Low-Altitude Intelligent Networks
authors: Mingyue Si, Wensheng Zhang, Bin Qi, Xiaotian Zhou, Jian Sun, Haixia Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: In low-altitude intelligent networks (LAINs), spectrum sensing is crucial to ensure efficient spectrum utilization especially for distributed unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) users. However, the dynamic and complex electromagnetic environments in LAINs pose new challenges to spectrum sensing. To address specific challenges such as sparse data, low signal-to noise ratio (SNR), and dynamic spectrum conditions in LAINs, this paper proposes a novel spectrum sensing scheme, which integrates convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and Transformers in a meta-learning framework, namely Meta-CT scheme. The proposed Meta-CT scheme adopts a meta-learning architecture, in which CNNs and Transformers are jointly integrated as the base learner. By combining convolutional operations and self attention mechanisms, the weak features of low-SNR signals can be extracted. Furthermore, the proposed scheme can achieve spectrum sensing with sparse data by leveraging the inherent few-shot learning ability of meta-learning. The Meta-CT scheme can also optimize the initialization of model parameters, enabling rapid adaptation to new dynamic spectrum environments. Simulation results indicate that the proposed Meta-CT scheme significantly outperforms traditional spectrum sensing schemes, achieving superior accuracy and better robustness in sparse data and low-SNR scenarios. The proposed scheme provides a novel and efficient spectrum sensing paradigm for dynamic and heterogeneous electromagnetic environments in LAINs.

title: AI-Driven Compliance and Audit Framework for Manufacturing Infrastructure in Automotive Connected Services and Financial Ecosystems
authors: Subramanian Sendil Kumar, Anil Lokesh Gadi, Goutham Kumar Sheelam, Dwaraka Nath Kummari, Hara Krishna Reddy Koppolu, Avinash Pamisetty
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The accelerated way of digitization in manufacturing and financial sectors has led to greater complexity of data, compliance burden, and risk of cyber attacks. Current compliance monitoring solutions are not scalable, cannot be easily extended in real time and do not preserve privacy resulting in costly audit of processes and delayed detection of anomalies. This paper proposes AI-Driven Compliance and Audit Framework for Interconnected Manufacturing Infrastructures, Automotive Connected Services and Financial Ecosystems. The system also embeds Federated Learning combined with MLOps orchestration, validating distributed model training, always-on monitoring and automatic compliance checks while preserving privacy for the data. Making use of Smart Manufacturing IoT-Cloud Monitoring and Internet of Vehicles, the system eagerly scrutinizes operational, transactional, and sensor data to detect irregularities or regulate compliance. This paper present experimental evaluation with superior performance achieving 98.6% accuracy, 97.8 % F1-score, 0.985 AUC-ROC, and a recall of 96.9%, surpassing the state-of-the-art accepted centralized AI models. The study validates an AI-derived compliance tool for privacy protection—scalable, explainable and that preserves such privacy by forming consistent decision and reinforcing real-time governance over various digital environments.

title: Stacking Deep Neural Networks to Detect Multiple Types of Cardiac Arrhythmias
authors: Din Sadia, KangYoon Lee, Sghaier Guizani, Ateeq Ur Rehman, Habib Hamam
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Heart arrhythmias are associated with serious cardiovascular diseases and can result in fatal outcomes if not diagnosed early. Electrocardiograms (ECG) are generally used to diagnose heart arrhythmias. Prior studies employ deep learning architectures including Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), and Transformer based methods to diagnose arrhythmias from ECG signals, achieving promising results. However, most of the deep learning models when used individually exhibit inherent limitations. CNNs can learn spatial features effectively but is limited in learning temporal characteristics. LSTMs capture temporal characteristics, but struggle with capturing long-range dependencies. Transformer models are effective at modeling long-range dependencies but they are often prone to overfitting. To address the limitations of individual models while leveraging their feature learning capabilities, this work proposes a two-level stacked ensemble framework for ECG arrhythmia classification. At the first level, deep learning models including a Deep Neural Network (DNN), CNN, LSTM, and Transformer models are trained as base learners while at the second level, the predictions generated by the base learners are combined and passed to the Multilayer Perceptron (MLP) which acts as the meta-learner. MLP learns how to weigh the predictions of the first level models to generate improved final predictions. The combination of these models in a stacked ensemble framework helps exploit the strengths of all models, enabling fusion of spatial, temporal, and long-range dependency features from the ECG signal. The proposed framework is evaluated on the MIT-BIH and INCART arrhythmias databases to classify the input ECG signals into five arrhythmia categories. Comparison against the base models and prior approaches demonstrates that the proposed stacked ensemble model outperforms the base models and state-of-the-art (SOTA) techniques, achieving an F-score of 99.79% on MIT-BIH and 99.62% on INCART dataset.

title: X-Ray Intelligence: Instant Hand Bone Fracture Detection & Localisation Using Yolov8 Deep Learning Model
authors: K. Kavitha, N Rajkumar, P. Anitha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Fracture diagnosis is difficult due to the complex structure of the hand. A radiologist needs to examine numerous X-ray images to arrive at a correct diagnosis. This process can cause treatment delays due to its length and the high likelihood of human mistakes. We hope that our deep learning-based automated fracture localisation and identification system will facilitate easier diagnoses and faster treatment times for patients. Medical picture classification and feature extraction are very effective with convolutional neural networks (CNNs), especially ResNet18. Our system can detect fractures using ResNet18 and localise them with YOLOv8. First, images from public medical libraries such as MURA, NIH, and Kaggle are acquired and calibrated to a 150x150 pixels. These are further fine-tuned to achieve a $99 \%$ localisation rate before being compared with state-of-the-art approaches. There are two groups into which they fall: those with fractures and those without.

title: Explaining Household Access to Heavy Rail Services Using Interpretable Machine Learning: Evidence from the 2022 U.S. National Household Travel Survey
authors: Sayed Shahriar Islam Lamun, Syeda Tamzida Akter, Jeba Tahsin, Sanupa Sarkar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Accurate modeling of household access to heavy rail services is vital for understanding transportation behavior, urban mobility, and infrastructure planning. Traditional methods often fail to capture the complex relationships influencing whether households have access to heavy rail, primarily due to assumptions inherent in classic modeling techniques. This study introduces an interpretable machine learning framework to predict and explain household access to heavy rail services using data from the 2022 U.S. National Household Travel Survey (NHTS). The analysis utilizes three supervised learning algorithms Random Forest (RF), Support Vector Machine (SVM), K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) to classify households based on 17 socio-demographic, vehicular, and location-specific variables. Data preprocessing involved handling missing values, checking for multicollinearity through Variance Inflation Factor (VIF), and normalizing the dataset. Model optimization was performed using grid search and 5-fold stratified cross-validation. To ensure transparency, SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) were applied to explain the impact of each feature on predictions. Empirical results indicate high model accuracy ($\geq 96 \%$) on the held-out test set, with Random Forest and SVM demonstrating superior generalization. SHAP analysis revealed that proximity to rail stations, household vehicle availability, and urban density were the most influential factors determining heavy rail access, highlighting spatial and infrastructural constraints over demographic factors like income or household size. By combining predictive power with interpretability, this study offers an insightful, scalable approach to understanding MSA heavy rail status for households, providing evidence to support data-driven urban transportation policies.

title: Real-Time Identification and Localization of Street-Roaring Vehicles Based on Lightweight CNN and FPGA Acceleration
authors: Can Lei, Liyan Luo, Jingyu Zhang, Tao Bin
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: To address challenges in automatically documenting high-intensity traffic noise caused by illegally modified vehicles on urban roads, this paper proposes an intelligent monitoring approach tailored for resource-constrained embedded platforms. The system adopts a joint framework of “lightweight INT8quantized CNN noise recognition on STM32 + FPGA-accelerated sound source localization.” At the front end, a multi-microphone array performs synchronous sampling and frequency-domain noise reduction. On the STM32 MCU, a lightweight CNN optimized via quantization and pruning achieves high-precision recognition. Backend processing utilizes FPGA parallel acceleration of the GCC-PHAT algorithm for localization. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed model maintains 87.2 % accuracy even at a challenging 0 dB SNR. The overall localization error is controlled within <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1. 2}$</tex> meters, enabling precise lane-level identification. Compared with traditional methods, the real-time spectrum subtraction carried out by the system on the FPGA before TDOA estimation improves the quality of the input signal. This enables the system to keep the error below 2 meters even at a 5 dB signal-to-noise ratio, meeting the requirements for lane-level vehicle positioning.

title: GraphRAG-Driven Multi-Stage Context Retrieval for Thai Text-to-SQL Using SLM Agents
authors: Chaiyanan Sompong, Kanyaphat Khanthacha, Thanapat Chaukampeng, Saranporn Panyakun, Neeracha Ngokwong, Vutthipong Puntumnunt
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Cybernetics and Innovations (ICCI)
abstract: Thai Text-to-SQL continues to face significant challenges due to semantic ambiguities and synonyms. These linguistic complexities often lead to inaccurate context retrieval in traditional vector searches, thereby exacerbating SQL generation errors in Small Language Models (SLMs) due to their limited reasoning capacity. To address this, this paper proposes the integration of a Knowledge Graph (KG) that creates the model of database schema relations and incorporates synonym mappings to resolve ambiguities in table and column names. A multi-stage context retrieval process is employed to extract precise relational context, ensuring high accuracy in query generation. Finally, Typhoon SLMs within the 4 B to 12 B parameter range are utilized to generate the final SQL queries based on the enriched context. The proposed framework was validated using a real-world database from the registration and admission systems of Sakon Nakhon Rajabhat University. Based on a test suite of 178 Thai natural language queries, the system achieved a peak Execution Accuracy (EX) of 70.22% using the Typhoon2.5-Qwen-4B model. These results demonstrate the effectiveness of integrating Knowledge Graphs with SLMs for high-precision Thai Text-to-SQL tasks.

title: An Optimised U-Net with Quantum Particle Swarm Optimisation for Multimodal MRI Brain Tumour Segmentation
authors: S. Anusuya, Sengole Merlin, S. Kannan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Automatic medical image segmentation helps in clinical diagnosis, treatment planning and disease prediction and monitoring. MRI brain tumour segmentation accurately identifies lesions or tumours in the image and assists physicians in distinguishing healthy tissue and its nature. This research work is a study of various deep learning models to segment brain tumours in multimodal MRI sequences, T1, T1Gd, T2, and FLAIR of BraTS 2021. The models include a basic U-Net and a U-Net optimised with Particle Swarm Optimisation (PSO). U-net optimised with Quantum Particle Swarm Optimisation (QPSO) is trained and evaluated on the BraTS 2021 dataset. Results are analysed with metrics such as Accuracy, Precision, Sensitivity, Specificity, Test Loss, Mean Intersection over Union (mIoU), Dice Coefficient (DC), and class-wise Dice scores of necrotic, oedema, and enhancing tumour. The results show that the U-Net optimised with PSO and QPSO is more powerful than the standard U-Net. U-Net_PSO has produced results with $99.78\%$ of accuracy, while U-Net_QPSO shows better performance regionwise, with a Mean IoU of 0.9971 and a Dice Coefficient of 0.9985. These results prove that U-Net optimised with PSO and QPSO contributes to effective feature representation and robust segmentation, especially in cases of dense tumour heterogeneity. U-Net_QPSO is effective for analysing scalable brain MRI features and helps achieve Sustainable Development Goal 3 related to Good Health and Well-Being.
