title: Design of a Multiband Microstrip Patch Antenna for WLAN and WiMAX Applications
authors: Md. Nasrulla Khan, Sk. Shariful Alam
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Since miniature microstrip patch antennas are familiarized in many gadgets, here and now, the antenna 2D layout has been reduced as much as possible; consequently, appliances will become affordable. In this paper, a trade-off has been highlighted among FR-4 substrate-based antenna dimensions, moderate bandwidths, and modest gains. CST STUDIO SUITE is used for shaping the proposed multiband antenna. In the proposed design, the bottom surface of the substrate is partially wrapped with copper. This adaptable and versatile slotted multiband microstrip patch antenna is proficient for portable WLAN and WiMAX implementation. The resonant frequencies as well as the operational bandwidths with this architecture are 2.41 GHz(2.332-2.482 GHz), 3.6 GHz (3.271-4.473 GHz), and 5.8 GHz (5.669-5.929 GHz).

title: Design, Modeling, and Optimization of Flexible Copper-Polyester Interfaces for Human Head Protection Against RF Exposure
authors: Bouali Lazhar
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: The rapid expansion of wireless communication, including 5 G networks at 3.5 GHz, has raised concerns about radiofrequency <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\text{RF})$</tex> exposure and its effects on the human brain. This study presents the design and optimization of a flexible copper-polyester shielding interface to mitigate <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R F}$</tex> field penetration at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$900 \text{MHz}, 1.8 \text{GHz}, 2.4 \text{GHz}$</tex>, and 3.5 GHz, covering <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 \mathrm{G}-5 \mathrm{G}$</tex> bands. A high-resolution numerical human head model was used to evaluate three configurations: (i) unshielded baseline, (ii) silver-polyester interface, and (iii) copper-polyester interface. Full-wave electromagnetic simulations assessed electric and magnetic field distributions, specific absorption rate, and overall exposure reduction. Results indicate that the silverpolyester interface reduces internal fields by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 5}-\mathbf{4 0 \%}$</tex>, whereas the copper-polyester interface achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 5}-\mathbf{6 0 \%}$</tex> attenuation across all frequencies. The copper-polyester design demonstrates superior shielding, improved field redistribution, and enhanced stability, particularly at higher frequencies. Its combination of high conductivity, flexibility, and lightweight properties offers a practical and scalable solution for wearable RF protection. These findings suggest that copper-polyester interfaces can effectively reduce brain exposure while providing a feasible approach for safer integration of current and emerging mobile technologies into daily life,

title: Automatic Detection of Total Sleep Time using Slope Changing Rate of Overnight EEG Signals
authors: Afifa Halim Prapti, Maysha Rahman Trisha, Effat Mahajabin Ahona, Md. Bashir Uddin
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Sleep plays very important role in maintaining physical and mental health. Sleep disorders such as insomnia and sleep apnea remain prevalent. Total sleep time (TST) is a crucial parameter to determine the severity of sleep disorders as well the sleep efficiency. This study proposes a novel approach for automatically detecting TST by classifying sleep-wake segments from overnight electroencephalogram (EEG) signals. From sleep heart health study (SHHS) database, 198 polysomnography (PSG) records were collected at random and EEG signals along with the scoring details were extracted for this study. EEG signals were preprocessed using bandpass and notch filters to remove noise and interference. Sleep and wake segments identification was done by automatically analyzing slope changing rates of EEG. A very small mean bias of $\mathbf{- 0. 1 4}$ hours (with $\mathbf{9 5 \%}$ limits of agreement of $\mathbf{- 2. 2 5}$ to $\mathbf{1. 9 6}$ hours) were achieved between the actual and detected TST. In addition, for detecting sleep-wake segments, the proposed method showed the overall accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, precision, and F1 score of $\mathbf{8 3. 1 0 \%, ~} \mathbf{8 8. 8 6 \%, ~} \mathbf{6 1. 2 4 \%, ~} \mathbf{8 7. 1 7 \%}$ and $\mathbf{8 7. 6 9 \%}$, respectively. The results of this study outperformed the existing literature. This method can be applicable to accurately detect TST, hence to identify sleep efficiency.

title: Federated and Incremental Multilingual Gynaecological Disease Diagnosis System with Explainable AI
authors: B. Rama Subbareddy, S. Moin Basha, Nossam Vivekananda Swamy, B. Hari Krishna, A. Nusrath Jaha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Gynaecological disease diagnosis in resourceconstrained and linguistically diverse areas is still a difficult issue because of limited clinical access, privacy matters, and unchanging decision support systems. Current expert systems are based on centralised learning and superficial language processing, which are limiting scalability, flexibility and reliability. To overcome these challenges, this research would introduce a Federated and Incremental Multilingual Gynaecological Disease Diagnosis Expert System (FI-GDDES) that combines privacy-preserving federated learning, continual model adaptation, and explainable artificial intelligence. The suggested framework allows distributed healthcare nodes to jointly and continuously train diagnostic models without sharing raw patient information, while also accommodating new diseases, symptoms, and regional languages. A voice-based natural language understanding component identifies symptom representations in context, thereby strengthening the diagnosis. Substantial experiments carried out on multi-disease gynaecological data reveal that FI-GDDES is more accurate (97.2), more precise (96.8), higher in recall (97.5), and higher in F1-score (97.1) than centralised baselines (by as much as 4.1 points). The federated paradigm stabilises at a set of limited communication rounds. It maintains data privacy and interpretability and, as such, applies to real-world healthcare applications.

title: Comparative Research Landscapes of Artificial Intelligence Application in the Sustainable Development Goals: A Bibliometric Analysis
authors: Wuttiyakorn Pumkham, Alisa Kongthon
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Cybernetics and Innovations (ICCI)
abstract: Utilizing technological transformation to align business operations with the United Nations' Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) has become increasingly necessary. However, compared to the international research landscape, Thai research shows a significant lack of systematic studies examining the role of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in supporting SDG achievement. To address this gap, this study conducts a systematic bibliometric analysis of global and Thai AI-SDG literature published between 2015 and 2025. Data were collected from the Scopus database and analyzed using the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}$</tex> programming environment and the Bibliometrix package. The findings reveal key research trends and practical AI applications in business sectors, including machine learning, Internet of Things (IoT), energy optimization, and precision agriculture. In addition, emerging themes related to AI ethics, data privacy, and green computing indicate growing awareness of responsible AI governance in SDG implementation. Comparative results highlight differing levels of awareness and research maturity between global and Thai contexts regarding AI's role in managing and advancing SDGs.

title: Adaptive Robust Self-Triggered Control of Polynomial Fuzzy Systems with Time-Varying Uncertainties and Communication Delays
authors: Dine El Houda Hammami, Saber Maraoui, Kais Bouzrara
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: This paper presents an adaptive robust self-triggered control strategy for polynomial fuzzy systems subject to time-varying uncertainties and communication delays. A novel self-triggered mechanism is designed to determine the control update instants, significantly reducing communication and computation load while guaranteeing system stability. The proposed control law integrates polynomial fuzzy modelling with robust design techniques to mitigate the effects of uncertainties and disturbances. An adaptive threshold strategy is employed to dynamically adjust the triggering condition based on the system's state evolution. Simulation results demonstrate the effectiveness of the approach, showing improved stabilization performance, reduced control updates, and resilience against uncertainties and delays, compared to conventional time-triggered schemes. The proposed method offers a practical solution for efficient and reliable control of nonlinear fuzzy systems under constrained communication networks.

title: Multi-Layered Cross-Domain Data Governance Framework to Ensure Privacy Compliance and Ethical Management in Highly Interconnected Smart City Systems
authors: S.Monika, Monika Khatkar, Abhishek Bhatt, Rishab Pastariya, Kathiresan Jayabalan, Aakanksha Upadhyay
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The area of smart cities is becoming critically dependent on interlocked infrastructures that are producing enormous, heterogeneous, and cross-disciplinary datasets in the wake of rapid urban digitalization. The current paper presents an original Multi-Layered Cross-Domain Data Governance Framework (MLCD-Gov) which can be used to establish privacy amenability, ethical handling of data, and the legal conformity in a cross-sector organization like transportation, healthcare, utilities, and public security. To handle the issues of interoperability, accountability as well as management of consent, the suggested framework combines decentralized policy enforcement, blockchain-based audit trail, adaptive privacy-preserving models, and AI-based anomaly detection. It comes with an agile trust assessment engine with a capability of constantly tracking data provenance and stakeholder activities across the domains. The compliance traceability gained was 27 percent, the number of incidents of unauthorized access fell by 34 percent, and the effectiveness of policy enforcement rose by 21 percent, compared to baseline models of governance, through experimental validation against actual smart city datasets. The outcomes are defensible to the strength of the framework in delivering sensitive data security alongside facilitation of the corresponding data sharing within the interdependent and complex urban environment.

title: Analyzing Genetic Diabetes Trends in the U.S. Using Data Mining-Driven Prediction Models
authors: Ekramul Hasan, Dipta Paul
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Diabetes continues to be one of the most significant causes of illness and death in the United States, affecting individuals across diverse demographic groups. While various clinical and lifestyle factors contribute to the disease, hereditary influence remains a key determinant in genetically driven diabetes cases. This study investigates diabetes that arises through familial inheritance and develops a prediction framework using data-mining techniques. Machine learning methods such as classification, clustering, regression, and decision analysis were applied to evaluate familial diabetes data spanning two generations. The model identifies individuals with genetic susceptibility based on parental and grandparental history and further predicts insulin dependency patterns according to gender and age. The results offer valuable insights for early detection and improved healthcare planning for genetically influenced diabetes. However, the approach applies only to genetically inherited cases and does not extend to lifestyle or pathology-driven diabetes. The proposed framework supports same-day predictive analysis and contributes to more informed public health strategies in the U.S.

title: Interpretable Fall Detection System for the Elderly Using LSTM and Attention Mechanism on WEDA-FALL dataset
authors: Ravija Pasalkar, Aditi Yawale, Rujuta Kulkarni, Divya Rupa, Chaitali Chandankhede
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Elderly people with impaired mobility often get injured due to unintentional falls. For immediate medical assistance and to reduce potential complications, early & precise detection of such incidents is crucial. A Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) based method for fall detection using sensor data gathered from accelerometer and gyroscope devices is presented in this study.We have selected LSTM Networks due to their capacity to extract contextual information and temporal dependencies from sequential data as these qualities are essential for differentiating between typical activity and fall events. Our model classifies each sequence as a fall or non-fall event based on 13-channel time-series inputs sampled over 500 time steps. Additionally, we have an alert system integrated in the architecture that notifies the caregiver on their registered WhatsApp number when a fall occurs. This marks the USP of our system, making it easy-to-use & cost effective as compared to traditional CCTVs or usage of dedicated devices.

title: An Explainable Ensemble of Transfer Learning Models for Plant Leaf Disease Detection in Bangladeshi Crops
authors: Saima Khan, Sumaya Ibrahim, Md. JobayerAhmed, S.M. Tanvir Anjum Fahim
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Crop illness is life-threatening for agricultural productivity and global food availability. Detecting these diseases using traditional methods is frequently time-consuming, costly, and prone to human error. This paper proposes an advanced deep learning framework for accurate and interpretable plant leaf disease detection, specifically focusing on widely grown crops in Bangladesh. Our contributions include developing an ensemble deep learning model that achieves 97% accuracy across 21 distinct classes. We addressed the multiclass imbalance problem and handled diverse datasets through extensive data augmentation techniques. Our method uses five pre-trained deep learning models-EfficientNet-B3, DenseNet-121, MobileNet-V2, ResNet-50, and Inception-V3-to learn how to recognize leaf diseases. After training each model, we picked the three that performed best and combined their predictions with a softvoting ensemble, which boosted the overall accuracy. To make the system’s decisions understandable, we added an Explainable AI component using LIME (Local Interpretable Model-Agnostic Explanations), which highlights the parts of the leaf image that most influence each prediction, giving users clear insight into how the model reaches its conclusions. The proposed system is demonstrated through a React frontend web application, allowing users to upload leaf images from the mendeley leaf disease dataset for disease detection. The model was developed and tested using a custom dataset containing 12,786 images of both diseased and healthy leaves from six commonly grown vegetables in Bangladesh, including Bitter Gourd, Bottle Gourd, Tomatoes, Eggplants, Cauliflowers, and Cucumbers, with a 70% training split, 20% for testing, and 10% for validation.

title: Deep Learning-Based Denoising for QPSK Signals Using LSTM Networks
authors: Chuhang Zhou
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: In wireless communication systems, channel noise significantly degrades signal quality, leading to increased bit error rate (BER) and reduced system performance. To address the limited denoising capability of traditional filtering methods in non-stationary noise environments, this paper proposes a QPSK signal denoising method based on Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) neural networks. The proposed method leverages the LSTM's ability to model long-term dependencies in time-series signals, learning the feature differences between noise and useful signals to achieve end-to-end signal recovery. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed LSTM-based denoising method significantly outperforms the traditional Savitzky-Golay filtering method in terms of BER performance<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">[1]</sup>, effectively recovering signal phase and amplitude information without complex parameter tuning, and exhibits good generalization capability. In the future, this method can be extended to denoising of higher-order modulation formats (such as 16QAM, 64QAM), and combined with channel coding techniques for joint optimization, providing new technical ideas for signal processing in intelligent communication systems.

title: Personalized Learning Path Recommendation in Mechanical Design Via Knowledge Graph Embedding and Prerequisite Reasoning
authors: Yufan Liu, Yuxiang Ye, Yukang Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: The Fundamentals of Mechanical Design course imposes a significant cognitive load on students due to its dense information and complex prerequisite dependencies. posing significant challenges for personalized instruction. Conventional recommender systems often suffer from data sparsity and a lack of transparency regarding the logical progression of engineering concepts. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a Knowledge-Aware Learning Path Recommender System (KLPRS) based on a domain-specific Knowledge Graph. By utilizing Knowledge Graph Embedding (KGE) techniques-specifically TransE and RotatE-we map the semantic relationships between physical components, failure theories, and mathematical constraints into a unified vector space. We further introduce a Prerequisite Reasoning Mechanism that analyzes student performance data to identify latent knowledge gaps. Experimental results conducted on a simulated engineering curriculum dataset demonstrate that the proposed system outperforms standard collaborative filtering baselines in terms of Hit Ratio (HR) and Normalized Discounted Cumulative Gain (NDCG). More importantly, the system provides explainable learning trajectories that strictly adhere to the pedagogical logic of mechanical engineering. This work demonstrates the potential of Knowledge Graphs to transform static educational resources into adaptive, intelligent tutoring environments.

title: Transforming Agriculture with Machine Learning: A Study of Imaging Technologies and Disease Management
authors: Ronak Nayak, Ankit Kaushik, Kunal Karole, Rekha Kaushik
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Impressively, modern agriculture engages in the superior management of crop health and yield. Precision agriculture, however, aims at having data and technology work towards the optimization of field-level management. This paper will look into the use of image analysis in crop monitoring. It considers some imaging technologies, including RGB cameras, multispectral imaging, and thermal imaging, for identification of crop health, pest infestation, and nutrient deficiencies. The paper also examines machine learning algorithms for the processing and analysis of these images and the merits and demerits of each.

title: Neural Network-Based Soft Sensor for Color Prediction in Turbid Media: Application to Agro-Food Quality Control
authors: Yassine Hachaïchi, Elyes Maherzi, Chiheb Naffeti
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: Predicting the real color of turbid liquids is a technical challenge. Since there is no analytic formulas to compute the noise made by the suspended particles, data driven models are the natural alternative to generate a model that can make this task. In this paper, we study the Neural Network regressor model to make this prediction. This data driven approach acts as non-linear observer, allowing real-time monitoring of quality in industrial process where physical sensors are limited by turbidity. The proposed model demonstrates high accuracy, with performance evaluated using the CIEDE2000 ΔE_00 metric, proving its viability for online quality control.

title: An Adaptive Machine Learning Approach for Real-Time Anomaly Detection in Unified Payment Transactions
authors: S. Jagan, K.Vijaya Lakshmi, G.Sasi Sankar Reddy
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: In India, the rapid proliferation of digital payment services has led to increased reliance on the Unified Payments Interface (UPI). Although UPI enables transactions to happen quickly and conveniently, there is an increased risk of all types of evolving fraudulent activity. Traditional fraud detection methods based on static rules may not be able to keep pace with adaptive fraud patterns. This work presents a machine learning-based framework to identify anomalous actions indicative of potentially fraudulent UPI transactions. The model will examine user transaction history, learn to identify deviations from a user profile, and detect fraudulent activity. The adaptive nature of machine learning allows the framework to improve its understanding of fraud concepts so that it can increase accuracy over time. This work is intended to analyze the application of Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) through the restructuring of the feature of transactions into structured representations fit in a neurological network. This deep learning model can understand a complex, high dimensional pattern of transactions over competitive fraud detection techniques unable to detect these nuanced patterns. The proposed framework will support real-time, dynamic fraud detection, therefore improving security, decreasing the financial impact of fraud, and increasing confidence in the adoption of new digital payments.

title: Defensive Architectures for LLM-Based Fraud Detection Against Data Poisoning and Prompt Injection
authors: Divija Vijaya Ganiga
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: The increasing adoption of Large Language Models (LLMs) in financial fraud detection has enhanced institutional capabilities for identifying sophisticated and dynamic fraudulent activities within large-scale transaction systems. However, the implementation of LLM-driven systems introduces critical security vulnerabilities, particularly data poisoning during training and prompt injection attacks during inference, which threaten the reliability and integrity of automated fraud detection. This paper proposes a Resilient Defensive Architecture for LLMbased Fraud Detection (RDA-LLM), a multi-layered framework designed to protect against adversarial manipulation while maintaining high detection accuracy. The proposed architecture integrates data sanitization, semantic prompt purification, auxiliary adversarial detection, ensemble-based validation, and uncertainty-aware decision gating into a unified defensive framework. Extensive experiments on the Kaggle Credit Card Fraud Detection dataset demonstrate that RDA-LLM outperforms state-of-the-art LLM-based fraud detection models, including LLM40FD and LLM-MalDetect, across several key performance measures such as accuracy, precision, recall, false positive rate, and resistance to adversarial attacks. The results confirm that RDA-LLM provides enhanced resilience without prohibitive inference latency overhead, making it suitable for realtime deployment. This work contributes to enhancing the stability, security, and technological advancement of U.S. financial systems by developing secure, reliable, and scalable solutions that mitigate critical security vulnerabilities in LLM-based systems.

title: NeuroSonoNet: A Deep Learning Enhanced Ultrasound Nerve Segmentation Systems
authors: Munmi Gogoi, Paromita Goswami, Sunit Kumar, Gaurav Kumar Bharti, Ibrar Jahan M A
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The brachial plexus, an intricate network of neural pathways, plays a crucial role in the innervation of the upper limbs and is highly significant in anesthetic practices. The precise and automated segmentation of the brachial plexus from ultrasound imagery is imperative for augmenting the accuracy of nerve localization, thereby facilitating enhanced patient outcomes and ensuring safety during the administration of anesthesia. This investigation seeks to fulfill the pivotal aim of segmenting the brachial plexus nerve from cervical ultra-sound images for anesthetic applications. The approach utilizes advanced methodologies in image processing and machine learning, strategically designed to address the complexities associated with cervical ultrasound data. The framework amalgamates conventional image processing techniques with leading-edge deep learning architectures, with a particular emphasis on convolutional neural networks (CNNs), to autonomously learn and de-lineate the anatomical structures of the brachial plexus nerve. The developmental phase encompasses comprehensive training and validation on a specialized dataset of cervical ultrasound images, which have been meticulously annotated to provide ground truth segmentations of the brachial plexus.

title: Deep Learning-Based Soil Quality Prediction Framework for Sustainable Agriculture in Bangladesh
authors: Sadikul Hasan Mridha Atul, Abdullah Al Zabir, S M Monirul Islam, Humayun Kabir Forhad Hai, S M Nawshed, Md. Isthiakul Hasan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Soil quality is a key determinant of agricultural productivity and food security. In Bangladesh, agriculture contributes nearly $\mathbf{1 2. 7 \%}$ of GDP and employs about $\mathbf{4 0 \%}$ of the workforce, yet soil fertility is declining due to intensive cropping, unbalanced fertilizer use, and organic matter depletion. National surveys by the Soil Resource Development Institute (SRDI) indicate that around $\mathbf{6 0 \%}$ of cultivable land suffers from multiple nutrient deficiencies. To address these challenges, this study presents a Deep Learning-Based Soil Quality Prediction (DL-SQP) framework tailored for Bangladesh’s agro-ecological zones. The framework integrates chemical ($\mathbf{p H}$, organic carbon, $\mathbf{N}, \mathbf{P}, \mathbf{K}$), physical (moisture), and environmental (temperature, humidity, rainfall, wind speed) parameters to evaluate soil fertility. Using a Deep Neural Network Regression (DNNR) model with ten-fold cross-validation, soil samples were categorized into six fertility levels (Very Low to Maximum). Although the model uses a regression-based approach, it performs ordinal classification by mapping continuous fertility scores into discrete classes. The proposed method achieved 96.7% accuracy, outperforming ANN $(88 \%)$, SVM (90.5%), and KNN $(85 \%)$. The district-level Soil Quality Reports (SQRs) demonstrate the model’s practical utility in sustainable fertilizer management.

title: Smart Notes Artificial Intelligence: Summaries, Flashcards and Advanced Study
authors: G Senthil Kumar, Balaraman Sundarambal, N Kirubakaran, M Vaishnavee, R Nithyashree, R Sivakuomar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: The swift increase in digital learning materials has contributed to information overload and disjointed studying processes among the students. It is suggested that Smart Notes AI is an intelligent academic assistant that converts unstructured learning content into a structured summary, flashcards, and custom learning plans. The system combines the natural language processing, multimodal content ingestion, and adaptive learning methods to enhance knowledge retention and learning performance. Streaks, points, and progress tracking are among gamification elements that are implemented to increase engagement. It has been experimentally shown that there is an enhanced recall, less cognitive load, and increased consistency of study. The suggested system offers an efficient and scalable system to contemporary AI-enhanced education.

title: CPGNet: Centre Prior-Guided Network for 3D Instance Segmentation of Multi-Plant Point Clouds
authors: Yuwei Hao, Fang Qu, He Huang, Youqiang Sun, Caiguo Tang, Junqing Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: High-throughput phenotyping of rice relies on accurate 3D instance segmentation, a task fundamentally challenged by point attribution ambiguity when dealing with heavily occluded, co-located plants. This paper introduces CPGNet, a novel Centre Prior-Guided Network that explicitly tackles this ambiguity. To facilitate robust evaluation, we also present the Grouped Rice Point Cloud Dataset (GRPCD), a new benchmark of grouped rice plants grown in close proximity, reconstructed with high fidelity using Neural Radiance Fields (NeRF). At the core of CPGNet is a Mamba2-based backbone for global context modelling and a Centre Prior-Guided Decoder that anchors each instance query to its geometric centre, effectively disambiguating spatial overlaps. Extensive experiments on GRPCD validate the superiority of our approach. CPGNet sets a new state-of-theart with an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F 1_{50}$</tex> of 73.6%, a significant 13.1 percentage point improvement over the OneFormer3D baseline. This research delivers a powerful and practical solution for instance segmentation in cluttered indoor agricultural settings, paving the way for nextgeneration automated rice phenotyping.

title: Diff-Conformer: A Dual-Input Difference-Aware Convolutional Transformer Network for Building Change Detection
authors: Shengchang Li, Shuo Chen, Zipeng Fan, Zeyu Li, Zishuo Zhang, Xinyu Yang, Sihao Zhang
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Change detection in remote sensing images analyzes and characterizes surface changes using multi-temporal data. Yet, in complex scenes with diverse targets, deep-learning methods often yield features that lack sufficient discrimination, leading to false alarms and lost details. To solve these challenges, we introduce Diff-Conformer, a building-change detector that unites Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and transformer strengths in a single Conv-Transformer-Branch (CTB), capturing fine-to-coarse details in one shot. Moreover, decoder parts use R-Decoder, enhancing the extraction of high-frequency change cues such as edges. Our model achieved F1-scores of 90.15% on the LEVIR-CD dataset, 86.75% on the WHU-CD dataset, and 79.48% on the SYSU-CD dataset, demonstrating superior performance compared to state-of-the-art methods.

title: MADGCN: a Meteorology-Aware Spatio-Temporal Graph Convolution Network for Long-term Air Pollution Forecasting
authors: Binwu Wang, Zhiqing Cui, Guanjun Wang, Zhengyang Zhou, Fan Meng, Jingjia Luo, Yang Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering
abstract: Air quality forecasting has attracted increasing attention as global air pollution worsens. Spatiotemporal graph neural networks have become a leading paradigm, thanks to their ability to capture complex spatial and temporal dynamics in Air Quality Index (AQI) data. However, existing methods remain limited by weak modeling of long-range temporal dependencies and insufficient integration of meteorological factors. Building on a publicly available nationwide air quality dataset spanning eight years, we propose MADGCN, a Meteorology-Aware Decoupled Spatio-Temporal Convolutional Network that jointly addresses long-horizon temporal modeling and meteorological context fusion. MADGCN includes a dynamic causality discovery module grounded in Granger causality, which captures time-varying causal relationships between meteorological conditions and AQI dynamics. The inferred causal structures further guide a causal graph convolution module and a PatchMixer module, enabling effective spatial interaction modeling and multiscale temporal dependency learning. Extensive experiments against 16 strong baselines show that MADGCN achieves competitive performance for long-horizon air pollution forecasting and generalizes well under high-pollution regimes.

title: Interleaved Three-Phase Series-Capacitor Boost Converter with Integrated Charge-Pump and Full-Range Current Sharing
authors: Yang Qin, Dylan Dah-Chuan Lu, Yam P. Siwakoti
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: The conventional three-phase series-capacitor (TPSC) boost converter offers high voltage gain, low current stress, and a common-ground configuration, but requires complex current-sharing control and exhibits severe performance degradation at duty cycles below 2/3, including increased voltage stress, distorted inductor currents, elevated input current ripple, and degraded current-sharing accuracy. To overcome these limitations, this paper establishes the essential conditions for accurate automatic current sharing over the full duty-cycle range—namely, a standard two-segment inductor current waveform and a unique current path for each inductor–diode pair. Guided by these conditions, an improved TPSC boost converter is realized by hybridizing a conventional boost with two charge-pump cells using voltage-source charging paths and clamping diodes. This distinctive architecture ensures that, under continuous conduction mode (CCM) with symmetrical control, the proposed converter maintains a voltage gain of 3/(1-d), high-precision automatic current sharing, and a clamped device voltage stress of Uo/3 across the full duty-cycle range. Despite the introduction of two additional diodes and capacitors, the higher efficiency and significantly reduced input current ripple are achieved. Experimental results from a 1000-W, 50-kHz prototype (40-100V input, 400V output) demonstrate a peak efficiency of 98.5%.

title: Redefining Vitiligo Severity Assessment: Development and Validation of VEIDI, a Multifaceted Objective Scoring Framework
authors: Anjali Thachangattil, Remya Sethulekshmi, Soumya Jagadeesan
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Vitiligo is a chronic depigmenting disorder characterized by progressive melanocyte loss, resulting in irregular hypopigmented or depigmented lesions. Despite its high prevalence and psychosocial burden, clinical assessment remains largely subjective and insufficiently standardized. Conventional evaluation techniques and existing scoring systems, such as VASI and VETF, are labor-intensive, observer-dependent, and limited in reproducibility. This study introduces VEIDI (Vitiligo Extent, Intensity, and Distribution Index), a comprehensive and objective scoring framework for vitiligo severity assessment. VEIDI integrates illumination-normalized image enhancement using Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization (CLAHE), intensity-driven lesion segmentation, lesion-wise geometric characterization, and spatial distribution analysis to quantify disease burden in a standardized and interpretable manner. Clinical evaluation across multiple longitudinal sessions demonstrated high agreement with expert dermatologists, achieving an intraclass correlation coefficient (ICC) of 0.957, a root mean squared error (RMSE) of 0.285, and 99.6% agreement within±1 severity grade. On CPU-only hardware (Intel Core i7, 16GB RAM), VEIDI required 1.18 ± 0.16 s per image for end-to-end automated inference, supporting its practical suitability for near real-time clinical use. VEIDI demonstrates reliability comparable to reported inter-dermatologist agreement levels while significantly reducing evaluation time and observer variability. By providing a robust, reproducible, and clinically interpretable severity score, VEIDI offers a practical framework for objective vitiligo assessment with strong potential for routine clinical use, longitudinal monitoring, and standardized outcome reporting in clinical trials.

title: Integration of Artificial Intelligence and Edge Computing for Adaptive Cyber-Physical Systems in Mission-Critical Environments
authors: Rajanikanth Sanku, Shreenidhi B S, Durga Bramarambika Sailaja Varri, Jaya Chandra Myla, Syed Riyaz Ahammed, Bachina Harish Babu
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The Cyber-Physical Systems (CPS) have been advancing fast becoming the cornerstone of mission-critical conditions, including healthcare, autonomous transportation, defense, and industry automation. The increasing complexity in these systems requires real time decision making, low latency, and adaptative intelligence in dealing with uncertain and dynamic situations. Edge Computing and Artificial Intelligence (AI) can offer an efficient framework to satisfy all these rigorous demands because it facilitates the concept of localized intelligence, less reliance on centralized cloud computing models, and high reliability. Under a current system, computation-intensive AI models are typically implemented on cloud infrastructures, which leads to high latency of communication, bandwidth usage, and sensitivity to network failures. Such centralized schemes are not scalable and do not ensure the ultra-low latency and high reliability that are required by mission-critical functions. More so, the conventional CPS designs have no adaptive behavior to dynamically optimize the allocation of resources and resilience in the face of unpredictable workloads or environmental variations. The system being proposed combines adaptive learning with distributed edge computing that is based on AI to improve the efficiency of CPS. The proposed system records above 90 % almost on all parameters whereas other methods remain less than 80 %. This proves that it is better fitted in its adaptability, security and performance of mission-critical CPS.

title: Quantitative design of perovskite X-ray flat-panel detectors enabled by numerical parameter optimization
authors: Yuhao Zhang, Zimeng Li, Xin Zhang, Xulong Pang, Yihong Qi, Xiaokun Liang, Zonghai Sheng, Dong Liang, Hairong Zheng, Mengling Xia, Yongshuai Ge, Haodi Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Nuclear Science
abstract: Metal halide perovskites have emerged as promising candidates for direct X-ray detection owing to their strong attenuation, excellent charge transport, and low-temperature processability . High X-ray absorption efficiency requires thick perovskite layers, while increased thickness simultaneously raises the probability of charge carriers being trapped by defects, leading to an inherent trade-off. Achieving an optimal balance therefore requires careful optimization of key parameters such as absorber thickness and operating electric field. However, recent studies have not systematically optimized these detection parameters. Here, we establish a simulation framework based on cascaded linear system theory to systematically optimize the performance of MAPbI<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>-based X-ray detectors. The model quantifies the trade-off between X-ray absorption and charge collection, identifying an optimal thickness of 460 μm and an electric-field range that maximizes sensitivity while preserving spatial resolution. Guided by the simulation, we fabricate pixelated detectors integrated with thin-film transistor backplanes. The optimized detector exhibits a high sensitivity of 6588 μC·Gy<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">air</sub><sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup>·cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-2</sup> and a spatial resolution of 3.23 lp/mm at a 200 μm pixel size, in good agreement with theoretical predictions. Furthermore, the detector demonstrates high-quality two-dimensional and three-dimensional imaging capabilities, highlighting its potential for advanced medical and industrial X-ray imaging applications.

title: Dual Layer Authentication System for Deepfake Detection and Lie Analysis using Artificial Intelligence
authors: S Amutha, Sankuratri Venkata Varun Krishna Chowdary, Namburi Surya Mahesh Varma
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: State-of-the-art generative artificial intelligence has made the generation of highly realistic deepfake media more accessible than ever, which threatens modern identity verification systems based on visual evidence alone. To handle this issue, we propose a two-layer authentication framework by jointly verifying the media authenticity and user behavior to ensure the remote verification security. The first layer is a modified convolutional neural network (CNN) with spatial attention that can recognize manipulated visual contents.Authenticated media is further sent through a bidirectional long short-term memory network to extract facial micro-expressions and speech characteristics in time for the evaluation of behavioral consistency. Our method achieves 96.3% accuracy in deepfake detection and 94.8% overall authentication accuracy with a false acceptance rate of 2.1% on the benchmark datasets. Ablation experiments validate the effectiveness of each module and cross-dataset evaluations demonstrate that the performance drop is lower than 7% on unseen data.Its end-to-end processing latency is 1.8 s, and it can be deployed in real-time scenarios (e.g., cloud-based exam, remote recruitment and high-trust access control systems).

title: Secure Network Function Computation for Linear Functions, Part II: Target-Function Security
authors: Yang Bai, Xuan Guang, Raymond W. Yeung
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Theory
abstract: In Part I of this two-part paper, we put forward the problem of secure network function computation.We investigated securely computing linear target functions with a wiretapper who can eavesdrop any edge subset up to a certain size r, referred to as the security level, where the security function is the identity function, i.e., we need to prevent the source messages from being leaked to the wiretapper. The notion of this security is called source security. In the current Part II of the two-part paper, we consider another interesting model which is the same as the above one except that the security function is identical to the target function, i.e., we need to prevent the target function from being leaked to the wiretapper. The notion of this security is called target-function security. We first prove a non-trivial upper bound on the secure computing capacity defined as the maximum average number of times that the target function can be securely computed with zero error at the sink node for one use of the network. The upper bound obtained is applicable to arbitrary network topologies and arbitrary security levels. In particular, when the security level r is equal to 0, the upper bound reduces to the computing capacity without security consideration. This upper bound is always not less than the upper bound obtained in Part I for source security, which accords with the relation between the secure computing capacities for target-function security and source security. We further obtain a bound on the gap between the two upper bounds, and show that the gap between the two upper bounds can be unbounded. Furthermore, we present an algebraic framework for linear (function-computing) secure codes for the target-function-security model by proving two equivalent conditions for computability and target-function security, respectively. With this framework, we develop a construction of linear secure codes for the target-function-security model and thus obtain a lower bound on the secure computing capacity. In the same spirit, we also prove an equivalent algebraic condition for source security, and so obtain an algebraic framework for linear secure codes for the source-security model. With this, we not only generalize the code construction developed in Part I for the source-security model but also can present it in a more succinct and understandable way. In addition, motivated by the fact that a source-secure code is target-function-secure but not vice versa, we compare our code construction for the target-function-security model and the generalized code construction for the source-security model, and show that the codes constructed by the generalized code construction for source security are only a very special subclass of the codes constructed by the code construction for target-function security. Further, a considerably smaller field size can be used for our code construction for target-function security compared with the generalized code construction for source security.

title: Demand- and Topology-Aware Resource Allocation in Non-Terrestrial Networks (NTNs) with Multi-Satellite Beam Hopping
authors: Samuel Martínez Zamacola, Israel Leyva-Mayorga, Ramón Martínez Rodríguez-Osorio, Petar Popovski
publish: IEEE Transactions on Communications
abstract: NGSO constellations, represented by LEO and MEO satellites, require resource-allocation frameworks that jointly address payload flexibility and communication performance. This work presents a hierarchical three-stage framework for resource allocation: demand-driven satellite cell coloring (SCC), topology-aware satellite–cell association (S2C), and multi-satellite beam hopping (BH). Our framework halves the downlink power required to reach 10% unserved capacity (UC) and reduces the peak load on inter-satellite links (ISLs) by approximately 40% compared to a baseline strategy, while controlling cell handovers. The results further show that architectural parameters dominate performance: increasing the number of beams, refining cell granularity, and scaling constellation density enable approximately 2×, 10×, and 50× reductions in power consumption, respectively. These reductions in the required downlink (DL) power and inter-satellite link (ISL) load for a given performance target can be directly translated into system-level gains at payload or constellation size level. The proposed framework provides a scalable foundation for end-to-end optimization of next-generation NGSO satellite networks.

title: Regional Differences in Sentinel-3 Radar Backscatter over Greenland and its Impact on Ice Sheet Altimetry Applications
authors: Stine Kildegaard Rose, Louise Sandberg Sørensen, Kirk M. Scanlan, Jérémie Aublanc, Filomena Catapano, Alessandro Di Bella, Pierre Féménias, Sebastian B. Simonsen
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Satellite radar altimetry operating at Ku-band has provided crucial observations of ice sheet elevation change for more than three decades, offering a direct means to assess climate impacts. Surface height is derived from radar waveforms using retracking algorithms, while waveform parameters such as the backscatter coefficient and leading-edge characteristics are commonly used to identify surface melt and complex topography. Because elevation estimates are sensitive to these parameters, corrections are required to reduce their influence. We investigate temporal variations in the Ku-band radar backscatter coefficient from Sentinel-3 over two regions of the Greenland Ice Sheet. A northern region, which generally exhibits stable backscatter conditions; however, during the summer of 2023, an anomalous decrease of 16 dB was observed, deviating from its typical behaviour. This unexpected signal motivates a comparative analysis with a southwestern region, where a pronounced and recurring seasonal cycle is observed, characterised by a 16 dB increase in backscatter and a concurrent 26 m decrease in elevation in 2023, robustly linked to summer surface melt. The contrasting responses between the two regions are interpreted in terms of differences in meltwater retention and percolation, which control the balance between surface and volume scattering, as quantified using statistical and geophysical waveform parameters. Incorporating a backscatter-height correlation coefficient into the elevation change algorithm improves agreement with ICESat-2 in the southwestern region. These results highlight the need for region- and process-specific adaptations in retracking strategies to ensure robust elevation retrievals under increasingly variable surface and climate conditions.

title: Candidates Announced for Board of Governors Election
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Communications Magazine
abstract: Dear ComSoc Members, In the following paragraphs, please find position statements and biographies of an outstanding slate of 2026 candidates to lead the IEEE Communications Society.

title: CFP: Quantum-Enabled AI for Future Communications
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Communications Magazine

title: Projected Gradient System-Based Trust-Tech Method for Computing Multiple ACOPF Solutions: Theory and Deterministic Solution Methodology
authors: Hsiao-Dong Chiang, Tengmu Li, Zhi-Yuan Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Systems
abstract: A theory-based Projected Gradient System (PGS)-based Transformation Under Stability-reTaining Equilibria Characterization (Trust-Tech, TT) method, termed PGS-TT, is proposed to systematically and deterministically compute multiple AC Optimal Power Flow (ACOPF) solutions within a feasible component. The theoretical basis for the PGS-TT method is rigorously established, including: (i) a one-to-one correspondence between stable equilibrium points (SEPs) of the PGS and local optimal solutions of ACOPF models; (ii) each feasible component is an invariant set of the PGS and consists of the closures of the stability regions of the SEPs located in that feasible component; (iii) each stability region contains exactly one OPF solution, which eliminates redundant searches for additional OPF solutions within the same stability region; and (iv) the existence conditions of multiple OPF solutions that are linked to the existence of PGS stability boundaries. Theorems and the proposed PGS-TT method are illustrated on 3-bus and 9-bus systems exhibiting multiple local OPF solutions. Additionally, the scalability of the implemented PGS-TT method is discussed based on large-scale systems, with network sizes ranging from 1,888 to 30,000 buses, underscoring its potential for complex power-grid optimization.

title: Beat Suppression Strategy Based on EEMF Harmonic Reconstruction for Electrolytic Capacitorless PMSM Drives
authors: Dawei Ding, Yuanyang Chen, Binxing Li, Haijun Xie, Chao Luan, Gaolin Wang, Qiwei Wang, Guangdong Bi, Dianguo Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification
abstract: In electrolytic capacitorless permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM) drives, when the harmonic component of the position fluctuation is in a canceling relationship with the corresponding harmonic introduced by the DC-link voltage fluctuation, simply suppressing the position fluctuation aggravates the beat phenomenon. This article proposes a beat suppression strategy based on extended back electromotive force (EEMF) harmonic reconstruction. Small signal analysis reveals the impedance relationship between position compensation and beat current, quantitatively clarifying the nonlinear increment of beat currents. Specifically, multiple complex filtering is utilized to extract EEMF harmonics induced by DC-link voltage fluctuations. By feeding back the electrical angular frequency as the center frequency, the right sideband harmonic component is reconstructed for position estimation based on established current and EEMF frequency characteristics. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed strategy is validated on an experimental platform, verifying beat suppression under various speed and torque conditions.

title: A Mechanically Robust Hybrid-Structured 3-D Printed Fresnel Zone Lens Antenna
authors: Xincheng Gao, Chen Zhao, Enqi He, Xiaoyi Liao, Yan Li
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: This letter proposes a hybrid-structured Fresnel lens (HSFL) tailored for 3-D printing. It addresses the low dielectric infill density and mechanical fragility of the outer subzones within the same full-wave zone in the conventional 3-D printed multi-dielectric Fresnel lens (MDFL). Static mechanical simulations are first conducted to identify a unit cell for 3-D printing with superior mechanical properties. Subsequently, based on the derived height-infill density relationship, the outer subzone height is moderately reduced to significantly enhance the mechanical strength. To validate this design methodology, a prototype operating at 31 GHz was designed and fabricated. Simulation and experimental results demonstrate that compared to the conventional MDFL, the proposed HSFL reduces the axial and lateral von Mises stresses by 60% and 43%, respectively, while maintaining equivalent electromagnetic performance.

title: FANet: Fovea Attention Network for Robust Aerial Geo-localization Across Diverse Weather Conditions
authors: Jiahao Wen, Hang Yu, Zhedong Zheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing
abstract: Cross-view geo-localization between drone and satellite images is severely challenged by rapid weather variations, which induce appearance shifts, occlusions, and texture degradation. Inspired by human foveal attention, we propose the Fovea Attention Network (FANet), a robust dual-branch framework comprising: (1) The Weather-Adaptive Global Branch (WAGB) that explicitly injects weather cues (e.g., ‘rain/snow’) into the feature space via a style-modulation encoder, then captures large-scale structural consistency through a Learnable Region Reassembly (LRR) mechanism; (2) The Local Semantic Attention Branch (LSAB) that leverages a pretrained segmentation model to generate high-confidence masks, distilling discriminative features from salient regions. An adaptive fusion strategy module fuses global context with fine-grained semantic cues. We further adopt multi-weather adaptive training, treating weather types as related tasks with shared parameters to reduce cross-weather confounding. Extensive experiments on University-1652, SUES-200, and CVUSA show FANet achieves competitive Recall@1 across all conditions, attaining the highest overall mean with the lowest variance. Notably, it improves Recall@1 by 6.79% under severe low-illumination (‘dark’) conditions, demonstrating robustness and stability.

title: Rise of AI and Machine Learning in India and Its Legal Impact on Evidentiary Norms and Judicial Proof
authors: Kosha Vishvas Zaveri
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The dynamic and rapidly growing assimilation of artificial intelligence and machine learning into the Indian forensic, administrative, and adjudicatory workflows necessitates the reevaluation of the rules of evidence and the different methods of proving. Presentation of a new Multi-Layered Evidentiary Reasoning Framework (MERF) is suggested in this paper that incorporates all three computational, probabilistic and normative reasoning to evaluate how algorithmic output renders new forms of reliability and admissibility as traditional paradigm shifts. The framework presents an Algorithmic Transparency Index (ATI) for quantitatively expressing the level of interpretability, reproducibility, and traceability of AI-generated evidence, which is connecting the technical notion of transparency with the legal notion of relevance. Further to this, complementing this, a Probabilistic Proof Calibration (PPC) model recalculates judicial reasoning as a Bayesian inference procedure in which machine-generated results are taken as probabilistic hints to be seen upon, and not as definitive proofs. The models are used in the study with simulated datasets as to human-AI evidentiary scenarios that are mixed in terms of their hybrid nature, and how different levels of algorithmic reliability will affect judicial decision-making thresholds. In this integrative one, MERF is expected to create a systematic procedure of assessing the epistemic and normative validity of AI-helped evidence in the context of Indian jurisprudence. The results can be taken as a point of trying to strike a balance in computational accuracy and a collection of fundamental principles with law as such that, with advances in technologies, the reliability of the purity of judicial evidence and impartial justice can be improved instead of being undermined. The proposed MERF achieved maximum accuracy of 94 percent meaning that it is more effective in interpretation of the evidence and reliable to the juridical reasoning as compared to the existing models.

title: Stabilizing Cloud Elastic Scaling with Risk-Constrained Reinforcement Learning Under Workload Drift
authors: Wen Huang, Ruoxuan Wei, Junnan Kou, Hong Zhuang, Xu Yan, Wenyou Huang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Elastic scaling in cloud native environments is essential for maintaining service quality and resource efficiency. In practice, frequent traffic bursts and shifts in workload distributions make rule-based methods or approaches with a single optimization objective insufficient. They struggle to ensure system stability and decision reliability at the same time. To address this challenge, this study formulates elastic scaling as a risk-constrained reinforcement learning problem from a sequential decision perspective. A unified framework is used to model resource adjustment actions, system state evolution, and potential instability costs. By explicitly incorporating risk constraints into policy optimization, the proposed approach achieves a dynamic balance between performance optimization and safety control. It prevents service level objective violations and system oscillations caused by aggressive decisions. Resource utilization efficiency and service response behavior are jointly considered, which improves consistency and controllability in complex cloud environments. Comparative evaluation based on real cloud cluster traces shows advantages in service reliability, response performance, and resource usage over existing baselines. These results confirm the effectiveness of risk-aware decision-making under non-stationary workloads. This work provides a systematic modeling approach for the safe application of reinforcement learning in cloud resource management and lays a methodological foundation for stable and efficient intelligent elastic scaling.

title: A Multimodal Data Fusion Model for Solar Radiation Prediction and Its Application in Smart Grids
authors: Jiapu Yuan, Yajing Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: With the increasing reliance of smart grids on renewable energy, accurate prediction of solar radiation has become crucial for ensuring stable grid operation. This paper proposes a multimodal data fusion model for solar radiation prediction, integrating meteorological observation data, satellite remote sensing images, and historical solar radiation records. Through a Multi-modal Feature Extraction Network (MFEN), efficient data fusion is achieved. First, a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) is used to encode features from satellite remote sensing images; a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network is employed to dynamically model historical time series data; and an attention mechanism is introduced to achieve weighted fusion of multimodal features. Finally, the predicted solar radiation value is output through a fully connected layer. Experiments on the public dataset SolarRadiationData show that the proposed model achieves a root mean square error as low as 41.7 W/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, demonstrating its effectiveness in improving prediction accuracy and robustness. This method provides data-driven support for renewable energy dispatch and optimization in smart grids.

title: Preprocessing-Oriented EEG Analysis for Measuring Stress and Anxiety Alleviation curtailment by Mantra Meditation
authors: Vandana Roy, Piyush Kumar Shukla
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Electroencephalography (EEG) is becoming the key technique of researching mental states, particularly when studies focus on stress and anxiety. Regrettably, EEG recordings are routinely contaminated by numerous sources of noise, including power line noise, motion artifacts, burst noise, Electrooculography (EOG), Electromyography (EMG), and Electrocardiography (ECG), which severely affect the quality of the signal. To enhance the information, an efficient EEG preprocessing framework is developed to study the effects of Mantra Chanting on stress and anxiety. The proposed algorithm automated an artifact-classification pipeline that would identify which type of denoising can be best applied. The performance is compared for filters, such as CNF-SFFDF, Butterworth, Kernel Density Estimation with Recursive Kalman Filter, and Sliding Window Adaptive Filtering, in eight channels of EEG signal at the pre-, during, and after-meditation periods. As results indicate, Chebyshev Notch Filter (CNF) + Spatial Filtering with Frequency-Domain Filtering is the best in all cases falling through in peak PSNR (9.13%), SSIM (3.47%), and SNR (38.74%) is improved for Channel Fp1, proving to be quite efficient noise suppression and signal retention. Meditation-induced neurophysiological changes can also be measured accurately with better-quality signals, which also supports the role of mantra meditation in reducing stress. The proposed preprocessing framework offers a solid foundation when it comes to real-time mental health monitoring based on EEG analytics.

title: Distilling the Knowledge of LLMs for Automatic Speech Recognition
authors: Hayato Futami, Tatsuya Kawahara
publish: IEEE Transactions on Audio, Speech and Language Processing
abstract: Recently, applications of large language models (LLMs) to automatic speech recognition (ASR) have been investigated, including post-processing and speech LLMs. In these approaches, ASR can benefit from the pretrained knowledge of LLMs, but suffers from the increased model size and computational cost. Knowledge distillation (KD) is a promising approach that transfers LM knowledge into ASR without increasing the model size and computational cost during inference. While bidirectional predictions of masked LMs perform better than causal LMs as teacher labels, the current mainstream of LLMs is based on causal LM architecture. In this study, we propose pseudo-bidirectional KD to obtain bidirectional teacher labels from a causal LLM by calculating the predicted probabilities of the subsequent context. In conventional KD, the vocabulary of ASR needs to be matched with that of LLM, but this is often impractical because the vocabulary of LLM is often too large for ASR, leading to performance degradation and increased model size. To address this, we also propose cross-vocabulary KD from LLM to ASR by aligning the token sequences and then the token entries. Experimental evaluations on the CSJ, LibriSpeech, and CommonVoice Galician corpora show that the proposed pseudo-bidirectional KD consistently yielded better performance improvement than the conventional KD. We also show the effectiveness of cross-vocabulary KD by adopting a vocabulary of a smaller size for ASR.

title: A Comprehensive Approach to Improving Programming and Problem-Solving Skills Using the HackerRank Platform
authors: Manisha Nirgude, Dipali Awasekar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: With emerging technologies, ability to write efficient code is most important. This study discusses how using HackerRank can improve programming and problem-solving skills, especially in the context of a Design and Analysis of Algorithms (DAA) course. Recognizing the significance of experiential learning and collaborative learning as a pedagogy, a systematic activity was undertaken consisting of daily problem-solving on HackerRank along with discussion sessions. Students shared and solved problems based on algorithms, developing a culture of collaborative learning. The approach included mapping the problems to the DAA curriculum, ensuring the mastery of one programming language. The findings from the interviews and surveys reveal marked improvement in problem-solving abilities, programming skills, and soft skills. This initiative not only prepared students for academic success but also enhanced their placement prospects, leading to successful placements in reputable MNCs. The study highlights the importance of practical, collaborative learning environments in bridging the gap between theoretical knowledge and real-world applications. This activity aligns with the institute’s Outcome Based Education (OBE) practices and Professional Learning Community (PLC) initiatives by promoting technology-enabled, learner-centric strategies to enhance programming skills and learning outcomes.

title: Federated Cloud-Fog Systems: Cost-Minimizing Offloading and Efficient Edge Resource Management for Delay-Sensitive Tasks
authors: M. Rajunaiak, Surabhi Shanker, Ranu Prakash Sharma, Udita Holkar, Ashok Kumar Verma, P. William
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: In the paper, we propose a novel optimization paradigm for offloading and fog resource management in federated cloud fog environments, which is cost efficient. The task allocation framework proposes dynamic task assignment to cloud servers and various types of fog nodes to satisfy strict deadline constraints in task execution for delay sensitive tasks using a coalesced resource pool. The approach achieves high resource utilization with minimum costs by using real time task prioritization, resource distribution, and cost analysis optimization strategies. Performance of the proposed framework is extensively evaluated through both simulations and experiments, which demonstrate bipolar electrical parameters are preferable for the design of energy efficient gels, compared to existing solutions in terms of task completion time, energy efficiency, and overall operational cost. Results are shown to indicate that the model can be used to deploy federated cloud fog systems to next generation IoT and edge computing applications.

title: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)

title: A Hybrid Topology Endfire Antenna Based on Spoof Suface Plasmon Polaritons
authors: Yi Wang, Lingping Zeng, Chao Han, Song Feng, Yanfei Yang, Zebin Li, Lu Liu, Guoqing Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: This proposes a novel high gain endfire antenna based on spoof surface plasmon polaritons (SSPPs). The letter proposes an antenna based on open topology SSPPs configurations and examines its advantages and limitations, and subsequently proposes a hybrid unit configuration, achieving a notable gain enhancement. The antenna employs a coplanar waveguide for feeding and utilizes an asymmetric SSPP structure as the radiator to achieve endfire radiation. Simulation results indicate that the improved design outperforms open topology, achieving higher gain at high frequencies than the open topology prototype.

title: Online Machine Learning Framework for Early-State SoH Prediction in Lithium Iron Phosphate Batteries
authors: HimaBindu Garikapati, Siva Kumari Orsu, Kamala Kumari Duru, Sujith Kalluri
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Reliable estimation of lithium-ion battery State of Health (SoH) during initial phases of operation is crucial to ensure reliability and enable successful use in downstream applications. Conventional offline SoH estimation techniques rely on long-term cycling information and cannot be used for real-time forecasting. Given paper introduces an online early-life SoH prediction model based on the Streaming Random Patches Regressor (SRPRegressor). This incremental ensemble learning algorithm can be updated at the end of every cycle. A 3000 mAh LiFePO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</inf> (LFP) cell was cycled under regulated charge-discharge cycles to produce 180 cycles of working data. Three physically significant features, such as discharge capacity, average discharge voltage, and voltage hysteresis, were obtained per cycle, and actual SoH was calculated based on discharge capacity obtained in each cycle. Data were split into 80% for online training and 20% for online testing, and both phases were implemented in a streaming, cycle-by-cycle manner to simulate live deployment. According to experiment’s results, proposed streaming model yields a correct early-life SoH prediction with a Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 0.0934. These results show that online ensemble learning is suitable for real-time battery health assessments with minimal input features.

title: Advancing Plant Disease Identification through Neural Network Prediction with Interpretable AI Insights
authors: L V Kiran, R. Tamilkodi, A. Harika, J R S V V D Satish, MD A Afrath, CH Manasa
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Diseases that attack plants are very bad for farming because they lower food yields and quality. These diseases have a huge effect on the local and world economies because they make trade and food security much less stable. DL algorithms can now find and classify plant diseases, making it possible to do accurate and automatic research in more complex ways. Nonetheless, their black-box nature generates questions of interpretability and reliability of their predictions, which motivates the incorporation of XAI into generating a user-trustful result when models make predictions This paper addresses these challenges by evaluating a DL-based approach for classifying and detecting plant diseases using the Plant Leaf Disease dataset. "To be classified, CNN, MobileNetV2, ResNet50, Efficient NetB0, Xception and NasNetMobile" algorithms are compared, and an ensemble model integrating Xception and NasNetMobile is used, which proved to perform better than the rest. Measures used to assess performance are recall, precision, and F1-score, providing a clear view of model effectiveness. The ensemble model of Xception and NasNetMobile achieved the highest classification accuracy, outperforming individual models. YOLO models were effective in detecting plant diseases, accurately localizing infected regions on leaves using annotated bounding boxes and data augmentation. This study emphasizes the need to integrate Explainable AI (XAI) with DL to improve transparency, reliability, and user trust, supporting more effective disease management in agriculture.

title: Evaluation of a New Model of Renewable Energy Economic Growth for Green Computing
authors: Xuewen Yan
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Purpose: Although the current development trend of renewable energy (RE) has played an important role in promoting economic growth, compared with the development needs of the RE industry and more comprehensive RE policies, the current RE development strategy still needs further improvement from the perspective of energy conservation, emission reduction, and resource conservation. Method: This article explored the new model of RE economic growth under green computing (GC), elaborated on policy recommendations for RE development, and investigated the existing problems in RE development. This paper also put forward relevant suggestions for the existing problems, analyzed the analysis of energy consumption utilization based on GC, and discussed the manifestations and ways of GC to help upgrade economic growth. Result: According to the survey, the application of GC can reduce energy consumption and improve energy utilization efficiency. After applying GC, the average annual energy utilization rate of the enterprise is 0.82, and the energy utilization rate has increased by 0.20. Conclusion: After utilizing GC, the energy consumption and management costs of enterprises gradually decrease, and the economic benefits significantly improve, promoting the economic development of local and enterprise.

title: Low-Complexity Parallel Syndrome Computation and Chien Search Architecture Based on Reed–Muller Transform
authors: Muxun Zhang, Suwen Song, Liyang Zhou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
abstract: Bose–Chaudhuri–Hocquenghem (BCH) and Reed–Solomon (RS) codes have been widely adopted in communication systems (such as Ethernet) and storage applications. The ever-increasing data transmission rates impose higher throughput demands on RS/BCH decoders, necessitating a higher degree of parallelism in their key computing modules–syndrome computation (SC) and Chien search (CS), which significantly increases the hardware complexity. The Reed–Muller (RM) transform has been introduced to reduce complexity, which decomposes a special class of Vandermonde matrices into a sparse matrix and an RM matrix. However, its fully parallel requirement renders it infeasible in real-world systems. In this article, we propose a novel partially parallel algorithm based on RM transform for SC and CS modules. First, the computation matrix required by the RM transform is partitioned according to power-of-two parallelism, and a set of transform matrices is constructed to enable mutual conversion, thereby allowing partial parallelization of both the SC and CS modules. Then, the original RM transform is applied to the partitioned matrices to preserve the inherent low-complexity advantage. Finally, we elaborately design the corresponding hardware architecture, in which a flexible matrix-reuse strategy is proposed to further reduce hardware cost. Synthesized under 28-nm CMOS technology, the proposed SC architecture achieves up to 45% area reduction compared with state-of-the-art algorithms.

title: Development and Evaluation of a Hybrid AI-Based System for Capstone Manuscript Retrieval: Validating Relevance Through Search Result Evaluation
authors: Joemarie Comeros Amparo, Eric Perez Ortega, Moma de la Torre Ortega, Jomar Branzuela Colao, Roche Arenas Plando
