title: RIS-Aided Handover Scheme for High-Speed Railway Communications: Codebook Design and Beam Training Approach
authors: Xuanhang Qu, Hui Huang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Severe system latency during handover processes may induce ping-pong handover effects, which threaten the security and stability of train-ground communication in high-speed railway scenarios. In this letter, we propose a handover scheme integrating codebook design and hierarchical beam training approach. By jointly training the beams of two cells, the difference in signal strength received by the train from the two cells is maximized. Specifically, considering the deterministic spatial geometry inherent in the railway environments, a customized far-field linear codebook is proposed, which reduces codebook redundancy by precisely aligning with the actual train trajectory. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed handover scheme can effectively reduce the ping-pong handover probability, significantly enhancing handover reliability under high-mobility conditions.

title: Enhancing Electoral Integrity Through AI-Powered Anomaly Detection and Blockchain Consensus
authors: Suresh Kottur, Lokesh Ramanathan Suresh, P. Deepalakshmi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Despite inefficiency, insecurity, and lack of transparency, democratic governments use electoral processes. Fraud, hacking, and logistical issues with paper and digital voting equipment lower public trust in election administration. AI and blockchain can improve national and corporate election security and transparency, says this study. AI-powered biometric authentication, fraud detection, and sentiment analysis can boost voter turnout, reduce election fraud, and verify voters. Blockchain technology records, audits, and automatically counts votes using smart contracts on a distributed, immutable ledger. Modern democracies allow election interference. Combining methods may address these issues and boost trust in democracy. Scalability, algorithmic bias, legal difficulties, and regulatory compliance must be addressed for these technologies to succeed. This study suggests AIblockchain election systems, supported by research, case studies, and technology. The findings call for more research and greater emphasis by policymakers on technology and voting.

title: Performance Analysis of PMCW-based Radar System for Cohesive Sensing and Communication
authors: Md. Mominul Hasan Shawon, Md. Selim Hossain, Md. Farhan Shahriar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: This paper investigates the performance of a phase-modulated continuous wave (PMCW)-based MIMO radar system for integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) with uniform liner array. The proposed design enables simultaneous detection of multiple moving objects and some static objects and also supports downlink data transmission using the same hardware system. A complete framework for velocity, range and angle estimation is presented, employing pulse compression, Doppler processing, and phased array techniques. Simulation results validate accurate multi-target detection with mean errors of 0.097 m in range, 0.124° in angle, and $0.202 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ in velocity, while maintaining reliable communication performance. Compared with recent works that emphasize only one or two parameters, the proposed system achieves joint range-angle-velocity estimation, highlighting the effectiveness of PMCW waveforms for next-generation ISAC applications.

title: Integration of IoT with Smart Battery Management Systems (BMS) in Electric Vehicles (EVs): A Cloud-Connected Framework for Ongoing Battery Health Monitoring and Optimization
authors: R. Sundar, Murthoty Satheesh Babu, S. Saranya, J Latha, A. Muthukrishnan, S. Jayanthi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: New battery management systems are critical to meet the demand for electric vehicles, which is growing at a dizzying rate across the globe. These technologies are crucial for electric vehicles because of the reliability, safety, and longevity they provide. Intelligent optimisation, long-term monitoring, and predictive diagnostics are features that conventional BMSs lack because they operate autonomously within the car. To optimise, monitor, and detect anomalies in battery health in the cloud, this research presents a novel data-driven architecture that combines the characteristics of Smart Battery Management Systems (BMS) with Internet of Things (IoT) technology. By revealing battery performance in real time, this method enhances the design of conventional battery management systems. It accomplishes this by integrating Internet of Things (IoT) connectivity, cloud analytics, real-time data collection, and machine learning models. A high-frequency battery system would send onboard sensor data, including temperature, voltage, current, internal resistance, charge/discharge cycles, and State of Health (SoH), to cloud platforms over secure IoT channels. Advanced cloud analytics can predict RUL, detect damage early, and analyse patterns. Machine learning models excel at charging optimisation, cell performance balancing, and early failure detection. Integrating in-vehicle edge computing with cloud services allows fleet-level long-term evaluations and faster local decisions. Real-time alarms and feedback loops help electric car makers and drivers optimise battery health-related performance metrics.

title: Automated Classification of Atrial Fibrillation from ECG Signals Using Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Md. Ali Hasan, Mohammod Abdul Motin
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Atrial fibrillation (AF) is the most prevalent form of cardiac arrhythmia and is strongly associated with an increased risk of stroke and heart failure, which has driven extensive research on its detection using electrocardiogram (ECG) signals. In this study, we propose a machine learningbased automated approach for AF detection from ECG signals. A comprehensive set of time-domain, frequency-domain, and morphological features was extracted from the ECG recordings. Among the three classifiers deployed: Support Vector Machine (SVM), Logistic Regression, and XGBoost-SVM demonstrated the most robust performance, achieving an overall accuracy of 95.00% and 95.60% F1 score. Furthermore, feature importance analysis was conducted to identify the most discriminative attributes contributing to $\mathbf{A F}$ detection. The experimental results highlight that the proposed method provides an effective and interpretable solution for automatic AF detection, offering potential clinical utility for early diagnosis and monitoring.

title: Research on UAV Trajectory Planning in Complex Mountain Environments Based on IDE Algorithm
authors: Tao Tong, Yawei Li, Qiwu Wu, Yunchen Su
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: This paper presents an Improved Differential Evolution (IDE)-based path planning method to overcome the dual challenges of rigorous terrain constraints and local optima susceptibility in Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) operations within complex mountainous terrains. The proposed methodology features three key innovations: (1) A novel mutation mechanism incorporating a 2D discrete memristive hyperchaotic map with superior dynamic complexity characteristics, which supersedes the conventional linear difference vector approach in standard Differential Evolution (DE) algorithms. (2) An advanced dynamic parameter randomization framework that applies interval-uniform distribution combined with adaptive Gaussian perturbation to crossover control parameters and scaling factors. (3) Comprehensive simulation experiments conducted in authentic mountainous scenarios demonstrate the algorithm's superior performance in UAV path planning effectiveness compared to standard DE, its variants, as evidenced by quantitative metrics and qualitative trajectory analyses.

title: A Review of Spatial Downscaling Methods for Forest Above-Ground Biomass: From Machine Learning to Deep Learning
authors: Jingjing Liu, Qiang Zhu, Hang Zhao, Di Wu
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Accurate estimation of forest above-ground biomass (AGB) is crucial for addressing climate change. Current methodologies for forest AGB mapping increasingly rely on harmonizing field survey data with multi-source remote sensing data. However, the spatial mismatch between field data and remote sensing data presents a persistent challenge. Spatial downscaling methods address this issue by reducing reliance on extensive field data and minimizing the spatial discrepancies between field observations and remote sensing datasets. This paper begins by introducing the research background of downscaling methods, including the technical frameworks and recent developments in machine learning (ML)-based and deep learning (DL)-based downscaling approaches. Building on this, the paper critically discusses strategies for enhancing the performance of these models. The paper then analyzes specific challenges in AGB downscaling, including the selection of downscaling covariates, scale effects, and uncertainty quantification. Finally, it explores future research directions, such as multi-source data fusion, the design of efficient network architectures, the integration of physical constraints, and enhanced uncertainty quantification.

title: Map My Campus – QR Based Navigation System
authors: Naga Surya Venkata Tejaswini Pemmireddy, Srinivasarao T, Shrija Madhu, Ramya Sri Nemani, S S D M Akshitha Pasalapudi, Lakshitha Rongala
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Navigating a large college campus can be overwhelming, particularly for first-time visitors, new students, and parents attending events. Traditional paper maps are often outdated, difficult to read, and easily misplaced. To address these challenges, a browser-based navigation tool called Map My Campus was developed to offer a simple and accessible solution. Users can scan QR codes placed at key locations across the campus to instantly access an interactive map directly through their mobile browser without requiring any application downloads or sign-ins. The interface is intentionally minimalistic, displaying only essential elements such as buildings, laboratories, and administrative offices, while supporting intuitive zoom, drag, search functions, and basic route guidance. As the tool is entirely web-based, it requires an active internet connection but operates without relying on third-party trackers or paid APIs, ensuring both privacy and cost-efficiency. During high-traffic periods such as admission days and final examinations, the system was actively deployed and observed to significantly reduce navigation issues and confusion among users. This paper presents the design and implementation of Map My Campus, outlines its practical advantages, and proposes it as a scalable model for institutions seeking to modernize their campus navigation systems.

title: Towards the Self-Optimizing Computer: A Review of AI-Driven Architectures for Kernels and Distributed Infrastructure
authors: P Karthikeyan, G D Krishna Kumar, S Priyanka
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: The current computing architecture is at a junction. The introduction of different forms of computing hardware (GPUs, TPUs, and Edge NPUs) and the blistering development of complex process workloads have presented us with the opportunity to realise the constraints of the traditional Operating System (OS) design to meet the requirements of this more heterogeneous mix of hardware and software. The classical OS kernel is developed using heuristic algorithms that include hand-tuning of process-scheduling parameters, memory-eviction algorithms, and I/O device access governed by fixed rules. These rigid guidelines do not account for the ongoing evolution of the diverse hardware and workloads that constitute the current high-dimensional trade-off space of infrastructure. We summarise the trends in ML-Enhanced OS kernel design a classify the three main phases of research in the field: AI-Powered (Parameter Tuning), AI Refactored (Including Neural Components), and AI-Driven (Autonomous Control). We critically evaluate the current state of Reinforcement Learning (RL) for scheduling CPU resource usage, predictive DL for prefetching memory, and AI-based control for managing Kubernetes clusters. Although self-optimising systems offer a significant opportunity to configure and optimise resource use most effectively, we also discuss the considerable challenges involved in such endeavours: inference latency and the black-box nature of existing implementations.

title: STRIKE: Secure Transmission via Reconstruction-Integrated Key-based Encoding
authors: Khan Farhan Rafat
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: Since its inception, the field of digital communication has been fundamentally defined by a dichotomy: compression seeks to eliminate redundancy, while encryption aims to induce it for secrecy. This conceptual division has solidified into two entirely separate research pipelines, leading to the entrenched sequential paradigm of compress-then-encrypt. The critical, unaddressed flaw in this architecture is the absence of a single, unified mathematical operation that inherently guarantees both minimal representation and semantic security. We propose STRIKE (Secure Transmission via Reconstruction-Integrated Key-based Encoding), a framework that collapses this historic divide. STRIKE achieves a deterministic <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2: 1$</tex> compression ratio via synchronized state evolution, independent of data statistics. It maps a 2-bit message to an angular coordinate, applies a secret key-driven rotation, and outputs a single projected bit. A synchronized 1-bit state variable, evolving deterministically at the encoder and decoder, provides the necessary context for perfect, lossless reconstruction without transmission overhead. The process is a singular, lightweight geometric transform that concurrently reduces size and obscures content. STRIKE thus delivers a unified axiomatic proof-of-concept, demonstrating that compression and encryption are not sequential necessities but can be emergent properties of a single, stateful, informationtheoretic function.

title: A Hybrid Framework to Fuse Sensor and Video Data for Human Activity Recognition
authors: Ishwarya Kothandaraman, R Arunadevi, M. Balasubramani, G Nandhini, R. Suganthi, M. Robinson Joel
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Human Activity Recognition (HAR) is an AI process that automatically identifies and classifies human actions using data from images, sensors, and videos. HAR applications are widely used in human-machine interaction, robotics, and surveillance. Two main approaches are employed: sensor-based and vision-based. In the proposed framework, a real-time lifestyle tracking system is developed using inertial and image sensors. Outlier detection is performed with a Kalman filter, and activity recognition is achieved using a modified 1D-CNN-LSTM, tested on the UCI-HAR dataset with the Performance Enhance Real Time (PERT)-HAR model. For video-based recognition, the EfficientNet architecture is enhanced using the UCF Sports dataset. Each video is converted into frames, which are then filtered with a bilateral filter to preserve edges and reduce noise. A Squirrel Search Optimisation Deep Convolutional Neural Network (SSD-CNN) further improves accuracy by optimising pose identification, followed by multiclass SVM classification. The novelty lies in integrating bilateral filtering with a squirrel search-based EfficientNet model. Finally, a hybrid HAR model that combines sensor and video data is proposed to address intra-class variations. This multimodal fusion improves recognition accuracy, achieving up to 97.8% efficiency in human activity classification.

title: AI-Driven RIS-Assisted OFDM-ISAC Framework for Smart City Target Detection in Terahertz 6G Networks
authors: Zid Souad, Becha Anis, Ammar Noemen
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: Smart cities will require 6 G networks to provide both high-rate communication and accurate sensing for applications such as autonomous driving and intelligent monitoring. Integrated Sensing and Communication (ISAC) enables joint data transmission and target detection using a shared waveform. In addition, Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces (RIS) can improve wireless coverage by intelligently controlling signal reflections. This paper proposes an AI-enabled RIS-assisted OFDM-ISAC framework for Terahertz smart city networks. A Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) agent is developed to jointly optimize RIS phase shifts and OFDM resource allocation under mobility and blockage effects. Simulation results demonstrate significant gains in spectral efficiency and sensing detection performance compared with conventional benchmark schemes.

title: Critical Time Step Sparse Adversarial Attacks for Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Ming Huang, Qian He
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Most existing adversarial attacks against deep reinforcement learning (DRL) adopt high-frequency perturbation strategies, which are hindered by high tampering rates, pronounced perturbation accumulation, and a heightened likelihood of being detected by defense mechanisms. As a result, they fail to encapsulate clandestine attack scenarios that better reflect real-world threats. To address these issues, we propose a sparse attack algorithm that targets critical time steps in DRL. By constructing a Hybrid State Importance Metric (HSIM), our method automatically identifies a small set of highly vulnerable critical time steps in the temporal domain and injects perturbations only at these points, thereby reducing the attack frequency while preserving attack effectiveness. Experiments conducted in the Gymnasium LunarLander-v3 environment show that our approach can significantly degrade the agent's return—and even induce task failure—while tampering with fewer than 20% of state inputs. These results further reveal that DRL agents can be highly sensitive to a small number of temporally critical nodes under specific key states.

title: Intelligent Assessment of Industrial Control Asset Health Using Deep Neuro-Fuzzy Inference
authors: Liu Sun, Jinliang Xue, Lin Wang, Xiuyuan Ye, Bo Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: To address the limitations of traditional methods in handling ambiguity and adaptability for industrial control asset security risk assessment, this paper proposes a novel intelligent assessment model based on a Deep Neuro-Fuzzy Inference System (DNFIS). The model constructs a multi-level assessment index system covering 10 dimensions to provide comprehensive perceptual input. Unlike conventional adaptive neuro-fuzzy systems (ANFIS), the proposed DNFIS introduces a deep residual learning architecture, inspired by ResNet, which effectively mitigates gradient vanishing and enables the construction of a ultra-high-dimensional evaluation space. This allows for thorough fusion of input attack information and assessment indicators, avoiding biases caused by separate mapping. By automatically learning from historical data to optimize membership functions and fuzzy rules, the model achieves enhanced self-evolution capability. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed framework significantly outperforms traditional methods in both assessment accuracy and early warning sensitivity, providing a data-driven solution for transforming the operation and maintenance mode from “passive response” to “proactive warning”.

title: Hybrid Framework for Detecting Multi-Class DDoS Attacks Using Probabilistic and Boosted Learning Methods
authors: Sombabu Chunduru, Naga Malleswara Rao Purimetla
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The rise of Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks poses a severe threat to the stability and reliability of modern network infrastructures, impacting over 35% of cloud-based systems and accounting for more than 40% of reported network disruptions worldwide. With growing traffic volumes exceeding terabytes per second, early and accurate DDoS detection remains a critical challenge for network defence mechanisms. To mitigate the above shortcomings, the current research outlines a smart architecture of the detection and categorization of Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks, based on the DDoS Detection Dataset and customized to handle the multi-class attack cases. The methodology starts with a stringent data preprocessing stage, where missing, redundant and noisy data are carefully edited to ensure quality inputs. This is followed by feature extraction with the help of a Probabilistic Neural Network (PNN) to obtain salient statistical and probabilistic patterns in network traffic, thus enabling a sound feature representation in a range of DDoS types. These features are then fed into a Boosted Rules Classifier which combines ensemble learning with adaptive rule optimisation to produce increased detection accuracy and simultaneously minimise false positive rates. Empirical tests indicate that the given model is more successful than the current methods in terms of detecting various DDoS subclasses, without sacrificing high accuracy, precision, and recall rates. The model, therefore, fills the gap between computational efficiency and real-time detection.

title: An Intelligent AI-Assisted Wearable Health Monitoring Framework with Reduced Cloud Dependency
authors: R Nandhakumar, R Vikram, B Vishnu, S. Pavithra, P. Vidhya Lakshmi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: In the proposed paper, it is proposed to design an intelligent wearable health monitoring system to integrate the lightweight machine learning methods with low-power body sensors to attain continuous, personalized, and energy efficient monitoring of health care. To solve the problems of excessive power consumption, delay in transmission, reliance on the cloud, etc., the proposed system will be able to perform ondevice inference with the assistance of TinyML-based models to extract features in advance and detect anomalies. Personalized health profiling is obtained through unsupervised learning techniques under the name clustering-based adaptive thresholding which considers inter-individual physiological variations. In addition, recurring learning models are utilized to model time-related dependencies and long-term health patterns to be able to produce predictive risk assessment and early warning. The framework as compared to the traditional cloud-based architecture employs the edge-assisted method to only selectively transfer only significant events or summarised data to move on with further analytics in order to use less bandwidth to use and have a longer battery life in the device. It is very scalable, real time responsive, interpretable and flexible and hence perfectly suited to serve wearable healthcare in the long term as well as serve next generation intelligent medical applications.

title: TRAP- Transaction Risk Assessment Prevention
authors: N Kirubakaran, Yagna Baskar Sangeetha Hemashree, H S Arshiya, A Shaini, T Karpagam, Varnikha S Janani
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: This project presents TRAP (Transaction Risk Assessment Prevention), a framework designed to improve fraud detection in digital payments. Existing fraud-detection techniques primarily rely on rigid, pre-programmed rules and thus have limited responsiveness to changes in user behaviour. Therefore, when cases of uncharacteristic transactions occur or exhibit zero-day behavioural patterns, they are often not detected. TRAP also overcomes this weakness by leveraging behavioural patterns derived from antecedent transactions rather than relying solely on static conditions. The novelty of TRAP lies in its system - level design rather than any single algorithm. Unlike conventional fraud detection approaches that rely on fixed rules or isolated checks, TRAP evaluates individual user behaviour and responds only when meaningful risk is detected by combining adaptive risk assessment with user verification in a single real-time flow. There are two elements of TRAP. The first component is a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) neural network, which assumes the processing of transactions as sequences of data, making it possible to identify temporal patterns and changes in the spending streams of the individuals. The second is the Isolation Forest (Machine Learning) algorithm, which isolates observations that do not fit the general data distribution. By combining LSTM-based temporal modelling and Isolation Forest anomaly isolation, the system can reliably identify the source of anomalous transactions.

title: Management and Finance-Oriented AIML Framework for Transformer Health Prognostics and Cost Optimization
authors: Ravi Kumar Bommisetti, Rajesh Prabhakar Kaila, Srinivasa Rao Dasaraju, Sathish Kumar Deekonda, Sree Lakshmi Moorthygari, Neha Choudhary
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Asset health prognostics for distribution and transmission power transformers remain a critical gap in utility asset management and corporate finance due to heterogeneous sensor modalities, complex ageing physics, and there is a need to translate prognostics into prescriptive capital and O&M decisions. The objective of this research was to develop an AIML framework that yields calibrated, unit-level remaining-useful-life (RUL) posteriors and integrates them with an economic decision layer for maintenance and replacement scheduling. A multimodal experimental study was conducted using fleet SCADA, dissolved-gas analysis, tap-changer logs, partial-discharge spectra, and a physics-informed accelerated-ageing simulator. Topological features were extracted via persistent homology (persistence diagrams → persistence images) and embedded with a Persformer module. A probabilistic Transformer (heteroscedastic aleatoric + epistemic uncertainty heads, ensembles + MC dropout) produced RUL posteriors that feed a scenario-based stochastic optimizer (scenario tree / QR-DQN). Empirically, the topology-augmented probabilistic model reduced RUL RMSE by ≈31% and CRPS by ≈35% versus a plain Transformer and achieved ~19 – 23% expected life-cycle cost savings over preventive scheduling (p < 0.01). The framework enables risk-adjusted CAPEX/OPEX trade-offs and decision-grade KPIs for board-level planning, while highlighting sensor coverage and governance as deployment constraints.

title: Design of a Compact High Isolation MIMO Antenna Using CSRR for 5G and GNSS Mobile Terminals
authors: Liwen Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Integrating multiple antennas within the limited clearance of modern smartphones inevitably causes severe mutual coupling. This creates a significant obstacle for concurrent 5G and Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) operations. In this paper, a compact four-port GNSS MIMO antenna system with high isolation is proposed. The individual antenna element employs a folded meander line structure to achieve a fundamental resonance at 1.6 GHz within a narrow 7.5 mm frame clearance. A Complementary Split-Ring Resonator (CSRR) is deliberately patterned into the ground plane to alleviate intense interference among neighboring ports. This configuration intercepts the localized conduction path, trapping leakage surface currents within the concentric slots without increasing the footprint. Simulated results demonstrate that the proposed resonator improves the isolation to better than 25 dB at 1.6 GHz. Considering the narrow 7.5-mm frame, a practical engineering compromise is made, maintaining an acceptable impedance matching of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S_{11}<-5.5 \text{dB}$</tex> (VSWR <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<3.5$</tex>) to satisfy the baseline requirements for compact terminals. Conventional isolation methods frequently impair radiation performance. In contrast, the presented architecture secures a peak realized gain of 1.5 dBi alongside an overall efficiency approaching 60%. The Envelope Correlation Coefficient is evaluated to be 0.08, ensuring excellent spatial diversity.

title: EmpathVision: A Real-Time AI Model for Sign Language to Speech Translation, Scene Description, and Emotion Recognition Using Machine Learning
authors: Harshal Shete, Sakshee Yande, Shrutika Yelgunde, Rajesh Tekawade, Archana Said
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: This paper presents a real-time, AI-powered assistive framework designed to enhance accessibility and communication for individuals with hearing, speech, and visual impairments. The proposed system addresses the communication gap between sign language users and non-signers, improves environmental perception for visually impaired individuals, and enables emotion-aware human computer interaction. The framework integrates three key functionalities sign language to speech translation, emotion detection, and scene description leveraging state-of-the-art deep learning architectures.The system receives real-time input through a webcam, supporting gesture detection, facial emotion detection, and contextual understanding of scenes. Sign language gestures are detected using Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and translated to text and speech using text-to-speech (TTS) synthesis. The emotions are detected using deep facial feature extraction, and Natural Language Processing (NLP) methods are used to create meaningful scene descriptions, helping visually impaired users sense their environment.The suggested framework obtained a total accuracy of 95.3%, proving the efficient combination of all three deep learning models gesture recognition, emotion detection, and scene description into a unique combined system. Such high accuracy confirms the system’s strong robustness and trustworthiness in real-time assistive applications

title: A Lightweight SVM-Based Approach for Pneumonia Detection from Chest X-Rays: A Non-Deep Learning Perspective
authors: Maitri A. Patil, Spoorthi Hiremath, Ria Javalagi, Rashmi Khot
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Pneumonia is a serious lung infection in which the air sacs become inflamed and may fill with fluid or pus, and chest X-ray imaging continues to be one of the most commonly used tools for its diagnosis. Although deep learning models such as convolutional neural networks have shown strong performance in medical image analysis, their dependence on large annotated datasets and high computational resources limits their applicability in many real-world and resource-constrained healthcare settings. A lightweight and efficient pneumonia detec tion approach based on a Support Vector Machine (SVM) using handcrafted Haralick texture features extracted from chest X-ray images through the Gray-Level Co-Occurrence Matrix (GLCM) is presented to address this challenge. By focusing on texture-based representations, the approach captures key visual patterns associated with pneumonia while maintaining low computational complexity. Experimental results show that the method achieves an accuracy of 86.26%

title: A Low-Cost Navigation System for Industrial Mobile Robots via NHC-Enhanced Robust EKF
authors: Yiping Yin, Hao Chen, Tiancong Ruan, Juxian Chen, Ziyang Chen, Lingfeng Zheng
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Industrial autonomous mobile robots require low-cost, low-power localization that remains stable under occlusions and multipath. This paper presents an embedded GNSS/IMU/odometry fusion system implemented on an automotive-grade MCU. Since many commodity GNSS modules provide only solved navigation outputs and do not reliably expose raw measurements (pseudorange/carrier phase), we adopt a loosely coupled Extended Kalman filter (EKF) using solved GNSS position updates. The estimator runs in a multi-rate manner: IMU/odometry-driven prediction and state output at 200 Hz, with GNSS measurement updates at 10 Hz. To improve robustness with minimal computation, we incorporate (i) a soft non-holonomic constraint (NHC) pseudo-measurement that suppresses lateral drift for wheeled motion and (ii) normalized innovation squared (NIS) chi-square gating to reject impulsive GNSS outliers. Experiments in semi-obstructed, multipath-prone environments demonstrate real-time performance of 0.5 ms per EKF predict-update cycle at 10% CPU utilization and reduce position RMSE from 0.92 m to 0.64 m compared with a standard EKF baseline.

title: Assessing the Effectiveness of AI-Guided Muscle Relaxation Therapy in the Reduction of Maternal Stress
authors: V.Tamizhkodi
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Maternal stress has been a very important issue during pregnancy as it has influence on both the physiological and psychological conditions of both the mother and the fetus. The proposed study will be able to assess the efficacy of an AI-based muscle relaxation therapy program that would monitor the state of stress and suggest adaptive relaxation workshops in accordance with the real-time information received through biofeedback. This sample was a group of 120 20-35-year-old pregnant women with 1 group having an experimental group that underwent AI-guided therapy and 1 control group that underwent traditional relaxation exercises. Measurement of data was done on the variability of heart rate (HRV), cortisol levels and self-reported stress scale during a period of six weeks. The findings showed statistically significant differences in the mean scores of stress (p < 0.01) between the control and AI. The mechanism of adaptive feedback allowed individualizing relaxation rate and time, which resulted in a higher degree of consistency and involvement. Nevertheless, there are certain limitations such as briefness of intervention, and variety of population samples should be more diverse. The use of multimodal AI feedback, including the use of the emotional tone analysis and fetal movement correlation are suggested as the future study tools that allow more accurate and generalizable AI-assisted maternal care systems.

title: Optimized Feature Selection and Soft Computing for Medical Data Diagnosis Using Rough Set Min-Max Classifier
authors: Shweta Bansal, Rahul Arora, Nirmala Reddy, M. A. Mujeeb, Sumit Nema, P.Mansa Raj
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The role of medical diagnosis in healthcare depends on proper classification model performance when analysing complex datasets. A new medical diagnosis predictive classifier known as Rough Set Min-Max Classifier (RMMC) represents the main subject of this study. The model combines rough set theory for dealing with vague and duplicated data entries and uses Euclidean distance-based neighbourhood relationships to optimize feature analysis. The RMMC model achieves better classification results when applied to the Epileptic Seizure Recognition dataset and also when analysing Breast Cancer as well as Liver datasets. It improves the system performance by picking the most useful features from the dataset while maintaining decision quality. RMMC demonstrates its ability to optimize medical diagnoses through improved classification precision according to the study findings. The proposed model seeks future optimization through integration of an Improvised Harris Hawks Optimization (IHHO) algorithm that will enhance both feature selection and classification operational efficiency. The developed research framework produces a solid system for medical data analysis which enhances the reliability of disease prediction methods. The evaluation results using precision, recall, F-measure, and Rand index metrics prove the efficiency of RMMC by reaching a 99.42% accuracy which produces superior outcomes compared to traditional methods like Neighbourhood Rough Set Classifier (NRSC), Support Vector Machine (SVM) and K-Nearest Neighbours (KNN).

title: Three-dimensional Reconstruction of Ship Targets with Complex Motion by Single-Channel Synthetic Aperture Radar
authors: Yifan Liu, Xiangguang Leng, Xinqi Xu, Jindeng Li, Kefeng Ji
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: The existing spaceborne synthetic aperture radar (SAR) systems are mainly single-channel systems, and it is usually difficult to directly recover the three-dimensional (3D) information of the target. Fortunately, due to the complex motion of ship targets under medium to high sea conditions, more perspective information is brought to SAR imaging, making it possible to recover the 3D information of the target. However, this technology still faces the following difficulties: (1) It is usually difficult to extract stable feature points in SAR images, which will lead to difficulties in key point matching in multi-perspective images. (2) Due to the possible non-cooperativeness of the target, the complex motion of the ship target is difficult to determine, which makes most of the 3D reconstruction methods for attitude-stable targets at risk of failure. To address these issues, we propose a voxel-based 3D reconstruction method for maximum sequence image energy accumulation. This method can quickly achieve voxelized 3D reconstruction of the target under the condition of known target rotation parameters without the need to match multi-view images. On this basis, through iterative calculation using an intelligent optimization method, the instantaneous attitude of the ship target with complex motion can be estimated, so as to recover the 3D information of the target with unknown rotational motion relying on the estimation results. The processing results of simulation and measured data verify the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Seamless Global AOD Reconstruction Based on Transformer Model and Fusion of Multi-source Satellite Products
authors: Meng Wu, Ning Wang, Lingling Ma, Jiajia Zhu, JiaQi Hu, Yongguang Zhao, Xingfeng Chen, Beibei Zhang, Jingmei Li, Qijin Han
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Aerosol optical depth (AOD), as a key parameter reflecting the atmospheric aerosol load, plays a crucial role in the study of climate change, air quality, and the Earth’s radiation budget. However, current satellite-retrieved AOD products suffer from high missing rates, spatial discontinuities, and inter-product inconsistencies, which significantly limit their global applications. In this study, we propose a seamless reconstruction framework based on a Cross-Dimensional Self-Attention Transformer, by combining optimal interpolation fusion of multiple satellite products with Transformer-based imputation to generate globally continuous AOD fields with high accuracy. First, eight AOD products from MODIS and VIIRS sensors are optimally fused, which substantially improves both coverage and accuracy across the entire sample range. Subsequently, a Transformer imputation model that integrates temporal, spatial, and meteorological variables is employed to seamlessly recover missing regions through cross-dimensional attention mechanisms, further enhancing data completeness and reliability. Experimental results indicate that: (1) compared with individual satellite products, the optimal interpolation fusion significantly reduces errors and improves correlations with AERONET observations, doubling the overall coverage; (2) the Transformer-based imputation achieves 100% coverage while further increasing the coefficient of determination (R²) and reducing the root mean square error (RMSE), outperforming the reanalysis product MERRA-2; (3) at the regional scale, the imputed results accurately capture the spatiotemporal evolution of typical pollution episodes. The final global product reaches an overall accuracy of R² = 0.701 and RMSE = 0.073. This work provides a feasible pathway for generating seamless global AOD datasets, offering essential support for climate model validation, air pollution monitoring, and environmental policy-making.

title: Interpretable Semantic Medical Image Segmentation with Style and Confidence_supp1-3689564.pdf

title: Optical Test Set for Microwave Vector Network Analyzers based on Homodyne detection
authors: Raffaele Vallifuoco, Mikel Sagues, Luigi Zeni, Aldo Minardo, Alayn Loayssa
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: We present an optical test set for a microwave vector network analyzer that transforms the instrument into a full vector optical analyzer (OVA), enabling measurements of the optical field transfer function of components and systems with Hz-level frequency resolution. The setup is simple, consisting of a laser source, a standard Mach-Zehnder electro-optic modulator, and a self-homodyne receiver based on a 90° optical hybrid and balanced detectors. Unlike other OVA methods that use RF-modulated optical signals, our technique avoids complex setups and multi-step procedures. It is also immune to errors caused by beating between unwanted modulation sidebands. We provide a detailed theoretical derivation of the mathematical framework underlying the method. In addition, we introduce a calibration technique that compensates for all optical and electrical non-idealities in the setup, including those introduced by the optical hybrid. We demonstrate the system's operation through proof-of-concept experiments, measuring both the magnitude and phase of the optical field transfer function of narrowband optical components.

title: DTHFormer: Dual Token Hierarchical Transformer Network for Fine-Grained Emotion Recognition
authors: Boxi Chen, Chunmei Qing, Jianxiu Jin, Xiangmin Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Affective Computing
abstract: The recognition of emotions through physiological signals has consistently remained a popular area of research. While utilizing physiological signals for emotion recognition provides the advantages of authenticity and objectivity, there remains room for improvement, particularly in subject-independent experimental conditions, due to the significant variability in physiological responses among different individuals. The development of multi-modal interaction methods offers the possibility of aiding emotion recognition based on physiological signals by introducing cross-domain information. This paper proposes a Dual Token Hierarchical Transformer Network to recognize fine-grained valence and arousal labels, which is dedicated to effectively integrating physiological data and emotional stimulus data. Specifically, DTHFormer performs indirect interaction through dual token between physiological signal and visual/audio modal extracted from video in order to protect unique information of each modality and avoid information confusion. Then, a hierarchical fusion module is used to preserve key emotional information across multiple modalities, enabling the model to have a deeper comprehension of emotional information. A variety of classification experiments demonstrate that our proposed method achieved good results. Further ablation experiments show the effectiveness of our proposed modules.

title: Adaptive 3D Convolution for Remote Sensing Image Fusion_supp1-3689418.pdf
authors: Liang-Jian Deng
publish: N/A
abstract: Remote sensing image fusion aims to create a high-resolution multi/hyper-spectral image from a high-resolution image with limited spectral information and a low-resolution image with abundant spectral data. Recently, deep learning (DL) techniques have shown significant effectiveness in this area. Most DL-based methods approach image fusion as a 2D problem by encoding spectral information into feature map channels. However, our research suggests that this strategy introduces notable spectral distortions. In contrast, some methods consider spectral data as an additional dimension, utilizing standard 3D convolutions to preserve spectral information. Nevertheless, in a standard 3D convolutional layer, the same set of kernels is applied across all input regions, which we have found to be sub-optimal for image fusion. Furthermore, standard 3D convolutions necessitate substantial computational resources. To address these challenges, we propose a novel convolutional paradigm called Adaptive 3D Convolution (Ada3D) for remote sensing image fusion. Ada3D applies a unique set of 3D kernels to each input voxel, enabling the capture of fine-grained details. These adaptive kernels are generated through a two-step process: (i) spatial and spectral kernels are derived from their respective image sources; (ii) these two types of kernels are then combined to form content-aware 3D kernels that effectively integrate spatial and spectral information. Additionally, adaptive biases are introduced to enhance the convolutional outcome at the voxel level. Furthermore, we incorporate the group convolution technique to reduce computational complexity. As a result, Ada3D offers full adaptivity in an efficient manner. Evaluation results across five datasets demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art (SOTA) performance, underscoring the superiority of Ada3D. The code is available at https://github.com/PSRben/Ada3D.

title: A Low-Latency Hardware Architecture Design for Five-Error-Correcting Reed–Solomon Decoder
authors: Haobin Xu, Zichuan Qiu, Suwen Song, Zhongfeng Wang, Liyang Zhou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
abstract: Reed–Solomon (RS) codes are widely used in modern communication and storage systems. However, conventional iteration-based RS decoder architectures are struggling to meet the stringent demands of emerging latency-critical applications. Although noniterative architectures have been proposed for <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$t \leq 4$</tex-math> </inline-formula> RS codes with low latency and low complexity, the design of such architectures remains largely unexplored for five-error-correcting RS codes. Motivated by this gap, we extend the Peterson–Gorenstein–Zierler (PGZ) algorithm to directly compute the error locator polynomial for <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$t=5$</tex-math> </inline-formula> RS codes, thereby eliminating the need for iterative computation in the circuit. Then, a systematic three-step optimization method is further proposed to mitigate the hardware complexity increase caused by PGZ, which significantly decreases the required number of multipliers. Besides, given the prohibitive complexity of noniterative root-finding algorithms for <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$t = 5$</tex-math> </inline-formula> RS codes, we instead adopt a hybrid PGZ-Chien search (PGZ-CS) architecture for the whole decoder. Based on the proposed methods, a low-latency and area-efficient <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$t=5$</tex-math> </inline-formula> RS decoder is finally developed and implemented under the example RS(255, 245, and 5)code. Synthesized under 28-nm CMOS technology, the proposed decoder reduces decoding latency by 65.6%, overall area by 29.7%, and power consumption by 32.6% compared to the compensated simplified reformulated inversionless Berlekamp–Massey (CS-RiBM) decoder.

title:
Design of a CMUT Structure Enhanced by ALD High-κ HfO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    and the LOCOS Process
authors: Lei Ren, Tianhao Yan, Wangyang Zhang, Jiandong Shen, Endian Cui, Hanjie Dou, Xianglong Chen, Ke Zhu, Jin Xie, Xiaojing Mu
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: This paper proposes an enhanced capacitive micromachined ultrasonic transducer (CMUT) architecture that integrates an atomic layer deposition (ALD)-deposited high-k HfO2 dielectric layer into the local oxidation of silicon (LOCOS) process to mitigate membrane warpage associated with Si3N4 and to improve electromechanical conversion efficiency. This research confirms the kinetic feasibility of HfO2 as an oxygen barrier in high-temperature processes by modifying the Deal-Grove model. Leveraging the compression effect of the high-k dielectric on the equivalent electrical cavity height, the proposed design strengthens the electromechanical turns ratio and electrostatic driving capability, while enhancing the transmitting performance (RF) and receiving performance (Rη) by factors of 1.24–1.72 and 1.32–2.41, respectively. Finite element method (FEM) simulations further confirm that the proposed HfO2 structure significantly reduces the collapse voltage (by 30%–45%) while overcoming traditional transduction limitations. Specifically, the electromechanical coupling coefficient (kT2) increases from 5.7% to 7.6% for 50 μm radius cells and from 11.66% to 16.66% for 70 μm radius cells, representing a substantial relative enhancement of 33.3% to 42.9%. Meanwhile, the proposed structure shows improved static capacitance, membrane displacement, and output acoustic pressure. These results support the potential of this structure for future single-chip, low-power CMUT systems.

title: Cross-Project Defect Prediction Using High-Dimensional Feature Selection and Decision Tree
authors: J.Maria Arockia Dass, B.Geetha Vani, T Dilli Kumari, G Chandana, B Harika, G Chaitanya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: This study addresses challenges in traditional software defect prediction by proposing a cross-project approach. It tackles distribution differences and high-dimensional features through a feature selection method. Experimentation shows its superiority, especially when paired with Decision Tree classification, outperforming existing methods based on the F1_score metric.

title: GranularCLIP: Structure-Preserving and Fine-Grained Knowledge Distillation for Efficient Vision–Language Models
authors: Wei Wang, Suting Chen, Yuwei Yang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: In recent years, large-scale vision-language models have demonstrated remarkable capabilities in zero-shot image-text retrieval. However, their enormous parameter sizes and high computational costs severely limit their deployment in resource-constrained edge computing systems. Most existing methods for model compression adopt a distillation paradigm that primarily focuses on aligning global features. These methods often neglect fine-grained semantic interactions and topological structures among samples, leading to a significant drop in retrieval performance when the compression ratio is high. To address this issue, we propose GranularCLIP, an efficient distillation framework that enhances the semantic expressiveness of compressed models. Specifically, we introduce two complementary modules: Fine-Grained Interaction distillation, which aligns the similarity structure between text tokens and image patches to preserve local semantic correspondences, and Structural Relation Preservation, which aligns the batch-wise similarity distributions to maintain relative structural relationships. Extensive experiments demonstrate that at a fifty percent unstructured sparsity level, GranularCLIP achieves a highly competitive text-to-image recall rate of 63.8 percent on the Flickr30k benchmark. Compared to the baseline model trained under identical settings, our method improves retrieval performance by 5.6 percent and successfully retains over 98 percent of the teacher model's capability, all without introducing any additional inference overhead. This study provides a practical paradigm for deploying efficient vision-language models.

title: A Mobile Application for Inventory Management to Support Digital Transformation in Small Retail Businesses
authors: Thanakrit Janchidfah, Pisit Chansuek, Pirat Worasiri
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Cybernetics and Innovations (ICCI)
abstract: Small retail businesses often rely on manual inventory management practices that lead to data inaccuracies and operational inefficiencies. This study presents Stock Easy, a lightweight mobile application designed to support inventory management and facilitate digital transformation among small retail businesses. The application was developed using a usercentered design approach and includes core functions such as product data management, stock tracking, and inventory updates. A quantitative evaluation was conducted with 132 users, including retail owners, online sellers, store managers, and inventory staff. Data were collected using a structured questionnaire and analyzed using descriptive statistics and Pearson correlation analysis. The results indicate high user satisfaction, particularly in terms of ease of use (mean <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=4.52$</tex>), process clarity (mean <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=4.41$</tex>), and user interface design (mean <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=$</tex> 4.36). Correlation analysis further reveals that perceived ease of use and perceived usefulness significantly influence overall satisfaction, which is positively associated with perceived readiness for digital transformation. These findings demonstrate that lightweight digital tools such as Stock Easy can effectively improve inventory management practices and serve as an entry point for digital transformation in small retail businesses.

title: Multimodal approach for PCOS Detection: Integrating Clinical Data and Ovarian Ultrasound with Grad-CAM Interpretability
authors: Samiha Muntaha Mahin, Tahmina Hasan, Shamima Sharif Omi, Farjana Kabir, Jahur Ahmed, Shafwat Mahiya
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS) is a complex endocrine disorder that benefits from multi-modal diagnostic approaches. In this study, we investigated both clinical tabular data and ovarian ultrasound images to develop an integrated predictive framework. Initially, we analyzed each modality independently: a multilayer perceptron (MLP) was trained on the tabular clinical dataset, while an EfficientNet-B0 network classified ultrasound images into infected and clean categories. To enhance interpretability of the image model, we generated visual explanations using Grad-CAM heatmaps. Finally, we merged the clinical and imaging features into a unified dataset and implemented a hybrid deep-learning model to exploit the complementary information from both modalities. The combined approach demonstrated improved diagnostic performance compared with single-modality models, highlighting the value of fusing structured clinical data with imaging features for more accurate and explainable PCOS detection.

title: Omnibot AI: Unified Multi-Modal Intelligent Assistant
authors: N.R. Rajalakshmi, V. Jeevanantham, S George Benedict, S Mohan Krishna, R Panneerselvi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Omnibot AI is a single multimodal web assistant that aims at streamlining AI processes that are currently being carried out through multiple applicationbased programs. Instead of switching between various text writing, document editing, or image creation software, Omnibot AI provides it all. It has two primary modes: Chat and Analysis Mode, used to chat and decode what is sent by a different user in real-time, and Image Generation Mode to generate images. Built on client-oriented architecture and state-of-the-art generative language models, Omnibot AI demonstrates that it is possible to perform more complex multimodal actions (e.g., reasoning over information from multiple documents and images) within a single assistant. It can now achieve low latency and enhanced reliability with serverless capabilities, including exponential back-off, making it a useful prototype for integrated AI systems in the future. prototype for the future.

title: Design Tradeoffs of Through-Silicon-Carbide Vias for High-Temperature 3D SiC IC Integration
authors: Sahithi Thota, Li Song, Feng Li
publish: 2026 IEEE Workshop on Microelectronics and Electron Devices (WMED)
abstract: Through-silicon-carbide vias (TSiCV) is a key enabling technology for three-dimensional (3D) integration of silicon carbide (SiC) integrated circuits intended for extremeenvironment applications such as Venus surface exploration. In these environments, conventional copper-based interconnects face challenges related to oxidation, diffusion, and long-term reliability at elevated temperatures. This work presents a full-wave electromagnetic simulation study of gold-filled TSiCVs in SiC substrates, focusing on geometry- and temperature-dependent electrical tradeoffs relevant to high-temperature operation. Using ANSYS HFSS, TSiCVs with radii of $5 \mu \mathrm{~m}$ and $10 \mu \mathrm{~m}$ and a depth of $100 \mu \mathrm{~m}$ are analyzed over a frequency range of $1-50 \mathrm{GHz}$ at temperature of $20^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ and $600^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. Scattering parameters and extracted resistance and capacitance values are used to quantify signal-integrity and power-delivery-relevant trends. The results show that increasing via radius reduces $\mathbf{A C}$ resistance and temperature sensitivity but significantly increases capacitive loading, leading to tradeoffs between signal integrity and power delivery. These findings provide practical design guidance for selecting TSiCV geometries in high-temperature, mixed-signal 3D SiC IC integration.

title: A Convolutional Neural Network with Bidirectional Gated Recurrent Units Architecture for Enhanced Stress Monitoring from Wearable Signals
authors: Md Rokonuzzaman Mim, Md Santo Ali, Mohammod Abdul Motin
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Chronic stress poses significant risks to physical and mental health, emphasizing the need for continuous and unobtrusive monitoring using wearable devices. Blood volume pulse (BVP) and electrodermal activity (EDA) are cost-effective, noninvasive signals that capture complementary cardiovascular and autonomic responses. In this study, we propose a lightweight multimodal deep learning framework that integrates convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and bidirectional gated recurrent units (BiGRUs) to jointly learn spatial and temporal features. BVP and EDA signals are processed through separate pipelines, and their fused representations are refined through convolution, pooling, and global average pooling layers. To address class imbalance, a sliding-window augmentation strategy is employed. The model is trained and evaluated on the publicly available WESAD dataset using a subject-independent leave-one-subject-out (LOSO) crossvalidation protocol. It achieves 98.56% accuracy, 99.19% AUC, and 96.87% Cohen’s K, outperforming existing baselines. With only $\mathbf{0. 4 8 M}$ parameters, the architecture is computationally efficient and suitable for real-time stress monitoring on resourceconstrained wearable devices.
