title: Hybrid Deep Neural Network–DevOps Pipeline Optimization for Risk Prediction in Cloud-Native Workers’ Compensation Platforms
authors: Subramanian Sendil Kumar, Ravi Shankar Garapati, Avinash Reddy Segireddy, Srinivas Kalisetty, Ramesh Inala, Kushvanth Chowdary Nagabhyru
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Insurance risk prediction is very difficult, due to the diversity and richness of customer information. The traditional statistical or independent deep learning models often face the challenge of trading off accuracy, interpretability, and deployment efficiency. We introduce a Hybrid DNN–DevOps Framework, as it integrates use of CNN, GRU and Attention to more efficiently capture the spatial-temporal relationships in claim data and manage for ongoing optimization using DevOps automation. With advanced features like containerized deployment, ML flow tracking, and Prometheus-based monitoring we can offer real-time reliability. Extensive experiments demonstrate the efficiency and effectiveness of proposed model with 98.4% accuracy, 98.15% F1-score for speech VR, and only 48 ms latency comparing with state-of-art methods such as Random Forest(94.8%) and CNN–LSTM (96.8%). The results verify the scalability of the framework, its stability to data drift and the ability to run efficiently in dynamic cloud environments. The combination of automated retraining pipelines, and predictive intelligence, enhance claim analysis, fraud discovery and policy optimisation making the proposed system competitive for advanced insurance analytics. The findings provide a solid basis for intelligent, explainable and scalable risk prediction systems in banking and insurance sectors.

title: Robust Unknown Parameter Identification in Double Diode PV Model Using Artificial Lemming Algorithm
authors: Krishnapada Mondal, Md. Abu Salman, Md. Parvej, Disha Hasan, Md. Akter Hossen, Md. Shariful Islam
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Enhancing the solar power generation efficiency, reliability, and safety in accordance with the requirements of relevant standards, precise evaluation of photovoltaic (PV) model parameters is essential. This paper introduces the concept of Artificial Lemming Algorithm (ALA), a novel inspired optimization procedure based on the behaviors and the collective movement of lemmings in the nature environment. This algorithm searches for the elements of the Double Diode Model (DDM) according to Newton-Raphson ideas. The Wilcoxon signed-rank test (WSRT) was utilized to scrutinize the beneficial effect of ALA against another three-optimization procedure. From the statistical analysis, it is evident that, ALA consistently outperforms those of Whale Optimization Algorithm (WOA), Harris Hawks Optimizer (HHO), and Gray Wolf Optimizer (GWO), where p-values equal to $\mathbf{2. 0 0} \boldsymbol{\times} \mathbf{1 0}^{\mathbf{- 6}}$ and at least favorable test statistics. The simulation results also show that ALA achieves faster convergence, higher robustness in network throughput, and lower RMSE than the other methods. In the case of the R.T.C. France solar cell dataset in MATLAB, it had the minimum RMSE for DDM of $7.52 \times 10^{-4}$. These findings indicate that ALA is a valid and efficient modality for the estimation of PV variables. Indeed, they demonstrate that it may enhance the precision and reliability of solar energy simulation.

title: Scalable and Efficient Solution for EV based-Lithium-Ion Battery SOH Estimation through a Novel CatBoost-Enhanced Predictive Analytics
authors: D. Manoj, F.T. Josh, Buvanesh Pandian V, Madamanchi Swathi
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The automobile industry has focused heavily on Electric Vehicles (EVs) to reduce carbon discharges and solve global environmental issues in the world. Battery performance and health deteriorate over time and may result in the lost efficiency of EVs. The age-related degradation process is complex, making it a major problem to determine the State of Health (SOH) in Lithium-Ion Batteries (LiB). This work present a unique and effective method for SOH estimation that uses an optimized feature selection method and a new gradient-boosting algorithm. The feature set is an optimized version of a feature set created to solve a multi-objective optimization problem using Harris Hawks Optimization (HHO) algorithm. The CatBoost base method for ensemble learning algorithm is used because the nonlinear characteristics of LiB are essential to Battery Management System (BMS). The methodology was verified with the "Battery Health" dataset in the Python Software, which demonstrates a significant increase in accuracy as well as lower Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) of 1.5871, Mean Square Error (MSE) of 2.5190, and improved R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.9497.

title: Edge Computing and Information Fusion Algorithm for Water Environment Monitoring Network
authors: Yili Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Aiming at the key problems of insufficient spatiotemporal coverage, redundant conflicts and noise interference of multi-source heterogeneous sensor data in water environment monitoring network of water conservancy projects, this study aims to improve the accuracy, completeness and real-time performance of monitoring data through information fusion algorithm. In terms of methodology, this paper first performs spatiotemporal alignment and preprocessing of multi-source data such as water quality, hydrology and meteorology; then uses an adaptive weighted fusion algorithm to fuse homogeneous data, and uses the LSTM-DS evidence theory model to deal with conflicts in heterogeneous data; finally, spatiotemporal Kriging interpolation is used to fill monitoring blind spots, and a Bayesian network is constructed to achieve multi-parameter collaborative analysis. Simulation and field tests show that in the monitoring network of a water conservancy hub in the middle reaches of the Yangtze River, the average accuracy of water quality parameters after fusion reached 95.2%, the COD’s MAE was reduced to 0.05, the coverage rate of monitoring blind areas was increased to 94%, and the data delay was controlled within 5 minutes. The conclusion proves that the algorithm effectively solves the key bottleneck of multi-source data fusion and provides reliable technical support for water environment risk warning and precise water resource scheduling.

title: Design and Analysis of Swarm Behaviours Using Centralized and Decentralized Communication Protocols
authors: Bommaganti Keerthana, Usha Rani. Nelakuditi
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Swarm drones, inspired by natural collective behaviors, advance autonomous robotics through centralized, decentralized, or hybrid control strategies. This work compares swarm coordination under different communication protocols, analyzing trade-offs in latency, resilience, and overhead. A hybrid model is proposed using Markov decision processes (MDPs) and probabilistic routing to optimize coordination, reduce bandwidth use, and adapt to failures or disturbances. Swarm performance is assessed via Swarm Cohesion Index (SCI), Coverage Efficiency (CE), Packet Delivery Ratio (PDR), and Mean Time to Convergency (MTC). Results show that intelligent communication architectures significantly improve resilience and mission efficiency in dynamic environments.

title: AI-Driven Fraud Detection and Risk Management in Cross-Industry Business Processes
authors: Anjaneyulu Medara, Gautam Thareja
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: AI-driven fraud detection identifies suspicious patterns and anomalies in financial transactions, improving security by automating the detection process. It reduces the need for manual intervention by enabling organizations to react swiftly to possible threats. False positives, which mark legitimate transactions as fraudulent, could be produced by the system. It also requires regular updates to stay effective against evolving fraud tactics. To overcome this problem, in this manuscript AI-Driven Fraud Detection and risk management in business processes: A cross-industry study (AIDFD-GGNN) is proposed. At first, the input data are gathered from Fraudulent Transaction Detection Dataset. The data are pre-processed using Adaptive Two-Stage Unscented Kalman Filter (ATUKF), which is used missing data and outlier removal the input data. Then the data are fed into Human Evolutionary Optimization Algorithm (HEOA) for feature selection. The selected features are provided to Gegenbauer Graph Neural Networks (GGNN) for detect the fraud and classify such as legitimate transaction and fraudulent transaction. The proposed technique is implemented in Python, and the efficacy of the AIDFD-GGNN technique was assessed using various performance measures, like accuracy, recall, precision, and the Receiver Operating Characteristic (ROC) curve. The performance of the AIDFD-GGNN method achieved 99.54% higher Accuracy; 99.14%higher Precision; 99.16% higher Recall when analysed through existing techniques such as Transparency and Privacy: The Role of Explainable AI and Federated Learning in Financial Fraud Detection (TPAI-FFD-ML), Enhancing Medicare Fraud Detection with a CNN-Transformer-XGBoost Framework and Explainable AI (MFD-EAI-CNN) and Explainable Artificial Intelligence and Causal Inference based ATM Fraud Detection (ATM-FD-GAN), respectively.

title: Bidirectional Grid-Friendly EV Charger Design Using Non-Isolated DC-DC Converter with ANN-Based Charge Controller
authors: Cinu S Robin, K.S. Saji
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: this research presents a bidirectional, grid-friendly electric vehicle (EV) charger that combines a non-isolated Interleaved Luo DC-DC converter with an Artificial Neural Network (ANN)-based charge controller. The goal is to provide efficient grid-to-vehicle (G2V) and vehicle-to-grid (V2G) energy transfer while ensuring high power quality and stability. The system is modelled and simulated using MATLAB/Simulink to examine performance metrics such as voltage regulation, current ripple, and overall charging efficiency. The proposed design achieves a peak efficiency of 97% while minimizing total harmonic distortion (THD), ensuring consistent bidirectional power flow and improved grid interaction. The findings show that the ANN-controlled converter provides a small, efficient, and intelligent solution for smart grid-integrated EV charging applications. The proposed charger achieves 97% efficiency, outperforming SEPIC (95%) and CUK (94%) converters, demonstrating its superiority over existing EV charging systems.

title: Establishing a Blockchain-Enabled Access Control Architecture to Support Secure, Distributed Smart Campus Applications within Large-Scale Urban Networks
authors: Rashi Goswami, Ch.Babaiah, Swati, Abhinay Kumar, Sudeep Kumar Gupta, Nitika Vats Doohan
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: In an age of ubiquitous computing and intelligent urban infrastructures, the challenge of managing secure and decentralized delivery of critical digital resources in the context of large-scale smart campuses is an important one. The classical centralized location-based access control systems are prone to single points of failure, poor scalability and weak privacy assurance. To deal with such drawbacks, this paper introduces the concept of a Blockchain-Enabled Access Control Architecture (BEACA) that would be used with secure, distributed smart campus services in large urban networks. This architecture combines blockchain, its non-mutability and decentralized ledger with smart access decision-making policies to offer tamper-resistant identity management and permission enforcement properties. The framework is based on a new algorithm, the Dynamic Multi-Tier Blockchain Access Control Protocol (DMT-BCAC), implemented in a four-layer model that unites Decentralized Identifiers (DIDs), the Role-Based Access Control (RBAC), the context-aware Oracles, and Byzantine Fault Tolerant (BFT) consensus algorithms. This solution guarantees safe authentication and real-time context validation and a transparent access log without compromising the privacy of users and scalability of the system. Smart contracts automatically grant the permission, and with the multi-level verification, there will be increased trust and interoperability on campus entities. The proposed model of BEACA shows an effective and evolution-compatible legend to the changing cybersecurity needs in smart campuses, which supports the security of communication and control of the access to various academic and administration services in the smart city environment. The suggested BEACA approach has an overall accuracy of 98.4 percent.

title: AI-Driven Framework for Automated Vulnerability Detection and Prioritization in Organizational Networks
authors: Deepak Tomar, Kismat Chhillar
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: This paper presents a novel intelligent framework developed to automate the identification and ranking of security vulnerabilities in organizational networks. Conventional vulnerability management techniques relying heavily on manual inspection and outdated signature-based scanning struggle to match the pace and complexity of today’s evolving cyber threats. The proposed framework integrates advanced machine learning, deep learning, and natural language processing techniques to autonomously detect, classify, and prioritize vulnerabilities with enhanced precision and efficiency. By leveraging real-time threat intelligence and contextual data from the network environment, it moves beyond static severity ratings to deliver adaptive, risk-based prioritization. This approach enables organizations to deploy their cybersecurity resources more strategically. The paper further explores the system’s architecture, core components, applied models, and the methodology adopted for implementation and performance evaluation.

title: SATransformer: Spectrally Aligned Transformers for Energy Forecasting with Heterogeneous Exogenous Drivers
authors: Junfeng Cheng, Baoqing Pu
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Accurate energy forecasting is a cornerstone of modern power system operation, market planning, and large-scale renewable integration, yet remains challenging due to the non-stationary, multi-scale, and exogenously driven nature of real-world energy time series. Classical statistical models struggle to capture nonlinear temporal structure and complex interactions among external drivers, while existing deep learning methods often degrade when faced with heterogeneous, asynchronous, and noisy exogenous signals. To address these challenges, we propose SATransformer, a frequency-aware Transformer encoder that explicitly models spectral structure and cross-variable interactions in energy forecasting. The proposed framework introduces Spectrally Adaptive Rotary Positional Encoding (SARPE), which extracts dominant frequency components and embeds frequency-phase information directly into the attention mechanism. In addition, SATransformer adopts a granularity-aware representation strategy, combining patch-level embeddings for endogenous dynamics with variate-level embeddings for exogenous drivers, mediated by a learnable series token for stable cross-variable alignment. By integrating spectrally adaptive attention with structured endogenous–exogenous interactions, SATransformer jointly captures fine-grained temporal variations and long-range periodic dependencies within a unified architecture. Extensive experimental results on real-world energy forecasting datasets demonstrate the effectiveness and superiority of our approach.

title: Quantum Fuzzy Cognitive Maps: A Step Forward
authors: Theofanis Kalampokas, George A. Papakostas
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: As Quantum Machine Learning (QML) continues to mature, there is an increasing demand for novel architectures that broaden the ways quantum models learn from data, represent knowledge, and perform inference. In this work, we address this need by introducing a hybrid Quantum Fuzzy Cognitive Map (QFCM) model for pattern recognition, in which the core arithmetic operations of the classical Fuzzy Cognitive Map (FCM) inference mechanism are replaced by a parameterized quantum circuit. The proposed model preserves the structural foundations and causal inference principles of classical FCMs, while leveraging quantum computation to model and process interactions among concepts. The proposed QFCM was evaluated on six benchmark datasets, demonstrating competitive performance and achieving a 9.37% increase in prediction confidence compared to the classical FCM. Overall, this work contributes to the growing QML ecosystem by embedding the fundamental principles of FCMs into a quantum circuit–based formulation, thereby extending their representational and computational capabilities.

title: Over-the-Air Backdoor Threats Against Pre-trained Models for Automatic Modulation Recognition
authors: Xu Gan, Hongjun Wang, Xinhao Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: Automatic modulation recognition (AMR) is a key technology for intelligent communications and spectrum sharing in cognitive radio networks. Existing supervised AMR methods typically rely on massive labeled signals and struggle to generalize to unknown classes. Recent advances in self-supervised learning-based large language models have inspired AMR researchers to develop pre-trained models (PTMs) from unlabeled signals and to deploy PTMs for various downstream communication tasks. However, open wireless propagation and publicly available PTMs can introduce potential security risks, particularly emerging and severe backdoor threats. To reveal how attackers can induce downstream classifiers built on PTMs to inherit malicious backdoor behaviors, we explore the first backdoor attack against PTMs in AMR, focusing on a modulation class-agnostic and data-free practical scenario. Our key and novel idea is to decouple latent representations of signals to implicitly bridge the trigger and target class through a proxy target prototype. We formalize trigger generation and backdoor implantation into PTMs as a joint optimization problem with dual-level constraints. We prove the non-convexity of this problem and derive an iterative projection descent method to solve it. Meanwhile, we propose a random design for trigger embedding positions and model the dynamic wireless channel for malicious trigger propagation. Furthermore, we implement the first real-world over-the-air backdoor attack in a non-line-of-sight (NLoS) wireless communication scenario, achieving an attack success rate that exceeds 86% despite lacking any prior knowledge of downstream tasks.

title: Robust prediction of shopper intent via heterogeneous feature interaction and gradient calibration
authors: Min Qu, Changyu Ao, Tianpei Xu
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Online purchase intention prediction using clickstream data is constrained by the inherent heterogeneity of tabular features and severe class imbalance (up to 1:9). Standard deep learning architectures often implement uniform processing for categorical and continuous variables, failing to account for high-order feature couplings and the gradient dominance of the majority class during optimization. This paper proposes an Adaptive Heterogeneous Attention Network (AHAN) to address these structural limitations.The architecture incorporates two primary components: (1) a Multi-Branch Contextual Attention Module (MBA-CM) that projects features into parallel subspaces to isolate and model high-order nonlinear interactions between numerical and categorical inputs; and (2) an Adaptive Gradient Focusing Loss (AGFL) that reweights the loss function to prioritize minority-class samples during backpropagation. The proposed framework was validated on two distinct datasets: the public OSPI benchmark and a real-world global e-commerce behavior (GEB) dataset. Empirical results demonstrate that AHAN achieves a Precision-Recall AUC (PR-AUC) of 0.702 on OSPI and 0.638 on GEB, representing a statistically significant improvement over Gradient Boosting Decision Tree (GBDT) baselines and standard deep tabular models such as FT-Transformer. The findings confirm that type-sensitive feature interaction and adaptive gradient modulation effectively improve predictive robustness across both public and production-scale imbalanced datasets.

title: Efficient RF Power Amplification and Combination Over-the-Air Based on Constant-Envelope Components
authors: Marius Diacu, João Guerreiro, Rui Dinis, João P. Oliveira, Paulo Montezuma, Pedro Viegas
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Energy efficiency (EE) and consumption have become critical concerns for mobile telecommunications operators, where the radio access network (RAN) accounts for the majority of the energy consumption. In 4G, 5G, and upcoming 6G systems, the multicarrier waveforms employed, such as orthogonal frequency-division multiplexing (OFDM), pose significant RAN EE challenges due to their high peak-to-average power ratios (PAPRs), imposing strict transmitter-level linearity requirements. However, RF power amplifiers (PAs) display a trade-off between their linearity and efficiency. This work explores power combination over-the-air (PCOA), where four constant-envelope components are individually amplified by switched-mode PAs (SMPAs) and transmitted by a 4 × 4 microstrip patch antenna array. A 2 × 2 subarray is allocated to each component, and beamforming is used to combine them at the channel level, minimizing circuit-based power combiner insertion losses. Analytical, optimized sizing strategies are presented for the SMPAs, at the system level, and for the array. Moreover, a high-level PCOA model is developed to simulate the impact of the component-level scalings, PA sizings, noise, insertion losses, array mutual coupling, mutual coupling compensation strategy, steering direction, and path loss on the PCOA system performance. For all scalings, it is shown that PCOA is correctly performed to any steering direction within a sector, introduces array gain, and reduces the interference to neighboring systems, with the array presenting radiation efficiencies as high as 81.4%. This suggests that the PCOA technique can play a key role in addressing the EE-linearity trade-off in upcoming 6G systems.

title: An Analytical Simplified Time-Domain Model based Frequency Feedforward-Controlled LLC Converter with Resonant Current Overshoot Suppression_supp1-3691361.docx
authors: Yuqi Wei
publish: N/A
abstract: Due to the inherent nonlinearity of the inductor-inductor-capacitor (LLC) resonant converter, traditional linear control strategies are inadequate for handling dynamic processes. Therefore, frequency feedforward control is widely adopted to improve dynamic performance. The feedforward frequency can be obtained by simplifying nonlinear time-domain equations. However, existing models rely on iteration, which limits accuracy and increases computational burden. To improve model accuracy and reduce computation complexity, an analytical simplified time-domain model is proposed. By applying Taylor Series to eliminate sine and cosine functions in time-domain equations, an analytical solution for frequency is derived. Among various simplified models, the proposed model achieves the highest accuracy and the fastest calculation speed. Based on the simplified model, a frequency feedforward control strategy is developed. The feedforward frequency is calculated in the controller, while a linear compensator eliminates the model error. Furthermore, existing feedforward control strategies suffer from abrupt frequency changes, causing severe current overshoot. Therefore, a resonant current overshoot suppression scheme is proposed. When the reference voltage steps up, the switching frequency and output voltage follows a quadratic path, allowing the frequency changing smoothly. Finally, a 500-W LLC converter prototype is built. Compared with existing feedforward control, the proposed method reduces the current overshoot by 84.1% (below resonance region) and 48.4% (above resonance region), while shortening the response time by 11.1% (below resonance region) and 39.0% (above resonance region), respectively.

title: Precision Wellness Management in the Digital Workplace: Leveraging Machine Learning for Tailored Nature-Based Interventions
authors: Nathaphan Meemuk, Mahasak Ketcham, Montean Rattanasiriwongwut
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Cybernetics and Innovations (ICCI)
abstract: Remote and hybrid work arrangements have introduced distinct psychological burdens-including technostress and social isolation-that standard employee wellness programs consistently fail to address through their uniform, one-size-fits-all design. This paper presents a DataDriven Precision Wellness Framework that integrates K-Means clustering with Nature-Based Solutions to enable cluster-specific, targeted intervention delivery.

title: Multimodal Museum Learning: Complex History Visualization through the Orchestration of Models, Interaction, and Narrative
authors: Zhichao Yang, Sisi Wu, Yang He
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 9th International Conference on Education and E-Learning
abstract: None

title: Exploring the Impact of Virtual Reality (VR) Technology on the Learning Motivation of Mainland China Grade 7 Students in Classical Chinese
authors: Jia Guo, Wing Cheung Alex Tse
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 9th International Conference on Education and E-Learning
abstract: None

title: A Deep Learning Model for Image Super-Resolution and Colorization
authors: Marella Venkatrao, Gopi Viswanath, Mutluri Praveen Kumar, Lakkoju Venkatesh, Mamidi Kiran Kumar, Madhuri Pocha, Yamini Chandana
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: This study introduces a deep learning-based framework for automatic image colorization, which aims to generate realistic color representations from grayscale input images. Leveraging the power of Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), our model is designed to learn the complex mapping between luminance and chrominance components in natural images. The architecture adopts an encoder-decoder structure enriched with skip connections to effectively capture both global semantic information and fine-grained spatial details. Training is conducted on large-scale color image datasets, including ImageNet and Places, enabling the network to generalize across a wide range of scene types and object categories. Quantitative evaluation using Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR) and Structural Similarity Index Measure (SSIM) indicates strong performance relative to existing methods. Qualitative results further confirm the model’s ability to produce vibrant and semantically coherent colorizations. Due to its compact design and end-to-end trainability, the proposed system holds promise for practical deployment in domains such as historical image restoration, content creation, and digital entertainment.

title: Design and development of drone-based electronic nose for monitoring of air pollution with precise spatial location
authors: Palak Gupta, Agamjit Singh, Gurjot Singh, Rajat Mohan Jakhar, Vishal Srivastava
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Air pollution is a pressing environmental concern that poses significant risks to human health and the planet’s well-being. Standard methods for air quality monitoring, such as fixed air quality monitoring stations or satellite-based remote sensing, provides useful information but are majorly limited by constraints in only spatial localities, navigability, and real-time live monitoring. To address these limitations, this study presents a drone integrated electronic nose system designed for real-time, high-resolution air quality assessment. The proposed system is built on a quadcopter platform (F450/Q450 frame) powered by 920KV brushless DC motors, 10*4.5 HD propellers, 30A ESCs, and an APM 2.8 flight controller, supported by a 5200mAH li-po battery. The payload comprises a multi sensor e nose array (MQ-2, MQ-5, MQ-9, MH-Z19 and Dust Sensor) capable of detecting carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and combustible gases, PM (Particulate Matter) supplemented with an infrared camera for contextual visualization. An ESP32 microcontroller ensures seamless integration of sensor data with GPS based positioning, data transmission in between drone and ground station by using ESP, and remote monitoring capabilities. Results from more than 20 field missions validate the system’s capacity to detect pollution hotspots, monitor gas plume dispersion, and deliver reliable micro-scale air quality measurements. The findings demonstrate the feasibility and effectiveness of UAV based platforms for environmental surveillance, offering a cost efficient, flexible, and scalable approach to urban air quality management.

title: Real-Time Financial Fraud Detection Using Hybrid CNN-LSTM with Random Forest Ensemble
authors: Siddartha Sunkara, Divyansh Mishra, Pasala Varun, Rohith Sekhar Kadali, N. Neelima
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Fraud costs banks billions every year - over $10 billion in 2023 according to FTC data. We built a hybrid system combining CNNs, LSTMs, and Random Forest to catch fraudulent credit card transactions. The dataset we used has 284,807 transactions but only 0.17% are fraud, which makes this problem really hard. Our approach works by having CNN-LSTM extract patterns from individual transactions and also from sequences over time, then Random Forest uses these features for final classification. On the test set we got F1-score of 0.80, precision 0.89, and recall 0.72. Cross-validation across five folds gave F1 of 0.388 ± 0.070 and AUC-ROC of 0.96 ± 0.01, showing the model is stable. We added SHAP explanations because banks need to understand why the model flags certain transactions for regulatory reasons. Our false positive rate came out to only 0.019%, so legitimate transactions rarely get blocked - this matters a lot since annoying customers with false declines is a real problem in production systems.

title: Smart Petition Handling System
authors: A Balamurali, V Udhayavasan, A Venith
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Processing public petitions in government and other institutions often suffers from many of the same issues as those found in other business processes, including delays, manual-heavy processes, and poorly defined routing. The project’s main goal was to design an AI-based petition management system that processes petitions by receiving them via email, reading them, classifying them, and routing them automatically. This automated approach, which leverages Natural Language Processing techniques to extract and classify text, significantly reduces the manual effort required to process petitions and dramatically decreases the time to complete the entire process, from receipt to routing.

title: A U-Net Approach for Denoising and Restoring Old Film Reels
authors: Swathi P Biradar, Vaishnavi Tiwari, Adarsh Dodamani, Siddarth Gayakawad, Uday Kulkarni
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The restoration of aging film reels poses significant challenges due to the complex and nonlinear degradations introduced by dust, scratches, fading, grain instability, and scanning artifacts. This paper presents a Residual U-Net based restoration framework specifically designed to address such real analog defects using the Old Film Restoration Dataset (OFRD). The model incorporates residual learning within a multi-scale encoder–decoder structure, enabling improved gradient flow, enhanced texture recovery, and stable reconstruction of fine visual details. A two-phase progressive training strategy is employed, beginning with low-resolution global structure learning at 192×192 followed by high-resolution fine-tuning at 256×256 for detailed texture restoration. Quantitative evaluation using Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR) and Structural Similarity Index Measure (SSIM) demonstrates significant improvements over CNN and U-Net baselines, achieving a peak performance of 35.15dB PSNR and 0.91 SSIM. Qualitative results further highlight the model’s effectiveness in restoring clarity and structural consistency in degraded film frames. The findings indicate that the proposed Residual U-Net offers a robust and practical solution for real-world old-film restoration tasks.

title: Explainable Machine Learning Agent for Forged Document Detection in E-Communication and Educational Services
authors: N Kamal, V Aarthi, G Yuvasri, S.N. Madhumitha, S Rogini, S Rohini
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Since even small forgeries can have major ethical and managerial ramifications, the quick development of electronic communication and web-based learning services makes document verification more difficult. Time-consuming manual checking is ineffective against complex forgeries. To close this gap, this paper proposes an Explainable Machine Learning Agent that leverages a refined ResNet50 model to identify forged documents and signatures. The proposed model uses a deep residual learning-based spatial feature extractor with SHAP-based interpretability that allows high accuracy along with transparency in decisions. The model was trained on an 80-20 stratified split of the combined dataset of handwritten signatures, namely CEDAR, BHSig260–Bengali, and BHSig260–Hindi. Key performance indicators for accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and AUROC were used to test the results. With an overall accuracy of 95% and an AUC of 0.96, this design surpassed previous benchmarks such VGG19, MobileNetV2, Random Forest, and Logistic Regression. In addition to performance, SHAP explainability analysis offered feature attributions that could be interpreted for forecast tracking and auditing. Therefore, the agent provides a workable, intelligible method that can be readily implemented for document forgery detection in real-world e-communications and educational verification systems.

title: Zero-Shot Beamforming for RIS Systems via Differentiable RZF
authors: Zilu Zhao
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces (RIS) are an effective solution for improving wireless coverage under severe blockage. However, existing deep learning-based methods usually require extensive offline training and suffer from limited generalization, while conventional optimization approaches may incur high computational complexity. This paper proposes a model-driven online gradient descent (MD-OGD) method for RIS-assisted multiple-input single-output (MISO) systems. A joint design framework is considered, where the active beamforming is computed via a closed-form regularized zero-forcing (RZF) solution, and the passive RIS beamforming is optimized online to maximize the system sum-rate under Rician fading channels. By embedding a differentiable regularized zeroforcing (RZF) layer into the optimization process, the proposed approach enables a training-free and interpretable beamforming framework. A manifold-based parameterization is adopted to satisfy the unit-modulus constraint of RIS elements. Simulation results show that the proposed method outperforms random phase configurations and non-RIS benchmarks. At a transmit power of 30 dBm, a sum-rate of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$21.48 \text{bps} / \text{Hz}$</tex> is achieved, demonstrating the potential effectiveness of the proposed zero-shot beamforming approach.

title: DRFEC-Net: A Framework for Accurate Diabetic Retinopathy Classification
authors: Veerendra Basreddy, Sachinkumar Veerashetty
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Diabetic retinopathy (DR) is a leading cause of vision impairment, where early detection from retinal fundus images is critical. Existing approaches using deep learning, such as Vision Transformers, achieve promising results but often struggle with low-quality images, subtle lesion detection, and class imbalance, limiting reliable automated screening. Addressing these challenges, this work proposes Deep Retinal Feature Enhancement and Classification Network (DRFEC-Net), a unified framework integrating preprocessing, segmentation, and classification for accurate DR detection. The framework employs multi-scale convolution and dilated convolution operations with dictionary learning for image enhancement, an adaptive encoder-decoder network with skip connections for precise vessel and lesion segmentation, and a hybrid feature-based regression-tree classifier for stage-wise DR grading. Evaluation on APTOS-2019 and DDR datasets demonstrated superior performance, achieving 93.2% and 90.5% accuracy, respectively for classification. The results highlight DRFEC-Net’s capability to provide reliable, automated DR diagnosis.

title: Computational Acceleration of Automatic Speech Recognition Based on Multi-Accelerator Heterogeneous Computing Systems
authors: Caixia He, Zhanpeng Li, Mengya Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Automatic speech recognition (ASR) enables realtime captioning and automatic transcription, and has become a key supporting technology for the digital transformation of modern offices. Since ASR systems typically need to perform a series of computationally-intensive tasks, such as speech enhancement, speech separation and speech recognition, they commonly have high processing latency. To realize a real-time ASR system, many studies resort to using heterogeneous computing (HC) architectures to accelerate ASR. By designing specific GPU and FPGA accelerators for the computationally-intensive ASR tasks, the processing time of ASR system can be significantly reduced. In the past years, the HC technology had demonstrated good performance improvements in ASR. However, most HC studies use single-accelerator heterogeneous computing architectures (SAHCA), e.g., CPU/GPU or CPU/FPGA. Given that different hardware architectures have different advantages and are suited to perform different types of tasks, the multi-accelerator heterogeneous computing architectures (MA-HCA), e.g., CPU/FPGA/NPU architecture, can theoretically achieve better performance than SA-HCA in case that each computationally-intensive ASR task is scheduled to run on the most suitable core at runtime. Therefore, this paper proposes a multi-accelerator CPU/NPU/GPU/FPGA heterogeneous computing architecture dedicated to the timeconsuming ASR tasks for the purpose of realizing a real-time and low-power ASR system. Experimental results show that the proposed MA-HCA system can effectively support high-performance ASR while still maintaining low energy consumption.

title: Innovative Machine Learning for Edge-Cloud Disaster Management: A New Approach to Analyzing Emergency Data in Real Time for Improved Crisis Response
authors: Kailasam Muthukumarasamy, Mahmoud Odeh, Gaurav Pushkarna, Akila Venkatraman, Selvin Ebenezer S, M. Shyni
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: The emergency response systems are facing a growing number of challenges due to the increased frequency and intensity of both natural and artificial disasters. When every second counts, traditional disaster management falls short due to sluggish data analysis and the inherent centralisation of cloud computing. To better respond to and make decisions in the aftermath of disasters, this study presents a machine learning architecture for real-time emergency data analysis that leverages cloud and edge computing. To keep things running smoothly, enhance situational awareness, and reduce latency, cognitive processing capabilities are shared between cloud platforms and edge devices. To identify outliers, forecast when risk will escalate, and prioritise emergency actions, machine learning algorithms at the network’s periphery analyse data from sensors, social media, surveillance systems, and communication networks. Cloud computing processes large amounts of data, improves prediction models, and aids planning. Due to its hybrid design, this system can manage global analytical data and respond quickly at the local level. Disaster management edge-cloud collaboration architecture, data flow, learning mechanisms, and operational benefits are studied. Dynamic and unpredictable environments foster adaptive learning, scalability, resilience, and real-time response. Results show that machine learning and edge-cloud computing improve decision reliability, resource allocation accuracy, and crisis response efficiency. This strategy greatly advances the development of proactive, robust, and intelligent disaster management systems capable of handling complex real-world situations.

title: Performance Optimization Model for Hybrid Communication Network Considering Possible Costs
authors: Yuerong Zhao, Bo Jiang, Chengdong Wu, Kang Li
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: With the rapid development of economic and technological progress, the power grid, as the core hub of power transmission, is accelerating its transformation from traditional models to intelligent systems. The growing demand for communication poses significant challenges to traditional power line communication. To adapt to the development of smart grids and ensure the efficiency and stability of communication, it is imperative to establish a new communication model. This paper proposes a cost-oriented hybrid power grid network performance optimization model by integrating power line communication with wireless communication. The model comprehensively considers power grid construction costs, communication converter deployment, and QoS metrics such as data latency, bandwidth, and link reliability. Simulation results show that the model exhibits good convergence after multiple iterations. By adjusting parameters such as the maximum communication distance, the final results align closely with theoretical expectations, validating the model's feasibility and practical value.

title: AIoT-Based Hybrid Framework for Victim Detection in Disaster Scenarios
authors: Vivek Sharma, Manish Dixit
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: In disaster scenarios such as earthquakes, landslides, and building collapses, rapid and accurate identification of trapped victims is crucial for effective search and rescue (SAR) operations. Traditional image-based detection models often fail under debris-covered or low-visibility conditions due to occlusions and complex backgrounds. This paper proposes a Hybrid Framework for disaster victim detection in debris environments. The proposed approach integrates with Internet of Things, Deep learning–based feature extraction using Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) with a Decision Tree classifier. Deep learning models extract spatial and contextual features from aerial and ground-level imagery, while the Decision Tree module enhances classification accuracy and reduces false detections through rule-based analysis. You Only Look Once (YOLO) algorithms to identify humans in complex catastrophe scenarios. This hybrid framework presents a promising direction for developing intelligent, explainable, and resource-efficient victim detection systems for disaster management. This integration reinforces the framework by combining YOLO’s real-time deep learning capabilities through the interpretability and fast decision-making of a Decision Tree, finally achieving more robust victim detection under complex disaster conditions.

title: Blending Serverful and Serverless Cloud Resources for Cost-Effective Workflow Execution
authors: Quanwang Wu, Qixin Zhou, Ruyi Sun, Mengchu Zhou, Ji Feng, Taolin Guo, Chao Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Serverless computing offers fine-grained billing and elastic scalability, making it appealing for workflow execution. However, it also suffers from cold-start latency and stricter execution constraints. In contrast, traditional serverful cloud resources, such as virtual machines, provide coarser provisioning granularity but benefit from relatively lower unit cost. This work explores the potential of blending serverful and serverless resources to harness their complementary strengths for cost-effective workflow execution. We propose a hybrid resource management framework that dynamically allocates workflow tasks across both types of resources. A Budget-constrained Workflow scheduling algorithm for Blended cloud (BWB) is developed to minimize makespan while respecting user-specified budget. Evaluation experiments are conducted under real-world cloud settings by using realistic workflow applications. BWB is compared against state-of-the-art approaches for serverful, serverless, and blended clouds. Experimental results show that BWB consistently outperforms its state-of-the-art peers, achieving makespan reductions ranging from 10.6% to 37.6%, thereby demonstrating the cost-effectiveness of blending cloud resources for workflow execution.

title: Green Edge Intelligence: A Hardware-Aware Evolutionary Boosting Framework for Sustainable Urban Traffic Forecasting
authors: Rongliang Lin, Guanqi Peng, Jiayang Dan
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Real-time traffic signal control in Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS) requires millisecond-level prediction latency, yet state-of-the-art deep learning models incur prohibitive computational costs on resource-constrained edge devices. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a Hardware-Aware Evolutionary Boosting Framework that integrates a spatio-temporal feature mining strategy with a Particle Swarm Optimizer (PSO) for lightweight inference. The framework explicitly optimizes tree structures and hyperparameters under strict edge hardware constraints while capturing complex spatio-temporal dependencies via rule-based meta-knowledge extraction. Experimental results on benchmark PEMS-BAY and METR-LA datasets show that the framework achieves an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2}$</tex> score of 0.9560 and an MAE of 0.8317, statistically comparable to advanced deep learning baselines like ST-GCN and LSTM. Deployment tests on a standard x86-based edge node demonstrate an inference latency of only 0.135 ms and a compact model size of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.159 \text{MB}$</tex>, reducing energy consumption by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$99.8 \%$</tex>. Furthermore, the model exhibits superior fault tolerance, sustaining reliable predictions even with up to 40% missing sensor data. Ultimately, this framework achieves Pareto-optimal performance, balancing high accuracy, computational efficiency, and resilience for sustainable urban infrastructure.

title: A Comparative Study of Missing-Value Handling and Feature Selection for Arrhythmia Classification Using Machine Learning
authors: Gawalee Phatai, Tidarat Luangrungruang, Kritsada Srikhirin, Pakawat Samong
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Cybernetics and Innovations (ICCI)
abstract: Cardiac arrhythmia classification using machine learning has gained increasing attention as a clinical decision-support approach. However, benchmark datasets such as the UCI Arrhythmia dataset present several challenges, including a high proportion of missing values, severe class imbalance, and high feature dimensionality, all of which can adversely affect model performance. This study systematically examines the impact of missing-value handling strategies, missing indicator features, and feature selection on arrhythmia classification. Decision Tree, Random Forest, and Support Vector Machine classifiers are evaluated under a unified experimental framework using repeated 5×5-fold cross-validation. Missing values are addressed through median imputation, missing indicator features are optionally introduced, and ReliefF is applied for feature selection. Experimental results show that the effectiveness of missing indicators depends on the learning algorithm. While missing indicators alone do not consistently improve tree-based models, they substantially enhance Support Vector Machine performance. When combined with feature selection, all models benefit, with Random Forest achieving the best overall results.

title: AI-Powered Diagnosis and Categorization of Gastrointestinal Diseases
authors: Sarthak Kaushal, Law Kumar Singh, Diya Agnihotri, Disha Garg, Bhavpreet Kaur, Vishan Kumar Gupta
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Early and effective diagnosis of gastrointestinal (GI) anomalies in endoscopic images can enhance patient care and clinical decision-making. This paper introduces an end-to-end, AI-driven pipeline for diagnosis and classification of eight GI findings on the Kvasir-v2 dataset (8,000 images; classes: dyed-lifted polyps, dyed-resection margins, esophagitis, normal cecum, normal pylorus, normal z-line, polyps, ulcerative colitis). We train a light convolutional neural network ≈3.3M parameters) on batch-normalized inputs with ELU activations, L2 regularization, and early stopping on 128 × 128 inputs. On a stratified hold-out test set (20% of the data), the model achieves 88% accuracy with macro F1-score 0.88; per-class performance is highest for normal-pylorus (F1 = 0.97) and normal-cecum (F1 = 0.95). Five-fold stratified cross-validation achieves mean accuracy of 76.97%, illustrating generalization across splits. Confusion-matrix analysis reveals ongoing confusion between esophagitis and visually similar normal z-line (esophagitis recall = 0.63), suggesting directions for targeted augmentation and class-aware optimization. The suggested pipeline is light, reproducible, and deployable on commodity hardware, and hence a strong baseline for GI image classification and a building block for future clinical studies that incorporate multi-view sequences and expert priors.

title: Residual-Based Destination Prioritization Using 3-Class Review Sentiment: Nepal Proxy Evidence Toward Three Religions, Three Rivers Community Tourism IS
authors: Patcha Treemongkol, Suwannee Hongwijit, Nathaphan Meemuk
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Cybernetics and Innovations (ICCI)
abstract: This study examines whether tourist sentiment toward Nepal's cultural and natural heritage sites varies systematically across locations and how such variation can be translated into actionable destination priorities. A corpus of 7,271 online reviews was collected for 10 proxy destinations and classified into three sentiment classes (positive, neutral, negative). A chi-square test of independence on the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$10 \times 3$</tex> contingency table indicated a statistically significant association between location and sentiment <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(X^{2}(18)=340.19,\ \mathrm{p}<0.001\right)$</tex>; Cramér's <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$V=0.153$</tex>), confirming that sentiment profiles are not uniformly distributed across destinations. Adjusted standardized residuals were then computed to identify locationsentiment cells that contributed most to the overall departure from independence, following applied practice that treats <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left\vert\mathrm{r}_{\text{ij}}^{\text {(Adj) }}\right\vert>2$</tex> as a notable deviation from the null expectation. The residuals were aggregated into a non-positive priority score for each destination, yielding an interpretable ranking in which Langtang and Lumbini exhibit the highest excess neutral sentiment, whereas Everest Base Camp Trek shows concurrent excess neutral and negative sentiment. These patterns indicate locations where visitor experiences are disproportionately ambivalent or dissatisfied relative to the global baseline, even though positive sentiment dominates overall. From an information systems perspective, the residualbased ranking provides a defensible, data-driven input for sequencing destination-level interventions, complementing existing tourism sentiment-mining approaches that leverage user-generated content for managerial decision-making. The study also highlights methodological considerations, including selection and evaluation biases in online reviews, class imbalance, and potential misclassification of neutral content, and outlines future research directions integrating aspect-based sentiment analysis and multi-platform data to refine locationspecific service improvement strategies.

title: Evaluating Locally Discriminative Learning for Hallucination Suppression in Remote-Sensing Super-Resolution
authors: Wai Phu Paing, JingTao Yao
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: While GAN-based super-resolution methods can synthesize visually sharp textures, they often introduce hallucinated high-frequency artifacts that lack structural reliability. This problem is particularly critical in remote-sensing imagery, where geometric fidelity is essential for accurate downstream analysis. This work evaluates the use of locally discriminative learning as an artifact-aware supervision strategy for remotesensing super-resolution using the UC Merced Land Use dataset. When locally discriminative supervision is applied within an ESRGAN-based framework under identical training conditions, reconstruction quality improves substantially compared with a standard ESRGAN baseline [3]. Specifically, Peak Signal-toNoise Ratio increases from 21.95 dB to 26.18 dB, Structural Similarity Index improves from 0.565 to 0.741, and Learned Perceptual Image Patch Similarity decreases from 0.400 to 0.241. Comparisons with non-adversarial and transformer-based methods further show that architectural changes alone are insufficient to suppress hallucinated artifacts. Qualitative results confirm reduced grid-like distortions in urban regions and more stable texture patterns in agricultural areas, indicating that locally discriminative, variance-aware supervision is effective for artifact-sensitive remote-sensing super-resolution.

title: Reference-Oriented Anisotropic Gradient Clipping for Differentially Private Federated Learning
authors: Cheng Liang, Jian Li
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) has transformed the landscape of distributed machine learning by enabling collaborative model training across decentralized devices. The integration of Differential Privacy (DP) into Federated Learning (FL) often induces substantial utility degradation, particularly in Non-Independent and Identically Distributed (Non-IID) environments, as traditional isotropic gradient clipping cannot distinguish critical global descent from detrimental local drift. We propose the Reference-Oriented Anisotropic Gradient Clipping (ROG-Clip) framework to resolve this. By leveraging a global momentum vector, ROG-Clip orthogonally decomposes local gradients into consensus and drift components. Applying independent, anisotropic clipping thresholds to these distinct subspaces optimally retains global optimization signals while strictly bounding heterogeneous noise, further enhanced by dynamic dual-threshold annealing and an initial isotropic warm-up. Experimental results demonstrate that ROG-Clip achieves favorable outcomes, providing good model utility and improved resilience against gradient inversion attacks.

title: Smart Bio-RF Interface: A Tissue-Integrated Rectenna for Efficient and Safe Wireless Power Harvesting
authors: Zarin Nushiba, Arafat Uddin, Mohammed Lokman, Avijith Roy, Parag Das, Shanchayan Barua, Moon Das
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Early and accurate breast tumor detection and reliable wireless power delivery are pivotal for next-generation implantable systems. This study introduces a dual-purpose ISMband platform comprising (i) a Y-shaped microstrip patch antenna for tumor sensing and (ii) a miniaturized implantable rectenna for power transfer. The Y-shaped patch, patterned with four slots, operates over $2.36-2.45 \mathrm{GHz}$ (center 2.404 GHz), achieves a return loss of -56.204 dB with $\sim 90 \mathrm{MHz}$ impedance bandwidth, and satisfies IEEE C95.1-1999 specific absorption rate (SAR) constraints. Distance-dependent characterization using a multilayer breast phantom demonstrates detection and localization via $S_{11}$ perturbations; for a 6 mm tumor, the reflection coefficient increases by 1.067 dB with a 0.4 MHz resonance shift, and lesions of 1 mm radius remain detectable. Ten-gram averaged SAR remains below limits ($1.25 \mathrm{~W} / \mathrm{kg}$ without tumor; $1.33 \mathrm{~W} / \mathrm{kg}$ with tumor; limit $2 \mathrm{~W} / \mathrm{kg}$). For power delivery, the 2.4 GHz rectenna is geometrically optimized for compactness and biocompatibility, and a harmonic-balance rectifier (ADS) attains 88.65% RF-to-DC conversion in multilayer tissue simulations. SAR compliance holds across implant positions. The integrated sensing-power platform enables safe, battery-free monitoring and paves the way for in vivo validation and motion-robustness testing.

title: QoS-Aware Resource Allocation for Edge AI Inference: Supporting Telemedicine Applications through Regularized Heart Failure Prediction
authors: Zihe Hao, Min Yin, Jingyuan Xu, Zhangqi Liu, Yinlei Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: The deployment of deep learning models on edge infrastructure for clinical decision support demands rigorous evaluation of both predictive accuracy and Quality of Service (QoS) reliability. This paper investigates QoS-aware resource allocation strategies for edge AI inference in telemedicine, using heart failure prediction as the primary application context. Four neural network training regimes are benchmarked: a Baseline model, Dropout regularization, Early Stopping, and L2 weight decay regularization. Experiments are conducted on a clinical dataset of 299 samples, partitioned into 209 training instances and 90 test instances, with evaluation spanning twelve complementary performance dimensions including accuracy, F1-score, area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUC-ROC), Matthews Correlation Coefficient (MCC), Cohen's Kappa, and a weighted composite QoS score that prioritizes clinical safety. L2 Regularization achieves the highest composite QoS score of 0.6675 and the best AUC of 0.8370, while also attaining the lowest aggregate rank across all evaluation criteria. The clinical cost analysis, which assigns a 5:1 penalty weighting to false negatives relative to false positives, confirms that L2 Regularization offers the strongest balance between sensitivity and specificity for edge deployment, whereas Dropout exhibits severe overfitting with perfect training recall collapsing to 0.4286 on unseen data.

authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)

title: AI in Islamic Banking: Smart Risk Governance
authors: Ibrahim Radwan Alnsour
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: Modern Islamic banking is marked by the increasing complexity of financial transactions and a need to be up-to-date with technological advances, in compliance with shariah law. This research interest can be attributed to the need to fix fair, transparent and technology-enabled risk management systems without recourse to speculation and usury. The objective of this paper is to develop a holistic framework for the Islamic banks in risk management using predictive analysis based on AI algorithms. The research methodology used in this study is phenomenological underpinned by the AI-Predictive Risk Framework that uses machine learning algorithms to forecast credit and operational risks. Time series and behavioral data are analyzed by algorithms integrated with Shariah ethical, and technological methods adopted to maintain equilibrium in prohibition undertakings. The findings validate the enhanced quality of risk assessment and decreased propensity towards ISNA when predictive model is incorporated. Its practicability is that the purpose model can be used to build corporate risk management system and its potentiality is that it can be used for constructing intelligent Islamic financial ecological environment.

title: Health Sense AI:Proactive Multimodal AI for ICU Patient Safety Monitoring and Post-Medication Guidance
authors: P Murugeswari, S Aswanth, R Ramanan, A Dhivya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: In intensive care units (ICUs), continuous safety surveillance is the key to preventing adverse clinical events; nevertheless, the traditional alarm systems are generally premised on fixed physiological values and frequently do not provide the ability to notice the early signs of patient distress. Minor indicators such as facial expressions of pain, abnormal body movements, and vocal agitation often precedecritical deterioration. Still, the constant manual observation is not feasible in the ICU with a high workload. The current paper describes HealthSense AI, a proactive multimodal model thatcan identify early signs of patient distress and provide safe post-medication advice. The suggested system combines computer vision,audiosignalprocessing,andnaturallanguage understanding to realise an overall evaluation of the patient’s conditions. Facial action unit analysis and pose estimation are used to measure pain and agitation, while noise-robust speech analysis measures vocal distress in real time. A decision-level fusion mechanism is employed to compute a dynamic risk score, prioritising alerts and alleviating alarm fatigue. Furthermore, a safety-conscious large language model, enhanced with retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), is included to provide clinically informed post-medication advice and reduce unsafe predictions. Simulated ICU experimental evaluation shows that the proposed framework achieves higher accuracy in detecting distress and near real-time execution compared to unimodalapproaches. Thesystemisappropriate for deployment in a real-world setting because of its modular, scalable, and cost-effective architecture. HealthSense AI is a move towards patient-centred, intelligent critical care monitoring.

title: Locomotion of an Elastic Snake Robot via Natural Dynamics
authors: Tristan Ehlert, Arne Sachtler, Annika Schmidt, Davide Calzolari, Alin Albu-Schäffer
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Nature suggests that exploiting the elasticities and natural dynamics of robotic systems could increase their locomotion efficiency. Prior work on elastic snake robots supports this hypothesis, but has not fully exploited the nonlinear dynamic behavior of the systems. Recent advances in eigenmanifold theory enable a better characterization of the natural dynamics in complex nonlinear systems. This letter investigates if and how the nonlinear natural dynamics of a kinematic elastic snake robot can be used to design efficient gaits. Two types of gaits based on natural dynamics are presented and compared to a state-ofthe- art approach using dynamics simulations. The results reveal that a gait generated by switching between two nonlinear normal modes does not improve the locomotion efficiency of the robot. In contrast, gaits based on non-brake periodic trajectories (nonbrake orbits) are perfectly efficient in the energy-conservative case. Further simulations with friction reveal that, in a more realistic scenario, non-brake orbit gaits achieve higher efficiency compared to the baseline gait on the rigid system. Overall, the investigation offers promising insights into the design of gaits based on natural dynamics, fostering further research.

title: Efficient Hybrid Collaborative Perception Based on Ternary Spatial Confidence Map Communication
authors: Jianhang Liu, Yunhao Bu, Mu Zhou, Shanchen Pang, Xuerong Cui, Jiangwan Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Multi-agent collaborative perception technology is attracting considerable attention because it can extend the perception range of autonomous vehicles, and thus improve road safety. However, challenges such as communication redundancy, low perception precision, and a single collaboration mode adversely impact the accuracy and timeliness of environmental perception, especially under bandwidth and latency constraints in complex real-world traffic scenarios. To address the above problems, we design a hybrid collaborative sensing architecture guided by a ternary spatial confidence map. First, we propose a hybrid communication strategy based on the discretization of the classification confidence map and regional overlap assessment, which effectively mitigates communication redundancy among agents by dynamically guiding the selective transmission of intermediate features and late detection results. Furthermore, a dual-branch intermediate feature fusion method is designed, together with a collaborative supplementation mechanism for late detection results. This method combines convolutions with transformers to capture local structures and model global semantics, thereby improving perception accuracy in real-world scenarios. Comprehensive experiments on the OPV2V and DAIR-V2X-C datasets demonstrate that the proposed method effectively reduces communication redundancy and significantly improves perception accuracy in practical environments. Specifically, on the DAIR-V2X-C dataset, compared to existing methods, the proposed method reduces the accuracy loss by approximately 10% at AP@0.5.

title: A Spark-Accelerated Machine Learning Pipeline for Market Trend Analysis and App Success Forecasting in the Google Play Store
authors: Anreddy Shanmukha Reddy, Kolla Sriram Charan, Nissankararao Naga Sai, Niharika Panda
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Millions of mobile applications are available on the Google Play Store and this makes the environment highly competitive and developers need to know the market and forecast the success of an app before launching. Nonetheless, the metadata of the Play Store available can be considered large, noisy, and heterogeneous, and manual analysis is not feasible, and standard small-scale machine learning pipelines cannot be relied upon. The paper in question tackles the issue of predicting the success of an app by posing the question of a monitored binary classification problem depending on the scale of individual installations. The data processing starts with massive data consumption through Apache Spark that allows the task to be effective in managing around 800,000 app records. The data is processed by cleaning up and standardizing it into installs, price and size, imputes the missing values, scaling the derived variables and then calculates an aggregate number of text length, words count and developer level aggregations. Term Frequency-Inverse Document Frequency (TF-IDF) transforms the descriptions of apps into meaningful numerical features and sparse vectors in the high dimension are then reduced using Truncated Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) to produce dense features that are fast and compact. Numeric values are put under normalization and the categorical variables under label-Encoding, leading to a combined feature matrix of both text, metadata, and developer features. Three machine learning models, namely the Random Forest, XGBoost and a Multilayer Perceptron (MLP) are trained on the engineered feature space. The stratified train-validation splits and oversampling are implemented to deal with the imbalance in classes. Preprocessing transformers, as well as model artifacts are stored to reproducible inference. The results of the experiments conducted indicate that the XGBoost and the MLP are the most powerful as they reach high levels of accuracy in differentiating between high-install apps and mid-install apps. The last system proves a scalable and successful method of predicting the success of apps and can be utilized in recommendation engines, market analysis dashboards and automated decision support systems.

title: Artificial Intelligence Models for End-to-End Supply Chain Optimization
authors: Narmin Sayeed, Udoy Sankar Saha, Farhana Karim, Md Mustafizur, Syed Mohammed Muhive Uddin, Md Asikur Rahman Chy
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The growing complexity, volatility and interdependence of current supply chains require smart decision-making frameworks that enable them to run end-to-end supply chain processes. Artificial Intelligence (AI) has become one of the transformational facilitators of optimization of procurement, production, inventory, logistics, and distribution in a holistic way. The current paper proposes a detailed AI-based system of end-to-end optimization of supply chains through the use of machine learning, deep learning, reinforcement learning, as well as hybrid and hybrid optimization algorithms to optimize operations, supply chain resilience, and sustainability. The approach proposed includes demand forecasting with the help of predictive analytics, inventory optimization through the adaptive learning mechanism, logistics routing through the reinforcement learning mechanism, and assessment of supplier risk with the help of explainable AI models. Moreover, the framework facilitates real-time decision making through the combination of IoT-enabling data streams and digital twin models of supply chain networks. Significant progress in the cost reduction, service-level compliance, demand fulfilment accuracy, and disruption reduction when compared to the conventional optimization and rule-based approaches have been shown through extensive experimental appraisals with benchmark and real-world supply chain data. These findings affirm that AI-powered end-to-end optimization is not only able to improve supply chain visibility and supply chain agility but it is also able to offer scalable and resilient solutions in dynamic and uncertain settings. This paper brings about a cohesive AI architecture between strategic, tactical, and operational supply chain decisions which provide practical insights towards smart and sustainable supply chain management.

title: A Multicenter Standardized Gait Database from the ORITEL Network for Cerebral Palsy Gait Analysis
authors: Rosario Ulloa, Esteban J. Pino, Mónica Morante, Lorena Llorente, Véronica Rattin, Manuela Galli
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Gait analysis is a cornerstone of clinical decision-making in cerebral palsy (CP), yet multicenter variability limits comparability and translation. This work reports the creation of a standardized, multicenter gait database and a machine learning (ML) classifier to distinguish gait patterns of CP hemiplegia, CP diplegia, and typically developing group to validate the database. Data is contributed by eight ORITEL motion analysis laboratories (156 sessions, 582 trials) using two systems (Vicon/BTS), with unified nomenclature, unit normalization, and resampling. For classification, 95 sessions (413 trials) meeting inclusion criteria were retained. Feature sets combined kinematic, spatiotemporal, and anthropometric variables. Class imbalance was addressed using sample weighting, dimensionality was reduced with PCA (95% variance), and models were tuned with Optuna and evaluated using stratified group cross-validation and a held-out set. A multilayer perceptron (MLP) with sample weighting achieved the best performance (test accuracy 82%; class F1-scores: diplegia 0.81, hemiplegia 0.63, control 0.98). Feature importance and nonparametric statistics highlighted clinically interpretable discriminants-double support, stride length, swing/stance phases, and minimum knee flexion. An exploratory linkage between classified patterns and reported treatments suggested distinct tendencies across hemiplegia and diplegia, aligning with evidence-based recommendations. The proposed resource and pipeline advance multicenter data standardization and provide a clinically grounded baseline for data-driven support of rehabilitation planning in CP.
