title: MADX for Intelligent Identification of Optimal Attack Path and Cyber-Physical System Attack-Defense Game
authors: Lingyao Wang, Liwan Bao, Wensheng Sheng, Jinliang Xue, Bo Chen, Xiuyuan Ye
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: In the task of intelligent identification of optimal attack paths and dynamic defense strategy generation within Industrial Control Systems (ICS), we proposes a novel MADX hybrid reinforcement learning algorithm to address the core challenges of high-dimensional state coupling, mixed action types, and strong dynamism of attack paths prevalent. The algorithm innovatively integrates the strengths of MADQN and MADDPG. Specifically, we design a hybrid interaction architecture that is serial across camps and parallel within camps, ensuring the strict temporal sequencing of the attack chain while significantly boosting training efficiency through intra-camp parallel execution. We use a centralized Critic-Actor structure to facilitate precise modeling of continuous defense actions and provide global value guidance. And we introduce a Global Trajectory Experience Replay Buffer, storing complete, continuous attack-defense trajectories to preserve the causal relationships and temporal dependencies. Furthermore, we conducted experiments on the DARPA 2000 (general multi-agent task) and SWaT (ICS specific scenario) datasets, confirming its high applicability and reliability in the non-stationary, high-noise ICS environment.

title: Machine Learning–Driven Big Data Architecture for Smart Construction Applications
authors: Guanyu Yuan, Xinyue Li
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: To address key challenges in traditional intelligent construction-inefficient multi-source heterogeneous data integration, inadequate temporal prediction accuracy, delayed anomaly response, and experience-dependent decision-making-this paper proposes a machine learning-driven big data architecture. It features a six-tier logical system enabling unified data access via standardized interfaces and domain ontology modeling, hybrid machine learning model clusters with attention mechanisms for enhanced semantic association and temporal feature capture, and hybrid storage with spatiotemporal indexing to boost retrieval efficiency. Experimental results show the architecture reduces data processing time by 45.8%, achieves a construction progress prediction R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.923, an anomaly detection F 1 score of 91.7%, and an average decision response time <56 ms, outperforming traditional approaches and providing a viable technical solution for intelligent construction.

title: Optimizing Deep Feature Extraction for Enhanced Object Detection in Low-Light Environments Using Fine-Tuned Convolutional Networks
authors: Vijilius Helena Raj, K.Pithambar, Amit Dutt, Rallabandi Venkata Santoshi Saraswati Swetha Nagini, Venkata Siva Bathula, Dinesh Kumar Yadav
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Due to low light levels and contrast, the environment is a critical factor that affects the object detection systems’ performance. This work introduces ResLightNet, a new method to improve recognition precision for object detection in such contexts through transfer learning with ResNet. Thus, ResLightNet enforces the feature extraction of inherent in ResNet and built on the basis of COCO dataset to enhance the performance in recognizing object in a badly lit environment. The method entails making changes to the final layers of ResNet to be suited for low light images so as to retain features that low light end-to-end detection models omit. Performance evaluation proves that ResLightNet achieves better performance compared to baseline models, providing the reliable solution for object detection applications in conditions of weak illumination, for example, surveillance systems and autonomous vehicles. The primary objective of this research is to improve object detection precision in low-light conditions utilizing the suggested ResLightNet model.

title: Fine-Grained Lifetime Control for Heterogeneous Service Provisioning in Energy-Constrained Edge-Edge Systems_supp1-3691568.pdf
authors: Tian Wang
publish: N/A
abstract: To support delay-critical applications, migrating services from the cloud to edge servers (ESs) can effectively reduce service delay. However, in such a resource-constrained scenario, providing heterogeneous services to meet diverse user needs poses significant challenges. Specifically, ESs have limited computational resources and are often energy-constrained, complicating service placement and provisioning. Existing studies propose collaboration schemes to improve resource utilization and reduce service delay. Nevertheless, these works heavily depend on full-service coverage by the cloud or rely on coarse-grained (e.g., time-cycle level) strategies that inevitably waste energy in idle time slots, which are unsuitable for energy-constrained settings and dynamic edge environments. To address these challenges, we propose a novel edge-edge collaboration method tailored for heterogeneous edge service provisioning in energy-constrained networks. First, we formulate the heterogeneous service provisioning problem with the objective of delay minimization under energy constraints and prove its NP-hardness. Our framework leverages latest task statistics to decide the service lifetime in a fine-grained time slot level, so that enables edge collaboration to maximize resource utilization and adaptability in resource-limited conditions. Specifically, we decompose the problem into three subproblems: service placement, service lifetime decision, and task scheduling, and we design targeted lightweight solutions for each, ensuring low delay and efficient energy usage. Finally, we validate our method through comprehensive simulations on real-world datasets and implement it on a testbed with three ESs. Results demonstrate that our approach reduces service delays by an average of 69.6% across various energy-constrained scenarios.

title: Optimised Brain Tumour Detection through Soft Computing and Evolutionary Algorithms
authors: Ankit Raj, Chethan Sharma, Laxman Tripathi, Abhijeet Barsaiyan
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Brain tumor detection plays a vital role in medical diagnostics, as early and accurate identification significantly impacts treatment outcomes and patient survival. Manual interpretation of MRI scans is often labor-intensive and subject to diagnostic variability among radiologists. Deep learning techniques, particularly Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), have demonstrated high potential in automating this task, with models like VGG16 and ResNet18 showing promising classification results. However, achieving optimal performance from these models typically requires precise hyperparameter tuning, which can be challenging.This study proposes an optimized brain tumor classification framework that integrates evolutionary algorithms—specifically Genetic Algorithm (GA) and Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO)—with deep learning models to automate hyperparameter optimization. Two benchmark datasets, Br35H and Brain Tumor MRI Dataset, are utilized to validate the proposed approach. Experimental results demonstrate that the optimized models achieve superior accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-scores compared to their baseline counterparts. This integration of evolutionary algorithms with deep learning offers a robust, reliable, and efficient solution for brain tumor classification, suitable for deployment in real-world clinical scenarios.

title: Deep Learning-Based Speech Analysis for Early Detection of Alzheimer’s Disease
authors: S. Rajaprakash, Sadish Sendil Murugaraj, K.Boopalan, Dinesh Kumar, Harish M, M.S Chandru
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: AD is one of the most important global healthcare challenges in the 21st century, and its early detection is of prime importance for effective intervention and treatment. This work proposes a broad, multimodal framework that leverages state-of-the-art self-supervised learning through Wav2Vec2 in the clinically validated DementiaBank speech corpus for early AD detection. Our proposed architecture integrates pre-trained Wav2Vec2 embeddings with rich linguistic features through a hybrid system that combines temporal convolutional networks with cross-modal attention mechanisms. The model processes samples of spontaneous speech from standardized picture description tasks to capture both subtle acoustic patterns and higher-level complex linguistic biomarkers of cognitive decline. Extensive experimentation on the DementiaBank dataset shows state-of-the-art performance with high rate.

authors: Zhonghao Liang, Qunying Liao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Theory
abstract: It is well known that maximum distance separable (in short, MDS) codes and linear complementary dual (in short, LCD) codes are very important in coding theory and practice. In 2023, Yue et al. [29] constructed three classes of LCD MDS codes via a class of TGRS codes with one twists, i.e., (∗)-TGRS codes. Recently, Wu et al. [31] also constructed several classes of LCD MDS codes via a class of TGRS codes with two twists. In this paper, we unify their constructions by defining the (∗)-(L,P)- twisted generalized Reed-Solomon (in short, (∗)-(L,P)-TGRS) code, give the parity-check matrix of (∗)-(L,P)-TGRS codes, and then construct four classes of LCD codes. Finally, some corresponding examples are given.

title: AnomalyX: An Adaptive Threat Detection System Using Retrieval-Augmented Generation and Advanced Time-Series Analytics
authors: Jnyanadeep B, Sneha M, Bipin Raj C, Khushal A
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: In the rapidly evolving landscape of cybersecurity, traditional anomaly detection methods struggle to detect zero-day attacks, polymorphic malware, and multistage threats. This paper presents AnomalyX, an adaptive threat detection system that leverages Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and advanced time-series analytics to intelligently identify anomalies in real-time. The proposed system integrates machine learning models with comprehensive public network datasets such as CICIDS and UNSW-NB15, offering intelligent real-time anomaly identification that continuously learns and adapts to emerging network behaviors. Using InfluxDB for efficient data storage and a sophisticated machine learning pipeline combining TimeGPT, LSTM-based RNNs, and Elasticsearch’s built-in ML capabilities, AnomalyX represents a transformative solution in network security. The hybrid approach ensures both real-time anomaly detection and long-term predictive insights, providing unprecedented insights and proactive threat mitigation capabilities.

title: A Comparative Study on the Learning Efficiency of AI Agents: Progressive Training vs. One-Shot Training
authors: Xin-Zhu Li, Hsiao-Hsien Hsu, Wen-Hung Chao
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 9th International Conference on Education and E-Learning
abstract: None

title: To Err Are LLMs: Profiling Hallucinations in Generated Summaries of Maritime Accident Reports
authors: Yu Yuan, Qinke Wang, Chuangchuang Ma, Weiping Ding
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computational Intelligence
abstract: Maritime accident reports (MARs) are vital for safety, but their complexity hinders effective utilization. Large Language Models (LLMs) offer a solution for automated summarization, yet their propensity for hallucinations presents risks in this safety-critical domain. This study addresses these challenges through five key contributions. First, we introduce a fine-grained framework for analyzing hallucinations in maritime contexts, featuring a specialized taxonomy and evaluation methodology. Second, our statistical analysis of summaries from leading LLMs reveals distinct behaviors: while capable models (e.g., ChatGPT) benefit from in-context learning, less capable open-source models (e.g., LLaMa, Qwen) often degrade with increased examples, exhibiting a “context distraction” effect. We also demonstrate that standard automatic quality metrics fail to correlate with hallucination frequency, invalidating their use for safety assessment. Third, we present MARitime Hallucination Annotated Summaries (MARHAS), a novel dataset with detailed hallucination annotations. Fourth, we conduct a Pareto efficiency analysis of human versus LLM performance, identifying LLM few-shot learning as the optimal strategy that outperforms human experts in speed while maintaining comparable safety. Fifth, we propose an automated factuality benchmarking system, showing that full-document context is crucial for accurate fact grounding. These findings provide a roadmap for reliable LLM deployment in maritime safety, emphasizing the need for capable base models and rigorous, domain-specific verification. These contributions benefit future computational and linguistic studies in maritime safety and other safety-critical domains.

title: AHighly Selective Third-order Filtering Cross polarization Converter Using Guided Wave Inspired Metasurface
authors: Hongyu Shi, Yujie Li, Junyi Wang, Xiaoming Chen, Jianjia Yi, Juan Chen, Anxue Zhang
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: This work proposes a polarization-independent third order filtering polarization converter with enhanced frequency selectivity (sharp roll-off) in the S-band. The design employs an L-shaped guided-wave structure on the bottom layer to efficiently convert linearly polarized incident waves into cross-polarized waves. The meandered metallic line and defected ground structure individually introduce one cross-polarized reflection zero at separate frequencies, collectively forming a third-order filtering response that enhances passband flatness while achieving sharp roll-off characteristics. An equivalent circuit model is established to analyze and validate the design mechanism. The proposed unit cell exhibits an insertion loss of 0.71 dB within the passband, out-of-band rejection exceeding 20 dB in both lower and upper transition bands, and the polarization conversion ratio (PCR) achieving 99.7% at 2.7 GHz. Simulated and measured results validate the superior frequency selective and polarization-conversion performance, indicating its potential for radar systems and satellite communications.

title: Shared-Aperture Wideband End-fire/Broadside Dielectric Resonator Antenna for 5G Millimeter-Wave Terminal
authors: Jian-Xin Chen, Yao-Xi Chen, Xin-Hao Ding, Lei Wang, Wen-Wen Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation

title: FlickerTac: Flickering LED Driven Photometric Stereo for Event Vision-Based Tactile Sensors
authors: Mohamad Halwani, Akram Khairi, Hussain Sajwani, Eslam Sherif, Laith AbuAssi, Sajid Javed, Abdelqader Abusafieh, Abdulla Ayyad, Yahya Zweiri
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Tactile sensing at high speed and resolution is critical for robotic perception and control. Existing vision-based tactile sensors (VBTSs) achieve high spatial accuracy but suffer from degraded performance in dynamic interactions due to motion blur and low frame rates, limiting their effectiveness in fast manipulation. Event-based cameras, with their high-temporal-resolution output, offer a promising alternative—but their inability to capture static scenes and absolute intensities has hindered their use for dense 3D reconstruction even under static contact conditions. We introduce FlickerTac, the first Event Vision-Based Tactile Sensor (EVBTS) capable of performing photometric stereo-based dense 3D reconstruction. FlickerTac replaces conventional 3-channel RGB illumination with time-multiplexed, phase-shifted flickering LEDs to encode directional shading into temporally separated channels. A lightweight neural network then maps these spatio-temporal representations directly to surface gradients, which are integrated to recover dense depth maps. Our design enables high-speed, accurate tactile 3D geometry estimation. Extensive experiments demonstrate that FlickerTac achieves millimeter-scale depth accuracy competitive with state-of-the-art VBTSs, while operating at 125 Hz—unmatched by existing frame-based VBTSs.

title: HOTFLoc++: End-to-End Hierarchical LiDAR Place Recognition, Re-Ranking, and 6-DoF Metric Localisation in Forests
authors: Ethan Griffiths, Maryam Haghighat, Simon Denman, Clinton Fookes, Milad Ramezani
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: This article presents HOTFLoc++, an end-to-end hierarchical framework for LiDAR place recognition, re-ranking, and 6-DoF metric localisation in forests. Leveraging an octree-based transformer, our approach extracts features at multiple granularities to increase robustness to clutter, self-similarity, and viewpoint changes in challenging scenarios, including ground-to-ground and ground-to-aerial in forest and urban environments. We propose learnable multi-scale geometric verification to reduce re-ranking failures due to degraded single-scale correspondences. Our joint training protocol enforces multi-scale geometric consistency of the octree hierarchy via joint optimisation of place recognition with re-ranking and localisation, improving place recognition convergence. Our system achieves comparable or lower localisation errors to baselines, with runtime improvements of almost two orders of magnitude over RANSAC-based registration for dense point clouds. Experimental results on public datasets show the superiority of our approach compared to state-of-the-art methods, achieving an average Recall@1 of 90.7% on CS-Wild-Places: an improvement of 29.6 percentage points over baselines, while maintaining high performance on single-source benchmarks with an average Recall@1 of 91.7% and 97.9% on Wild-Places and MulRan, respectively. Our method achieves under 2 m and 5<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{\circ }$</tex-math></inline-formula> error for 97.2% of 6-DoF registration attempts, with our multi-scale re-ranking module reducing localisation errors by <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\sim \!2\times$</tex-math></inline-formula> on average. The code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/csiro-robotics/HOTFLoc</uri>.

title: Design and Optimization of a Novel Rectangular-Inset Fed Antenna with Slot-Notch for Dual-Band Operational Applications
authors: Shyampradeep K, Madhushankara M, Ribu Mathew
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Microstrip patch antennas are widely used in wireless compact radio-frequency devices due to their ease of fabrication, low profile and low cost. Conventional rectangular inset-fed antenna with a 3.6 GHz operating frequency provides excellent impedance matching but with limited bandwidth and single-band resonance. Thus, there is a need for multiband operation with a miniaturized antenna footprint for energy harvesting applications. Geometric optimization of the antenna is performed by varying the patch length and width. The optimized geometry of the proposed antenna is 26.6×35.3×1.575 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> with Rogers RT5880 as a substrate. Optimization of the design converges the return loss at -36.62 dB as the optimized value. A modified antenna with rectangular slots and circular notches is proposed with symmetry for multiband operation. The modified antenna resulted in dual-band operation at 3.6 GHz and 9.66 GHz, providing better gain, surface current distribution and radiation efficiency of 99.79%. This novel design finds its application in multiband operating systems for energy harvesting applications in wireless communication systems.

title: Fake News Detection Using Large Language Models (LLMs)
authors: Amey Medewar, Ankush D. Sawarkar, Atul R. Halmare, Rajesh Mapari, Shrutika Sumit Mankar, Shrinivas Khedkar
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The rapid proliferation of misinformation, often referred to as fake news, has emerged as one of the most critical challenges in the digital information age. Social media platforms, online forums, and instant communication channels enable the large-scale dissemination of misleading or fabricated content within seconds, making it nearly impossible for human fact-checkers to respond in real time. Such content has profound social, political, and economic consequences. This paper investigates the use of Large Language Models (LLMs) for automated fake news detection. We explore how retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), claim verification, and adversarial robustness can enhance the reliability of LLM-driven misinformation detection frameworks. The study evaluates multiple datasets, including LIAR, FakeNewsNet, and FEVER, and introduces a modular architecture to improve interpretability and resilience under adversarial conditions.

title: Design of a Boost Converter and Dual RF Energy Rectifying and Voltage Multiplying System for AS7038RB Biosensor Using 180nm CMOS Process
authors: Angel Grace D. Dairocas, Shaina Joyce B. Galula, Teofe M. Osin, Re-Ann Cristine O. Calimpusan, Edgardo Ricardo B. Sajonia, Jeffrey T. Dellosa, Jefferson A. Hora
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: This study presents the successful design and implementation of a dual RF energy harvesting system and a dedicated DC-DC boost converter for the AS7038RB biosensor. The system collects ambient energy from two RF sources—2.4 GHz and 5 GHz—using a voltage multiplier to charge a 1.5 V battery. This battery then powers the boost converter, which steps up the voltage to a regulated 3.8 V output across a load current range of 0.5 μA to 1 mA. To maximize efficiency under light load conditions, the converter operates in Pulse Frequency Modulation (PFM) mode, minimizing switching losses. The design incorporates a current sensor, zero-current detector (ZCD), off-time controller, and bandgap voltage reference to maintain precise output regulation, low quiescent current, and stable operation. The system achieves a peak efficiency of 85.08% at 900 μA load current, proving the feasibility of efficient, self-powered operation for compact, wearable biosensing applications without external power sources.

title: CRC-Aided External Iterative LDPC Decoding Algorithm for Slow Frequency-Hopping Interference Scenarios
authors: Xianbo Yang, Lei Xiao, Ye Liu, Yinzhi Luo, Yu Du, Huawei Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: In short-burst slow frequency-hopping (SFH) gaussian minimum shift keying (GMSK) systems under strong partial-band interference (PBI), the slot-varying interference state causes severe mismatch of the input log-likelihood ratios (LLR) for new radio low-density parity-check (NR-LDPC) decoding when channel state information (CSI) is unavailable. To address this issue, this paper proposes a cyclic redundancy check (CRC)-aided external iterative interference estimation and decoding scheme. Instead of using CRC only for post-decoding error detection, CRC-validated code blocks are exploited to reconstruct reliable reference symbols, based on which a slot-level residual metric is formed to estimate the interference impact and adaptively reweight the LLR in an external loop. The proposed approach effectively suppresses the propagation of high-confidence errors in the Tanner graph and accelerates the convergence of LDPC decoding. Simulation results under typical PBI scenarios demonstrate that the proposed scheme significantly improves the bit error rate (BER) performance and approaches the ideal CSI benchmark without introducing additional pilot overhead.

title: Distributed Congestion Accounting and Auditability for Traffic Control Networks
authors: Sumana S, Tintu George, Ananda Ranjith Kumar Mukkala, M. Elakkiya, Venkata Narayana T., Veeraswamy Ammisetty
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Urban traffic control increasingly relies on distributed edge sensors and controllers, but lacks auditable accounting of congestion and allocations. This paper proposes a lightweight distributed congestion accounting and auditing framework that combines normalized credit units (NCUs), signed local claims, and periodic reconciliation to ensure accountability and detect misreports. Researchers design a unified schema and dataset (real and SUMO synthetic variants), implement a signed-log with Merkle-aggregation and gossip reconciliation, and evaluate under Clean, Noisy, Adversarial, and Combined scenarios, measuring accuracy, detection (precision/recall/F1), audit overhead, latency, and scalability. Experiments show near-oracle accuracy in benign conditions, high detection AUC (>0.9) in most settings, and audit overhead an order of magnitude lower than full-replication blockchain, with tuneable trade-offs between latency and overhead. The approach enables practical, privacy-aware auditability for real-time traffic control on resource-constrained edge deployments, informing pilot rollouts and future work on ZK proofs and hardware acceleration.

title: Towards Robust Brain Tumour Segmentation: ResUNet+ with Residual Blocks and Combined Loss Approach
authors: Shubhankar Gulati, Garv Mittra, Tisha Patel
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The precise segmentation of brain lesions in magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) is essential for the effective diagnosis and treatment planning. Traditional deep learning models, especially those that just use ResNet encoders, frequently struggle to strike a balance between spatial accuracy, class imbalance, and contextual feature representation. In this work, we provide a 3D ResUNet+ architecture that combines UNet-style skip connections with residual encoding to accomplish both fine-grained localization and deep semantic comprehension. We utilize a composite Dice–Cross Entropy (DCE) loss, which combines region-overlap sensitivity with categorical discrimination, to counteract the imbalance among tumour subregions and enhance delineation accuracy. To further increase data heterogeneity and improve model resilience, we also use structured rotational augmentation (90°, 180°, and 270°). In comparison to a typical ResNet baseline, experiments on the BraTS 2020 dataset show consistent improvements in segmentation performance compared to a 3D ResNet baseline, with better border identification and higher mean Dice scores. We note that the evaluation is limited to a single baseline and not directly compared against state-of-the-art models such as nnU-Net or TransBTS, which will be addressed in future work. The suggested method emphasizes how important customized hybrid architectures and loss optimization techniques are to improving automated medical image segmentation systems' dependability.

title: Automated Feature Engineering in Big Data Environments
authors: Aravind Satyanarayanan, Gayatri Tavva, Vinaychand Muppala, Ankit Srivastava, Mihir Dhakan, Quazi Taif Sadat
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: With the fast increase in large, heterogeneous data, the feature engineering problem has become highly complicated and, until now, one of the most expensive and labour-intensive steps in the analytics of big data. Conventional manual feature engineering methods tend to be ineffective, prone to errors, and cannot support the scale, speed, and diversity of the modern data streams. In order to overcome such challenges, the paper provides a holistic approach to Automated Feature Engineering (AFE) in big data environments that combines scalable data preprocessing, intelligent generation, selection, and transformation schemes in distributed computing systems. The suggested algorithm takes advantage of statistic learning, meta-feature-based learning and optimization-based search methods in building high quality feature representations out of raw data automatically. There is also parallel execution and adaptive resource management in the framework so that it can be scaled in cloud-based and cluster-driven systems like Apache Spark. Large scale experimental analysis of benchmark data sets has shown that the proposed AFE framework leads to a substantial improvement in predictive performance with a lower feature engineering time and human intervention as compared to traditional handcrafted and semi-automated approaches. The findings validate the usefulness of automated feature engineering as an enabler to robust, scalable, and efficient machine learning workflows in the decision-making system based on big data.

title: ReportSent-Net: A Hybrid BiGRU–Attention Model for Analyzing Suicide Reporting Compliance and Public Sentiment
authors: C.Renuga, Nakshatra N S, Sadhanaa S. P, Saanvi, Monika D, Liana Samuel
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Suicide reporting in digital media significantly influences public perception, mental health discourse, and potentially vulnerable audiences. However, many reports fail to comply with ethical standards, such as those outlined by the World Health Organization (WHO), often sensationalizing incidents or omitting mental health resources. The paper presents ReportSent-Net, a novel hybrid deep learning framework designed to evaluate suicide-related content through a dual-task approach—assessing reporting compliance and analyzing public sentiment. The model integrates Bidirectional Gated Recurrent Units (BiGRU) for contextual understanding and a multi-head attention mechanism to focus on ethically and emotionally salient segments. Pre-trained RoBERTa embeddings are combined with engineered features aligned to WHO guidelines, including indicators like omission of suicide methods, presence of helplines, and avoidance of glorification. The system was evaluated on a curated dataset comprising news articles and social media content, manually annotated for compliance and sentiment. ReportSent-Net achieved 92.75% accuracy in compliance classification and 91.35% accuracy in sentiment prediction, outperforming conventional baselines including LSTM, CNN-BiLSTM, SVM, and RoBERTa classifiers. An ablation study confirmed the importance of both ethical and emotional features in enhancing model performance. The framework also includes an explainable AI layer using SHAP and attention heatmaps, aiding in the interpretability of predictions. The research contributes to responsible media analytics by offering an automated, real-time tool for evaluating the ethical integrity and emotional tone of suicide-related reporting, which can be deployed in editorial workflows and mental health oversight systems.
